# Thread: Please help with AM cortisol...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-am-cortisol.6612/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trinity — 2013-04-04T01:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-am-cortisol.6612/post-82750>

Hi All,

I have been struggling with an inverted cortisol rhythm and accompanying chronic insomnia for at least 4 years now (low AM cortisol, high PM cortisol). Up until January this year, I had been on 15-20 mg of Cortef per day in an effort to correct this imbalance. After continuing to struggle with my sleep and taking multiple ASI tests, I started to suspect that the Cortef might not be fixing the underlying problem. While it did boost my AM levels enough to mask the worst of the fatigue from a horribly disrupted sleep pattern, it never really seemed to fix the nighttime cortisol surges and sleep has been a constant struggle.

In the beginning of this year I found Dr. Kruse's website and began to implement some of his suggestions.

1) I started paying more attention to light cycles (getting light first thing in the AM and wearing blue blockers in the PM)

2) I cleaned up my diet. My current diet can best be described as low-ish carb lacto-paleo . Based on prior food allergy testing, I do not have any evidence of dairy intolerance so I have been hesitant to eliminate dairy. I do, however, make an effort to purchase organic, pastured dairy products as much as possible.

3) I began CT. I have been doing almost daily CT (cold water immersion for 30 minutes) for almost 3 months now and have seen some evidence of increased alpha-MSH (based upon a slight, but noticeable all over darkening of my very fair skin tone in the absence of significant sun exposure). I can now sit in 55 degree water for 30 minutes without any shivering during or after.

My sleep is still a bit up and down, but has improved significantly from where it was a few months ago. I should also note that I've tapered my Cortef from 20mg down to 5mg per day.

I recently did another ASI to gauge progress. While far from optimal, it showed a significant improvement (lowering) in my PM cortisol levels. Unfortunately, there was only a very modest improvement in my AM cortisol levels (they are still low normal). While I'm pleased to be sleeping somewhat better, my daytime mood, energy, and motivation could use some help. I can function, but I feel like a zombie and I can't muster up enough energy or motivation to do much more than the bare minimum most of the time. As a person who has always been successful and self motivated (prior to developing these issues), this is very frustrating.

Basically, I need some advice about how to improve my AM cortisol. I am considering 2 options:

1) increase cortef again -- tempting because i'm tired of feeling tired, unmotivated, and mildly depressed all of the time and my daytime mood and functioning was actually better when I was taking more cortef.

2) try cycloset - i actually have a prescription for it, but have not filled it. I have a really good, open minded endocrinologist who agreed to let me try it. I am not diabetic, but my fasting blood sugar is not great (typically runs 100-110) and I am obese (5'5" and ~200 pounds). I have had the cycloset prescription for a couple of months now but have been hesitant to try it because I can't find any clear cut information about whether or not I could become dependent on it. After almost managing to finally get off the Cortef, the last thing I want to do is substitute one "addiction" for another. I had been hoping that the CT and light would improve this without having to resort to another drug.

3) ? - maybe there is another option I don't know about -- please inform me if there is  I am really tired of dealing with these hormonal problems. I've been struggling 5-7 years now. Since this all began in my mid twenties, I feel like I've lost some of the best years of my life to this. Its really depressing to think about dealing with this for the rest of my life.

My current daily regimen is:

25 mg DHEA
25 mg 7-Keto DHEA
50 mg Pregnenolone
5 mg Cortef
30 mcg cytomel - finally got the reverse t3 under control
25 mcg synthroid
100-200 mg progesterone (sublingual, 100 during 1st half of cycle, 200 during 2nd half)
10,000 Vitamin D
200 mg CoQ10
400 IU vitamin E
Daily probiotic
Weekly Vitamin B injections

Thanks,
Trinity

### andee — 2013-04-04T03:11:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-am-cortisol.6612/post-82781>

Trinity - wow you have done so much! And showing some really good improvement too. I have the same adrenal pattern of low-middle of range a.m. (which we both know really means way too low) and a previously very high level in the evening and night time - those p.m. levels have been coming down while the a.m. has been rising - but it's not perfect yet.

Like you I have used cortef but decided not to use it any longer to crank up my a.m. cortisol - there must be a way to heal my adrenals. I found that when I used cortef or over-the-counter isocort I tended to keep increasing my doses cause I still felt exhausted. This did not help my adrenals...

My adrenal profile has improved every time I've tested it over the past 4-5 months, since embarking on the Leptin Reset Also I do take Cycloset, and it really lowers my a.m. fasting blood sugar, which should mean it's restoring my dopamine levels. I do not feel any addictive energy around Cycloset, and recently forgot to pack it on a trip so just skipped it - that affected my blood sugar a bit but did not give me any kind of withdrawal feelings, if that's what you're concerned about.

Anyway, here are a few questions I had for you -

\- Did you actually do the Leptin Reset, or just bits and pieces? Specifically, did you use the BAB? For me, that really was a metabolic reset.

\- How are your thyroid labs? Adrenals and thyroid are so intertwined, usually if one is off the other will be too. One relatively new protocol for healing adrenals without more cortef is to take a good portion of your Cytomel 3-4 hours before you officially get out of bed. This is when the adrenals should be kicking into high gear and the extra T3 helps them rock - at least that is the theory. I can find a link to that if you're interested. Or just look around at [http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com) and you should find some posts.

Like you I take cytomel (also originally started because of Reverse T3). Am now working my way back to Armour thyroid. Synthroid and other phramaceutical T4s make me gain half a pound a week regardless of how I eat - so I don't take any synthetic T4. Once I got my thryoid balanced, I shed about 20 pounds just eating lo-carb (which had never worked for me before) - and then the last 10 lbs went on epi-paleo - so I am thinking it might be helpful for you to look at your thyroid labs, including thyroid antibodies etc. if not done recently.

If you're not at the top 10% of the range for Free T3 and have no reverse T3, there's room for improvement - you probably are aware of this but am mentioning it just in case. Also as you probably know, depression is one of THE top symptoms of hypothyroid.

\- I assume you're taking progesterone without estrogen because you're in the estrogen-dominant years... I'm past those years and find that if my estrogen level goes too low I get depressed and bloated and begin to crave carbs and gain weight till I raise my E.

Anyway for me, I would check out the thyroid situation completely before going back to cortef and perhaps try an earlier morning dose of T3. I would also add an adrenal adaptogen - rhodiola is a good one - to help heal the adrenal imbalances naturally. And if you're curious about Cycloset, I can't see where that would be harmful as you endo has already approved it.

Except for the early a.m. thryoid protocol, I am not aware of any way to repair low adrenals quickly - that's part of what's so frustrating about adrenal exhaustion.

Before I forget -I think the sleep is one of the hardest things to correct - it is for me too - and lately Dr. K has emphasized the effects of artificial light and EMF on sleep - so you might think about your EMF exposure, over and above what you're already doing about artificial light.

But honestly you have accomplished a lot since the beginning of the year! Wishing you continued improvement...

**Consider eating according to (Epi-Paleo Rx).**

Abreviated

by calories count:
(30%Proteins)+(70%Fat)+(Green and other very low gycemic index vegetables, practically 0% calories)
Proteins=(50%shelfish)+ (some saltwater low on a food chain, fish) +(50% beef, pork, other mammals, eggs, no birds flesh)

Another way to describe proteins:
half, predominantly omega3 sources
the other half,
mostly omega6 sources
for starters
later 75%omega3, 25%omega6

Fat=coconut oil, palm oil, olive oil, macadamian nuts/oil, walnuts, beef tally, lard, clarified butter

Lately I have seen dr K recomending 60-80% fat, so, if you are able, go for 80%
the more fat you eat the easier to loose weight

spring water, mineral water, RO water only

\------------------------------------------------
When eating Cycloset (at wakeup)
monitor prolactin
Personally I adjust dose by aiming at lower number in prolactin range, only minimally below it. Say aim at 4ng/mL

Prolactin Female: 4.8-23.3 ng/mL
\-------------------------------------------------

> 25 mg 7-Keto DHEA
> 30 mcg cytomel - finally got the reverse t3 under control
> 25 mcg synthroid

**Thyroid**
Consider using NaturalThyroidHormone, either ERFA, Armour (by script),
or Thyroid-S, or Thiroyd from internet

1(T3)=4(T4)
25+4\*30=145
You are eating equivalent of 145mcg T4 or 2.2grains of NaturalThyroid Hormones, NTH

1grain=1Grain=(38mcg-T4)+(9mcg-T3)----->equivalent of 65mcgT4
38+4\*9=65mcgT4

145/65=2.2grains

You should not be eating thyroid hormones until you have
good morning cortisol levels
and
micronutrients in good standing

in this context, micronutrients are analytes measured by this test (plus iodine/iodide):

Spectracell Comprehensive Nutritional Panel
[http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular\_vitamin\_analysis\_spectracell.html](http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular_vitamin_analysis_spectracell.html)
Get this test, make corrections per test results.
Repeat test every 6 months until stabilized.

\---
Consider replacing you T3 & T4 with 2 grains of NTH
change to
100mg 7ketoDHEA

\----------------------------------------------------------------------------
**Steroid Hormones**

> 25 mg DHEA
> 50 mg Pregnenolone
> 5 mg Cortef
> 100-200 mg progesterone (sublingual, 100 during 1st half of cycle, 200 during 2nd half)

Lets make attempt on getting rid of 5mg Cortef

Use DHEA Micronized Lipid Matrix from Allergy Research

Use enough of DHEA pills until your blood test (preferably at LabCorp shows:
Women 275-400 μg/dL
preferably top of range (unless xyz)

\-----
Ideally in first half of cycle you want

E2~30-40 pg/mL
progesterone~1.5ng/mL

[http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikiped...terone,\_LH\_and\_FSH\_during\_menstrual\_cycle.svg](http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c8/Hormones_estradiol,_progesterone,_LH_and_FSH_during_menstrual_cycle.svg)

to get there, you are taking now
50 mg Pregnenolone
5 mg Cortef
100-200 mg progesterone

I suggest that
you drop all above and replace it with
as much
pregnenolone MLM (Micronized Lipid Matrix 150mg tabs scored (from Nutricology)
as it takes to get (progesterone~1.5ng/mL)
when blood is drawn at LabCorp on a day 3-4 of menstrual cycle

You may want to start with two of those tabs,
then titrate up or down as per blood test results.

for now
you could keep the
additional 100mg sublingual progesterone that you taking in second half,
or drop it and replace with
additional pregnenolone tab
then draw blood on day 19-21

goals
E2~120pg/mL
prog~15-25ng/dL

\---------------------
Dr Kruse is a proponent of high P/E ratio, up to 500
Dr Lam likes high P/E but up to 200

You may want to aim at estradiol/estrogen numbers as per population averages
(see my numbers above)
but you may want to aim at larger progesterone values.
Use table below to guide you.

Ideally, using pregnenolone MLM should give you overall better result (and closer to natural ways).
Your progesterone and cortisol levels should be more natural.
\----
I am a 73yo man.
Since I figured to use MLM pregnenolone, I was able to get much better control over my Steroid Hormone Panel
Doctors, even those who would give a lip service to pregnenolone are still missing great deal on it.

It takes a while for swallowed pregnenolone to raise your morning cortisol.
If you are going to bathroom 1-2 hrs before actual wakeup time, take first 150mg at that time.
Otherwise, do not interrupt your sleep.
But when you get up, take it right then and there.
The next thing you do is to open window and look at the sun.
Better, if you have backyard, go there (barefoot, scantly clothed) watch sun there.
After 2-3 minutes outside, do BAB, or eat BAB standing on the grass.

All this to improve morning cortisol.

### JanSz — 2013-04-04T15:11:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-am-cortisol.6612/post-82819>

cont from previous post

Your demand for (pregnenolone MLM) will depend on your current pregnenolone level and overall response.
This must be guided by blood tests.
I am man. Man's pregnenolone levels are similar to women's, progesterone is similar to woman's but only in first half of cycle.

I started with hardly detectable pregnenolone and progesterone levels.
At first I had to go up to 900mg/day to reach good levels, that lasted few months.
Latter, likely I reached saturation point and had to titrate down.
I am in that phase now. I am using now 150mg/day (preg MLM).
Now is two years since I started this program. I think I will likely settle at something higher than 150mg/day.

As you may gather, this project requires patience and persistence and ability to track numbers.
You have to be in charge,
even willing doctor does not have a time to track so many numbers.
Preferably build spreadsheet so you can monitor your progress.

\-------------

Pregnenolone
Males (18-58 Years) 13-208 ng/dL
Premenopausal Females (18-51 Years) 7-188 ng/dL

### nonchalant — 2013-04-04T18:15:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-am-cortisol.6612/post-82845>

Thanks for the refresher/update on pregnenolone, JanSz. I didn't realize taking pregnenolone very early in the day could help raise cortisol.
Trinity, please be sure to look at the sun just as it rises. You can look at it directly when it is low on the horizon. Hope it isn't TMI, but often after staring at it for a minute, I have to go inside to the bathroom because that brief exposure raises my cortisol and stimulates the bowels.

Also, how is your sun exposure during the day? I find that it is a great source of energy if you can sit outside during lunch. It has been cloudy for several days here and though it is heresy in Texas to wish for the sun to come back...

### Trinity — 2013-04-05T04:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-am-cortisol.6612/post-82924>

Hi guys,

Wow! I did not expect to get so much good feedback. Thank you all for your help

> Consider eating according to (Epi-Paleo Rx).

I know there is still some room for improvement in my diet, but I've been trying to approach this as a lifestyle change rather than a "diet". Therefore, as much as I want to be "optimal", I am also aware of the need to ensure that the changes I make are sustainable for \*me\* in the long term.

I am planning to make an effort to increase my seafood intake, but I'm not sure if I am ready to eat the amount of seafood Dr. K recommends on a daily basis  Right now I would estimate that I am somewhere around 75-100 carbs per day with my primary source of carbs being green vegetables and smaller amounts of organic whole milk, organic greek yogurt, low sugar fruits (primarily berries), nuts (primarily almonds and macadamias), and the occasional square of 86% dark chocolate. I also get a decent amount of fat, but no where near 80% of my calories. I have also made an effort to incorporate more omega 3 sources of fat into my diet including pastured butter, macadamia nut oil, and coconut cream. I would like to say that I only eat grain free pastured meat, but it tends to be very expensive and difficult to find around here so I usually settle for the publix green-wise brand. Although I eat a grain free, sugar free diet, I still haven't managed to entirely give up the artificial sweeteners and that is an area that I'm working on. I have cut back on them, but I still use some splenda sweetened syrup in my coffee and have the occasional diet coke.

I understand that Dr. Kruse advocates a much lower carb, ketogenic diet and I do realize that reducing my carb intake might help facilitate weight loss, but I am hesitant to reduce my carbs that low right now. In the past I have tried to reduce carbs to a ketogenic level, but it wasn't sustainable for me due to severe adverse effects on my mood, energy, and sleep. A ketogenic level of carbs seems to significantly worsen my adrenal fatigue and cortisol problems. That may eventually change as my hormone function improves, but right now I need to balance my efforts to improve my diet and lifestyle with my ability to function at work and in my life. I am already struggling with mood and energy issues and I simply cannot afford to make them worse right now.

I have not done the official leptin reset yet, but I have made an effort to incorporate the BAB and reduce snacking. Right now my breakfast consists of:

\- 2 scoops of unsweetened organic whey protein (~50 g protein)
\- 1 cup of organic, pastured milk (~ 10 g protein )
\- 1 tbsp unsweetened cocoa powder
\- 1 packet of stevia

I am pretty sure that I am no where near leptin sensitive because the BAB does not seem to reduce my appetite at all. I am not sure why that is except that I'm probably insulin resistant with fasting blood sugars in the 100-110 range. I have also reduced my daytime snacking. I used to have small snacks mid morning and mid afternoon to prevent my blood sugar from crashing, but I don't seem to have those crashes as much anymore after cleaning up my diet and eliminating the sugar and grains. Although I seldom snack I have not yet succeeded in giving up my mid morning and early afternoon coffees. I have, however, switched over from using milk to using coconut cream in them since coconut cream has a negligible amount of carbs and sugar compared to milk.

I see my endocrinologist quarterly and he is pretty thorough about testing. Thankfully, I have decent insurance so most (but not all) of my labwork is covered.

Here are my two most recent ASI tests:

MOST RECENT CORTISOL TEST RESULTS: DATE COLLECTED 3/14/13

Time Me Range %
8AM 4.38 3.5-6.3 32%
12PM 2.19 1.4-2.8 55%
4PM 1.85 .8-2.4 65%
8PM 1.31 .6-1.6 71%
12AM 1.15 .3-1.2 95%
4AM 1.33 .3-1.7 72%

DHEA: very bottom of the normal range

PREVIOUS CORTISOL TEST RESULTS: Note this was right before i began CT

Time Me Range %
8AM 4.13 3.5-6.3 23%
12PM 2.67 1.4-2.8 90%
4PM 2.06 .8-2.4 40%
8PM 1.12 .6-1.6 50%
12AM 1.25 .3-1.2 105%
4AM 1.87 .3-1.7 112%

DHEA very bottom of the normal range

As you can see, there is a noticeable improvement in PM cortisol (12 AM and 4 AM samples). Though they are still higher than optimal, they have comedown so that they are no longer out of range. AM cortisol also improved a little bit (from 23% of range to 32%) although I feel like it is still way too low for good AM functioning. When I took the second test I had also reduced my Cortef from ~20mg to ~5mg so the fact there was \*any\* increase in AM cortisol is probably a good sign.

> Lets make attempt on getting rid of 5mg Cortef
>
> Use DHEA Micronized Lipid Matrix from Allergy Research
>
> Use enough of DHEA pills until your blood test (preferably at LabCorp shows:
> Women 275-400 μg/dL
> preferably top of range (unless xyz)

I agree that it would be nice to get off the Cortef once and for all and I'm seriously considering the cycloset option in addition to increasing my current DHEA and pregnenolone intake. I like your idea about taking the pregenolone before waking. I almost always wake up in the night so that shouldn't be a problem

I am also a bit confused about my DHEA. Between the first and second test I increased by DHEA from 15 to 25mg and added 25mg 7-keto DHEA. It does not, however, appear to have made any difference at all. As a woman with estrogen dominance I am concerned about overdoing the DHEA, but from my labs it looks like I definately need more. I am also uncertain when to take it since some information indicates that DHEA can reduce cortisol levels. I currently take 1/2 of the DHEA first thing in the AM and the other half at noon. I have wondered if the early AM DHEA might worsen my already deficient AM cortisol levels? Does anyone have any info on that? Currently, I am taking sublingual DHEA and pregnenolone from Douglas Labs. I am, however, interested in the micronized pregnenolone you mentioned.

> If you're not at the top 10% of the range for Free T3 and have no reverse T3, there's room for improvement - you probably are aware of this but am mentioning it just in case. Also as you probably know, depression is one of THE top symptoms of hypothyroid.

Yeah, there is probably still some room for improvement...When I began thyroid replacement, my TSH was only modestly elevated, but my reverse T3 was through the roof (50+) and my Free T3 was at the very bottom end of the range. It took me almost a year to work my way up to my current dose of cytomel (30 mcg) due to its tendency to worsen my adrenal fatigue and insomnia. Persistence did eventually pay off and I finally cleared out the reverse T3 about 6 months ago. I do realize that my Free T3 is probably still WAY too low, but I am hesitant to try to increase the cytomel much more while I'm still trying to taper the Cortef and improve my adrenal function. I have learned from my mistakes (after having several major crashes in the past resulting from trying increase my cytomel dose too quickly)!

> Consider using NaturalThyroidHormone, either ERFA, Armour (by script),
> or Thyroid-S, or Thiroyd from internet

Eventually, I might consider switching over to a natural thyroid replacement like Armour. My endo is not completely opposed to prescribing natural thyroid replacement but for now he thinks I need a greater percentage of T3 than Armour provides (since with the adrenal fatigue I still have a tendency to convert most of the T4 into reverse T3).

MOST RECENT THYROID TEST RESULTS:

TSH: 1.14 (.4 - 4.5)
Free T3: 2.8 (2.3 - 4.2 pg / mL)
Reverse T3: 6 (11-31 ng/dL)

> \- I assume you're taking progesterone without estrogen because you're in the estrogen-dominant years... I'm past those years and find that if my estrogen level goes too low I get depressed and bloated and begin to crave carbs and gain weight till I raise my E.

Yup, definately estrogen dominant. A couple of months ago, I did a full female hormone panel that tested E and P and multiple points accross the full monthly cycle. I suspected E
dominance in spite of progesterone replacement (200mg sublingual in 2nd half of cycle) because my cycle was consistently 22-23 days. Here are the results:

MOST RECENT SEX HORMONE TEST RESULTS:
E (pg/ml) P(pg/ml)
Day 2: 1.43 (1.3-4.3) 92 (50-100)
Day 12: 4.77 (3.0-8.0) 217 (100-300)
Day 14: 3.64 (1.0-5.1) 318 (200-600)
Day 16: 3.44 (1.0-5.0) 371 (200-600)
Day 18: 3.85 (3.0-6.5) 265 (100-600)
Day 24: 2.72 (2.5-6.5) 182 (50-300)

P/E ratio (extrapolated from Day 18 and Day 24 data points) : ~65

As you can see, my E / P ratio still was not great even on 200mg of progesterone. In an effort to counteract the E dominance, for the past 2 months I have also added a couple of pumps of Life Flow bioidentical progesterone cream (40 mg) during the last 7-10 days of my cycle. It may be working because my most recent period increased to 27 days. 200 mg sublingual + 40 mg transdermal seems like an awful lot of progesterone, but maybe my body needs that much. I am now testing my E/P ratio monthly until I achieve an optimal and stable ratio. I think optimizing this ratio is key because the severity of my adrenal fatigue and insomnia appear to be strongly tied to my monthly cycle as evidenced by a long standing tendency for my sleep to tank badly in the week prior to my period.

Thanks,
Trinity

### Trinity — 2013-04-11T01:50:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-am-cortisol.6612/post-83681>

Hi All,

OK, so these are the changes I'm currently making.

1) I've decided to try the cycloset. I am currently taking .8mg and will be slowly increasing (based upon severity of nausea side effect).

2) I'm changing from Douglas labs sublingual pregnenolone to the reccommended MLM brand.

Before I was taking 50mg pregnenolone:
25mg sublingual preg first thing upon rising
25mg sublingual preg at noon

Now I'm taking 100-150mg pregnenolone:
50mg MLM preg @ 4-5AM (if I wake up)
50mg MLM preg first thing upon rising
50mg MLM preg at noon

3) I'm changing from Douglas labs sublingual DHEA to the reccommended MLM brand.

Before I was taking 25mg DHEA and 25mg 7-Keto DHEA:
12.5mg sublingual DHEA first thing upon rising
25mg 7-Keto DHEA first thing upon rising
12.5mg sublingual DHEA at noon

Now I'm taking 50mg DHEA and 25mg 7-Keto DHEA:
25mg MLM DHEA first thing upon rising
25mg 7-keto DHEA first thing upon rising
25mg MLM DHEA at noon

Do those changes seem reasonable based upon my labs? I plan to retest early AM cortisol, DHEA, and testosterone (to make sure I'm not going out of range with that much DHEA) in a few weeks to see where things are heading.

Thanks,
Trinity

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-04-12T18:12:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-83908>

> [Rachelle said:](/goto/post?id=82832)
>
> I was thinking about getting the earthsafe whole house but have no experience with them. I hope someone chimes in here.
>
> Here is the site. [http://www.lessemf.com/schumann.html](http://www.lessemf.com/schumann.html)

I have the EarthSafe Whole House unit in my bedroom, and it is plugged into the grounding connection on one of the wall power sockets. I'm running it 24/7. I noticed a difference almost immediately upon powering it on. Also, when I walk into the house after work, it feels like a weight has been lifted from my shoulders...uncanny. In conjunction with earthing, and a Magnetico 10 gauss sleep pad, I'm experiencing a full night's sleep with no awakening and no need for bathroom breaks.

I started out just using an earthing strap attached to the K-1 kidney meridian on my foot, and noticed that my sleep seemed more refreshing. I did this for about a month and then decided to purchase a magnetic sleep pad. I then had a week's experience using the grounding strap/magnetic pad combo, and there was significant sleep improvement but I still woke up every once in a while. When I added the resonator, there was yet another significant improvement, and my sleep has been uninterrupted ever since. Every night feels like a small but noticeable improvement over the previous one, so I'm still moving toward some sort of equilibrium.

So...this has been my n=1 experience so far. As always, your mileage may vary. ;-)

Just wanted to mention that all of this is in addition to ditching the wi-fi, turning off the cell phone and other emf-generating appliances at night, and practicing light 'hygiene' after sundown. A meter for testing yourself is absolutely necessary, IMHO. My home environment appears to be relatively low emf, and I have a newer house with low-e windows which block a lot of external radiation. I do have a smart meter, though... :-( fortunately it's on the other side of the house away from the living areas.

Seems like all of the puzzle pieces that Jack is giving us are coming together. I sure am learning a lot!

### JackFrake — 2013-05-22T23:43:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-86684>

What's the difference between sleeping on a Magnetico vs. sleeping with a Schumann Generator under your mattress or next to your bed?

Aren't they basically doing the same thing? Is there a noticeable difference b/w sleeping with a Magnetico compared to adding a Schumann Generator, or is it marginal?

A Magnetico vs. Schumann for DUMMYs summary would be greatly appreciated!

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-05-23T04:22:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-86701>

> [JackFrake said:](/goto/post?id=86684)
>
> What's the difference between sleeping on a Magnetico vs. sleeping with a Schumann Generator under your mattress or next to your bed?
>
> Aren't they basically doing the same thing? Is there a noticeable difference b/w sleeping with a Magnetico compared to adding a Schumann Generator, or is it marginal?
>
> A Magnetico vs. Schumann for DUMMYs summary would be greatly appreciated!

Magnetico Pad = produces a static (not a wave) magnetic field...augments the Earth's field which has diminished over the decades.

Shumann resonator = generates electromagnetic wave resonating at 7.83 Hz which mimics the field in the cavity between the Earth's surface and the ionosphere. The presence of man-made EMF has greatly diminished its natural effect, hence the need to augment it with the resonator.

Two different things are going on... our bodies evolved directly exposed to the Earth's magnetic field and the Schumann wave. The present condition in most of our living environments is such that we don't 'see' these fields very well, if at all, and hence our bodies are not optimal as a result.

My personal experience... started off sleeping grounded, and that made a noticeable difference in my sleep, but I was still getting up at night and the sleep wasn't deep. With the addition of the Magnetico pad, the sleep quality jumped to the next level, and as time has progressed it has gotten better and better. I'm asleep no longer than 5 minutes after I crawl into bed, and the next thing I know it's time to get up. It's awesome, and I feel so good in the morning. The Schumann generator has provided a noticeable but more subtle effect. When I first turned it on I became aware of feeling very calm, yet alert, within 30 minutes of activation. I haven't turned it off since, so I may have acclimated to its effects. I think that the combination of these three technologies has produced my result, and I'm amazed and thrilled with it.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-07-21T20:11:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-90516>

Meghan .... have you reviewed this website? [http://emfwebinar.com/three-types-emf.html](http://emfwebinar.com/three-types-emf.html)

As this page shows, there are 3 types of EMF. Any device you have mentioned will only reduce EMF ... not eliminate it.

There are 2 keys to eliminate EMF (as much as possible) ....
1) turn off the emitting device (i.e. laptop, WiFi, circuit breaker, etc.)
2) Distance .... when it is not possible to turn off a device, the more distance between you and the device(s), the less EMF you face
[http://emfwebinar.com/guidelines-distance.html](http://emfwebinar.com/guidelines-distance.html)

I suggest reading all pages of the above website. In addition, below you purchase any of the devices you have asked about, it would be a good idea to purchase and review the EMF bootcamp by Michael Neuert. Michael is an engineer, electrical contractor, and health enthusiast who has provided specialized EMF services with clients for over 20 years now.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-07-22T04:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-90547>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=90520)
>
> Thanks ATL\_Paleo... This is great....
> Do you have to have the "electronics" UNPLUGGED for them to stop emitting EMFs or is it OK to just have them turned off... if turned off and they still emit, does it make the distance you need to be away from them lower?
> I'm definitely going to get that computer grounding wire... I was just afraid it's the same concept as the grounding mats and by using it I will actually be getting MORE dirty electricity??? Any truth to that?

In order to know how much EMF you are exposed to, you really need to measure it. There are a number of EMF meters available ... see Michael's website for more info. I have used both a gauss meter and body voltage (BV) meter. Without testing (measuring) I would not have known how much EMF was in my life.

Based on the test results, I have rearranged my computer desk area, turned off a motor that was emitting unreal amounts of EMF, unplugged some seldom used electric devices, and turn off my bedroom circuit breaker every night before bed. In addition, I no longer use WiFi (hard wire from cable modem to computer), and ditched my cordless phone for plain, old boring corded landline.

Michael stated in the boot camp, that focusing on your sleeping area is an excellent starting point since your body is in "repair" mode as you sleep. As an experiment, after more than a month of sleeping with the bedroom circuit breaker off, I tried going to sleep w/ the breaker on. After about 10-15 minutes of tossing and turning, I turned the breaker off, and quickly fell asleep. So in my case, I am sensitive enough to ELF fields from the wiring in my bedroom walls that it impacts the quality of sleep.

As Dr. Kruse says ... test, test, test ....... this advice also applies to EMF.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-12T05:07:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-137624>

Colder temperatures increase the strength of a magnetic field. Why? Cold can have an effect on magnetism, because cold can have an effect of how fast or slow electrons move. This links the sun’s power to the magnetic field of the Earth. It also links the **_Schumann resonance_** strength to the sun. When the sun is shining the Schumann frequency is strongest. When the sun is shining the Earth’s magnetic field is weakest. This implies that the Schumann resonance strength varies inversely with the magnetic field’s strength. _**At night time,**_ **_the magnetic field is stronger on Earth._ So when you use a fake generator better be well thought out.**

# Thread: Oysters...Love at First Taste!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-love-at-first-taste.6622/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### beach bunny — 2013-04-04T05:11:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-love-at-first-taste.6622/post-82802>

So decided to stop at the fish market today with my bf to pick up some dinner- fresh Malpeque oysters for appetizer and lovely Sea Breams...

My poor baby forgot his shucker at work and struggled to open them with a whole slew of kitchen utensils haha!
Then after squeezing a lil lemon on the first ones we sucked them back together...I don't know what I was expecting but I loved everything about it!
The slippery texture, the salty zestiness, the subtle butteriness. Then put a dab of cocktail sauce on the other two and the flavour elevated a ten fold.
I am proud to say I am no longer an oyster virgin and can't wait to experiment with oysters for years to come

### caroline — 2013-04-04T05:51:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-love-at-first-taste.6622/post-82804>

Excellent ... so glad you had such a positive experience!

### MeghanK — 2013-04-04T15:49:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-love-at-first-taste.6622/post-82827>

Anyone know a "CHEAP" but good brand of oysters to buy either online or at a store?

### Rachelle — 2013-04-04T16:30:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-love-at-first-taste.6622/post-82830>

I don't know of a brand but check with your local fish market. Be picky and don't buy from the gulf because from my understanding the water is too warm and you risk bacteria in them. I always ask for the smaller ones because I can't swallow the larger ones. I am not buying them in the shell of course because they weren't as good after December. I buy them straight out of the container. Normally I buy 2 pints a week and that costs me about $30. I am sure you will be able to find them in your local fish market.

### kathylu — 2013-04-04T18:07:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-love-at-first-taste.6622/post-82843>

Well, this is not oysters, but make sure you try mussels too! Very cheap, I can buy them for $2 to $3 per pound. A real bargain! Scrub them and soak in cold water while you chop a small onion, some garlic and cilantro or parsley. Melt butter in a pan, saute the garlic and onion, add the drained mussels and steam until they open, 3 to 5 minutes, add a splash of white wine and some lemon, a little salt and pepper. Of course, discard any that don't open. I usually remove all the mussels from the shells and put them back in the warm liquid, sprinkle with parsley and eat all of it...yum!

### beach bunny — 2013-04-04T18:38:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-love-at-first-taste.6622/post-82850>

> [kathylu said:](/goto/post?id=82843)
>
> Well, this is not oysters, but make sure you try mussels too! Very cheap, I can buy them for $2 to $3 per pound. A real bargain! Scrub them and soak in cold water while you chop a small onion, some garlic and cilantro or parsley. Melt butter in a pan, saute the garlic and onion, add the drained mussels and steam until they open, 3 to 5 minutes, add a splash of white wine and some lemon, a little salt and pepper. Of course, discard any that don't open. I usually remove all the mussels from the shells and put them back in the warm liquid, sprinkle with parsley and eat all of it...yum!

OMG Kathy! that sounds divine! I don't remember the last time I had mussels but now that you posted that gorgeous recipe I must get some asap haha!

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-04T20:11:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-love-at-first-taste.6622/post-82855>

In upstate NY 3 hrs from nearest ocean the best deal on fresh in the shell oysters is at the asian market $7.50/doz. I don't know where they come from as communicating with the chinese fishmonger is impossible. Next best deal is fresh shucked in the pints at the supermarket, some come from the west coast, some from the east coast $8.00. Pound for pound this is the best deal and most convenient. I have yet to find a canned oyster product that isn't packed in cottonseed oil. Cottonseed oil? WTF. At the ocean prices do not get any better for some reason. Hopefully the closer to the water the fresher they will be.

I have yet to find a canned oyster product that isn't packed in cottonseed oil. Cottonseed oil? WTF. At the ocean prices do not get any better for some reason. Hopefully the closer to the water the fresher they will be.

My fish monger said they are best from the East Coast. Wow $8 a pint is good, I pay 15 a pound in Michigan.

# Thread: Grounding in the bath?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-bath.6624/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### agatha — 2013-04-04T12:46:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-bath.6624/post-82810>

If i lie in a bath with the tap gently running am i getting a grounding effect?

I assume the water in the pipes is grounded via the metal pipes runnning through the earth, if I then leave the tap running would the stream of water act as a kind of conductive wire so that my whole bathwater is grounded?

### Destiny — 2013-04-04T12:59:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-bath.6624/post-82811>

I was thinking the same thing. Also my bathtub is the iron one ..... that should help some as well, no?

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-04T14:22:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-bath.6624/post-82816>

If the water is running in a continuous stream you will be grounded. The water itself is the ground path. If your iron tub is plumbed with plastic waste and/or supply lines and you turn off the water your ground path is broken. The tub itself is coated with enamel which is insulating. So for your iron tub to be grounded with the water off all the waste line components must be metal from the tub to wherever the waste line enters the earth.

### Destiny — 2013-04-05T03:08:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-bath.6624/post-82915>

> [Dali Dula said:](/goto/post?id=82816)
>
> If the water is running in a continuous stream you will be grounded. The water itself is the ground path. If your iron tub is plumbed with plastic waste and/or supply lines and you turn off the water your ground path is broken. The tub itself is coated with enamel which is insulating. So for your iron tub to be grounded with the water off all the waste line components must be metal from the tub to wherever the waste line enters the earth.

Thanks, Dali Dula. Everything is metal. My bathroom has not been renovated yet which is a good thing now.

### agatha — 2013-04-05T23:20:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-the-bath.6624/post-83015>

This is good - makes a soak in the (cold) bath even better value.

# Thread: Chocolate Dipped Everything
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate-dipped-everything.6628/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RayB — 2013-04-04T16:57:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate-dipped-everything.6628/post-82838>

Mix together equal parts of Coconut oil(melted) and raw organic Cocoa Powder add Stevia and Vanilla to taste. I like a dash of sea salt too. Dip everything in it. Things are best when chilled.. the chocolate coating comes out thicker. Of course, double dipping is also an excellent option.

We do fruit...all sorts of berries, prunes, apricots.

We do nuts...pecans, walnuts, macadamias are the greatest.

We do bacon! Most excellent!

Haven't had the nerve to try oysters...yet. Somehow that would seem sacreligious.

### Optimalbound — 2013-04-04T23:28:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate-dipped-everything.6628/post-82874>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate-dipped-everything.6628/post-83152>

When oranges are in season (think fresh off the tree), I think segments of it dipped in chocolate would be wonderful. How about a some meat from a freshly cracked coconut?

### RayB — 2013-04-07T18:30:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate-dipped-everything.6628/post-83233>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=83152)

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-06T01:27:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-ubiquinol-source.6631/post-83035>

What I would like to find out is what I can do to make sure my body is the most efficient at reducing ubiquinone back to ubiquinol. I keep reading sources that say CoQ10 is constantly being transformed from ubiquinone to ubiquinol and back again. If I give my body a very large dose of oxidized ubiquinone I want to be efficient at reducing it to ubiquinol. What chemical reaction in the body is responsible for that? Would supplementing with A,C, E, alpha lipoic acid, and NAC accomplish this? NAC increases glutathione, so would the extra glutathione help the oxidation/reduction of CoQ10?

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-06T02:02:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-ubiquinol-source.6631/post-83042>

> [GlenPDQ said:](/goto/post?id=83035)
>
> What I would like to find out is what I can do to make sure my body is the most efficient at reducing ubiquinone back to ubiquinol. I keep reading sources that say CoQ10 is constantly being transformed from ubiquinone to ubiquinol and back again. If I give my body a very large dose of oxidized ubiquinone I want to be efficient at reducing it to ubiquinol. What chemical reaction in the body is responsible for that? Would supplementing with A,C, E, alpha lipoic acid, and NAC accomplish this? NAC increases glutathione, so would the extra glutathione help the oxidation/reduction of CoQ10?

redox pair ubiquinone/ubisemiquinone

another redox pair ubisemiquinone/ubiquinol

I think I found it. Ubiquinone absorbs electrons and protons from succinate and NADH to become semiubiquinone and then finally to ubiquinol. Ubiquinol donates electrons and protons to the mitochindrial cytochromes a and b, transforming to semiubiquinone and then ubiquinone, starting the process all over again.

Hmm... NADH contains niacinamide which is made mostly from niacin and to a lesser extent from tryptophan. Sustained use of high dose niacin or niacinamide has been reported to cause liver damage and increased chance of stroke. Homocysteine levels increase in relation to niacin which depletes methyl groups using up SAMe to methylate niacin. Well.. perhaps high dose niacin isn't the way to increase CoQ10 redox.

[http://web.archive.org/web/20130116...etics.utah.edu/content/epigenetics/nutrition/](http://web.archive.org/web/20130116092036/http://learn.genetics.utah.edu/content/epigenetics/nutrition/)

Succinate is oxidized to fumarate in the ubiquinone/ubiquinol cycle, if I understand correctly, but what are dietary sources of succinate?

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-06T03:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-ubiquinol-source.6631/post-83058>

Found this neat chart, not really about ubiquinone though.

[http://web.archive.org/web/20111111....edu/content/epigenetics/nutrition/table.html](http://web.archive.org/web/20111111005306/http://learn.genetics.utah.edu/content/epigenetics/nutrition/table.html)

# Thread: Tourettes and shingles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tourettes-and-shingles.6632/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vlynnb — 2013-04-04T22:23:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tourettes-and-shingles.6632/post-82865>

I confess I don't know the best place to post this but here goes. My 38 yo son has just developed shingles above his eye. He tries to live healthy by CW although on a tight grocery budget. I haven't been successful in convincing him to go epi-paleo. He is struggling with several stressful life situations. Though almost everything in life is stressful to him as he also has Tourettes, ADD, mild OCD and some learning disabilities He was sent to a university medical center for brain mapping and some sort of brain therapy using electrodes and some kind of video game type format that he controls with just his thoughts to supposedly help him with his anxiety and other problems. Did that for several months but not for the last few.

For the shingles Dr. has prescribed a high dose oral antiviral and eye drops. He also suggested the shingles vaccination.

I am concerned about the vaccination in relation to his existing Tourettes and other problems. Does anyone know if this would be a problem?

Dr. Sheen, M.D. on a natural healing site recommended one mg B12 IM every day until the shingles symptoms disappeared. He also recommended a daily oral regimen of 1000 mg Vit C, B12, E and Zinc. He said 3000 mg lysine daily until symptoms disappeared but not routine afterwards because of an arginine problem with continual use.

I will have son check all of this out with his physician but would like opinions from this forum. Being OCD and anxious etc. this situation is causing him greater stress than usuall and I would like to help him out as much as possible with info to take back to his physician.

### cantweight — 2013-04-04T22:52:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tourettes-and-shingles.6632/post-82870>

I am no doc but I had a few bouts of shingles in 2009 and I have a husband and 2 sons with tourettes and similar issues to your son.

I don't understand vaccinating someone with an active infection at all! I think with the obvious breakdowns in his system this would be a huge stress on it. My daughter was given the chicken pox vaccine in 2000 and has had them 4 times! I would seriously skip that advice.

I think the recommendations by Dr Sheen are quite sound. I would also add 5000 - 10000 IU of D3 and some serious fish oil if he will not adopt epi paleo diet.

I have also had very good results with Taurine and L-Theanine in treating anxiety in myself and my daughter who has some issues with it. These were used in conjunction with B12.

Based on what we have learned from Dr Kruse I dont see the benefit of any sort of electronic brain therapy....seems counter intuitive.

I have had great success in treating tourettes with diet....it has not been a 100% cure but it has become subtle and my son is able to stay in school as his tics are not disruptive any more. Grain free was key. Decreasing inflammation, reducing EMF...all key. CT would be so good for him but it is quite tricky for me to convince him to get into a 50 degree tub. However ice packs to the occipital lobe have seemed beneficial.

The antivirals may prove quite helpful to him. I wonder if his doc would consider monitoring something like HHV6 level and perhaps keeping him on some antiviral therapy....kind of a way to keep some of the fires in his body under control. I have seen myself, my children and other children respond quite well to famivir/acyclovir when the viral load in the body is high.

I know I have rambled all over the place in this response...I hope it was somewhat helpful. This stuff is no fun.

Christi

### vlynnb — 2013-04-04T23:09:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tourettes-and-shingles.6632/post-82872>

Thanks for your quick reply, cantweight. I am not sure the Dr. was advocating the vaccine immediately, I agree that during an active infection would not be the time to get it if at all. I'll check on that.

Hadn't thought to ask if son is taking D3 though I have suggested it before and I think I will have him get some krill oil. He did have a freezer of Alaskan wild caught fish from a friend but think he has run out.

I will also check out the Taurine and L-Theanine. Are you or your daughter on any prescription meds for anxiety?

After the EMF series I am no longer encouraging him to go back for the brain therapy until I know more about it. It did seem to be helping him refocus his brain when he started to get anxious.

I know long run he would be much better off grain free, reduced EMF, and CT. It is just hard to make any changes in his life as each little change is a big change for someone with his stress, anxiety, and OCD. Perhaps if I lived close to him I could help make the changes easier by grocery shopping, cooking for him, etc. but even then I realize this is all something he needs to decide to do on his own.

I don't recognize HHV6. Is the therapy just a low dose antiviral on a continual basis? If so is that harmful to the gut as an antibacterial might be?

Thanks again for you input.

### cantweight — 2013-04-05T00:06:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tourettes-and-shingles.6632/post-82882>

Yeah, Id skip the vaccine all together.

My daughter is not on any meds just the B12 taurine and theanine. Helps a lot!

At his age he really has to decide to do these things on his own like you said. Perhaps you could print the tourette blog for him? Great info...maybe he would listen if it didnt come from mom..lol.

HHV6 is just a virus carried by like 90% of the population. I believe it comes from scarlet fever and is easily transmitted. It is just a guess but I know when my kids and myself are having flares in our health our HHV6 number rises as the body is unhealthy and the viral load it is carrying becomes somewhat active. All these meds are tough on the gut so there is that aspect....I was just throwing out the idea that a decrease in his viral load may lessen some of his other issues. It may be something to pay attention to while he is on the famvir. And yes it was a continual low dose. Again..this is just based on my experience.

### Hope — 2013-04-05T00:40:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tourettes-and-shingles.6632/post-82888>

This is fascinating.

I just wanted to chime in on the brain therapy....it sounds like he was receiving Neurofeedback? If it is NFB it may be helpful...though it's mostly not covered by insurance so maybe it isn't that? I have heard Dr K speak well about it. It's part of the EMF Rx:

10\. Embrace meditation to offset EMF affects. Embrace neuro biofeedback as a way to cope.

(although, now I wonder if there is something different than neurofeedback?)

Christi,

may I ask...how does your daughter get the B12? I've been thinking of adding that...

### cantweight — 2013-04-05T01:48:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tourettes-and-shingles.6632/post-82901>

We add more as needed when she has an anxiety attack.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-05T05:18:51+0300

Thanks, everyone, for these suggestions. I have forwarded them to my son.

On being referred to an eye doctor he was told that it probably is a cold sore virus above his eye rather than shingles. Physician is still not definitely sure of the diagnosis which surprises me and still a cause to worry for him but all the supplement suggestions still apply I think.

Universe working in his favor sometimes though. After going to multiple locations to get the shingles vaccine after being told he needed it, he found it was unavailable. Finally found it at one pharmacy but the pharmacist took over from the tech and told him he should wait as he had had two vaccinations over the last two months which were required by a hospital corporation in order for him to do volunteer work on the grounds crew at the hospital - completely outside the hospital and not an employe but corporate required it. His volunteer work was required by his horticulture degree program. Thank goodness for the pharmacist.

Also, I think this whole scenario is a blessing, it is getting him to reconsider his diet,circadian rhythm and other things I have been trying to get him to take seriously.

# Thread: EMF Webinar Q&A- Would someone ask a ? for me pls?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-q-a-would-someone-ask-a-for-me-pls.6633/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kiera2 — 2013-04-05T00:35:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-q-a-would-someone-ask-a-for-me-pls.6633/post-82887>

I am unable to attend the Q&A later today, and I would love for someone to ask about Ethernet Over Power.

It's an alternative to wifi; it plugs into your power outlet and travels through the power lines instead of through the air.
I don't imagine that it's great, but I would like to know if it's an acceptable alternative.

Thanks for your help.

### Hope — 2013-04-05T00:43:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-q-a-would-someone-ask-a-for-me-pls.6633/post-82889>

I'll try if i don't forget!

### Kiera2 — 2013-04-05T00:54:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-q-a-would-someone-ask-a-for-me-pls.6633/post-82892>

Thanks Hope!

Maybe someone could remind her?

### Hope — 2013-04-05T03:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-q-a-would-someone-ask-a-for-me-pls.6633/post-82921>

So he said....Ethernet over power is better than wifi, but still not great. You will have RF via wires, create more of your own dirty electricity. RF from internet on there too…so dirtier electric field unless fields are low (electric) bc then you can be less concerned about electric field…..because dirty E won’t do much….since it's low to begin with, rather than making a high electric field dirtier, which is worse.

Measure Dirty E on skin and wiring.

If choice b/w wifi and EC, do EC. But anything wired = better.

Also the call is recorded

### Kiera2 — 2013-04-05T10:09:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-q-a-would-someone-ask-a-for-me-pls.6633/post-82946>

Thankyou so much Hope. At least it's better, and not worse, than wifi. My son has this set up in a part of the house, and he claims it's more stable than wifi. I would love to test!

### tellmisty — 2013-04-07T17:24:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-q-a-would-someone-ask-a-for-me-pls.6633/post-83227>

I'm ordering Home PNA Ethernet Bridge to Coax to get rid of wifi ... if you have cable internet, and your house is already wired for cable, you can simply attach a box to each cable output, attach an ethernet cord to the box and wa-lah! I'm putting one of these in each room of the house so we can wire in to internet and TVs can have internet.

### Hope — 2013-04-08T00:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-q-a-would-someone-ask-a-for-me-pls.6633/post-83273>

Misty, awesome. In my case, I was told by Verizon, we only have FIOS anymore...no more broadband. Not sure if by cable internet you include FIOS. I haven't been able to figure this out and VZN couldn't tell me either. ugh.

Love what you are doing....

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-04-08T02:03:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-q-a-would-someone-ask-a-for-me-pls.6633/post-83299>

Hope, FIOS is their brand name for broadband.

Open the link below, and then click "click here" link below the graphic. Look at the PDF that opens .... you will see ethernet connections that allow you to use ethernet cables. So the main challenge will be to disable the WiFi. That can probably be done in the FIOS software.

[https://teleproducts.verizon.com/fios/index.cfm/eh/DisplayDetails](https://teleproducts.verizon.com/fios/index.cfm/eh/DisplayDetails)

### Hope — 2013-04-08T02:09:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-q-a-would-someone-ask-a-for-me-pls.6633/post-83303>

AH, thanks ATLP!!

Yes, they told me they can help me turn off wifi...once I'm ready.

### nonchalant — 2013-04-08T03:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-q-a-would-someone-ask-a-for-me-pls.6633/post-83323>

I have FIOS. I was disappointed that the fibre optic cable stops when it gets to our house, and is then converted to ethernet, and then to wireless. But yes, I can turn off the wireless and just use ethernet.

Hi endless, it doesn't look like your multimeter has a low enough sensitivity to read down to millivolts (mV). Bummer.

I'm looking at getting the Radio Shack one, but don't have the model # in front of me now. I'll wait until the store is open and get it.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/havent-been-eating-red-meat-for-over-20-years.6641/post-82945>

Haven't been eating red meat for over 20 years...only Chicken and seafood (and added eggs back about 6mnths. I was wondering if I might have a deficiency and if this way of eating can influence my female hormones/leptin/insulin levels. I'm 33 year old female and want to lose about 20lbs, that I have gained in a year.

Any advise would be much appreciated

### Destiny — 2013-04-05T14:09:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/havent-been-eating-red-meat-for-over-20-years.6641/post-82953>

Elle,
Can you get some blood testing done? That would be the best way to asses your possible deficiencies.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-05T17:45:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/havent-been-eating-red-meat-for-over-20-years.6641/post-82980>

Elle -

seafood, especially shellfish and crustations have a greater nutrient density than grass fed ruminants (beef, lamb, pork). Chicken is probably one of the least nutrient dense foods you can consume.

meal timing, using artificial light and technology have a greater impact on your hormone status to include leptin and insulin than food does as Jack laid out in the EMF series.

Question for you - are you grain free? if not you should consider moving that direction.

### diane — 2013-04-06T03:17:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/havent-been-eating-red-meat-for-over-20-years.6641/post-83061>

### caroline — 2013-04-06T03:30:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/havent-been-eating-red-meat-for-over-20-years.6641/post-83065>

# Thread: Your wired internet experience/speed
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-04-05T19:21:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-82989>

So I am still on the phone with our provider....it's been 1.5 hours and 4 people. I can't switch back to anything DSL. The FIOS folks actually had to look up if you could turn off wifi on our router...guess no one asks that? Ugh.

My head is spinning. It appears that we can turn off the wifi feature and then use the same high speed via the ethernet?? Would ethernet over FIOS be just as bad as wireless, or slightly better than wireless? Hm.

Gotta get out in nature and hug some cherry trees...

Have any of you made the switch, besides Audrey in Oz? Would love to hear...

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-05T20:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-82996>

hope -

first your need a modum like this one [http://www.bestbuy.com/site/Motorol...odem/8229044.p?id=1168043113583&skuId=8229044](http://www.bestbuy.com/site/Motorola+-+SURFboard+Cable+Modem/8229044.p?id=1168043113583&skuId=8229044)

2nd you need a wired router like this one [http://www.bestbuy.com/site/D-Link+...uter/7650473.p?id=1134699413401&skuId=7650473](http://www.bestbuy.com/site/D-Link+-+4-Port+Ethernet+Broadband+Router/7650473.p?id=1134699413401&skuId=7650473)

then run your cables to where you want to use your computer...

### tweety — 2013-04-05T20:31:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83001>

I'm looking into this setup like shijin posted. we switched providers and I specifically asked for direct connection, no wireless. Guess what... we have wireless. And even though we are directly connected, we can't turn off the wifi during the day. I forwarded shilohman's post to my husband so we could figure it out but it's slow going around here.

### Hope — 2013-04-05T20:54:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83005>

ooooooooooh Gretchen, brilliant. THANKS!!!!!!

### lioness7 — 2013-04-05T22:22:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83011>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=83005)
>
> ooooooooooh Gretchen, brilliant. THANKS!!!!!!

I was told that I could take my wifi hub, call the company and have them explain how to turn off the wifi. Once it is wired with an ethernet cable, the computers and hub do not talk anymore with the wifi off. The hub can be placed in the basement or anywhere else far away enough so that it is only receiving the signal to the hub and not transmit to the computers in the house. Also , I found out a while ago that my printer (about a year or more old) eventhough I had never set it up wirelessly, was still on wireless mode by default....ugh. I went into the programming mode and was able to turn it off. Just for some further info for those who have gaming systems: Wi , once off is off, but the controllers are emitting wifi everytime your kids play. The more hand sets, the more signals. Some systems such as xbox, even when turned off emit a signal , apparently that is how they come from the factory...i guess just another thing to worry about/consider. Without an ethernet connection, I will have to go back to dial up (no, horrors, 24k speed) in which case I can no longer watch webinars, download talks etc...shudder the thought!

### Hope — 2013-04-05T23:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83022>

Lioness, that is fascinating. It's like they are purposely trying to fry all our brains out!!!! lol I guess it's to make it all super easy for you when you get it...just plug and play. I never had any electronic games, and I never missed them (as a kid)...nor now. I like playing outside, still.

I just still wonder if it's truly off...and if Gretchen's path isn't better. I have to look into it, if DH lets me. But my gut says, there's still something bad...otherwise, why would Michael Neuert have a slower connection rather than doing this (just turn wifi off). It can't be the price.....

### caroline — 2013-04-06T07:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83096>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=83022)
>
> Lioness, that is fascinating. It's like they are purposely trying to fry all our brains out!!!! lol I guess it's to make it all super easy for you when you get it...just plug and play. I never had any electronic games, and I never missed them (as a kid)...nor now. I like playing outside, still.
>
> I just still wonder if it's truly off...and if Gretchen's path isn't better. I have to look into it, if DH lets me. But my gut says, there's still something bad...otherwise, why would Michael Neuert have a slower connection rather than doing this (just turn wifi off). It can't be the price.....

I had this very conversation yesterday with the Internet provider ....on my modem the wireless lite was lit. Even tho I had had 2 glasses of wine - I was very concerned ....so he went thru the steps with me to turn off the wifi and he did something too. I recognize that I really don't know for sure without testing but-
But I questioned him multiple times about it and he kept assuring me that there would be no Emfs....

He was very kind and patient and knowledgeable - so I will trust him for now. After we were done - I told him that he now needed a glass of wine. He said his wife doesnt like him drinking but will tell her that I said it was necessary!

### Inger — 2013-04-06T10:04:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83104>

The modum I got for wired internet had the wifi on too! I did not know before my bro come and told me! He turned it off on the internet site of the providers. I would never have managed it for sure, I had no idea at all.. I lived about 1 week in the believe I had no wifi, but it was on all the time..lol You can check it when looking on the modem, there is a light that must be lit (green), the wifi - light.

I have way better connection now. The wifi did not worked well here. So slow and bad connection, especially when bad weather. The wired works great! I took the fastest connection they had, to make it more efficient and spend less time on the computer. Slow connections sucks. High speed is more expensive but it really pays back.

### Hope — 2013-04-06T21:40:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83137>

Inger, wow - so glad you are all set now.

I thought I could just buy an ethernet cable but maybe not.

here is a website I just found.....about what wifi does even when not in use:

[http://www.emfwise.com/low-emf-computer.php](http://www.emfwise.com/low-emf-computer.php)

"Your internet access via cable modem, DSL, or FiOS, may have come with a wireless router by default. These wireless routers transmit all the time by design, even when the internet is not in use. See just how strong wi-fi signals are and recommended distances Some models of wireless routers allow users to disable the wireless capability using the web-based setup interface. A few models even allow for power level reduction (e.g., up to a factor of 10) and/or scheduling capability."

...I should have not been OK with getting wifi in 2010. I was always against it before...ugh. Would be so much easier. Definitely learned my lesson, though. Must totally change my day. Have specific times to be on the net - and nothing more, and turn off wifi - i.e. the wireless router...until we're on ethernet. DH is NOT happy I want to get rid of wifi. It's so so sad.....it's like his god. He doesn't care I guess how much it affects me (not him, he thinks, I guess). anyway......

### tellmisty — 2013-04-08T21:36:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83376>

So, I've got an Apple setup and today, I successfully killed my wifi. No signal coming from the Time Capsule or my computer. And my internet is actually faster.  I've got to figure out the printer piece now ... how I'm going to get things to print ... and I have to order a new wired trackpad. But relatively small obstacles to overcome.  I took an RF meter around last night with Michael on the phone to test for RF fields ... fortunately, the electric fields in my house are really low, so we just had to find where all the RF fields were coming from. I even threw the breaker on the whole house to test. It felt very ghost busters; heh.

I might need to look into some shielding now for one side of the house, but I feel much better knowing that I've taken out the biggest source of RF in my home / office!

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-04-11T22:50:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83792>

I recently received and installed my 'network over power' kit and finally disabled the wi-fi on my Airport Extreme. The relative performance of my desktop computer, Playstation 3 and Roku streaming video box is noticeably faster now that they're hardwired. Not only that but my emf readings are considerably diminished.

### MeghanK — 2013-04-12T16:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83896>

OK..... still reading through the EMF.... how terrible is it to have Wireless? What if you only use your computer while having your feet on an Earthing Mat...

### Vicster — 2013-04-12T17:02:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83898>

> [JeffMacDougall said:](/goto/post?id=83792)
>
> I recently received and installed my 'network over power' kit and finally disabled the wi-fi on my Airport Extreme. The relative performance of my desktop computer, Playstation 3 and Roku streaming video box is noticeably faster now that they're hardwired. Not only that but my emf readings are considerably diminished.

Jeff, which kit did you buy? There are a lot of options and I can't figure out what is best.

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-04-12T17:45:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83904>

> [Vicster said:](/goto/post?id=83898)
>
> Jeff, which kit did you buy? There are a lot of options and I can't figure out what is best.

I purchased the ZyXEL 500 Mbps Mini Powerline AV Adapter Kit and the ZyXEL Powerline AV 500 Mbps 4 Port Gigabit Switch Wallplug Adapter, both from Amazon.com. This combo gives me the ability to run a network/internet connection from the cable modem in my office to two other rooms. The 4 port switch allows me to have multiple connections... it's in use in the family room with the PS3 and the Roku box. The other unit can go into another room if needed. You may not need all of this capability, so you could consider just the Mini Powerline Adapter Kit, or a set from another manufacturer with less bandwidth and therefore a cheaper price. The ZyXEL gear looks to be well made and is working fine so far.

### Vicster — 2013-04-12T17:54:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-wired-internet-experience-speed.6648/post-83905>

Thanks! I'll take a look at it.

# Thread: 23 year old toe nail fungus spontaneously resolving with full on CT and BAB
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23-year-old-toe-nail-fungus-spontaneously-resolving-with-full-on-ct-and-bab.6651/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### curves64 — 2013-04-05T22:25:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23-year-old-toe-nail-fungus-spontaneously-resolving-with-full-on-ct-and-bab.6651/post-83012>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/imag0626-2-jpg.83/)

I've had toe nail fungus for 23 years. I know exactly when I got it because I was pregnant with my now 22-year-old daughter. I have tried just about everything to resolve it. I've tried every topical and oral medication conceived of, and even had my toe nails removed (ouch!). Nothing worked for very long. What I came to realize after discovering Dr. Kruse a year ago, was that my toe nail fungus was most likely an outward representation of internal inflammation (hsCRP last October was 2.15). When I decided to go full out CT early this year, I never would have guessed that it would have an impact on my long-standing problem. If you look at my pic you can see that right below the ugly part of my toe nail is a clean, brand-spankin new clear nail. Now, I don't know how this will turn out but I'm really encouraged. It takes about 18 months to grow out a big toe nail so I'm keeping my fingers crossed!!

### cantweight — 2013-04-05T23:07:50+0300

That is so great curves!!!

Along the lines of gross things fixing themselves.... I have had a mole under my left breast for about 2 yrs. Kind of rapid growing odd shape....the kind you "keep an eye on" and "mean to get checked". I read in the iodine thread, I think it was Grizz that just started putting iodine on any random growth on herself, her husband, her dog....so i figured what the hell??

Put some on it....damned if that thing isnt shrinking! Could be diet, CT, or the iodine. Whatever it is I'll take it!

Keep us posted on the toe

> Bad about the ants.. but def let me know if the iodine helps. I thought I had read that Lugol's used to be sold at cvs/walgreens... but I guess I'll just order some online. They have other iodine stuff, just not Lugol's.
>
> Btw.. I missed this somehow, but what exactly is the Photoelectric effect?

Lovelife, no you didn't miss a blog on the photoelectric effect. I asked Dr. Kruse about it on the members QA last month and he told me to google it as well.  -- Turns out I had already googled it and read about it from more than one site but I didn't get how it all tied in. On that call he said something about it's dual function as a particle and a wave.

I'm not sure this will help but here's a bit on it from Wikipedia: "Study of the photoelectric effect led to important steps in understanding the quantum nature of light and electrons and influenced the formation of the concept of waveâ€“particle duality.\[1\] Other phenomena where light affects the movement of electric charges include the photoconductive effect (also known as photoconductivity or photoresistivity), the photovoltaic effect, and the photoelectrochemical effect. It also led to Max Planck's discovery of quantized energy and the Planck Relation (E = hv), which links a photon's frequency with its energy. The factor h is known as the Planck constant."

Don't get me wrong. I'm completely fascinated by the science, but honestly, I don't understand most of it. I know this will sound odd, but I just "feel" my way around and let my feelings/intuition guide me. I'm not driven to act by the science, i'm driven to act by my "inner knowing." If you find a layman's description of the photoelectric effect please share

As for the iodine, it is definitely accelerating the healing of my fire ant bitten toes -- next stop, skin tags and moles!!

I will

I live in a small city 30 km away from Helsinki (biggest city in Finland, where I also work, part time). there is lots of EMF for sure.. I use the train to get there, about 40 minutes - and I have measured it and it was not too bad actually if sitting in the mid of the wagon! I do have looked for another workplace for summer and I got the job too. It is right by the sea! A restaurant where I get to work outside too.. I am sure there are way less EMF than the other place I use to work. That is why I picked it, it is a bit outside the big city too. I am only working there 1-2 days / week as that is what I want, it is enough to do here in summer - I take care of the yard and animals so I need to pay no rent. But yes, I do am concerned about my work place too so that is why I changed. I do loved my old place but I still can work there once in a while if I want.

This small city I live in here has about 30 000 people living on a quite large area. Not too much EMF but sure, here are 3G too.. sadly. And some towers and stuff... and smart meters. And people have wifis and all this stuff.. a lot. But closest neighbours are more than 100 yards away so no wifi signals to catch in this house, thanks God.

When my Lugol's arrives I am going to make my N=1.
I am going to paint some moles, and I am going to paint my small lipoma too for sure! Ha. It does not really bother me, and I tried some halfhearted CT on it last winter.. but it is still there. Maybe Iodine helps? It won't hurt to try!
