# Thread: Restaurant and the oil they cook with.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restaurant-and-the-oil-they-cook-with.6652/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rachelle — 2013-04-05T22:40:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restaurant-and-the-oil-they-cook-with.6652/post-83013>

I rarely go to restaurants anymore because I am straight up afraid of the oils they use to cook food in. I am going to a really nice seafood restaurant tomorrow night and feel like I need to question the chef about what he cooks his fish in. I would like to know how to handle this. Any suggestions? I don't want to ingest even a tiny amount of bad oils.

### Hope — 2013-04-05T23:52:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restaurant-and-the-oil-they-cook-with.6652/post-83020>

Just ask. If it's a 'really nice' one, they'd probably be open to using an oil that's OK - IF they have it. Even olive oil. I have done that, ask what they use and if they can use a certain oil like OO or butter if possible.....and I hear you on this. My father in law's retirement place, they used some sort of soy concoction. I had eggs, awful. Never eaten there again, just plain veggies. Ate my own stuff before so I wouldn't be hungry.

Have fun!! I am sure you can enjoy it.....let us know how it goes!

I wonder what Jack would do.....WWJD.

### cantweight — 2013-04-06T01:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restaurant-and-the-oil-they-cook-with.6652/post-83030>

WWJD...lol!

I have brought my own stuff to places before and they were quite accommodating. Like GF tamari to a thai restaurant. Throw a stick of kerrygold in your purse or a little disposable container of coconut oil

I would address it as a dietary condition with your server, sounds better than...I don't want to eat anything crappy you may use
We have had great response to all of our issues when we have been out, GF etc. Family get togethers are a whole 'nuther story!

### caroline — 2013-04-06T01:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restaurant-and-the-oil-they-cook-with.6652/post-83036>

We had a restaurant for many years and owners/ chefs are more than happy to address concerns. There is one huge caveat tho. Please, please call the day before and tell them about your limitations and requirements. A good restaurant is all about taking care of their customers in the best possible way. They will generally do what ever it takes to have a happy and satisfied customer.

If you wait until you arrive and the restaurant is busy .... The kitchen is stressed and the wait staff are stressed.....so simply chose an offf time to call the day before and do say it is dietary restrictions/ allergies and be polite and courteous.

It is a really good idea to develope a relationship with a favorite restaurant and they will bend over backwards to accommodate you. A restaurant is all about hospitality and great food and they want you happy!

I would often remember people's allergies/tastes when they had forgotten momentarily and I would have to tell them ....no you can't have that - it has xyz in it or you don't really like that!

It is very much like being a good host/hostess in your own home. If someone comes to dinner and they haven't told you ahead of time about restrictions - it can be very difficult to accommodate them.

People /customers often think that a restaurant has everything at their disposal at any given meal service which couldn't be further from the truth. A small restaurant, especially these days is running quite a small menu to enable them to serve the very freshest of foods and also to make a profit.

### Hope — 2013-04-06T02:39:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restaurant-and-the-oil-they-cook-with.6652/post-83043>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=83036)
>
> We had a restaurant for many years and owners/ chefs are more than happy to address concerns. There is one huge caveat tho. Please, please call the day before and tell them about your limitations and requirements. A good restaurant is all about taking care of their customers in the best possible way. They will generally do what ever it takes to have a happy and satisfied customer.
>
> If you wait until you arrive and the restaurant is busy .... The kitchen is stressed and the wait staff are stressed.....so simply chose an offf time to call the day before and do say it is dietary restrictions/ allergies and be polite and courteous.
>
> It is a really good idea to develope a relationship with a favorite restaurant and they will bend over backwards to accommodate you. A restaurant is all about hospitality and great food and they want you happy!
>
> I would often remember people's allergies/tastes when they had forgotten momentarily and I would have to tell them ....no you can't have that - it has xyz in it or you don't really like that!
>
> It is very much like being a good host/hostess in your own home. If someone comes to dinner and they haven't told you ahead of time about restrictions - it can be very difficult to accommodate them.
>
> People /customers often think that a restaurant has everything at their disposal at any given meal service which couldn't be further from the truth. A small restaurant, especially these days is running quite a small menu to enable them to serve the very freshest of foods and also to make a profit.

This sounds wonderful, Audrey..wish I could have come into your restaurant!!  sounds wonderful. I haven't eaten out much at all either for similar reasons. Though I used to have a favorite....the Tuscan Roasted Veggies at Bertucci's are roasted in olive oil, and they'd fill me up pretty well. Love your idea about calling the day ahead. Never did that thing.....

Thanks for your wisdom ~

### Rachelle — 2013-04-06T14:33:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restaurant-and-the-oil-they-cook-with.6652/post-83112>

Thank you, Caroline, for your suggestion. I called and they will see no problem with my requests. That was easier then I thought.

I asked for no olive oil unless it is certified and then I don't want my food cooked in it. I have been reading about the olive oil fraud for 20 years and this problem still exists. Did you know REAL olive oil is flammable? Also real olive oil will solidify in the fridge. I, highly, doubt that most restaurants buy real certified olive oil, mainly, because of the cost. Truly, most of us can't chance ingesting bad olive oil mixed with the likes of soy, corn or canola oil. I believe Trader Joe's California brand is certified real. There are tons of articles on the net about fraudulent olive oil.

# Thread: Hey friends!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MrPinkies — 2013-04-06T00:02:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83023>

Hi! My name is Kate.. I'm new to the forum stuff, but, I'm getting the hang of it- I think?!??

Anyway... I'm a mom of two kids living with Autism. I married a man with Autism. I probably have Autism, but, after one turns 30 years old- whatever..

A year ago January, it was own personal mission to lose 150-160 lbs. Starting weight was 309 pounds. By June, I was beyond frustrated, beyond hungry, and injured from running (fractured my tibia at the knee). I had a personal trainer, who I love, but, obviously, things didn't work out. It was mid-summer that it was suggested to us by the ABA therapist and an OT to change my son's diet for sensory purposes. Well, ok, back up.. I have a feeling no one is going to understand "sensory purposes". See, when we found this website, EVERYTHING began to make sense to us. My son's nervous system is very very very under-developed. So.. before finding this website, the OT said to me, ok, so, you've had wine before, right? and I said yes, of course.. Ok.. so.. what happens when you have one too many glasses of wine? I get drunk! Right, your behavior changes! I said yes, but in confusion.. She explained that over half of our nervous system in is our gut and certain foods- like wheat and dairy can make nerve endings go "crazy". So... after finding this website, we got excited.. VERY excited... The excitement actually died the first day of the leptin reset. Actually, the first month, I felt like a crack addict with no crack. However, it started to sink into my brain of the addiction to sugar. The sugar I knew about and wasn't being honest, but also, the sugar that I didn't know that would be harmful, etc. This website gave a clear understanding of WHY.. I am incredibly grateful. Seven months later, I am FINALLY under 200lbs, but, I still have a good 40-50lbs to go. I am pretty sure that I no longer have PCOS, my cycles are on time and manageable. On the subject of my husband and kids, there was a very noticeable difference when we changed our lights to amber, orange, and red... My kids SLEEP!!! AMEN! lol We also sold our TV and iPad. We got rid of our Android phones and got cheap pay as you go phones that we keep off and just for emergencies only. We turned off our WIFI and hard wired our computers. My kids are more calm.. They still have their moments, but, over all, more calm. Less meltdowns. Less screaming.. I'm sure there is more to be done!! One day at a time...

I would love to hear thoughts on what you guys pack in your kids lunches???

### BJK77 — 2013-04-06T00:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83027>

Welcome Kate! Sounds like you have made some great progress and your entire family is benefitting from the changes you made. I love hearing about the positive effects reduced EMF can have on kids!! We saw a great improvement in my kiddos too

### MrPinkies — 2013-04-06T01:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83034>

The lights and the tv were the big ones with the most noticeable difference.. and I mean, night and day kind of difference. We were SHOCKED. My son would get up at 1 and 4am to jump, literally. My daughter would get up at 3am to move around. They wouldn't disturb each other or anything. It has been before they were born since I have slept through the night.. they FINALLY are BOTH sleeping through the night! that makes me one HAPPY Mama to sleep through the night too!!! lol

### caroline — 2013-04-06T01:42:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83037>

Hi Kate .... I can't even begin to tell you how amazing I think you are! You dived into all of this and look what rewards you have reaped! You are an awesome woman and more importantly an awesome wife and mother. You are one smart lady!
And ..... You are sleeping .... Just simply ...WOW....

Please keep coming here and posting and asking questions. - there are a lot of really smart people here who can help if needed. We love, love, love to hear from people who take all of this on board and don't look back.

All of this must have been pretty daunting given all your difficult circumstances - but you didnt say "I can't" you just did!
,

### caroline — 2013-04-06T03:27:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83064>

Kate - I don't know if you eat fruit ....but that was huge for me when I gave it up ...... in the long run it is just sugar. It took a few weeks but I don't crave fruit at all now - in fact - I bought an apple the other day and didn't enjoy it at all. That was huge for me - I have loved apples all of my life!

Have you thought of starting a journal here? we would love to follow your progress.....it is amazing when you look back months later and see how far you have come ...... and all your trials and tribulations - successes and failures ...all part of this amazing journey to wellness.

When I started a journal - I was just going to keep track of food and C/T before my next set of labs ... but it has turned into so much more....

### MrPinkies — 2013-04-06T03:51:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83072>

Caroline-

I'll think about it! Where can I go to start one if I decide to do so?

Thanks!

### caroline — 2013-04-06T04:03:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83074>

Kate - just go to the forum page ...it is the third category down ....90 day reset and then - my journal. It is really easy to find and do - just start a new thread!

Just start by posting a few sentences and then soon enough - you will want to go there to post your thoughts - It helps Dr. K. to see how people are doing as well ...he says he likes journals. It really helps to focus our thoughts even when busy. Some people have been keeping a journal for a year and it is amazing to see their progress and everyone is very supportive....

### caroline — 2013-04-06T04:06:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83075>

### MrPinkies — 2013-04-06T15:24:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83113>

HA! I found it! Thanks!

Yes, this is one of my FAVORITE quotes... A very special friend of mine told me this quote about a year after my first kids diagnoses. At that time, life was very confusing because at the time of diagnoses, they literally said "your son has autism, good luck" and then showed me the door.. That's the thing about Autism, is, there is no book.. there is no one therapy or one answer. It's called a spectrum for a reason.. All of these kids, that will eventually become adults, are different. Both of my kids are very different from each other and have different struggles. I think I finally (starting my fifth year on this journey with Autism) got my act together. On the tough days, where my kids nervous system completely goes haywire, this is the only quote that gets me through my day because being strong IS the only option I have.. Over time, I have learned to love being strong.

It's interesting that when I explain to other people why I eat the way that I do and do the things I do- like the amber lights, etc, and not owning a tv... the typical response is "I can't do that- it's too hard.. and I wouldn't be able to keep this lifestyle"... but.. the more I think about it.. these things make life EASIER... not harder... We are so programmed to believe things.. and until we "TRY", how will one ever know that it's too hard.. or one would be unable to keep this lifestyle up??!?? I'll tell you what.. my kids put themselves in bed at 8pm.. they are WILLING to go to bed because they get tired at the right time...

AMEN! hahahahahaa...

### caroline — 2013-04-07T01:59:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83154>

Kate - I just love how strong you are and how motivated. Why do people always put things in the too hard box? Surely your life isn't going to end if you give up wheat and sugar and TV.......people seem to be so unmotivated to even try to help themselves but as Jack says - its the brain. They just can't make descisions to save/help themselves

### caroline — 2013-04-07T02:01:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-friends.6653/post-83155>

I really hope that you start a journal Kate .....you have so much intelligence and insight to offer. .....you never know who might see your story and be motivated....

# Thread: school
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MrPinkies — 2013-04-06T00:19:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83024>

What do you pack in your kids lunch box???

~Kate

### caroline — 2013-04-06T01:59:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83040>

I don't have young kids anymore but I am sure you will get lots of ideas here. Have you gone to the website ...Nom Nom paleo? She has great recipes and two little boys....

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-06T17:24:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83120>

We homeschool, but often pack up food to take with us wherever we go. Our staples include things like cut up veggies like carrots, bell pepper, celery, cucumbers. We also often grill veggies like bell peppers, stuffed jalapenos and mini peppers wrapped in bacon, zucchini, eggplant and mushrooms, and they love the grilled veggies. Zucchini "latkes" is another favorite.

Meats like salami, leftover grass-fed burgers, meatloaf, sausage, or meatballs; salmon, sardines (or salmon, or tuna) mixed with Bubbie's relish, mustard, and hard boiled eggs, crab cakes,; soups and stews in a thermos like green chile stew, clam or seafood chowder, texas-style chili, etc. I also make a variety of casserole type dishes like red chile enchiladas made with grass-fed beef and eggplant for the "tortillas," butternut squash lasagna, frittatas, etc.

Thankfully my kids are not picky at all, so they happily eat lots of veggies. We also always keep frozen blueberries and rasberries on hand, too. I don't bake a lot, but if I did, I can think of a whole lot of other ideas. Some of the paleo cookbooks have good ideas for make-ahead food items that can be frozen and then used later.

### BJK77 — 2013-04-06T17:52:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83123>

I have a middle schooler and one in elementary school and we do a variety of the following for packed lunches:

\- meatballs
\- salmon spread with cut up veggies (smoked salmon/cream cheese mix)
\- roast beef
\- burgers
\- lettuce wraps (with ham, bacon, etc)
\- salads with bacon
\- beef stew / chili in a thermos

For sides they'll often take carrots or cut up peppers with ranch dip, muffins (grain free, of course!), homemade 'larabars,' berries, raw cheese and yogurt. It's definitely more difficult with my middle schooler because she tends to get made fun of for having "different" lunches. My 8-year old son couldn't care less what people say, so he's pretty easy!

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-07T02:19:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83157>

Oh Yes! Lettuce wraps for sure. Another favorite, for those not opposed to deli meats (we buy the best quality we can find) is using ham or roast beef as the wrap material, and stuffing with cukes, peppers, avocado, jicama, etc.

A good friend of mine has a daughter who has life-threatening allergies to nuts, dairy, and soy, and she has become very creative with her food ideas. I like this list of ideas, which is where I got the meat-wrap idea.

[http://www.consciouseatery.com/2012/08/whats-for-lunch.html](http://www.consciouseatery.com/2012/08/whats-for-lunch.html)

### cantweight — 2013-04-11T21:13:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83781>

I have an 8, 10, and 12 year old. Lunch is a tricky one for us. My 8 yr old especially is concerned about not having a sandwich.....he really wants a damn sandwich..lol

A lot of what I do I know is still not optimal. It is grain free and organic and I am trying!!

Apples and nut butter
Larabars
Kefir
organic lunch meat rolled up
cheese sticks
shrimp
leftover meats...especially beef and chicken kebab style
chicken salad made with organic mayo, almonds and grapes
seaweed....they love seaweed!
sometimes I make mac and cheese with corn pasta...probably a big no no but they loooove it!

For his birthday my 8 yr old kept saying all he wanted was a lunchable! what the hell! This kid does not have any memory of junk food like my older ones do...it is so strange.
He also tells me that as soon as he learns to drive he is driving to mc donalds. SMH.

### kathylu — 2013-04-12T02:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83844>

My son demands bread too. It's a constant struggle, occasionally I give in but only gluten free breads. He likes "bread-like" items made from coconut flour, so I will make "paleo" pancakes and muffins once a week. He also eats more fruit than I would like. However, his diet is light years better than any other child I know.

# Thread: What the heck happened to my sleep?!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-04-06T15:31:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/post-83114>

I am beyond baffled right now. My latest labs (posted in another thread, but I'll post them below too) are the best I've had in years and yet two days after having these drawn my sleep totally tanked! That was over 2 weeks ago and it hasn't improved yet. I am so exhausted and just frustrated, confused, angry . . . my labs are still far from perfect, but they do show a huge improvement, so what the heck happened to my sleep?!

When we started cutting wifi and the breakers to the bedrooms at night several weeks ago my sleep was awesome! I noticed a difference immediately. A few weeks after that we got a 10 gauss Magnetico pad and I was sleeping on that for about a week before my sleep tanked this time . . . could it really just be a detox effect??
We've tested and tested for EMF and I'm not finding any hidden/terribly high sources beyond things we can't control like the refrigerator/oven, etc. I cut back use of my laptop to maybe an hour a day and even that I've been doing outside while barefoot on the ground. I'm getting at least 2-3 hours a day of earthing in.

I tried increasing my Progesterone to 300 mg and while it did help my sleep for the few nights I did it, it also ended up making me extremely nauseous on the fourth night and I woke with major muscle weakness, which I later read is due to high Progesterone. So I cut back down to 200 mg last night and sleep was crap again. I wake in the middle of the night and feel wide awake and HOT for several hours before I can finally fall back to sleep again for a short while.

Considering the magnetico and my labs below, any thoughts on what may be causing this?? The only other thought I have is with my DHEA being a little high. I don't think Jack ever mentioned this could be a problem, BUT I know in the past when I was taking a higher amount of DHEA my sleep tanked even though I expected the opposite to happen. I'm thinking about skipping or lowering my DHEA the next couple of days to see what effect, if any, that has, but if anyone has any other thoughts/suggestions I'd love to hear them! Thanks

Current Supplements
\- 5 mcg T3
\- 1 gr Naturethroid
\- 200 mg Compounded Progesterone (I did start 100 mg Prometrium vaginally 3 days before testing, but I donâ€™t think that would have really impacted the results yet, right?)
\- 65 mg DHEA (Iâ€™m now decreasing that to 50 mg since my testosterone was high and Iâ€™m assuming the DHEA is converting to T; thinking about switching to 7-keto??)
\- 25 mg Pregnenolone (Iâ€™m now increasing to 50 mg)
\- 12,000 IU Vitamin D (going to increase this to 50,000 IU/day and hit tanning hard)

Hi BJK, so sorry for your sleep troubles....how wonderful for all you have done to minimize EMF. What a dream!

ugh. That is tough though....

Just on the Magnetico thing, we got ours this week and when I spoke with Dr Bonlie, he said it can take up to 2-3 months to really detoxify if you ever had metals, especially if you ever had amalgams. So maybe it's that? Maybe you could talk to them just to see?? Rule stuff out.

So sorry but you sound like you are going to figure this out too. You have come SO far and that is SO awesome and inspiring!!!!
Not sure this is helpful.....

### BJK77 — 2013-04-08T15:31:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/post-83344>

> [Joann said:](/goto/post?id=83264)
>
> DHEA....too high? thought it should be 350 optimally.

I was wondering about that too. I know before when I first started takign DHEA I was using a much higher dose. I didn't get testing done, but my sleep did get crappy and I ended up cutting back on the DHEA and sleep then improved. I have been cutting back the last 2 nights now though with no change at all.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=83265)
>
> did the expert check you car and work and not just your house?

He did check my vehicle, which I already knew wasn’t great, but I rarely drive it. At most, it’s 20 minutes a day to get to and from work. I didn’t have the EMF Engineer go into my workplace, but I took the meters in and I sit in front of a computer, so obviously it wasn’t great either, but it wasn’t as bad as I expected. I do use a laptop, but I have it in a docking station with an extra monitor, wired keyboard and wired mouse and the laptop itself is a good 3’ or more away from where I sit. I moved all the cables as far away from where I sit as possible and I brought in a huge Himalayan salt lamp to help as well. I also don’t have wifi in my office, thankfully. Plus, I only went into the office one day last week so I was mostly home and we have very low levels at home, not to mention the fact that I spent most of every day outside earthing last week.

I know I could be wrong, but my gut tells me this can’t be EMF. I 100% believe in the dangers of it, but we have really been doing so much over the last several weeks to minimize it as much as we possibly can. Like I said above, short of cutting out the refrigerator and oven I don’t see much more we can do. And to that regard, I have even started doing my long-term cooking like broths and roasts outside on the patio or in the garage in a crockpot or small convection toaster oven.

My neighborhood consists of 1 to 3-acre lots, so we’re fairly well spread out. I can see 2 of my neighbor’s wifi’s, but not at a strong enough signal to ever connect to them.

What doesn’t make sense to me is that my sleep greatly, HUGELY improved when we started cutting wifi and the breakers at night, so I know we are on the right path, but something else is obviously at play here.

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=83268)
>
> Hi BJK, so sorry for your sleep troubles....how wonderful for all you have done to minimize EMF. What a dream!
>
> ugh. That is tough though....
>
> Just on the Magnetico thing, we got ours this week and when I spoke with Dr Bonlie, he said it can take up to 2-3 months to really detoxify if you ever had metals, especially if you ever had amalgams. So maybe it's that? Maybe you could talk to them just to see?? Rule stuff out.
>
> So sorry but you sound like you are going to figure this out too. You have come SO far and that is SO awesome and inspiring!!!!
> Not sure this is helpful.....

Hope,

Thanks so much for sharing this info with me. I think I’ll give Dr. Bonlie a call today just to discuss. I do remember reading on their site that it may take some individuals 2-3 months to really notice an improvement and that there could be some detox effects, but I never expected to not sleep for 2-3 months!! It’s been just over 2 weeks now and I’m really feeling it . . . I can’t imagine going on like this for several more weeks  I did have a total of 9 amalgams removed about 2 years ago. 7 were done properly, but the first 2 were, unfortunately, done before I really knew better and though the dentist told me he took proper precautions I know it wasn't all done exactly like it should have been.

Thanks for the encouraging words! I hope to figure it out soon!!

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-08T16:04:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/post-83350>

BJ

listening to your post - we know - that you are mitigating EMF - my question to you is what are you doing grounding wise? also - with the time change and the move towards spring - have you noticed anything with regards to your "wind down" time before bed?

IME - I've noticed that b/c of the natural light coming through the windows until 730 or so - I'm forgetting to put on my "blue Blockers", I've also noticed that with the increase of seasonal allergies I'm not sleeping as well either. I know I've mitigated EMF at the house to the best of my ability, thus far still have a few more things to do, but EMF at work is a killer - looking into new options... we'll see what happens...

It might be a combination of both EMF, and hormones - btw How's your Thyroid doing? or possibly the increasing daylight or spring in bloom...

### BJK77 — 2013-04-08T20:03:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/post-83368>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=83350)
>
> BJ
>
> listening to your post - we know - that you are mitigating EMF - my question to you is what are you doing grounding wise? also - with the time change and the move towards spring - have you noticed anything with regards to your "wind down" time before bed?
>

I know I've mitigated EMF at the house to the best of my ability, thus far still have a few more things to do, but EMF at work is a killer - looking into new options... we'll see what happens...
>
> It might be a combination of both EMF, and hormones - btw How's your Thyroid doing? or possibly the increasing daylight or spring in bloom...

Hi Gretchen,

As far as grounding, I have been earthing a minimum of 3 hours each day for the last week and less time in the weeks prior. We used to have a grounding sheet on the bed, but we took that off when learning that it may actually have been acting as an antenna. I am anxiously awaiting the video we've heard so much about to learn to ground my laptop for the limited times I now use it. Other than that, I guess I'm not really doing anything else. What am I missing? I'd love any suggestions.

I really haven't been wearing my blue blockers, but we have a pretty consistent evening routine where we just use all natural light until sunset when we turn on our lanterns. We're either outside until then playing with the kids or we're inside reading - absolutely no electronics in my house past 6:30! It used to be 7:00, but when we all got used to that I started even earlier. I even cut the breakers to the bedrooms at that time. I'm a Drill Sergeant when it comes to that

I agree about the work environment. It's by far the toughest, but I'm so thankful I normally only have to go into the office 3 days a week. Last week it was only 1 day and my sleep still sucked, so I don't think I can blame it on any EMF at work.

Thyroid is actually decent. Well, Free T3 is anyway - for the first time since I've been paying attention to my labs it's actually in range. Free T4 is a bit low still and TSH is high, but it's at least better than it was.

### BJK77 — 2013-04-08T20:47:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/post-83372>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=83370)
>
> but how do you feel???? thyroid wise???

I was feeling really good until my sleep tanked! I actually was even starting to lift again which I hadn't done in years. Now I'm so exhausted and weak I doubt I could lift much of anything. I keep wondering if it's a detox effect that's causing me to feel so poorly right now or if it's simply lack of sleep.

My eyes are dry too (even though I drink at least a gallon of water a day), which I think I've heard Jack say is a sign of low Pg from EMF exposure?? But then again, couldn't it just be lack of sleep?! I'm not sure what's causing what these days. I do have a lot of muscle weakness and fatigue and definitely don't feel even remotely rested when I wake in the morning. It's beautiful outside today and I did sit on the grass for my lunch, but I'm trying to force myself to get out and take a few breaks and walk, but honestly my legs don't even want to move! Thyroid, lack of sleep, detox, hormones?!?! I have no clue . . .

### Joann — 2013-04-08T22:01:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/post-83379>

BJK, reread your post....Waking up in the middle of the night hot.....sounds hormonal to me. I don't know what dhea numbers were before, but what they are now, doesn't seem like there is a need to supplement.

I would try skipping dhea and or P to see what happens.

### BJK77 — 2013-04-08T22:54:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/post-83385>

You all are awesome! Thanks so much for helping me brainstorm. Just knowing I've got some support here and others to help me think through this makes me feel light years better than I did

Gretchen - Funny you should mention about sleeping somewhere else since that's exactly what I was thinking of last night when I couldn't sleep! I'm just not sure where to go. The basement seems like it would be a good option, but the last couple of times I slept down there with the kids my sleep was crap too . . . more from an uncomfortable air mattress than anythign else! I need to find something comfy to sleep on and then I can give that a try. Otherwise, it will be pointless.

We were also supposed to head to the beach this weekend which would have been a great experiment to see how things worked out, but that's now been put on hold until next weekend . . . if nothing works before that though I'll at least have it to look forward too.

I just can't think of a single thing I changed in my diet. It's tight as always. I am super strict about what I eat; carbs are always very low; all organic, grass-fed meats; tons of seafood; no sugar/grains/dairy/eggs, etc.

Hmmm . . . looking at my labs estrogen seems a bit low. It was really high back in December at 331, but now is only 63 with a range of 35-400.

Joann - Initially I kept thinking hormones too, but then I look at my labs and they look better than they have in years! I know I shouldn't go by numbers on a piece of paper though. How we feel is so much more important . . . it's just hard not to want that stinkin' gold star for having perfect numbers! I did cut DHEA the last 2 nights and I think you're right - I should continue to cut that until I get new labs done, which I'm scheduled for in about 2 weeks. I've been taking it in the morning.

Pg I take in the evening right before bed and it has always helped me sleep before. In fact, when I upped from 200 mg to 300 mg last week to see if it would help my sleep it did the first couple of nights I did it, but then I got super nauseous and woke with even worse muscle weakness. I later read that's a sign of high Pg, so I backed down to 200 mg again. I have 100 mg capsules, so last night I tried splitting it up and taking 250 mg, but that didn't seem to do anything at all.

I don't want to mess around with all the hormones willy nilly, but at this point I'm getting somewhat desperate!

# Thread: Amazing results in 3 months!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-results-in-3-months.6662/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 25

### kleinb87 — 2013-04-06T17:22:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-results-in-3-months.6662/post-83119>

So it has been 3 months from getting first lab tests and fully implementing all of mitochondria rx and emf rx. My labs were trashed to say the least even while eating paleo. Zero on all hormones, inflammation high, and my only saving grace was a decent vit. D at 60. Digestion was horrible and my body was not optimal at all.

Yesterday, I got my DHEA, Vit. D, hs-crp, and homocysteine levels.

DHEA-s: 235 (98-340)
Vit.d: 96.5 (100)
Hs-crp: 0.96
Homocysteine: 7.8 (7-12)

Biggest improvement for me was hormones and hs-crp. Hs-crp before was 5.2. Body composition is slowly improving, and my menstrual cycle has returned after not having it for two years (no kids, 26 yrs old).

Photoelectric effect, grounding, CT and epipaleo work.

I am so thankful for Jack and his insight. Am I optimal? Not yet, but I have already made a 180 degree turn around from January. Another couple of months and I can guarantee I will be even better. Thanks for all of the support from the forum and Jack!

### kleinb87 — 2013-04-07T17:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-results-in-3-months.6662/post-83228>

Thanks everyone! I may not live by the ocean, but moving there soon. After everyone thought I was crazy for laying outside in the snow with shorts and a tank top, it has proven to work plus minimizing all sorts of EMF. when you know better, you do better.

What has also improved immensely is my level of thinking, normal is no longer my standard. I am rising above and quickly seeing how "normal" people think. Roadblocks have been present especially with my new methods for teaching curriculum in Spanish from my department, but the students' response has been incredible. They are truly learning and not going through the motions.

My writing has improved as well as my thoughts on life. The impossible is now what I am challenging and I know that it can happen as long as the work is put forth.

Lets all keep moving forward and change the world.

### diane — 2013-04-07T23:21:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-results-in-3-months.6662/post-83258>

Amazing results Bobbie! Keep it up!

### kleinb87 — 2013-05-20T05:31:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-results-in-3-months.6662/post-86511>

1 month later and feeling better than ever. I have now found that my BUN/ creatine ratio has improved and is now 12! Na is 142 mmol/L (a little high). Body composition is slowly going down with increased CT and photoelectric effect (until I move to the ocean in the late Spring it is 2- 22 pound bags of ice while I am laying out in the sun). But best of all, my thinking has improved 100%, brain fog is gone from the fall, and I feel like I can accomplish anything. I have since quit a job that was not fulfilling and went all in working on my own business full time and rebranding it to do not only what I am good at, but what I am passionate about (creativity and making the impossible possible). Full launch of new rebranding is tomorrow, and I'm not worried at all. Scary, right? Without Dr. Kruse's help this would have just still been a dream because my thinking would still have been lacking and clouded with fear. This is no longer the case.

"When you know better, you do better." -Dr. Kruse

Results are possible when you put forth the effort.

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-11T01:12:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-niacin-harmful-or-helpful.6665/post-83676>

I can't speak to the niacin, but several varieties of medicinal mushrooms might be more effective than niacin without any potential side effects. Maitake (Grifola frondosa) and Enokitake (Flammulina velutipes, which can be found in most grocery stores), as well as Shiitake are known to lower LDL. Shiitake can lower both HDL and LDL, though, so it might be safer to use Maitake. Maitake are fabulously wonderful mushrooms with a whole host of benefits.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11520942](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11520942)

# Thread: DHEA and Pregnenolone Dosing Question?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-and-pregnenolone-dosing-question.6668/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trinity — 2013-04-07T04:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-and-pregnenolone-dosing-question.6668/post-83176>

Hi,

Based upon my most recent ASI and DHEA labs, I think I need to raise my pregnenolone and DHEA to help the AM cortisol but I am not sure exactly how much (don't want to overshoot and screw up my sleep which has been slowly improving  I was actually surprised at how pitiful my DHEA still is on my last ASI given that I am taking 25 mg DHEA and 25 mg 7-keto DHEA. From what I've read, that seems like a high dose for a woman (I am 34 if that is relevant).

My current dosage schedule is:

25mg DHEA (12.5 first thing upon rising, 12.5 at noon )
25mg 7-Keto DHEA (first thing upon rising)
50 mg Pregnenolone (25 first thing upon rising, 25 at noon )

The DHEA and Pregnenolone are currently sublingual formulations by Douglas Labs. I am, however, considering either switching entirely or adding some of the micronized lipid matrix DHEA and pregnenolone JanSz recommended because the brand I'm taking clearly isn't getting the job done (or I'm just not taking enough). I realize that everybody is different and this isn't an exact science, but I would appreciate it if some people experienced with tweaking DHEA and pregnenolone to optimize their cortisol could suggest a ballpark adjustment based upon my current labs and dosing schedule.

NOTE to JanSz: In my other post you suggested starting with 2 of the 150mg MLM tabs. Does that still seem right to you based on my labs (I don't think I had posted my labs when you replied last time). Also, what would u reccommend for DHEA if I switch over to the MLM version of the DHEA?

[http://www.amazon.com/Douglas-Labs-...=1365295872&sr=8-2&keywords=douglas+labs+dhea](http://www.amazon.com/Douglas-Labs-Sublingual-Health-Beauty/dp/B00380WKEI/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1365295872&sr=8-2&keywords=douglas+labs+dhea)
[http://www.amazon.com/Douglas-Labs-...915&sr=1-1&keywords=douglas+labs+pregnenolone](http://www.amazon.com/Douglas-Labs-Pregnenolone-25mg-tabs/dp/B0006SY6TW/ref=sr_1_1?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1365295915&sr=1-1&keywords=douglas+labs+pregnenolone)

MOST RECENT CORTISOL TEST RESULTS: DATE COLLECTED 3/14/13

Time Me Range %
8AM 4.38 3.5-6.3 32%
12PM 2.19 1.4-2.8 55%
4PM 1.85 .8-2.4 65%
8PM 1.31 .6-1.6 71%
12AM 1.15 .3-1.2 95%
4AM 1.33 .3-1.7 72%

DHEA:
8AM 3.2 ng/ml (2.8-12.7)
8PM 3.5 ng/ml (2.7-9.0)
Midnight 3.8 ng/ml (1.8-8.1)

Thanks,
Trinity

### cantweight — 2013-04-07T23:45:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-some-insight.6679/post-83263>

Posed a question to Jack....thinking perhaps I didn't word it well. I guess what I am trying to figure out is...if we unleash all kinds of bacteria and pathogens with EMF exposure then do we need to treat them once they have been unleashed or just deal with the EMF exposure and let the body fix itself.

Does that make sense? I don't want to keep treating all kinds of illnesses with various meds, but if once they are able to dominate in the body through the EMF destruction must they be treated into submission while the body is strengthened through diet, CT, earthing, and of course limiting EMF exposure.

Jacks reply was that I didn't get it and should move.

It's extremely possible that I don't get it....there is a lot to "get". I have moved. Maybe not as remotely as he did, but not everyone has the means and ability to do what he has done and retain gainful employment. I am on over 8 acres in an agricultural area, surrounded by farms where people have shit for internet and cell reception. We have well water and copper pipes, have begun turning everything off in the house. Were just waiting for our tax bill which we got yesterday...grrrr...to see if there was money left to get some other goodies. There is not. But we are getting the magneticos anyway, because I feel like I do "get" the importance of addressing anything I have the power to.

My kids are all being treated for some serious crap and their little bodies are a mess. This is why we moved. A primal urgent need to protect ourselves. I'm just wondering if I can protect them from more pharmacological onslaught or if it has become a necessary evil.

Appreciate any insight and education y'all can offer.

Christi

We've only been here since last summer, so I believe it will take time. As bad as the EMF situation was in our old place I will admit total ignorance about our day to day carrying on. The ipods, cell phones, wi-fi etc. So the losnig those things has been very recent.

I noticed big improvement quite soon after moving and the funny thing is we were all forced to sleep in the basement (slab) from June to Sept as the house is older and was undergoing some renovations. We slept great!! My husband wants to move us all back down there. I want magneticos..lol.

Organic and whole food since 2009 Gluten free and very low grain has been over a year. Really pushing the seafood has just been this year (2013) and the response from the kids is excellent.

We are opening our pool next Monday and hoping to switch it to ozonated. Should be quite chilly. Hard to get the kids to CT. I have tried icing the occipital lobe on my little guy with tourettes but am thinking maybe I'll switch to frontal lobe since it is motor? Feel like I am doing this all so blindly sometimes. Desperation I suppose.

I have given up on conventional medicine and am getting a degree myself so i don't have to deal with the BS anymore. We got kicked out of pediatricians office...lol. I pitched a fit. Called bullshit on them ranted about what a joke of a practice they were, ripped a vaccine poster off the wall (this all happened in the hall loudly, my kids were so embarrassed!) They mailed me all 3 kids medical records from birth and said dont come back..lol!

I just want to get them off these meds but am scared of some of the seriousness of what they have. Giardia, babesia, batonella, positive ANA, stuff that I am not sure can be cleared with diet, CT, and grounding/low EMF, light cycle etc. Would love for someone to tell me it can

# Thread: Savvy Analysis of this Recent Article?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/savvy-analysis-of-this-recent-article.6680/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-08T01:16:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/savvy-analysis-of-this-recent-article.6680/post-83286>

All kinds of alarms go off when I read it...anyone care to analyze, I've been doing too much yard work today to give it a shot.

First read about it here: [http://www.forbes.com/sites/larryhu...w-pathway-linking-heart-disease-to-carnitine/](http://www.forbes.com/sites/larryhusten/2013/04/07/researchers-find-new-pathway-linking-heart-disease-to-carnitine/)

And here is the research article:

[http://www.nature.com/nm/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nm.3145.html](http://www.nature.com/nm/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nm.3145.html)

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-08T01:45:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/savvy-analysis-of-this-recent-article.6680/post-83294>

quick glance at the actual study - says its how the gut microbes convert the choline, phosphatydle choline, and caratine from meat links to heart disease - since I don't have access to the full article - I infer the following: if your gut microbes are based upon SAD - then yes there might be a link - but if you've got proper gut microbes - say those you get from eating epi-paleo (especially raw) then it shouldn't be an issue

this is all about the microbes - and whether you have the right ones or the wrong ones...

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-08T05:36:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/savvy-analysis-of-this-recent-article.6680/post-83328>

I read the full article. The study is based on the correlation of trimethylamine-N-oxide (TMAO) levels and atherosclorosis. The authors state may not fully know mechanism for this. This is not the point of their study. this study is about TMAO production by specific intestinal microbiota in the presence of carnitine. When they wiped out the gut bacteria of human subjects for two weeks then fed them meat they produced no TMAO. Vegans and Vegetarians also produce little TMAO when fed l-carnitine. there is no discussion about how the diets in the TMAO-artherosclerosi studies were controlled for. I think the title implies a link that may not be fully established.
The best part of the article is the following:
The high level of meat consumption in the developed world is linked to CVD risk, presumably owing to the large content of saturated fats and cholesterol in meat1, 2. However, a recent meta-analysis of prospective cohort studies showed no association between dietary saturated fat intake and CVD, prompting the suggestion that other environmental exposures linked to increased meat consumption are responsible3. In fact, the suspicion that the cholesterol and saturated fat content of red meat may not be sufficiently high enough to account for the observed association between CVD and meat consumption has stimulated investigation of alternative disease-promoting exposures that accompany dietary meat ingestion, such as high salt content or heterocyclic compounds generated during cooking

Finally it is starting to be accepted that the fat-cholesterol CVD connection doesn't hold up.

# Thread: Can I have some help understanding thyroid?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tweety — 2013-04-08T02:06:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/post-83301>

I have been on NDT/Armour successfully since last summer. I cruised through the summer at 90-120mg and did very well. In Nov my numbers looked great, (really low TSH - .1something, which is where it was recommended I aim) though I can't remember the rest of them now. In Dec hypothyroid symptoms showed up and we bumped up to 180mg of Armour. I did really well. In March, I started to get heart palps and extreme hunger, so I backed off to 150mg. That worked well, for a week. Hyperthyroid showed up again, palps and hunger, so down to 120mg. Continuing this pattern, now I am down to 60mg of Armour. I realize it is April in the NE USA and light cycles are lengthening.

I still feel really warm- much warmer than everyone else around me. We keep our house at 55-60 degrees. I have not CTed in a while, but I did start walking my dog outside barefoot in early March, even in the snow. We're talking 1 minute or less several times a day. Not 30-40min of grounding but still a connection. I also had to quit my progesterone after 15mos- weight gain around the middle and depression during period, swollen feet and ankles. I have felt wonderful the last month, very happy and just feel good in my bones. I know quitting P and lowering thyroid was what I needed, though my sleep is still iffy and today I had a killer migraine like I haven't had in years. Therefore I think i will need to try a different form of progesterone and maybe a combo of E and T in the near future.

What I am struggling to understand is the thyroid. Did I go hyper and now cannot tolerate any thyroid supplementation? I still find myself ridiculously hungry and able to eat much more than I should. Or am I healing and my body is trying to take over on its own? I am 34 and not really overweight (but at my heaviest and worst body composition in the past 12yrs) so its possible that I wasn't as far off track as I felt. I still have much healing to do. Leaky gut symptoms seem to be increasing after never really having any. I can't tolerate alot of foods that I used to (almonds, avocados, nuts, coconut meat...). They all cause bloating.

I have read and reread so much here and just when I start to connect things I lose sight of it for a while and have to try again. I am not able to do labs for a while, but intend to redo my ASI/hormone panel in a month or two. Last time I had low cortisol for all four readings. My energy is excellent, but I do not tolerate any caffeine (mild headaches in the morning) which makes me feel my adrenals still need addressing. Sorry for rambling, trying to include the main points.

Any thoughts thyroid gurus?

Thanks

### MeghanK — 2013-04-08T02:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/post-83315>

Have you read Stop the Thyroid Madness? Check out that book and website.....

### tweety — 2013-04-08T03:18:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/post-83321>

yes, but I should revisit it I guess

### Grizz — 2014-01-18T23:48:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/post-106148>

This changes everything:
The Thyroid-Gut Connection ✰ ✰ ✰

**Healing the gut-thyroid axis**

> ....All of these connections make it clear that you can’t have a healthy gut without a healthy thyroid, and you can’t have a healthy thyroid without a healthy gut. To restore proper function of the gut-thyroid axis, both must be addressed simultaneously.
>
> Healing the gut is a huge topic that can’t be covered adequately in a few short sentences. But I will say this: the first step is always to figure out what’s causing the gut dysfunction. As we’ve reviewed in this article, low thyroid is one possible cause, but often hypochlorhydria, infections, dysbiosis, food intolerances (especially gluten), stress and other factors play an even more significant role. The second step is to address these factors and remove any potential triggers. The third step is to restore the integrity of the gut barrier. My preferred approach for this last step is the GAPS diet....
> Rest of the article:
> [http://chriskresser.com/the-thyroid-gut-connection](http://chriskresser.com/the-thyroid-gut-connection)

For MUCH MORE on the thyroid see Thyroid, Hypothyroid in Testimonials:
[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-testimonials](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-testimonials)

Grizz

### Albert83BCN — 2014-01-19T00:09:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/post-106153>

These resources pointed by Grizz and MeghanK are great ones. You might be interested as well in getting Dr Kharrazian book 'Why Do I Still Have Thyroid Symptoms?' IMHO is a really great book, it will give you a lot of insight and references so you can keep digging. Of course you'll find a lot of gold in the blogposts by JK as well.

You're not alone, I feel the same when I start understanding something just to realize there's a ton more of things that I missed completely. This is big in nutrition and health, opening a new door leads to a lot of confusion... but hopefully one day things will start falling into place.... hopefully

Lots of homework to do I guess. Good luck!

I have done wakame seaweed smoothies witch chocolate too.. But IDK I would personally not use protein powder ever. I get way too much protein anyways with my foods, huh. I do not believe in such unnatural foods anyway, if there are better options.

I sometimes make raw egg smoothies for hubs, he loves those! Raw Colostrum milk, raw eggs, raw honey, vanilla beans and - ready. They are yummy!

In summer I do smoothies from nettles and other wild edibles - pur with water. Very nice and makes me feel great! You could do them with fruits too - like nettles + papaya. Those are good too. But I am not a smoothie fan really.

### cmegan — 2013-04-08T14:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83342>

Caroline, I have been using Tera's Whey from goat milk...I do not know about Dave Asprey's protein powder but will look into it...

I got back into doing smoothies several weeks ago after contracting a stomach flu. I couldn't bear to look at meat or seafood for at least a week, I was eating mostly broths with vegetables and then started using smoothies to get some protein. It was the only protein I could digest at the time. I have to admit now that I am feeling better and the weather is getting warmer in NY I am having a difficult time eating red meat (I eat lamb burgers) in the morning. I have just been mixing the goat whey with water and coconut oil in a blender. I am allergic to casein but have had no problems with this whey and my morning glucose has been steady...

### cmegan — 2013-04-08T15:37:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83345>

massage therapist
local farmer
chiropracter/acupuncture/alternative health professional

engineers........we need smart brains to reverse this mess.

### freshveggies — 2013-04-09T18:27:53+0300

### tweety — 2013-04-09T18:31:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/safer-career-options-for-our-kids.6689/post-83501>

I know a woman locally who does quite well with art as a career. She works with sculpture materials and projects on a large scale. But the communities around us always have her working on something. Art is an excellent option, he just has to be smart about what the job entails. I actually was thinking illustrator, but came to the same thought about it being mostly computer work these days.

### freshveggies — 2013-04-09T18:38:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/safer-career-options-for-our-kids.6689/post-83502>

But the communities around us always have her working on something. Art is an excellent option, he just has to be smart about what the job entails. I actually was thinking illustrator, but came to the same thought about it being mostly computer work these days.

We have a sculpter in our town who does very well also. He is mostly into drawing. I know he is young, but we need to start thinking about it and maybe have him shadow some different occupations.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-09T21:14:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/safer-career-options-for-our-kids.6689/post-83513>

I have a friend who works as an artist. She has a multi-pronged approach to keep her dream alive and still earn a living. She teaches art/drawing at the community colleges to seniors, art classes in her home, and she displays art in a couple of galleries. She also donates her art for a banner that lines the street of her small town during an annual festival (20 artists volunteer art and all different banners are hung...it's very cool).

I think if he wants to pursue art or anything for that matter he needs to think outside the box on how to make this an income bearing business but not in the normal sense. It can be done.

### Hope — 2013-04-09T21:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/safer-career-options-for-our-kids.6689/post-83514>

For seasonal jobs.....Liberty Tax services in my area has people standing on the street waving them to their offices every day. LOL.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/safer-career-options-for-our-kids.6689/post-83521>

She teaches art/drawing at the community colleges to seniors, art classes in her home, and she displays art in a couple of galleries. She also donates her art for a banner that lines the street of her small town during an annual festival (20 artists volunteer art and all different banners are hung...it's very cool).

# Thread: Remineralization of water Concentrace/Salt and how much?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-08T20:56:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-83374>

So I'm thinking I want to add minerals to my RO water I buy. I buy 6 5 gallon jugs for my water dispenser at home and was thinking about using either Concentrace trace mineral drops and/or Himalayan/Real Salt.

So whats the better plan and how much do I need to add per gallon. I thought I remembered reading 1/4 teaspoon per liter which would be 4 and 3/4 teaspoons of salt? Sounds like a ton.

I don't remember where I read that so I'm not sure it was 1/4 teaspoon per liter.

Any recommendations on how much Concentrace/Salt to use?

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-09T03:16:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-83419>

We have always added minerals to our RO water but just a couple of days ago decided to add Real Salt to the water. I love the taste of the "salt water". It tastes sweet. We are only adding about 1/4 tsp/40 oz so that may not be enough but it sure does make the water taste good!

### Ariel8100 — 2013-04-12T20:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-83921>

If the body is properly charged with minerals it will be able to deal with the minute amounts of those heavy metals. Currently I am using 1/4 teaspoon per40 oz. It tastes sweet. I think accurate quantity per40 oz.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-14T02:36:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-84009>

> [Ariel8100 said:](/goto/post?id=83921)
>
> If the body is properly charged with minerals it will be able to deal with the minute amounts of those heavy metals. Currently I am using 1/4 teaspoon per40 oz. It tastes sweet. I think accurate quantity per40 oz.

That's around 4 teaspoons of salt per 5 gallon jug. I can do that. Would there be any problem with adding the concentrated minerals and salt to the 5 gallon jugs before going to the place to get them filled? I figure that's the easiest way to do it.

Rather than going in there and adding my Concentrace and Salt to the bottles while they are filling them I can take off the caps and put the minerals/salt in the bottles put the caps on and than get them filled at the place.

The minerals/salt should mix in the water just fine. I wonder how much plastic if any leaches into the water. I don't really have a better option than my 5 gallon jugs though. I can't afford a whole whose filter or anything at this point. I hope that a good eating plan in Epi-Paleo and water with plenty of minerals will deal with the toxins including any leaching plastic...if that's even a concern. Do plastics like those in a 5 gallon jug leach in room temperatures? I suppose when they are in the car/trunk they might get warmer and perhaps leach some..don't know enough about the leaching of plastics.

Also does anyone know what kind of plastics is generally in these bottles? Does it have BPA?

### Celeste — 2014-07-04T08:25:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-133116>

I use three gallon glass bottles. That way I never have to worry about them in the car. I just was about to post a question about Concentrate and fluoride levels when I saw this thread. I have the same general question about using Concentrace. Is it okay?

### jim demetz — 2014-12-30T23:00:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-151204>

Jack, can you elaborate why you wouldn't add salt? Does it make a difference if it's on food or in water? I have pretty much changed everything diet wise since I came across your information. I was following Dr. Robert Cassar and his protocols until then. Everything that he says is so different and as always the science and explanations behind it makes so much sense. He is all about being vegan, except honey I believe. He talks greatly about eliminating parasites from pork, beef and fish(especially sushi). He's very big on hydrating, especially with blue-green algae water, with Himalayan sea salt added. Also can you finally straighten out once and for all, what is the best water to drink? So tired of the bullshit science behind all of the theories. One week I'm drinking distilled, the next RO, the next Spring, then alkaline. Much appreciate any feedback and keep the crusade going. The work you do is amazing and the fact that you afford everyday people the opportunity to take control of their own health will pay back in dividends!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-31T15:17:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-151233>

salt destroys the EZ formed in water.........so if you add salt to water you must restructure it and that costs you energy. You dont need salt in your water.......you can use it on your food.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-31T15:18:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-151234>

^^^^this is in Pollack's book, the Fourth Phase of water.

### yewwei.tan — 2014-12-31T18:03:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-151248>

I was following Dr. Robert Cassar and his protocols until then. Everything that he says is so different and as always the science and explanations behind it makes so much sense. He is all about being vegan, except honey I believe. He talks greatly about eliminating parasites from pork, beef and fish(especially sushi). He's very big on hydrating, especially with blue-green algae water, with Himalayan sea salt added. Also can you finally straighten out once and for all, what is the best water to drink? So tired of the bullshit science behind all of the theories. One week I'm drinking distilled, the next RO, the next Spring, then alkaline. Much appreciate any feedback and keep the crusade going. The work you do is amazing and the fact that you afford everyday people the opportunity to take control of their own health will pay back in dividends!

**The recommendation**: RO water or Northern Hemisphere Spring Water is the best

Can you get away with other types of water? Yes, if your redox potential is good.

Jack is right that adding salt to water decreases the EZ of said water. Gerald Pollacks book is the go-to source of info -- [http://www.amazon.com/dp/0962689548](http://www.amazon.com/dp/0962689548)

I asked before if drinking "good water" actually leads to more EZ water in the cell. I suspect the answer is Yes. This is probably due to Onsager's Reciprocity Relationship, whereby higher-energy, lower-density water that is in the GI tract naturally moves towards areas of lower-energy, higher-density water.

\----

Is getting good water absolutely important? I don't know, but if you're in a bad environment, you don't want the extra handicap of bad water.

For me, getting good water from the Northern Hemisphere didn't move the needle on health when I was in Melbourne (37 degrees south of the equator). I move to Cairns, which is 17 degrees south of the equator, and the lousy tap water didn't stop my recovery at all.

.....

### ssj3 — 2015-01-03T01:38:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-151417>

Would there be certain situations where salt is favourable??? Just a few thoughts... we have EZ on both sides of cell membrane, if salt exists on outside = diminished EZ, but a more negative EZ. Will this pull more hydronium ions from inner cell thus reducing its side? A high salt concentration can also create a crystalline structure that excludes, so we eat a fresh oyster, lots of salt (water), will this then increase this salt concentration outside the cell and prevent any 'toxic' material from entering the inside of the cell naturally??? Could this then be exploited, could this salt concentration then be removed and draw nutrients inside the cell?

# Thread: Hot Thermomgenesis Valid?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-thermomgenesis-valid.6695/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-04-09T03:01:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-thermomgenesis-valid.6695/post-83413>

Just wondering anyone and especially Jack's thoughts on this since he is mentioned in the article.....[http://180degreehealth.com/2013/04/hot-water-vs-cold-water-thermogenesis?utm\_source=feedburner&utm\_medium=feed&utm\_campaign=Feed%3A+180degreehealth%2Frss+%28180+Degree+Health%29&utm\_content=Google+Feedfetcher](http://180degreehealth.com/2013/04/hot-water-vs-cold-water-thermogenesis?utm_source=feedburner&utm_medium=feed&utm_campaign=Feed%3A+180degreehealth%2Frss+%28180+Degree+Health%29&utm_content=Google+Feedfetcher)

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-10T00:50:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-thermomgenesis-valid.6695/post-83538>

I'm not familiar with Matt Stone, but based ona cursory view of his website, he appears to be one of those people trying to make money by being on the anti-paleo side of things, the "I'm going to help fix you when Paleo breaks you," mentality. Clearly, he is orders of magnitude removed from all the information Jack has posted these past several months.

One statement in the blog post:

"Cold temperatures in winter are not conducive to better health and a higher metabolic rate either - or at least not greater leanness. Winter is fattening and exacerbates most health conditions, whereas the hot temperatures of summer or the tropics tend to favor greater leanness and lessen most health conditions. Most on thyroid meds have to up their dosage in the winter time just to keep hypothyroid symptoms at bay."

I don't think he did any actual historical research to verify the first statement. Many traditional cultures have a history of cold water plunges during winter, and often cite increased immunity as a reason for doing them. Secondly, the blog post is limited in what it is presenting as the PURPOSE of cold water immersions, and he appears saying that increased metabolism and weight loss are the only reasons people are doing CT.

His next statement about winter being fattening and exacerbating most health conditions is especially interesting, given all that I have learned these past several months. If he spent any time at all learning about what Jack has been presenting since the beginning of this winter, I think he might have a different perspective. I wish I could summarize it all in a snap, but I'd have to go re-read several blogs and forum posts to sum it up into a concise statement, but anyone who has been paying attention can see the flaw in his logic here, I assume.

"None of this of course takes into account the unrealistic idea that one is going to submerge themselves into icy water, intentionally, with any kind of regularity. Talk about going against instincts â€“ especially if you have a subpar metabolism and already have a major aversion to cold"

I don't know if this guy Matt has tried CT or not. I am doubtful that he's experienced CT outside where one gets the benefit of grounding, CT, and possibly the photoelectric effect, all combined into a session of ultimate awesomeness. He thinks it's an unrealistic idea to expect someone to submerge oneself in cold water regularly. Especially in winter, I presume. I have done more CT this past winter than I did last summer, and I found myself abandoning other enjoyable activities so that I could go hike up into a canyon and CT in my snowy, mountain environment. In fact, I still wish I had more time weekly to pack in all the things I like to do so that I could still ensure regular, weekly CT sessions outside. It's a juggle and I'm still trying to figure it all out. But very worth it to me.

### SCRN2007 — 2013-04-10T02:10:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-thermomgenesis-valid.6695/post-83546>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=83413)
>
> Just wondering anyone and especially Jack's thoughts on this since he is mentioned in the article.....[http://180degreehealth.com/2013/04/hot-water-vs-cold-water-thermogenesis?utm\_source=feedburner&utm\_medium=feed&utm\_campaign=Feed%3A+180degreehealth%2Frss+%28180+Degree+Health%29&utm\_content=Google+Feedfetcher](http://180degreehealth.com/2013/04/hot-water-vs-cold-water-thermogenesis?utm_source=feedburner&utm_medium=feed&utm_campaign=Feed%3A+180degreehealth%2Frss+%28180+Degree+Health%29&utm_content=Google+Feedfetcher)

Matt Stone has no qualifications whatsoever. When he first came on the scene I googled him to see if he had any education whatsoever regarding physiology . . . nope. Listen to him at your own peril. He recommends eating Snickers bars and such to "recover" the body from dieting. As Jack has stated, carbs in the short term might seem to help your thyroid from the possible pitfalls of traditional low-carb paleo, but the price you will pay in the end is HUGE. Hopefully Jack will chime in here if I am incorrect, but the preceding is my understanding of the situation. My take on Matt Stone is that he is profiting from telling carb-craving paleo blogger women with crappy hormone panels that it is okay to eat carbs to "recover" their basal body temps. I think it is a line of crap, as Jack's statements regarding "safe carbs" point out.

### cantweight — 2013-04-09T16:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83474>

Jack's facebook post about affection has the wheels in my head turning....

I am an admitted slacker when it comes to keeping up with blogs. I am in school and it takes up 95% of my reading time. Summer is my time to catch up.

Is there a blog I have missed that covers the phenomenon of human contact. Why we crave it? Why we need it? Why the baby that has it thrives and the baby that does not fails?

Oxytocin and hormone production being an obvious...I am curious about electricity. Do we share electrons? What about when we pet animals? It's not just the nicety of it all, it never is. There is always biology. My husband and I are different people when we are connected, skin touching skin is much more constant in summer months or less clothed due to cold adaption winter months. The more disconnected we become from any aspect of life Earth or human the closer we get to death. Loneliness I would bet rivals many other neolithic diseases. Where does this tie in?

If I missed it please point me to the blog as my curiosity is piqued. Again.

Thanks,
Christi

She touches on this stuff a little there, too. And she said loneliness is a huge contributor to disease. And I see that. Some people are lonely in marriage, too. We were literally, actually MADE for connection. Dr Pert also talks about the environment, like how we are disconnected from nature etc...not nearly the way Jack does, but it's still great stuff.

I read somewhere that you should hug for 30 seconds. Even smiling at another person is a good thing.....

You are going to be an awesome NP!!!!!

### cantweight — 2013-04-10T04:03:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83567>

I was just thinking on this so much recently went I went to my uncle bobs 85th bday party. My kids asked if he was going to be on oxygen or if he could do anything and I realized they are thinking of my grandmom that at that age had already run her race so to speak.

Uncle Bob was the life of his party Jim Beam in hand. I thought about him and how he lived.....he always had love in his life, happiness, and contentment. Human contact and strong circle of friends and family. It has to be a piece of the puzzle
