### justmisunderstood — 2013-04-15T03:18:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-with-bloodwork-please-help-me.6780/post-84120>

Hello!
I am a 33-year-old female, 5'5", 170 pounds. I have a horrible quality of life that I want to improve. My main motivation is that I want to get pregnant as soon as possible, but not before obtaining optimal health. I need advice! Please help me!

Issues:
(For the last 8 years): muscle/joint pain; pinched nerves; extreme stiffness upon waking; muscle weakness; complete lack of stamina; run out of energy quickly; constant fatigue; always freezing cold hands & feet; intolerance to cold; periodic night sweats; periodic insomnia; bad acid reflux; constipation or diarrhea; excessive thirst/dry mouth; excessive phlegm; acne; irregular cycles (31-47 days); dry itchy skin; never feel rested (sleeping 11-12 hrs a night without an alarm). Waking up with less than 9-10 hours of sleep feels exactly like trying to come out of anesthesia, I will be nauseated for several hours, and I will be sleepy all day and need a nap at some point. Some days I awake to find my body feeling like I was hit by a truck.

(Since going Paleo - 3 months): always hungry, even after eating; blurry vision/headaches/shaking between meals; foggy brain; feeling like I'm going to faint; nausea; dizziness when standing; tinnitus; rollercoaster moods; mid to late-cycle spotting/bleeding; migraines for 3 days before period; crawling sensations on my skin; weight-gain; energy is better but still too tired to exercise; still struggling with the energy/stamina to prepare meals

My recent bloodwork:
Cardio CRP: 0.6 (Ref <1.0)
Hemoglobin A1C: 5.1 (Ref <5.7)
Vitamin D: 33 (Ref 30-100) \*low (this is with 8000 IU/day for 3 months)
TSH: 6.34 (Ref .40-4.50) \*high
Free T4: 1.1 (Ref 0.8-1.8)
Free T3: 3.0 (Ref 2.3-4.2)
Reverse T3: 20 (Ref 7-24)
DHEA: 1193 (Ref 102-1185) \*high
FSH: 4.9 (Ref 1.9-9.1)
LH: 5.2 (Ref 0.5-16.9)
Fasting Insulin: 11 (Ref <10) \*high
Fasting AM Cortisol: 28.4 (Ref 4.0-22.0) \*high
LDL-C: 136 (Ref <100) \*high
Triglycerides: 156 (Ref <100) \*high
HDL-C: 50 (Ref 60-80) \*low
(Heavy Metals Test: normal)

What's going on here? The Paleo diet alone is apparently not enough to "cure" me.

\>I need to lose weight, but I'm still too tired to exercise, and even mild exertion will result in extreme pain and stiffness the next day. It takes a long time to recover. If I eat very little food, I do not lose weight, but if I eat 3 "normal" meals, I gain weight.
\>I don't understand why my cortisol is still so high, since I've eliminated most of my stress.
\>I can't seem to get my blood sugar normalized, no matter what I eat. Before going Paleo, I could go all day without eating. Now it seems I can't go 2 hours before getting uncomfortable symptoms.
\>I need to get my adrenal, thyroid, and sex hormones normalized, but I don't know what to do.

Here's some background, in case it helps...

When I was about 8 years old, I fell from a high sliding board and broke both of my wrists; concussion was suspected but never confirmed. (I never considered this relevant until I read one of Jack's articles about head trauma...)

At some point around puberty, I started sleeping a lot. I was always sleepy, no matter how long I slept. I woke up tired; I went to bed tired. I could fall asleep in the middle of a loud party. I became known as the girl who was "always tired" or "always sleeping in the corner". With the earlier and earlier school times, it got worse, and I was late every morning because I could not be woken up. I would sleep right through alarms. My mother would pour cold water in my face, and drag me onto the floor, only to find me asleep again. I became grumpy and angry all the time because I was so tired. All I wanted to do was sleep. My parents thought I was just lazy, and gaining too much weight, so they made me exercise after school for a few years. Although I love exercise, it only made me more tired, I never lost any weight, and I would often get home and collapse into bed until the next morning.

I also developed bad acne, and was prescribed antibiotics for several years (which did nothing).

Around age 16, I caught mononucleosis and was told I would experience extreme fatigue. I remember thinking, "this is how I always feel". It was then that I realized something was wrong with me. I spent the next several years going to traditional and "alternative" doctors, only to be laughed at or told there was "nothing wrong" with me, or that I was "just depressed". I tried Prozac for a year, and it did nothing for the fatigue, so I stopped. I completely lost faith in the medical system and doctors in general. I started working full-time at 18, and survived on (very strong) willpower and up to 10 cups of coffee a day, right up until bed, and had no trouble sleeping.

(I now believe that I have narcolepsy, as it runs in my family, and I have enough symptoms that I have been "diagnosed" verbally by doctors. An "official" diagnosis requires a 2-day sleep study, which I have attempted, but was too worked-up to sleep.)

Around age 25, at my highest weight of 180, I entered a competition to lose weight. I spent 6 months dieting and exercising and lost 35 pounds.

From about 26-29, I experienced an intense bout of stress at work, which slowly eroded my health even further. Every 3-6 months I would need my glasses increased in strength. I started having panic attacks, and (what I would later discover as) gallstone attacks. My menstrual cycles became irregular, ranging from 30 to 50 days long. By the end, I would shake uncontrollably at any sign of conflict, cry constantly, have headaches daily, and lost my libido. I developed a large rash across my back for several weeks, treated with prednisone (didn't work), which eventually went away on it's own. I gained back most of the weight I had lost.

I am now 33, and I have not worked since, because I am afraid for my health. I (regrettably) had my gallbladder removed. I've had a dozen mercury fillings removed. I've eliminated most stress from my life, and sleep 12 hours a night. I've tried dozens of supplements. I believed that my body would "fix" itself eventually, but it hasn't. I've spent the last 3 years researching, reading, and documenting everything I can find on "health". I'm overwhelmed, and feeling like no one really has the answers. I'm tired of being a guinea pig. I'm tired of trying things that don't work. I'm tired of putting my life on hold "until I feel better". At this point, I'd be happy with enough energy to feed myself 3 times a day and keep my house clean.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-16T01:32:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-with-bloodwork-please-help-me.6780/post-84183>

I would suggest making sure you are doing the Leptin Rx Epi-Paleo style. Get that BAB in within 30 min of waking. It took me almost 9 months of this program before I started to feel more energy. CT will help with energy as well.

It appears you have some evidence of a thyroid problem. Your TSH is too high and your FT3 and FT4 are low. Plug your number into this calculator and see where your rT3 ratio is. Best is above 20....if below you have a problem. This could be a contributor to your energy issues.

Vit D is way too low. I had to take 20,000 just to get my level up to normal. It looks like 8,000 is not enough for you. Bump that up ASAP to a level that you are comfortable with.

You need to get an ASI with melatonin saliva test done. This will tell you if you are experiencing some adrenal challenges as well. Other testing you could consider: hormones,

Read, read, read the blogs here. There is a lot of info (don't forget the comments too) and you need to educate yourself so you can slowly start to put the pieces together and make the changes to help you feel better. This is just the beginning of a fun journey! Remember it took 33 years for you to get to this place and it is going to take some time to get healthy again. I commend you on your decision to make your life better!

### judy32 — 2013-07-03T08:09:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-with-bloodwork-please-help-me.6780/post-89428>

Hello all, I am Judy and I am new here. I have recently join this community to get in touch with the latest information and make discussion on issues related to different topics. I suggest you to Study, read, look at the weblogs here. There is a lot of details (don't ignore content too) and you need to inform yourself so you can gradually begin to put the items together and create the changes to help you experience better.

### caroline — 2013-07-03T10:05:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-with-bloodwork-please-help-me.6780/post-89434>

Welcome justmisunderstood ......Patty Cakes has great advise for you. I would also suggest you consider getting Dr. Kruse's book ....THE EPI-PALEO RX. \[from Amazon\] It is an easy way to understand all of this quickly and where Dr.K. is coming from. Also - if you can - listen to all the webinars - they are great.

There is a lot of reading for you to do - just start slowly and ask questions .... we are here to help....

Can we call you by your name? It is much nicer to have a positive image........

# Thread: Explore how human body reacts and adapts to cold
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/explore-how-human-body-reacts-and-adapts-to-cold.6781/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ruby — 2013-04-15T07:16:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/explore-how-human-body-reacts-and-adapts-to-cold.6781/post-84127>

Valery Malkov made the headlines when he fell out of a train in Siberia in -40 degrees Celsius wearing practically nothing, ran for 30 minutes to the next station and survived.
The man said that he had gone to the trainâ€™s vestibule for a smoke. As he was coming back to his carriage, he missed his door and opened another one to a non-functioning vestibule. He stepped into the dark â€“ and then found himself in taiga forest, on the railroad, all alone.
We explore how human body reacts and adapts to cold and meet enthusiasts who subject themselves to extreme temperatures.
Cold weather has a dramatic effect on human health. According to a University of California, Berkeley economist, deaths related to cold reduce the average life expectancy of Americans by a decade, if not more.
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player\_embedded&v=eAm3PA7x\_ek](http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player_embedded&v=eAm3PA7x_ek)

# Thread: flooring materials and grounding in a home addition

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeBranca — 2013-04-16T00:10:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flooring-materials-and-grounding-in-a-home-addition.6787/post-84176>

putting travertine on concrete... should be conductive for a room floor. But what about the adhesives? Are there some that are conductive and others would be insulating?

edit: actually i probably just need cement based adhesive

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-16T02:37:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flooring-materials-and-grounding-in-a-home-addition.6787/post-84191>

We used Thin Set as the adhesive/leveler for our travertine floor. Usually a layer is put either on top the concrete (I think that is where) like a linoleum. This helps with movement so the travertine doesn't crack. We did ours w/o the linoleum. There are some spider cracks in a few tiles which probably wouldn't be there if we have the linoleum. I'll take the spider cracks in the few tiles in exchange for the grounding. Hope that helps!

Obviously she is craving magnesium....she must be because that stuff tastes horrible! Any suggestions on the best way to supplement magnesium to a dog? She eats a raw diet.

### Inger — 2013-04-16T10:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnesium-for-my-dog.6790/post-84221>

Maybe you could mix the crushed pills in her food?

I just read dogs and cats need selenium and iodine too. I so much would like to give my cat but I fear she is so very picky with foods she would never tolerate it. I am going to try though, when I get some selenium. Now I have only the Brazil nuts for that, and those she will not touch for sure. You always need both you know. You could try to give your dogs those too? I bet dogs are easier to feed stuff then cats? My cat is so crazy picky.. I do feed her 100% carnivore and raw except of the shrimps she love, they are cooked. She only wants the one with peel so I peel a lot of shrimps for her. She did not want the peel or the head tho.

### nonchalant — 2013-04-16T17:55:51+0300

# Thread: Berberine...is anyone taking it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cmegan — 2013-04-16T16:39:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/post-84228>

Hi!

Has anyone taken (or is taking) berberine, and if so, what has your experience been?

Thank you!

### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-17T22:05:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/post-84356>

I take Glycosolve which has Berberine. Are you talking about Berberine in some other formulation or Glycosolve? I'm not surprised if there are others, but Glycosolve is the only supplement I personally know about with it.

Jimmy Moore has used to and mentions it working very well to help stabilize his blood sugars.

[http://livinlavidalowcarb.com/blog/n1](http://livinlavidalowcarb.com/blog/n1)

Read through the N1 Nutritional Ketosis articles they are a good read. Glycosolve/Berberine is also supposed to lower cholesterol. I'm not sure if that is a detriment or not as I know we need cholesterol. I don't remember if it lowers all cholesterol or LDL or what though.

I've been using it and it certainly works for the blood sugar for me as it did for Jimmy. I haven't taken any cholesterol tests to confirm what it does it regards to that, but my best guess is that it isn't a problem in regards to cholesterol.

There are known side effects of taking Metformin which either aren't an issue with Glycosolve/Berberine or perhaps because the Berberine's effect isn't as strong. I've heard Metformin works in a similar way but more so.

The side effects like stomach upset and some other issues might be related to that.

It seems like its a good supplement, but I'm not aware of possible negatives besides the side effects that might happen. They didn't happen with me or Jimmy, but that doesn't mean they don't with everyone.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-18T02:59:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/post-84360>

As I understand it, the liver makes cholesterol on demand. If one has normal or lower cholesterol levels then perhaps the liver is receiving fewer requests to make it. Also I heard that only 10% of dietary cholesterol gets absorbed though I can't quote any studies on that. Wouldn't changing metabolic and hormonal demands alter cholesterol signaling?

### cmegan — 2013-04-18T18:31:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/post-84415>

thank you both for your replies...I bought the glycosolve and am going to start again this week. I recently read Jimmy Moore's experiment on it which made me decide to take a chance. I took this last fall but I went off of it very quickly because of the constipation, and now thinking that a little bit of Mg+ will do the trick!

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-19T04:19:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/post-84460>

Interesting. I hadn't heard of glycosolve. I am an herbalist, and I am familiar with the many different berberine-containing medicinal herbs out there. Thank you for the link to Jimmy Moore's N1. I look forward to reading through that information and seeing how people are using the extracted berberine as opposed to the traditional herbal method of utilizing a whole plant for its many biochemical properties. Here is a nice read on one popular berberine containing herb, Oregon Grape Root:

[http://www.methowvalleyherbs.com/2012/11/oregon-grape-root-it-could-save-world.html](http://www.methowvalleyherbs.com/2012/11/oregon-grape-root-it-could-save-world.html)

# Thread: Can we talk Progesterone??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 73
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-04-16T21:07:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84263>

I've worked my way up to 300 mg Pg. I take this orally before bed and it does seem as though it's finally helping my sleep. Strange thing though is that I seem to be having reactions to it that are opposite what CW says should be happening . . . I've never been a normal one

While the Pg initially seemed to be decreasing my appetite and thirst, now it's causing immense cravings. Thankfully I haven't caved yet, but potato chips, french fries and chocolate chip cookies are about all I can think of these days. It's pretty odd considering I haven't had a single food that isn't on the strict AI epi-paleo plan for over a year now and for the last three + years I haven't had any processed / non-paleo food at all! What gives? I thought low Pg caused cravings . . .

I'm grateful that my sex drive seems to be coming back (yay for DH!!), but along with that I feel like I'm an emotional mess . . . depressed doesn't even begin to cut it. Not only am I craving junk food, I'm craving a change in life. I feel compelled to just make a huge jump and do SOMETHING . . . not even sure what! I just have this drive and desire to move, buy a beach house, get a new job, go on a big vacation . . . anything, just SOMETHING. I'm usually pretty rational and logical and think things through, but I'm honestly scared I'm going to dive into something without any planning just because I've got this innate desire to make something happen.

Is this all from the Pg??

Almost forgot - the hot flashes! Again, I thought Pg should be helping this, but I'm having random moments throughout the day where I'm just burning up. It mostly affects my face and upper body. My face gets flushed, bright red and SUPER hot.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-16T21:55:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84268>

I was this way when we finally started seeing forward progress w/my Pg. I've seen it balance out more - as we increased the dose - but that's my N=1. It also could be Estrogen Detox too, especially considering the HOT FLASHES.

Though I do have a question for you on the HOT FLASHES - when are they occurring? and are you just HOT - like you feel like your internal furnace just kicked on - or are you HOT, Sweaty, sticky and Cranky?

### caroline — 2013-04-16T22:27:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84271>

I can't help with the pg stuff - struggling there too - to balance erything but the rest ...

It seems to me that you are innately craving/needing/wanting something that is very, very important to you - deep in your soul - you have time - everything will come together - just relax into it and it will become crystal clear.

Maybe try to worry less and make love more ...... connections with those you love are the most important and the rest will fall into place.

Can you rent a beach house for a couple of weeks? maybe relax into it and enjoy the moment and just let life happen .....

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-16T22:59:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84281>

I'm taking a step back here and I'm Looking at the flow of how hormones are made - what do you need to make Progesterone?

LDL + Vitamin A + T3 allows for the conversion of LDL to Pregnenolone. Pregnenolone then if everything is working properly should convert to Progesterone... and you have the whole hormone cascade.

so If we're having problems getting our Pg up - even with supplementation - I think we should be going back and looking at our thyroid. T3 is critical for production of the hormone cascade. so - that leads me to the question of how's your thyroid labs looking?

I know mine are freaking rockin' it right now and I'm making Pregnenolone - but my PG is still in the tank.... and ohhh the hormonal chaos that's on going...

So... I'm thinking a couple of things the HOT your feeling - could be your thyroid kicking on. It could be E-dumping/detoxing. It could be E-dominance fighting the Pg.

I understand your desire to make a change. This is the longest I've lived in one place (13yrs) prior to that I've moved every 1-2years, even growing up. Change is a good thing... you've just got to figure out how to make it work towards your advantage and leverage it to make the most out of life!!

### caroline — 2013-04-16T23:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84282>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=84281)
>
> I'm taking a step back here and I'm Looking at the flow of how hormones are made - what do you need to make Progesterone?
>
> LDL + Vitamin A + T3 allows for the conversion of LDL to Pregnenolone. Pregnenolone then if everything is working properly should convert to Progesterone... and you have the whole hormone cascade.
>
> so If we're having problems getting our Pg up - even with supplementation - I think we should be going back and looking at our thyroid. T3 is critical for production of the hormone cascade. so - that leads me to the question of how's your thyroid labs looking?
>
> I know mine are freaking rockin' it right now and I'm making Pregnenolone - but my PG is still in the tank.... and ohhh the hormonal chaos that's on going...
>
> So... I'm thinking a couple of things the HOT your feeling - could be your thyroid kicking on. It could be E-dumping/detoxing. It could be E-dominance fighting the Pg.
>
> I understand your desire to make a change. This is the longest I've lived in one place (13yrs) prior to that I've moved every 1-2years, even growing up. Change is a good thing... you've just got to figure out how to make it work towards your advantage and leverage it to make the most out of life!!

So my Pregnenolone is quite high but my Pg is low - coming up - but low. My Thyroid is sluggish - not on anything ...so that could be the missing piece? But I don't understand why my pregnenolone is not converting to cortisol or Pg??? It seems to be just sitting there - not converting to anything. When I asked JK - he said to just keep doing what I am doing....

### caroline — 2013-04-17T01:19:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84298>

Absolutely! just feeling a little sorry for myself for a minute! I just need to understand this myself .....I would have thought my high preg was a problem - When I asked Jack- he said ..."good sign then - keep it up and I would strongly suggest you push the epi -paleo template and ocean C/T to keep it moving north" I am already above the guidelines. I even questioned him further in case I hadn't explained properly and he said "keep doing what you are doing"

I am wondering if I am lacking the enzymes responsible for the conversion to cortisol? and how do I figure that out?

I am just reading in my notes ....High Testosterone may be due to low tissue response or other hormone imbalance - eg. low thyroid or low cortisol ...... symptoms consistent with low thyroid.

Initially I think JK thought I might be able to improve my sluggish thyroid on my own with C/T etc. the numbers are all going in the right direction - all be it slowly - except for the testosterone.

### tweety — 2013-04-17T01:27:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84300>

The longer I was on Progesterone, I noticed that right before my period I started feeling more and more depressed. Initially I felt much calmer and so much reduced PMS irritabilty that I knew I was soaking up the P. But towards the end I really started gaining weight in weird ways and feeling depressed, hating everything (in this last way reverting back to pre-progesterone days).

I have always felt random impulses to move across the country, buy/sell a house/ get a dog, change cars... paint... whatever. I just needed change. For a while, in the good moments of progesterone usage, this calmed down and I was so happy. Then it came back. My poor husband.

Now, to complement what Shijin is saying, I noticed I could let go of my P (at least so far) and my thyroid medicine is really making the difference for me. So she's right about the components. It's finding the balance. I think I mentioned to you in another thread that maybe you should consider underlying issues. As far as diet goes, maybe you are not getting all that you think you are? I take HCL 3x day, I eat super healthy foods, and yet cannot for the life of me improve my keratosis pilaris (vit A deficiency) and everytime I stop taking fish oil, my nails go to pot. This is despite taking FCLO/butter oil blend and eating ridiculous amounts of fish/oysters/sardines...

Do you have methylation issues? Lyme? Absorption? Additional food/chemical sensitivities?

This is an awesome discussion! Love your attitude Gretchen.

I am reading a new book called the Hormone Cure, [http://www.amazon.com/The-Hormone-Cure-Naturally-Gottfried/dp/1451666942](http://www.amazon.com/The-Hormone-Cure-Naturally-Gottfried/dp/1451666942)

I disagree with her view on fats. But overall it is very easy to read and her advice includes medical, herbal, lifestyle, and some other complementary medicine type stuff.

I'll be reading some more later this evening as my daughters dance, so I'll see if I come across anything useful that might pertain to this discussion.

### HoneyChild — 2013-04-17T14:28:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84327>

I assume that progesterone increases metabolic rate by increasing temperature which I think could then lead to a relative iodine deficiency/dehydration. I'm not taking any hormones of any kind but the last couple of cycles I've been waking up in the middle of the night very hot and thirsty around days 19-22 of my cycle which is when progesterone is supposed to peak. Still haven't had my ZRT tests back so I can only speculate on what's happening, I think my samples may have got lost in the post.

> "When one begins eating a paleo diet your nutrient density and food substrate improve so your iodine needs go up as your mitochondria are being asked to do more. So with a paleo diet you get a relative iodine deficiency for the first 3-6 months of the adaptation of the diet.
>
> \[...\]
>
> Why does the classic paleo diet make your LDL rise so often? The answer is simpleâ€¦â€¦dehydration is favored when you eat lots of protein and starchy carbs. A large amount of protein and starchy carbs draws water and even will make your temperature rise. For every degree your temperature increases your basal metabolic rate increase 13%. This increase in temperature, causes water dehydration in your carbon nanotubes. If you add back water that is fluoridated you will not improve. The water must be sping, mineral, or treated with reverse osmosis or you will have severe low carb flu present. ironically this is what the paleosphere calls it. I do not."
> [http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-rx-the-top-ten-emf-faqs/](http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-rx-the-top-ten-emf-faqs/)

Progesterone apparently also allows thyroid hormones to function better. As Jack has said, the thyroid is needed for uncoupling proteins to work so that you can release excess calories as heat at night. So I think this is what's happening to me.

> "According to the late clinician John R Lee, M.D., estrogen dominance is behind many cases of midlife hypothyroidism, in which there are inadequate levels of thyroid hormone. When estrogen is not properly counterbalanced with progesterone, Dr. Lee surmised, it can block the action of thyroid hormone, so that even when the thyroid is producing normal levels of the hormone, the hormone is rendered ineffective and the symptoms of hypothyroidism appear. In this case, laboratory tests may show normal thyroid hormone levels in a womanâ€™s system, because the thyroid gland itself is not malfunctioning. This problem is compounded when a woman is prescribed supplemental estrogen, which then leads to an even greater imbalance. Prescribing supplemental thyroid hormone in that case will fail to correct the underlying problem: estrogen dominance. "
> [http://www.drnorthrup.com/womenshealth/healthcenter/topic\_details.php?topic\_id=59](http://www.drnorthrup.com/womenshealth/healthcenter/topic_details.php?topic_id=59)

This is also an interesting article about progesterone and thyroid function: [http://www.health-truth.com/126.php](http://www.health-truth.com/126.php)

My thyroid finally seems to be headed in the right direction, but it still has me a bit confused. My T3 is in range for the first time since I've been testing it the last several years, but my T4 is still low and TSH high. ### Shijin13 — 2013-04-17T16:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84336>

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=84329)
>
> This is what I'm worried about - that I'll have a "honeymoon" period with the Pg. That's what seemed to happen when I first started taking it, but then it's like I have to constantly increase every few weeks/months in order to maintain that good feeling. If I really think about it though, I think I have always had random impulses for most of my adult life - much before I started the Pg, so I guess I can't blame it completely on that. And it's a totally cyclical thing for me too. It comes and goes. **I really need to start doing a better job with my journaling so I can pinpoint if it happens at a certain time in my cycle or if there's some other cause.**
>
> My thyroid finally seems to be headed in the right direction, but it still has me a bit confused. I was supposed to have a consult with JK last week and am really bummed that it wasn't able to happen. My T3 is in range for the first time since I've been testing it the last several years, **but my T4 is still low and TSH high. That part confuses me since I thought T4 converts to T3 . . . if that's the case why would my T3 be ok, but my T4 low??** I just can't figure all this out.
>
>
>
> I love your attitude Gretchen!
>
>
>
> Thanks for this Chrysanthemama. I'm going to check that book out. I really think I do have a major unbalance. All my symptoms seem to point to both E dominance and low E2. I've always been the oddball

so the first thing I bolded is really important! I've used both spreadsheets and written journals - its what's helped me figure out what's going on, and it can definitely help show your doc what's going on as well...

As for the High TSH, low T4 and T3 being in range - HOW DO YOU FEEL? Do you feel great w/a HIGH TSH? if not push for more meds! I've finally gotten my TSH down to .45, my T4 is still low, but my T3 is freaking out of RANGE! - BUT I FEEL AWESOME! Ranges are just reference points for you to start from - and compare where you're at with HOW YOU FEEL at that given point. Not all of us are going to fit the "Bell curve". Remember N=1. your thyroid results are telling you this. Now go back and look at your thyroid labs - sit quietly and listen to your body - what does it say about your thyroid labs?

going back to your question - about how can T4 be low and T3 be high - well what T4 your making is converting to T3 - but the question is your T3 high b/c the receptor cells are being blocked by rT3? what's that look like? Additionally your TSH is in high gear - trying to get traction - your pituitary is telling your Thyroid - HEY WAKE UP AND MAKE T4!!!!!!

here's a good explanation of how T4 is converted to T3 and some things that can get in the way of conversion
[http://www.naturalendocrinesolutions.com/articles/do-you-have-a-t4-to-t3-conversion-problem/](http://www.naturalendocrinesolutions.com/articles/do-you-have-a-t4-to-t3-conversion-problem/)

And this one does a nice job explaining how to look at the process of how thyroid funcitons in the body
[http://health.howstuffworks.com/human-body/systems/endocrine/understanding-thyroid-metabolism.htm](http://health.howstuffworks.com/human-body/systems/endocrine/understanding-thyroid-metabolism.htm)

Here's the thing - we're all oddballs, why b/c we're swimming against the tide as we try to fix ourselves. we're not willing to settle for feeling crappy or Ok. We want to feel great. We want to feel awesome. WE DON'T WANT TO BE SICK ANY LONGER! WE'VE HAD ENOUGH OF THE CURRENT SYSTEM. WE'RE TAKING CONTROL OF OUR HEALTH AND HOW WE'RE GOING TO LIVE OUR LIVES... this is a good thing. Embrace being the odd duck out. go ahead - Let your freak flag fly!

### tweety — 2013-04-17T19:40:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84350>

Such good comments in this thread. HoneyChild you are so right about progesterone and thyroid- without tests, I intuitively feel my P is way too high, and wouldn't you know that I shifted from 180mg armour down to 60mg (where I am perfectly happy!) in a ridiculously short amount of time.

I haven't done labs yet, and won't for a while so I am totally following intuition. Interesting thought about when the need to change or make impulsive changes comes. For years I would go through a love/hate cycle with my husband. For two weeks out of the month I was convinced we should divorce. Then, something would happen (didn't realize at the time it was hormones shifting) and I was totally in love with him again. I said it before, my poor husband. This was every month for the majority of our marriage. Adding P almost eliminated it completely. But then P went too high, and my emotions have gotten messy again. At least now I can rationalize through this insanity. I still feel it, but I can see rationale waiting on the other side.

I just know my body is so unhappy. It's acting out in all kinds of ways, I just can't speak its language yet. I guess that's all of us here.

### BJK77 — 2013-04-18T17:15:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84396>

I'm beginning to wonder if a lot of this doesn't have to do with the seasonal change. I am absolutely craving the sun! Unfortunately the 10-day forecast shows very little chance of me getting sun anytime soon  I'm planning to hit the tanning bed this afternoon though to see if that helps.

Would the seasonal change cause me to have an increased need for Pg? It seems to me it should lower my need for T3, but I can't figure out why extra light would cause my body to need more Pg??

This overheated feeling has been driving me crazy, so I decided to try cutting back on Pg last night to 250 mg before bed instead of 300 mg . . . my sleep was total crap! And then I woke up and couldn't have a BM - definitely signs of low Pg. This also tends to happen at the beginning of my cycle. This concerns me that it's happening mid-cycle now because just a few weeks ago I was fine on 200 mg Pg (at least in the BM department). Why the sudden need for so much Pg . . . I've definitely massively decreased my EMF exposure and increased my spring water consumption, so that shouldn't be the cause.

I do feel my thyroid is humming along pretty well right now. I can't remember the last time I had cold hands or feet which was my tell-tale sign of hypoT before. The really strange thing now though is that I'm gaining weight. When I was hypoT everyone always acted so surprised because I was really thin; now that my thyroid is actually working I'm putting on the pounds! Is it the Pg? I have no clue!

My gut is beginning to tell me it's driven from E2, but my last labs showed low E2 (with a decent Pg:E2 ratio of 231). I'm trying to CT as much as possible to detox any extra E2. I just ordered some DIM too . . . anyone have experience with that? Also trying to up my intake of cruciferous veggies since they should help clear E2, but I don't want to overdo it and have those affect my thyroid either.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-18T17:34:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84403>

Seasonal changes can have big impact on hormones in my experience.

it could be, that your body is actually leveraging what Pg your giving it, along with the water to address dehydration at the cellular level and as you increase your hydration, you would have a need for greater Pg - to sustain it. Think of pregnancy. high Pg, larger blood volume = more water.

so as your water/hydration increases, so does your need for more Pg.

as for T3 -what you think you need, vs what you actually need are two different things... You might need more T3 b/c your body's actually trying to make Pg itself... in addition to what you're supplementing... you might just be laying some good ground work right now.

One things for sure - with the recent spring forward my cycle is completely discombobulated. and until your post, I didn't even think that it could have been the impact of changing seasons, in addition to EMF and the need for more Pg...

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-19T00:27:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84449>

Dzugan gives the following ranges for Women:

Total Estrogen 250-437 pg/ml
Progesterone: 6-28ng/ml
Testosterone: 60-76ng/dl
DHEA-S 250-380 ug/dl
Pregnenolone: 200-230ng/dl
cortisol (am) 15-22.4 ug/dl

these are his optimal ranges - I think Jack has a different view on ranges as well. THe question is HOW DO YOU FEEL - given where you're at in the RANGE. Good? Bad? So-So? super crapy?

This is why journaling, and detailed information is so key! each of us has our own bodies, and they each respond differently to seasonal changes, light cycles, foods, BHRT and supplements... it all comes back to how you feel

### tweety — 2013-04-19T00:29:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-84450>

Stress plays a role in lowering hormones, food intolerances, underlying conditions and unchecked health problems, and I would think maternal health at the time of our conception and pregnancy... I am about to be 35, and this started at puberty, but really the peak was a few years ago probably. I am thinking our mothers' generation had less nutrition than theirs, and that alone set us up for a different profile than they even had. Likewise, our children will be in a different boat than us. Now add to that our increased environmental stressors (emf, toxins, etc) and our life quality stressors (work, school, make more, do more, be more, etc)- well we are fighting a losing battle. Unless we start attacking everything we can.

One of my biggest emf offenses would be carrying my cell phone in my pocket. While walking the dog, cleaning the house, or just when out running errands... it was always in my pocket. It was wreaking havoc on my hormones the whole time just by directly radiating my ovaries. I try very hard to keep it away from me, but at this point no distance is safe enough.

looking at your symptoms - and going back to Dr Reiss's book [https://www.uzzireissmd.com/book\_naturalhormone.html](https://www.uzzireissmd.com/book_naturalhormone.html) your symptoms are classic ESTROGEN DOMINANCE - you don't have enough Pg at this point in your cycle - give Dzugan an update ASAP! don't supplement w/E! Tell him exactly what your feeling and when it started. you need more Pg - a lot more is what I'm guessing.

I can't speak as to whether things would get better or worse with pregnancy - I can' only tell you that my pregnancies weren't the most pleasant experiences for me - I was miserable -with the exception that I didn't have migraines. I would say that you need to have an OB on board w/what you're doing. you also need to be aware that an upside down Pg:E2 ratio is one of the reasons women have pre-mature babies. I spent last friday afternoon w/a dear friend who's on medical bed rest in the hosptial b/c she was having pre-term labor at 26wks -right now the goals is to get her and the baby to 28wks.

> [nuttmegs17 said:](/goto/post?id=84830)
>
> Gretchen - in your Quanton Guest Post, what was it that suddenly got you into that pathway, grounding? and that finally changed your body comp and got you in smaller clothes? Bc youve been doing everything so consistently...it seems you just added in Grounding, addressed hydration right? How much do you ground a day? Perhaps this will straighten out my P

I would say ground helped, but it wasn't the MASTER KEY for me. the MASTER KEY was my thyroid. I have come to believe - that when you put everything thing together - you still need a MASTER KEY to turn the lock. I think that is the thyroid. why? LDL + vitamin A + T3 = pregnenolnone, from pregneonlone you make progesterone. Add in Cold and you enter the PPP. Ground is just one more way to bring in the electrons, which help "Fill up" your tank.

my most recent labs while not great confirmed for me my Thyroid is what's limiting my success. I'm finally on a dose of Armor that's working for me. While my T4 is still kind of low, my TSH is .45 but my T3 is out of the range - but I FEEL GREAT. I'm not doing anything different food wise. but weight is dropping off me. people I haven't seen in while keep commenting about how I'm loosing weight and what I'm doing.. my last set of test confirmed for my PCP that my thyroid has been untreated for a while...

Grounding, CT, and eating right is part of the solution...but for me it was fixing the thyroid.

> Also - it has me thinking. I've been so scared and also annoyed/impatient to delay pregnancy due to my health issues. We really want to start bc Im 31 and not getting any younger...however, we wory about the babys health obviously and mine. However...
>
> If HCG makes me feel better - and it does - if it were possible to be on it nonstop I would! -is it possible that getting preggers (which naturally produces HCG of course) and also during that time doing everything "right": lower carb, seasonlly eating, reducing EMF wherever possible, grounding, amber glasses, hydration...eating nutritionally dense foods, is it possible for pregnancy to actually help a body HEAL? is that crazy? I know many women usually come out of pregnancy with more hormonal problems than they began but so many of them are usually on an ice-cream and potato-chip diet at the time or at the very best the CW diet of "healthy"...
>
> I'd be interested in anyone who has used DrKruse's info while pregnant (the ones appropriate for pregnant ladies of course!)...anyone out there? It would be so intersting to track Kruser babies

Hi Nuttmegs, I hope to speak from experience in that don't bring a baby into the world if you are not optimal as you can possible be. I am a product of a non optimal mom and grandma. Low P all the way and I can see how it has affected me and my children. By the time I was 15, there was something obviously wrong and of course no way to know what to do. My son was born with life threatening allergies to peanuts, wheat and dairy. The doctor said that 30 years ago, he would not have lived but since there was a very special formula that cost $24.00 per can, he could survive. I would love to rewind and go back and fix me before I had any kids. Keep plugging away and you will find optimal. Sometimes it is so close, but yet so far away. I too think thyroid is a huge key for me.

it appears as classic E Dom but when I was using just p I gained weight and swelled up so much in the boobs it was painful. And as much as I want to push the P my body rebels. They had me on lower and lower dosed bc I got incredibly dry eyes and felt catatonic. I guess I hope that by supp E maybe it will allow me to handle the P a bit better and perhaps even down regulate the E my body is making. I am so confused though. One other side effect I'm experiencing right now is dry eyes and sleepiness. And right now I'm not on anything yet I feel like I did t when I had too much P...

I put in another update to Dugan giving them the symptons I gave u. I look forward to their feedback! I'm taking your advice at updating them more regularly

I am so happy that you are getting the thyroid under control. That is HUGE. HUGE!

At my last appt the NP drove me nuts bc she kept saying that all my probs (high LDL among other things) were thyroid and adrenal related but basically was of no help. Like you my T4 is a bit lower and now my T3 is slightly elevated but I show no signs of hyper. Again so confused she wanted me to back off the t3 but I see no need. I certainly have the LDL plus vit A( i take fermented cod liver oil) and I think the pregnelone (dzugan upped me to 300) so I pray it all clicks soon

I haven't been CTing the traditional way. I've just been biking outside in the cold a lot. The other day it was 45 degrees and pouring on my way home. I was freezing and soaked but i told myself i was CTing! in fact my legs especially were that mice pink color for hours afterwards. I will be increasing the cold showers again though

Interesting stuff Gretchen! I always learn so much from reading your posts on this forum. For people like myself who need things simplified, you really do a good job of making everything easier to understand. Thank you so much for that.

I am a 30 year old male, and most posts on here are related to women and their hormone levels at various periods during their cycle. But for me, what are your thoughts on taking T3 only when one has a T3:RT3 ratio that is bad and below 20? My Free T3 is really low and out of range, and TSH is 4.0 and out of range on last tests almost a year ago. T4 was low barely in range as well. My dhea-s was right in the middle but my testosterone was very low as well. So from trying to put the pieces together from the hormone 101 blog and all the other blogs and forums relevant to this topic, my very low Free T3 is causing low hormones across the board more than likely, correct?

Also, since I am getting up to urinate at least 3x a night, this would also indicate a low Progesterone more than likely, correct? I have read about preg steal syndrome, and have no doubt this is what happened with me. I went through a 2 year period of fasting for 2 days, serious overtraining and over exercising despite having about 7% bodyfat, eating disorders, taking 1200 mgs of caffeine, no sleep at all, being extremely stressed all day every day due to high stakes gambling as a living, and sitting with a computer on my lap for 8+ hours everyday. I am trying to piece this together, and I would appreciate it if you think I am on the right track.

Such a great thread!

### JanSz — 2013-04-29T01:29:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-85067>

> [nuttmegs17 said:](/goto/post?id=84448)
>
> What should estrogen be? I always see things like "you want progesterone at least 11 or higher...Testosterone should be in the 60s, etc" but besides saying you want P 300 times higher then E, I never really see any other rules of thumb.

Try thick blue line at estradiol chart:
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

Thick blue line on progesterone chart is for orientation only.
Use that as a minimum that you would fall into when you can't help it.

Dr Kruse recommends P/E2~500
use calculator (link included)
or
use table I included there.
use your E2 then on right row read corresponding value for progesterone (when P/E2=500)
example
you have:
E2=40pg/ml------>progesterone=23ng/mL ---->P/E=498
E2=60pg/ml------>progesterone=34.6ng/mL--->P/E2=500

note that chart on progesterone posted by Wiki goes only to 25ng/ml, that is lots of progesterone that dr Kruse is asking for, nobody have that much.

(So, practically, nobody should have a reason to talk about having to much progesterone).
..............................

At first I push from both ends

pregnenolone
and
DHEA

using pills
that works for me, (I am man), I do this already for over 1.5years

man & woman are equal in natural healthy progesterone levels in first half of cycle
woman needs more progesterone in second half

I suggest that women work first on stabilization of first half of cycle by using pregnenolone and DHEA only
for second half some women may need (additionally) direct progesterone

\------------------

I have tried variety of pregnenolones, pills, trasdermal, sublingual, variety of different bases and applicators... etc
The only one that works for me is

pregnolone Micronized Lipid Matrix 150mg tabs from Nutricology

also

DHEA Micronized Lipid Matrix 50mg tabs from Allergy Research

I use 2 pills/day of each of the above

\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> **cycloset is made for increasing AM cortisol**

About Cycloset.
It differs from other (bromocriptine mesylates) available that it is absorbs rapidly (therefore the nausea and some gastric upsets when first using it.

As any (bromocriptine mesylates) it lowers Prolactin.

Dr Kruse mentions 6 tabs dose of 0.8mg Cysloset in the morning.
I am using Cycloset but only two tabs and already my Prolactin is slightly below bottom range

I am male,
prolactin=7(4-15.2)ng/ml without Cycloset
prolactin=3.4 with 2 tabs 0.8mg Cycloset

prolactin Female: 4.8-23.3 ng/mL

Hopefully dr Kruse will find time to discuss aspect of using Cycloset when prolactin falls into low numbers.
\==================================================
\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?2927-Cycloset-and-FBG-update-oh-my!](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?2927-Cycloset-and-FBG-update-oh-my!)

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=44090)
>
> This is the OSA battling the cycloset......I'd tell you to give it some time to work itself through. If sleep is still an issue You may want to increase DHEA to help foster sleep. The cycloset can cause an upset stomach because of how it dramatically alters the gut flora because of the acute changes in cortisol and dopamine in the brain and the gut.
>
> Remember the **cycloset is made for increasing AM cortisol......Works awesome and is a great way to stop taking hydrocortisone in LR states or in Adrenal fatigue.** Lots of educational consultants are hearing about how I might consider to use it now.
>
> **Cortisol is a stress hormone that is linked with dopamine production in the brain. Levels of cortisol in the bloodstream are highest during rising in the AM, which in turn leads to higher dopamine levels during daytime. Dopamine, a neurotransmitter, is directly responsible for a number of important functions in the brain, including attention and learning when we rise.**
>
> Recent studies have suggested that the circadian neuroendocrine rhythm mediated by dopamine and serotonin plays a large role in insulin sensitivity. In people without diabetes, the postmeal state causes a decline in endogenous glucose production due to suppression of glucagon and inhibition of lipolysis. In individuals with T2D, a drop in dopamine levels is thought to lead to an inadequate hypothalamus response, resulting in elevated levels of blood glucose, free fatty acids (FFA), and triglycerides (TG), which contribute to insulin resistance, visceral adiposity, and beta-cell dysfunction. **Clinical studies suggest that bromocriptine-QR administered in the morning (within 2 hours of waking) mimics the normal peak of dopamine in the central nervous system, consequently thought to reset this circadian rhythm. The main target organ of bromocriptine is the brain.**
>
> However, subsequent effects have been observed in adipose tissue (due to decreased levels of glucose, FFAs, and TGs) and the liver (reduced hepatic gluconeogenesis due to reduced cortisol levels in response to dopamine). Higher brain dopamine levels will reduce the cortisol spikes seen in Leptin resistant states like T2D or IR.
>
> 1\. Meier A, Cincotta A. Circadian rhythms regulate the expression of the thrifty genotype/phenotype. Diabetes Rev. 1996;4:464-487.

> [JanSz\]OSA what is it? \[/QUOTE\] \[QUOTE=Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=44198)
>
> Its not just about your cortisol.....its about a lot of context. \*IR, PG/E2 ratio/ FBG/Sleep/ and other related hormones like DHEA/melatonin/IGF1

obstructive sleep apnea (OSA)

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=44281)

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-30T22:37:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-talk-progesterone.6797/post-85206>

> [bennyha said:](/goto/post?id=84998)
>
> Interesting stuff Gretchen! I always learn so much from reading your posts on this forum. For people like myself who need things simplified, you really do a good job of making everything easier to understand. Thank you so much for that.
>
> I am a 30 year old male, and most posts on here are related to women and their hormone levels at various periods during their cycle. But for me, what are your thoughts on taking T3 only when one has a T3:RT3 ratio that is bad and below 20? My Free T3 is really low and out of range, and TSH is 4.0 and out of range on last tests almost a year ago. T4 was low barely in range as well. My dhea-s was right in the middle but my testosterone was very low as well. So from trying to put the pieces together from the hormone 101 blog and all the other blogs and forums relevant to this topic, my very low Free T3 is causing low hormones across the board more than likely, correct?
>
> Also, since I am getting up to urinate at least 3x a night, this would also indicate a low Progesterone more than likely, correct? I have read about preg steal syndrome, and have no doubt this is what happened with me. I went through a 2 year period of fasting for 2 days, serious overtraining and over exercising despite having about 7% bodyfat, eating disorders, taking 1200 mgs of caffeine, no sleep at all, being extremely stressed all day every day due to high stakes gambling as a living, and sitting with a computer on my lap for 8+ hours everyday. I am trying to piece this together, and I would appreciate it if you think I am on the right track.
>
> Such a great thread!

Sorry for the late response Bennyha, been overwhelmed at work! My thoughts on addressing RT3 is that 1st you need to identify why you're having the conversion problem in the first place.

initially I started w/T3 bt it didn't make me feel any better. Instead my BHRT doc and PCP decided to switch me to Armour. slowly and steadily we fixed the RT3 problem which for me was the result of a thyroid set on idle for years. after 6mths of Armour we cleared the RT3 issue, but once that cleared we discovered that I wasn't producing enough T4... and my pitutary kept pumping out TSH - eventually pushing my TSH level almost to 8. Once I got on the right dosage of armour split across the day -things began to turn around quick for me.

add in the grounding, magnetico sleep pad and CT and suddenly sleep became awesome, breakfast became almost non-existent - there are some days I just can't eat breakfast - I'm too full... so I have my bulletproof coffee and off I go. I've lost weight and now fit in to my pre-pregnancy clothes that I wore 6years ago and they're starting to get loose! I've also moved into a period in which I have more energy, more brain function. My biggest win though is I've been migraine free for 2 whole cycles.

Given your history - you need to start focusing on eat Epi-Paleo using the Leptin reset, focus on circadian cycles and limit your EMFs and CT. If you can run some labs and start working to find a doctor to help you figure out whats going on with your Thryoid. With out labs - we can't pinpoint where your major issues are but at least you can focus on the basics.

Since you're a guy - I think you'll bounce back much faster as in general Leptin Levels in men are lower than women. My DH saw real results within 60days of eating Epi-paleo, using the Leptin Reset, monitoring circadian cycles and applying CT.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-01T16:08:14+0300

it can but its not always the case - which is why its important to figure out why you have a conversion problem. knowing you have a thyroid problem already - have you stopped by and read Grizz's iodine thread. worth a read if you have thyroid issues. you need Iodine, plus other essentials like selenium and zinc to support the proper conversion... initially I started on selenium and zinc supplements, then 6mths later started adding the lugols protocol.

I actually felt better on Armour than I did on T3. n=1. I just gave you how I cleared my RT3... it doesn't work like this for everyone...

First off - congratulations on your amazing progress! I have been interacting with you since the MDA thread and to see things clicking is exciting! God knows you have worked hard for it and deserve it!

Also, thanks for saying that about the T4. I think sometimes people focus on T3 and lose sight of the fact that perhaps balance is key. Pure T3 doesnt work for everyone. My TSH is .004 when the range is .4 - 4.5.... I have been confused lately bc my levels are off but my T3 is slightly elevated yet I still am symptomatic. My T4 is on the low side though and your experience makes me want to addresss that even more!

Problem is, I'm on a combo of armour (T4 AND T3) and Cytomel (T3 only). I can't really increase my T4 with my armour without also increasing my T3. I am on 5 mcg total of Cytomel a day. But perhaps if I go off that cytomel (minus 5 mcg of T3) and instead take one more half grain of armour (adding 4.5 T3) that would address it? another thing I might run by my doc is dropping armour and going to Synthroid and Cytomel combined to have a bit more control over the ratio...

Thoughts?

Morning: 1 grain armour, 2.5 cyto
Noon: half grain armour, 2.5 cyto

T4, Low end of the range,
T3, slightly too high - not crazy high but slightly.

### beach bunny — 2013-04-17T17:44:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-hormone-testing.6803/post-84345>

Hi Guys,

I was going to consult Dr. K yesterday but in light of the changes I need some help with what my next step should be:

1- WHICH Salivary Hormones I need to test for
2- WHERE is the best place for ME to order them

Do I need SALIVARY:
Melatonin, ASI, Total testosterone, Free testosterone, % Free T, DHT, LH, FSH, Estradiol (E2) high sensitivity, DHEA-S, IGF-1, progesterone, pregnenolone...
Is Reverse T3 a saliva test?

I am 28 yr old Female (125 lbs). Here is my recent blood work:

TSH 3.69....... 0.30-5.60
T3 Total RIA 1.9...... 1.3-2.5
T4 free 11.1..... 7.2-21.0
Free T3 4.2....... 2.9-6.0
Vitamin B12 478...... >133
Ferritin 51...... 11-145
Cortisol AM 401..... 185-624
PTH 3.2..... 1.3-8.2
Vit D 82..... 75-200
Testosterone 1.6..... 0.4-2.6
Free
Testosterone 9.6...... 0-14.2
DHEA Sulphate 5.6....... <11.0
Homocysteine 4.9...... 5.1-15.4
IGF-1 273....... 117-329
hs CRP <0.2..... <1.0 Low Risk
Thyroglobulin AB Negative..... Negative
Microsomal AB Negative..... Negative
Anti-Thyroid Peroxidas 15..... 5
Cholesterol 7.15...... Below 4.20
Triglycerides 0.51..... Target <1.71
HDL 1.62...... Target>1.29
LDL 5.30....... Moderate/High <2.0 or reduce by 50%
CHOL:HDL 4.41
Absolute Lymph 3.4....... 1.1-1.3

Thanks so much!

### MeghanK — 2013-04-18T17:28:41+0300

Where did you get all these tests? Did your insurance cover it?

### beach bunny — 2013-04-19T02:06:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-hormone-testing.6803/post-84455>

Sooo, after some research and asking around I have decided to order:

ZRT labs: Diurnal Cortisol (C1, C2, C3, C4) (Saliva)
Meridian Valley: 24hr Urine Hormone Panel (must be ordered through physician)
I will also be sending out my OAT urine test next week to get some insight into my methylation cycle function.

I will start with those for now, then likely order a 28 day saliva test to see what Estrogen/Progesterone (Female Hormone Panel-Diagnostechs)

Now... to find a way to afford all of this stuff!!!

>
> TSH 3.69....... 0.30-5.60 (aim at (0.5-1.5) using natural, pig, thyroid)
> T3 Total RIA 1.9...... 1.3-2.5
> T4 free 11.1..... 7.2-21.0
> Free T3 4.2....... 2.9-6.0
> Vitamin B12 478...... >133 (get it to a 1000)
> Ferritin 51...... 11-145 (ok)
> Cortisol AM 401..... 185-624 (get to the top range, 10% over), work on Circadian Rhythm, (work on preg & prog, should help with cortisol), progesterone should be very high to achieve prog/E~500 that dr K wants, or even 200 that dr Lam recommends)
> PTH 3.2..... 1.3-8.2
> Vit D 82..... 75-200 (get it to 100 or 150) but first double check your units, the 200 in your range is a flag
> Testosterone 1.6..... 0.4-2.6 (get DHEAs high, that should also raise testosterone)
> Free
> Testosterone 9.6...... 0-14.2
> DHEA Sulphate 5.6....... <11.0
> Homocysteine 4.9...... 5.1-15.4
> IGF-1 273....... 117-329
> hs CRP <0.2..... <1.0 Low Risk
> Thyroglobulin AB Negative..... Negative
> Microsomal AB Negative..... Negative
> Anti-Thyroid Peroxidas 15..... 5
> Cholesterol 7.15...... Below 4.20
> Triglycerides 0.51..... Target <1.71
> HDL 1.62...... Target>1.29
> LDL 5.30....... Moderate/High <2.0 or reduce by 50%
> CHOL:HDL 4.41
> **Absolute Lymph 3.4....... 1.1-1.3**
>
>
> Thanks so much!

It would help if you at least posted lab name and country, and units.
It would be even better
if you posted labs twice
1-with units as they come

# Thread: Coconut oil Nutiva or Tropical Traditions...hmm
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-17T21:07:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84353>

So I can get a 5 gallon of Nutiva or Coconut oil for maximum price savings. If I factor in shipping Tropical Traditions is 7 dollars cheaper. 199.80 for 5 gallons with 24 dollar shipping. Nutiva is 259 with a 10% discount and free shipping on amounts over 50 dollars. That comes out to 231.10.

Nutiva says its not fermented its organic and Jack has recommended it before. I've read that Tropical Traditions is fermented. Is this kind of fermentation good for coconut oil? Does that make it more healthy or less healthy.

Nutiva or Tropical Traditions..hmmm

Thanks for any and all input but please hurry sale ends tomorrow at least on the TT

### SeaJane — 2013-04-17T23:24:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84358>

Nutiva is by far my favorite coconut oil based on taste. I can't tell you if the fermenting is better for you or not.

### endless — 2013-04-18T04:58:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84366>

Tropical Traditions has a very good website that will tell you in great detail about how their different grades are processed....why not just go there and read up on it? I've always used TT and I think it's great, but I have no information or opinion on the fermenting thing....

### Dextery — 2013-04-18T05:13:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84367>

I get Vitacost.com private label coconut oil 54 oz for $21.49 each and freight prepaid with an order of $50 for any of their products they sell. I suspect Vitacost has Nutiva private lebel their product as I cannot taste the difference. Your delivered cost of 5 gal at 231.10 is .36 per oz and mine is .40 oz. My wife and I use 2 of the 54 oz containers in 6-7 weeks.

### RGP — 2013-04-18T14:31:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84374>

Can't beat Tropical Traditions for taste.

### kathylu — 2013-04-18T17:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84394>

Put yourself on the Tropical Traditions email list. They have a free shipping day the first week of every month. That's when I order from them.

Personally, I don't like the coconut taste of either Nutiva or TT. I use TT organic expeller pressed coconut oil. No smell or taste!

I do like Nutiva coconut manna though...very yummy.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-21T19:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84580>

### kathylu — 2013-04-22T18:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84629>

I'm sure it's not as good as using totally unrefined oil, but I won't eat unrefined because I find the taste very off-putting. I guess we all have to decide what are compromises will be. I'm comfortable as long as the oil is not subjected to high temps or chemical extraction of any kind.

# Thread: Another Reason to Avoid Wi-Fi ... Top WiFi Routers Easy to Hack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-reason-to-avoid-wi-fi-top-wifi-routers-easy-to-hack.6807/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-04-17T23:02:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-reason-to-avoid-wi-fi-top-wifi-routers-easy-to-hack.6807/post-84357>

[http://news.cnet.com/8301-1009\_3-57579981-83/top-wi-fi-routers-easy-to-hack-says-study/](http://news.cnet.com/8301-1009_3-57579981-83/top-wi-fi-routers-easy-to-hack-says-study/)

The Wi-Fi router you use to broadcast a private wireless Internet signal in your home or office is not only easy to hack, says a report released today, but the best way to protect yourself is out of your hands.

The report notes that all 14 of the devices had critical security vulnerabilities that could be exploited by a "remote adversary" and could lead to unauthorized remote control of the router.

Before you dismiss router hacks as exceptionally rare, it's important to note that they've been a small but growing segment of computer security threats.

Ted Harrington further explained why router hacking could turn into a big problem. "What's notable about this is that if you compromise the router, then you're inside the firewall. You can pick credit card numbers out of e-mails, confidential documents, passwords, photos, just about anything," he said.

### Martin — 2013-04-18T14:45:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-reason-to-avoid-wi-fi-top-wifi-routers-easy-to-hack.6807/post-84376>

Good stuff, ATL. Hackers - they're out there all the time workin' it.

Gone wired myself.

### Optimalbound — 2013-04-18T17:26:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-reason-to-avoid-wi-fi-top-wifi-routers-easy-to-hack.6807/post-84399>

That's exactly why I've always used wired networking at home. I do have a wi-fi access point that I sometimes switch on when I have guests, but it sits _outside_ my firewall. And it runs on 25% signal strength.

# Thread: Improved Joints
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2013-04-18T15:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84378>

More and more often I'm noticing an old feeling that comes when I move around on my feet - a smooth, warm, fluid range of motion. Instead of painful creaking and groaning, I move like the teen of my youth. I can walk on the balls of my feet silently and assuredly, and more than once have broken out into a little trot just because I can! Downright giddy sometimes! Moving like a child again!

Less hip pain as well. I notice I can stand on one foot and pull the other leg up and put on a sock or shoe while balancing and there is no pain when I bring my leg up. I used to limp constantly, and now I walk "like a cat" (my DW's words, not mine!).

Good solid info and a way of life that is taking me back to our ancestor's ways. I always thought that there was an answer in the "old" ways before modern life, and never found it until I came here.

Thanks to all that have researched, posted, shared, spoken up, asked, and revealed.

"Martin"

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-18T15:11:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84382>

Yeah, that's one of the unexpected benefits of the ancient pathway. I got into this to deal with diabetes and in the process everything about my physicality improved.My 52 yr old body is pain free and I have bounce in my stride and sometimes I feel so good it's ridiculous. It seems to keep getting better. I haven't seen a massage therapist or a chiropractor in over two years and don't miss it at all.

### Inger — 2013-04-18T15:17:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84383>

That sounds Amazing, Martin!  Thank you for sharing! DaliDula too.. yay!

Me too feels like I am getting younger and younger instead of older..lol pretty cool feeling! I have decided that I going to go the opposite way with my age, at least for a few decades...
It is such a blessing to have no pains, no issues.. just feeling like a child all over. huh!

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-18T15:41:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84390>

Excellent Success Story Martin! that's awesome...

### nonchalant — 2013-04-18T16:49:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84392>

Great to hear it, Martin! It's nice that you are finding relief, finally.

### Optimalbound — 2013-04-18T17:22:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84398>

Awesome improvement, Martin! I never had much joint pain, myself, but I certainly move with greater strength, fluidity and agility now than what I did a year ago. Downhill skiing was incredibly much more enjoyable for me this season than last year.

### caroline — 2013-04-18T22:31:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84440>

That is so awesome Martin - great news! Now all the things you have put on hold can become your new reality.

I, too, have been completely free of joint pain, arthritis etc. seems unbelievable - but there it is.

We have a few more decades to keep getting younger.........

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-19T06:46:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84465>

this is a sign to begin to do more with your collagen semiconductors........that means massage/structured healing modalities......

### nonchalant — 2013-04-19T17:37:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84483>

Martin, I have a similar story.
Last year a chiropractor was working regularly on my back to try to free it from stiffness and immobility in places. (CT helped so much with the pain and inflammation!) He did get some mobility in it after a few months, but when I would do a "hoola-hoop" motion with my hips, I could hear all the grinding in my lower back. It was progress, however. Later I started the 30 day seafood challenge, and I noticed that my lower back was getting quite flexible. And the grinding sound also went away. I still saw the chiropractor, but fewer times per week. Then I started squatting several times per day, and my back got so good and flexible I only went in once per week. But it wasn't until I started laying my spine on my grounding sheet, and drinking good water, and also sun water, that I was able to say goodbye to the chiropractor. I know the secret now.  Anytime it tries to stiffen up, it gets grounded asap.

Thank you, Dr. Kruse, for your various protocols. They have helped me tremendously. A massage sounds marvelous, but I'm still paying the chiropractor bill... ;-)

### kathylu — 2013-04-19T22:07:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-joints.6810/post-84495>

Same story here. 18 months ago I was walking with a cane from inflammatory arthritis and popping pain meds like candy. Now...no pain! No cane! I also had a problem with my left knee that was quite stubborn...painful, clicking and grinding. My doc wanted to send me for an orthopedic consult, glad I didn't go. The knee is completely healed (as far as I can tell).

Out of the blue a few days ago, my newly 6 year old son said "Mama, you used to be handicapped...I remember your cane! I'm glad you're better." I'm still smiling.

### caroline — 2013-04-20T01:10:44+0300

These stories are all wonderful successes.... we have gotten our life back in so many ways......

Oh, wow! You have made me think of my hubby's knees. For decades, he had a "trick" knee and over the years we developed the habit of holding hands while walking, because we never knew when his knee would give out. But it has been a very long time since his knee has given him such problems. He still has difficulty if he kneels, however. He isn't epi paleo, or even paleo.
