# Thread: Feet swelling
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-04-18T15:04:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84379>

Hey all! I've been in NYC this week - the first time here in 6 years. It's a business trip so they are keeping us busy and lots of walking of course.

For the very first time my feet and ankles are so swollen they don't even look like my feet. I put them on the metal on the airplane as much as I could during the flight, drank a boatload of water. I haven't drank as much water since I've been here. Is it dehydration? Maybe related to takin the thyroid and progesterone? I'm sure it's a sign of bring disconnected but I have never experienced anything like this.

It's also been difficult on the diet, as all of our breakfasts and lunches are brought in. Which means wheat at pretty much every meal - and so hard to avoid.

Hope everyone is having a great week.

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-18T15:23:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84384>

Stomping around on concrete all day can be brutal and exhausting. When I do city stuff I have found that clogs work very well, but they may not be fashionable

### diane — 2013-04-18T15:28:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84386>

I actually found a pair of flats in the hotel shop with leather soles - regular $143 for $36!! I scooped them up!

Didn't pack any clogs lol - but yes the footwear hasn't been the most comfortable.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-18T15:33:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84388>

Diane

my n=1 for me says dehydration... push the CoQ10, drink, drink drink, and ground as much as possible - given you're in the middle of EMF soup. it can be done as I somehow manage to do it daily - it may not be ideal but I pull it off.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-04-18T16:02:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84391>

Assuming you are normally grain free, I would be thinking wheat, wheat, wheat. Can you eat more fat and protein and avoid the wheat??

My n=1 was that I lost a ton of inflammation all over my body when I dropped grains.

### cantweight — 2013-04-18T18:14:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84410>

I have been dealing with swollen feet and hands myself. I was thinking it was directly related to the heat for me.

### Rachelle — 2013-04-18T18:27:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84414>

For me it is always food like wheat and potatoes that give me swollen feet and hands. Since being off them totally I have no problem at all and I live also in an EMF sesspool.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-18T21:03:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84427>

Yep, it could be the EMF smog. Also, a close older relative tells me she tends to lose excess fluid when she takes the Iodoral (iodine) I gave her. Are you getting enough iodine and getting 8 or 9 hours sleep each night? You might try elevating the feet above the head to let your body reprocess the fluid that is collecting in the lower legs. Avoid flouridated and chlorinated drinking water and drink plenty of chilled spring/distilled/RO water. If you are magnesium deficient, you could supplement with Magnesium Glycinate. Are you getting enough vitamin D3? I don't trust the D2 (ergosterol).

### caroline — 2013-04-18T21:49:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84431>

Hi Diane! I love NYC......I have had the same problem the last few years when travelling. It has been really, really bad. In retrospect I think too ......wheat, wheat and wheat and MSG!!!

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-18T22:05:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84436>

Micro-brewed beer is my absolute favorite beverage, and I succumb to indulging in it on occasion, after which my ankles always swell. I presume it's the carbs and the grains.

### diane — 2013-04-20T19:55:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84550>

Thanks everyone! Yes, I was taking Lugols before I left, but didn't take it with me. I get D3, and take mag malate each night (that I did take with me).

I first thought it was the flight - which really concerned me. But I got home yesterday, and could actually see the veins in my feet this morning. Which then rules out flying because I flew yesterday to get here. My ankles are still a little swollen - but holy cow I had cankles! And was wearing short pants - god why did I take those, if only I had known - so my big fat ankles were all out in the open.

Maybe it did have something to do with walking in improper shoes? Or maybe elevation (my hotel room was on the 47th floor, although the office was only on 22). Perhaps the alcohol, but I mainly drank red wine (other than the first night where I had a most delicious jalapeno margarita lol) - but I drink red wine at home occasionally too and don't get swelling.

It's so weird - but thanks for all your input! And I'm pretty relieved to see it going down today.

### Inger — 2013-04-20T21:08:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84555>

Oh.. Diane.. I am sorry you had big fat ankle issues! Sucks. I did not got swelling from flights so far me thinks. But I have never traveled more than 2 hours at once with a airplane so that might be why. Next time maybe try to drink lots of good water and take Q10 in larger amounts too and then some Iodine like you did. Maybe using compression socks would help too? And to try to hold your feet elevated on the flight if possible. I always try to do that and it works somehow, I use my bag or the chair in front of me to get my feet a bit higher.

47th floor...??? We do not have any buildings even close to that high here.. in the whole country! wow!

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-04-20T21:57:06+0300

### diane — 2013-05-04T21:41:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-85450>

So, this week has been pretty bad - not just my feet and ankles were swollen, but my calves were rock hard (no definition, couldn't see my calf muscles). My calves are kinda thick to begin with, but holy cow my legs were like stumps! Had some in the upper leg (above the knee) too. Funny - TOM started this week, so now I wonder if it's related to progesterone increasing then falling. My guess is that it's a clear sign I'm dehydrated.

I sent a message to my anti-aging doctor - she thinks it's the DHEA. I've been taking DHEA since last August with no issues. I started taking BHRT progesterone in April - I think it's related to that. But what do I know lol (ie. possibly more than my doctor??).

So I started hitting the CoQ10 and d-ribose harder. Then it struck me that a cold bath may help as well - so today it was into the bath. Definitely didn't feel as cold as it did in the winter! The good news is that my ankles are almost back to normal (little swollen) and my calves are pretty much back. Today I've been peeing like a race horse (my apologies for the visual) - have also been drinking water with lemon.

What I'm thinking is that this is an indication that my mitochondria are not functioning properly, so I need to still work on that while I take the BHRT. I don't know any of this for sure - just trying to tie some of the pieces together - the timing, what I've been doing - and it seems to correlate.

### diane — 2013-05-07T14:28:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-85562>

It's been 4 days and my feet are almost back to normal. Now I'm really wondering if it's related to my hormones and cycle, as they started swelling on Day 14, and then pretty much went down once TOM was over.

I'm pretty sure the cold bath helped - man I was peeing about every 15 minutes on Saturday! Think that was it all coming out. LOL. Will see what happens this cycle.

It's funny because when I looked down at my feet when they were swollen, I've seen that exact look before in many women. Also, wondering if people who are having weight gain with progesterone are really experiencing water retention from dehydration.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-07T16:11:24+0300

Diane - I think you've just picked up on something very important here! I think hydration is key to one's ability to leverage Pg well. I'm currently re-reading Dr. Reiss's hormone book and in the progesterone chapter he mentioned that there are some women who can't tolerate much Pg, even though they're deficient. His clinical experience shows that usually this problem is related to candida overgrowth. and once its been eradicated the women don't have problems w/Pg any longer. I'm wondering if this might also be a similar reaction for those ladies here on the board who have Lymes and diseases/co-infections associated with Lymes?

In his book, he does state that even after clearing the candida, there is a small percentage that can't tolerate traditional dosing of Pg. This highlights for me how important your own journaling, and documenting is in helping you figure out what you need, but its also helpful for your doctor as well.

I am bummed bc I got the stomach flu something feirce last week (threw up in excess of 7 or so times, fatigued, headaches, weakness, the works) and had to miss my much anticipated lyme appt. # Thread: Dr Robert O. BECKER  Silver Ionotophoresis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-robert-o-becker-silver-ionotophoresis.6812/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2013-04-18T15:05:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-robert-o-becker-silver-ionotophoresis.6812/post-84380>

In researching what to do with my abscessed toofer, I ran across this about our boy Becker.

[http://www.rexresearch.com/becker/becker1.htm](http://www.rexresearch.com/becker/becker1.htm)

"Silver can help regenerate human cells that have been destroyed by disease or damaged in accidents."

"The silver-based procedure has been so successful in clinical tests, that one patient who had sustained three crushed fingers in an accident grew new tissue immediately. Within 2-1/2 months, skin coverage was complete and there was normal, full sensation, good blood supply and all joints had a normal range of motion. If left untreated, the 3~year-old electrician's fingers would have fallen off after turning black with gangrene, and he would have been left with a totally useless hand. In fact, his orthopedic surgeon recommended amputation of al1 three fingers, but the patient requested silver-ion therapy that was successful."

"Becker discovered that when positively charged silver ions are electrically introduced into wounds with a proprietary silver-coated nylon fabric used as the positive electrode, large amounts of primitive embryonic stem cells are produced. These stem cells are responsible for the reconstruction of destroyed tissue at a pace considerably faster than if the wound had been left to heal by itself. In other cases, the wound might not heal at all without the introduction of these stem cells."

### caroline — 2013-04-18T22:56:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-robert-o-becker-silver-ionotophoresis.6812/post-84443>

so interesting Martin ... are you able to access this treatment? Sounds like you are clicking on all cylinders these days!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-19T06:40:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-robert-o-becker-silver-ionotophoresis.6812/post-84462>

Hello all you cold water people
I'm just a feeling a little wobbly right now. I went across to Ray Cronise's blog to see what was going on over there and happened to mention in the most recent thread that I hadn't done well on an all plants diet. He went for me big time! Why are all these vegans so aggressive? I had a similarly unpleasant response from Colin Campbell when I emailed him to ask about why the plants only thing had made me ill.

I don't know wny I'm upset - perhaps it's the being accused of lying which hurts. I'm eating bacon to comfort myself.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-18T20:27:16+0300

### Lava — 2013-04-18T20:37:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84425>

Agatha, I had the same problem on an all plant diet..Thank goodness I got over that and got my brain and life back. It's hard to change the dogma of others...Bacon is good comfort food!!!!!!! Hang tough and don't let them bug you...

### David — 2013-04-18T20:58:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84426>

While I am fascinated by Ray Cronise's "potato diet" and the concept of starch consumption as a path to great health (Healthy starch! Healthy whole grains!), I think Epi-paleo is much better for one's well being.

You have obviously scratched some sort of wound by pointing out your problems with the vegan diet.

Perhaps vegans run on a principal based on the "news media factor", ie "if everybody thinks something is true, which makes it a fact." So, you have experienced something that runs counter to these "facts", and expressing your contrary opinion to these folks means to them that you must be evil, misguided or crazy. I wouldn't sweat it.

(Along these same lines, I think I am beginning to see why Dr Kruse is sometimes disheartened by the reception he gets from people he believes should know better...)

### caroline — 2013-04-18T21:51:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84432>

Bacon always, always works for me!

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-18T22:00:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84434>

I didn't realize he had a blog, so curiosity got the better of me and I headed over there to read through it. Whew! That was exhausting.
He told you that he could see the reason why you didn't feel good on your diet, but then declines to actually give an answer. Probably because he doesn't have one.

This is the second time this week I've encountered people who claim to be nutritionally enlightened who are recommending low fat, but for the life of me I haven't been able to find what their rationale is. Is Ray saying it's strictly a matter of calories?

I found his blog fascinating given all that I've been learning here. It's like he's talking a good talk, but I am not sure I buy it.

Regarding diet and cold soaking and weight loss, I'm in the midst of my own observations.

My weight loss stalled in November, for which I think several things are a factor...winter being the primary one. When I upped my cold soaking and was eating very small epi-paleo meals with no hunger at all for November, December, January, February, and March, I had zero weight loss, but my clothes fit differently and my skin felt completely different. Now that I'm moving through Spring, I feel like things are shifting in my body, yet nothing has happened yet. Hard to describe what I mean here, but it seems like the change of season is impacting hormones and a desire to be outside moving more, even though I did quite a bit of outside movement all winter (big skier, here!). I went paleo last April, nearly one year ago exactly. I lost 35 pounds in 4 months, and then fluxuated + or - 5 pounds between August and November. Then weight completely stable since then. I'm just wondering if I will start going into weight loss mode as spring heads to summer.

My own N=1.

Working on my will power.

### sjoshua — 2013-04-18T22:11:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84438>

Hmm... I have read quite a bit of Ray's stuff, and although he supports the "potato diet" (which in my opinion he came up with randomly in hopes to counter both keto and paleo diets, since it is not fat nor commonly accepted as a "safe starch" due to being white) and has documented successes using it, I am surprised to hear he went after you... The blog posts I have read seem to lean towards veggie/vegan, but he always seems to go back to leaving the choice up to the reader. (Mind sharing a link to the post/reply? Curiousity )

My impression is that Ray's approach is targeting shortcuts that primarily promote fat loss, and in that sense I believe he is doing some awesome work with his calorimeter and CT/Activity experimenting. Although I myself enjoy my primal diet and have worked towards keeping it even more keto since discovering Jack's work, I think if Ray's method is effective for an individual it could be utilized to trim some fat or even bypass a fat loss stall in the short term.

On the other hand, Jack's primary aim seems to be increasing overall health, longevity, and optimizing our own true potentials far beyond diet. Along the way, you will likely sculpt a beautiful physique and drop excess fat, but this is more of a secondary benefit due to living and feeding optimally. Jack's principles are derived from a much more scientific and logical analysis, and supported by his background as a neuro Dr.

### caroline — 2013-04-18T22:23:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84439>

Mama - what I noticed first - I went down 2 sizes but my weight didn't change or maybe went up a Kilo or 2. Nothing much changed until I upped the fat and cut down the protein - I have drifted down about 4 Kilos now and my weight is very stable - no wild fluctuations like I use to have. I know that being healthy and in control is absolutely our first priority and that is what Jack is all about .....but it sure does feel good to rock a pair of skinny jeans!!!

### agatha — 2013-04-18T23:34:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84445>

Thanks to you all for your encouragement - miuch appreciated. To satisfy your curiosity sjoshua here is the link to the whole (long) thread.

[http://hypothermics.com/2013/04/muscling-your-metabolism-part-2/#more-1895](http://hypothermics.com/2013/04/muscling-your-metabolism-part-2/#more-1895)

My part is near the end - here below are the final posts in a back and forth discussion. You'll notice he isn't able to produce an answer to my question of what was wrong with my vegan diet - he relies on accusing me of lying.

Me:
So, can you see where the problem was in my plant based diet? For two years, I ate daily:
At least 8-10 portions of colourful veggies including crucifers,
some starch each day (barley, oats, potatoes, sweet potatos), some legumes each day, herbs, spices, a couple of fruits daily, some nuts, seeds and avocados, very small amounts of olive oil. No sugar or processed foods. All food cooked at home from fresh. I didn’t lose weight so I know I was eating an appropriate amount of food.
I agree I have some health problems – this is why I tried eating a plant diet (after reading The China Study) to see if it could help me. Instead I felt much worse after a while. I don’t think the plants caused the deterioration (I still eat 8 portions of plants per day) but I think the diet lacked some things which I need. I started out with mild MS, I ended up with a host of other problems on top and these new sympoms resolved within a few weeks of adding butter and eggs into my diet. Just saying, some people might need more than just plants. I now feel at my best eating lots of plants and some high quality animal foods (fish, eggs, butter offal). Others may do well on plant based and that’s great.

Reply

Cody Fyler says:

April 18, 2013 at 7:00 am

Well, the fact that you felt better eating eggs and butter is telling to me. Maybe you were deficient in choline? Egg yolks are a good source of choline. Liver is, too. Butter is a great source of vitamin A, vitamin K2, and a little D. Not to mention, some folks (Ray Peat) believe that saturated fat is anti-inflammatory. Butter is a mix of saturated and monounsaturated fats, If memory serves…

Reply

admin says:

April 18, 2013 at 7:27 am

Actually it doesn’t mean that at all. If you are a smoker and quite – one “feels better” when they resume. Alcohol addicts have tons of symptoms when they quit. Feeling better is important, but it doesn’t “tell you” anything.

This is exactly how we end up with all of the anecdotal, summarized wrong information. This is an example of nutritionism at its finest. People discussing technical sounding words with “placeholder” notions of what they mean – 125 years ago it was protein, carbohydrate and fat. That predates vitamins and to this very day most people don’t even understand those three – even people that should know better.

I’m not interested in going there here. If that causes a few people not to come back, its okay. But it’s a case of blind leading the blind and I don’t want to participate.

Elle, there is a way for you to figure out what is going on. Butter an eggs are likely not the only or even best solution. If you care enough to talk about it publicly then care enough to figure out how to make it work – out destiny is no in the hands of extract another mammals lactate or ovum.

Challenge status quo a bit and figure out exactly what is happening. So many other variables are in the mix. There are already too many holes in the story that I’m not sure I’m getting the real facts. I’ve personally coached people that have told me “I ate perfect” when in fact they have not.

There are many other issues associated with the solution space you’re working in, but that’s for another time.

Thanks for input. Lets get back to metabolism.

Ray

Hilarious.

First thought that I had is B12 deficiency, not enough protein for rebuilding.

My take on vegan's getting upset......denial....they think they are not mammals.....been there myself.

### nonchalant — 2013-04-19T17:18:12+0300

# Thread: Salt?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tweety — 2013-04-19T02:34:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84458>

What kind of salt does everyone prefer? I have been using sea salt almost exclusively. But I recently had a chat with a naturopath who felt sea salt was not the way to go, that it led to problems like excessive swelling and increases in bad cholesterol. He also felt it contributed to thickening of the blood. I'm not sure I agree in the least with him, but it gave me cause to go read up on my salts. I know there are so many different products out there. Himalayan salts, sea salts, kosher salt....

His recommendation was kosher salt, but I didn't get to ask why.
What does everyone use? And why? I know alot of people in the paleoshpere swear by Redmond's Real Salt.

### nonchalant — 2013-04-19T03:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84459>

Kosher salt refers to the size of the salt crystals. Kosher salt (koshering salt) doesn't have the anti-caking ingredients many fine-grained salts have. I personally like to have a variety of salts.

### Inger — 2013-04-19T07:29:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84468>

I use
Celtic sea salt, Himalayan crystal salt, 100% natural "salt flowers" from France. I love them all

### caroline — 2013-04-19T08:26:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84470>

I love Celtic sea salt the best but also use Himalayan....

### johnnyb — 2013-04-19T16:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84478>

I mostly use the Real Salt brand. It applies like table salt but has other trace minerals. The Himalayan pink salt has particular taste that I am trying with more foods. It seems to enhance some foods more then others. Since it comes as rocks the grinder it is in changes it's utility. I still use kosher salt some. Mostly for brines or when I want better control sprinkling a pinch. Since I started drinking mineral water I add less salt as I cook.

### nonchalant — 2013-04-19T17:12:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84481>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=84468)
>
> 100% natural "salt flowers" from France.

Fleur de Sel! Oh that is fun to pop in your mouth. I love it too.

### MeghanK — 2013-04-19T17:54:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84484>

Himilayan... and BLACK LAVA Seas Salt (has activated Charcoal)

### tweety — 2013-04-19T21:39:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84493>

black lava sea salts sounds awesome!

### Rachelle — 2013-04-20T01:54:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84509>

I have been using the Black Lava sea salt too. It's great for finishing a dish.

### diane — 2013-04-20T19:29:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.6817/post-84548>

I use mainly Celtic sea salt. My naturopath recommended Celtic sea salt - but now it escapes me why lol. But I like the taste so have stuck with it.

I eat a ketogenic diet so all of these things would make a lot of sense and I can relate to all of the symptoms you mention in the Top Ten EMF FAQ post. # Thread: People think we're loopy-loo

### agatha — 2013-04-19T18:54:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/people-think-were-loopy-loo.6820/post-84489>

Has anyone else noticed that people make a particular facial expression - a kind of amused and disbelieving smile - when you tell them about EMF and earthing - it's kind of like the face people make when a child tells them that the tooth fairy came last night....

My husband made that face when I bought the earthing sheet. He's smiling in a different way now that his blood pressure has come down 20 points!

This is even more fun than cold exposure conversations.

### caroline — 2013-04-20T01:31:52+0300

Totally agree - when you talk about electrons and grounding and EMF's - people think you are from Mars.....
except my grandson ... he loves it and is so interested......he wants me to buy him earthing shoes - and he has always hugged trees and rolled on the grass ........and he has always been his own little man - couldn't care less what others think!
He is such a smart little guy - his future is so bright ....except for his parents and their CW.....

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-20T02:46:24+0300

I'm leading an earthing class in June, and I'm doing an experiment with it. This is my first "tester" class. I live in a community with an abnormally high number of PhDs (one of the highest in the nation, in fact). So, for this first class, my description is more generic and about getting in touch with nature, although I do mention grounding. For the next offering, I'm going to change the description to being more true to what I'm really gearing up for, earthing, grounding, CT, mindfulness...

I'm very curious to see how it will change who signs up, and the number of participants.

### caroline — 2013-04-20T03:37:46+0300

Absolutely! can't wait to participate somehow... maybe we can do a skype retreat! Have you thought of doing an earthing class for children? I talk to my grandson all the time about electrons etc. He is so receptive ....I have bought him a crystal for his birthday and going to get him a book on crystals as well.
Of course - my daughter rolls her eyes and gets that look that Agatha is talking about!
Why am I the health nut that everyone rolls their eyes at???? I am the healthy one - my daughter is very unhealthy...... and won't listen to anything I say - no matter how gently....

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-20T08:46:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/people-think-were-loopy-loo.6820/post-84542>

For my birthday I bought a quart of goat milk for lunch. Its the only dairy I've had in a year except for butter. I showed it to an older lady at work who had both knees replaced and asked her if she wanted a swig cause it was good stuff. She declined and replied that her Dr. Pepper tasted good. I quipped back that cocaine tastes good to a drug addict, too.

I've been giving a quiz at work to a few people. I ask them how much white sucrose sugar the average person ate in the year 1900 (5 pounds). Then I ask them how much sucrose sugar the average person eats today (150 pounds).

This thread reminds me of something my mother told me about from her childhood. She and the neighborhood kids would run behind the city pest control vehicle in the street as it was spraying a fog of DDT to control mosquitos.

### Inger — 2013-04-20T10:13:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/people-think-were-loopy-loo.6820/post-84543>

> [GlenPDQ said:](/goto/post?id=84542)
>
> For my birthday I bought a quart of goat milk for lunch. Its the only dairy I've had in a year except for butter. I showed it to an older lady at work who had both knees replaced and asked her if she wanted a swig cause it was good stuff. She declined and replied that her Dr. Pepper tasted good. I quipped back that cocaine tastes good to a drug addict, too.
>
> I've been giving a quiz at work to a few people. I ask them how much white sucrose sugar the average person ate in the year 1900 (5 pounds). Then I ask them how much sucrose sugar the average person eats today (150 pounds).
>
> This thread reminds me of something my mother told me about from her childhood. She and the neighborhood kids would run behind the city pest control vehicle in the street as it was spraying a fog of DDT to control mosquitos.

OMG! about the DDT..huh

I wanted to put some mussels in the freezer yesterday.. and then it was so full of stuff my stuff almost did not fit in, something my mom had filled it with a few hours before coming from a shopping tour.. it was filled with those Finnish traditional Easter porriges that you eat with sugar and cream/milk. It is made of whole rye and sugar basically, baked in the oven. 100% grain and sugar. Poison to me. I love the taste for sure but I stopped eating it many many ears ago. I said, mom! What is this poison filling up the fridge...?! (I said **poison** a bit louder) She said, ohh it is Memma, it tastes so good! I said, yes mom, many things taste good, sure. But are they good for us, no! My mom got so silent and looked ashamed. She is the one that have the hardest time staying away from sweet stuff BTW. Even if she sees her own kids suffer lots and lots of health issues.

It is so funny her own mum died from a massive heart attack way too early, she seemed healthy until then.. and her dad suffered years and years bedridden in hospital because plenty of infarcts he got in he's brain. I always say to my mom, do you want to end like that? Remember you have lots of grand kids that needs your love!

To me it seems to stay way from grains (and sweets) seems to be harder to do than a drug addict to stay away from he's drugs....
I do love my parents for sure. And I try to keep my mouth shut most of the time..lol But sometimes it is hard to understand what choices people make for sure!

### agatha — 2013-04-20T20:06:49+0300

# Thread: Minimum effective dose for CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 38
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2013-04-20T02:31:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84511>

So this is a bit of a strange inquiry, and my guess is there is too little data to really give an effective answer... but it never hurts to ask

I am using CT solely for the vast array of subliminal health benefits (immunity, well-being, longevity, etc.); I am already ~8% BF so don't need it to help me lose fat really. So far, I have experimented with daily cold showers (almost 2 months now). I really like doing them, and at this point I can basically jump right in without much more than a couple seconds of adapting... When I first started I would have to count to 90 or so before my body was tolerant without being uncomfortable. I also can feel myself radiating heat at night now as it gets cooler outside, and find I get overly warm if I wear something more than an A shirt or T shirt. (Aside: I live in San Jose; night temps only dip to ~50's this time of year)

My question is, is there a minimum effective dose to CT? Or, what is the minimum amount that must be done in order to obtain the majority of the benefits? Is it merely a linear sliding scale of "more is better"? Are my showers sufficient or are the benefits 10 fold by switching to extended baths?

If relevant, my diet is primarily keto: 6 days/week I eat 1 large evening meal composed of meat (grass fed beef or various seafood) and veggies cooked in coconut oil. Occasionally I will have a handful of walnuts afterwards, but usually my meal is more than sufficient. One day a week, typically on the weekend, I will allow myself to eat outside of keto, but still keep to 1 meal and use choices like sweet potatoes or trail mix (nuts, seeds, dried berries) and would estimate I don't surpass 150g carbs in the day.

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-20T03:13:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84514>

Any exposure below body temp is theoretically having some effect and it is cumulative. More frequent sessions are more effective than less. More is better. Dr K's protocol does not advocate for full immersion temps below 50 deg although many here practice at temps as low as 32. I am of the opinion that full immersion up to your ears is the way to go. If you have a way to swim at cold temps there is nothing better. Your skin should turn pink. I think Dr k would say you can't do this too much. As for diet try switching your big meal to breakfast.

### caroline — 2013-04-20T03:27:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84519>

Dr. K. told me that cold showers aren't good enough ... nor is the ocean if it isn't cold. Tub and ice is the way to go!
Don't get me wrong - the ocean is great - but if it isn't cold - and it hasn't been this summer ...then you still need a tub and ice......

### MeghanK — 2013-04-20T05:38:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84534>

@sjoshua.... I'm in the same situation... i don't need to lose weight but am interested in the minimum dose of CT... I have been doing the 20" of cold 10" of warm for 5 minutes (Ray Cronise Protocol) in the shower.... I was hoping I get similar benefits without having to buy a bag of ice a day to do bath CT... what do you do in the shower? Do you measure the temp of the water....?
Are you only eating one meal a day as per the "Warrior Diet" or doing a Lean Gains type IF 16/8....

### sjoshua — 2013-04-23T22:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84698>

Thanks for the feedback all!

I wish I could relocate my office to a meat locker and just CT all day while at work ;-P Alas, not realistic for a desk jockey... I guess to clarify my inquiry, my intent for asking is to find the most efficient way if time is a factor (sure, the philaspohical answer is likely whether my time is worth more now vs. the benefits/longevity gained, but let's not get into that). From the sounds of the replies, my cold showers aren't enough and I need to up my game to baths. When adopting the baths, what would be the ideal temp/time allotment for achieving the MED? (For more info or some examples of MED, please check this article out: [http://gizmodo.com/5709902/4+hour-body-the-principle-of-the-minimum-effective-dose](http://gizmodo.com/5709902/4+hour-body-the-principle-of-the-minimum-effective-dose) ).

@Dali Dula: Although I realize Jack supports a heavy morning meal, my diet is designed around convenience. I'd much rather spend the time to cook and eat in the evening then try to throw something together before going to work. My compliance is 100%, so I don't consider this to be an adjustable factor.

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=84534)
>
> @sjoshua.... I'm in the same situation... i don't need to lose weight but am interested in the minimum dose of CT... I have been doing the 20" of cold 10" of warm for 5 minutes (Ray Cronise Protocol) in the shower.... I was hoping I get similar benefits without having to buy a bag of ice a day to do bath CT... what do you do in the shower? Do you measure the temp of the water....?
> Are you only eating one meal a day as per the "Warrior Diet" or doing a Lean Gains type IF 16/8....

@MeghanK: I keep a consistent, cold temp on the water for my whole shower time (~5-7 minutes); I have not tested the temp prior but I will attempt to check it tonight. Sadly my kitchen thermo recently broke, so I will do the best I can with the laser temp guage (the type for tracking skin/surface temps)... Do you know the temps yours run?

My one meal/day was initially inspired by lean gains/IF, but it is strongly driven by and maintained due to personal convenience. Not having to cook, clean, or otherwise stop what I am doing to spend time pertaining to eating more than once/day is ideal for my lifestyle.
When only cooking once/day, I tend to put more time and effort into crafting a gourmet-esque meal vs. just cookin' up some grub. Also, I have always been one to eat big when I eat, so this allots me the ability to do so without much issue.

### AndrÃ©s — 2013-04-24T11:42:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84726>

I suppose full immersion is best, nevertheless I have gone [the DIY ice-vest way](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5263-DIY-ice-vest-2-0) for convenience (I comment the adaptation process on [the 1.0 post](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?1603-Effective-Efficient-CT&p=30867&viewfull=1#post30867)). There is the [professional ice-vest](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?3365-59-99-Ice-Pack-Vest) too, although it focuses on shoulder-blade BAT only.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-24T15:32:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84731>

any CT is worthwhile, but some need more than others due to their personal context.

### Danco3636 — 2013-04-24T18:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84742>

sjoshua. Interesting that you make it one meal a day. Do you include any coffee or Bulletproof coffee dur

### Danco3636 — 2013-04-24T18:09:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84743>

sjoshua do you have any coffee or Bulletproof coffee during the day? Any other snacks? Also do you incorporate workouts during the day? I generally fast 16-20 hours a day and have two meals. For me one meal and I would be stuffed eating the required calories I would need. Unless it was mostly fat.... Now that may work with bacon as a meal.  I generally skip breakfast as well having a lunch or late mid day meal.
Sounds like it is working for you.

### sootedninjas — 2013-04-24T23:45:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84759>

it's the keto diet that makes IF natural.

I'll eat 1 meal around 4pm, following day around 8am or 9am I'll have a variation of bulletproof coffee then around 2pm do some kind of workout martial arts or strength training at the gym. and literally WILL NOT be hungry even after 2 hours.

### sjoshua — 2013-04-25T02:03:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84764>

> [Danco3636 said:](/goto/post?id=84743)
>
> sjoshua do you have any coffee or Bulletproof coffee during the day? Any other snacks? Also do you incorporate workouts during the day? I generally fast 16-20 hours a day and have two meals. For me one meal and I would be stuffed eating the required calories I would need. Unless it was mostly fat.... Now that may work with bacon as a meal.  I generally skip breakfast as well having a lunch or late mid day meal.
> Sounds like it is working for you.

Danco3636: Aside from a coffee in the morning with a splash of cream on the weekends only, I really just drink water. If I drank coffee before work I would be wayyy to wired to sit at a desk! I have maintained a much higher natural energy level since switching my diet ~2 years ago now. No snacking either, never have a need. I waste time with walks (2-2.5 mi) or bike rides (~10 mi) on my daily lunch breaks. 2-3 days/week before dinner and after work around 5-7PM I also do some sort of strength lifting using the reverse pyramid training style (a variation of a high intensity/low set/low rep lifting protocol).

I aim to eat an avg. around ~140g protein/day, which is approx. 1g/lb lean mass (I think this may be more than I really need; I've been doing some rough experimenting with reducing protein and upping fat recently but need to do so in a more organized manner to really dial it in). My fat comes from whatever the protein sources I am eating contain, but I always aim to eat at least 1/2 my protein from a high fat source. Pair that with a pile (1-2 lbs) of broccoli/cauli/carrots/spinach cooked in coconut oil.

Like I mentioned above, I have no problem putting away a lot of food in one sitting. But don't get me wrong, I'm not just force feeding myself. It typically takes me around an hour to eat my whole meal, and sometimes I will be snacking on it for ~2-3 hours.

> [sootedninjas said:](/goto/post?id=84759)
>
> it's the keto diet that makes IF natural.
>
> I'll eat 1 meal around 4pm, following day around 8am or 9am I'll have a variation of bulletproof coffee then around 2pm do some kind of workout martial arts or strength training at the gym. and literally WILL NOT be hungry even after 2 hours.

Sootedninjas: (cool name! ha) Sounds like you are living similarly to me. Curious - do you get hungry at all anymore? On rare occasion I will be too busy and have very little or no meal one day, and then continue my fast to the next evening out of habit (~45ish hours total). When this happens, I may feel a momentary pang earlier in the day, but find that I actually start to feel a little hungry again around my typical meal time that second night.

not really. I don't get hungry at all. sometimes I eat just because it's been 18 hours since my last meal. the beauty of keto diet is that you are not starving at all, not just hungry because being in ketosis is basically being a fat burner. the longest I've been w/o a meal is 25 hours last Saturday. When I moved around, like teaching Martial Arts or lifting heavy things, the less hungrier I get.

looks like you got your diet dialed in. just tweaking it a little here and there. my current tweak right now of Epi-Paleo because it just make sense. I will putting seafood as the main food source in my pyramid then the standard keto paleo of grass fed offal and beef yadi yadi yada....

What is your carb intake ?

Mine is like 50g daily ala Volek & Phinney, Art and Science of Low Carb Performance.

Since, I'm tweaking my diet right now to include more seafood and sea vegetables NOT sure how much more carbs that will add to my diet.

I might just need to start weighing and counting calories for the next 2 to 3 weeks. I hate counting because I find it very stressful BUT it is a necessary evil sometimes when I tweak my diet BUT I soon as I get dialed in to the change I stop counting.

### sjoshua — 2013-04-26T00:30:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-84829>

> [sootedninjas said:](/goto/post?id=84765)
>
> What is your carb intake ?
>
> Mine is like 50g daily ala Volek & Phinney, Art and Science of Low Carb Performance.
>
> Since, I'm tweaking my diet right now to include more seafood and sea vegetables NOT sure how much more carbs that will add to my diet.
>
> I might just need to start weighing and counting calories for the next 2 to 3 weeks. I hate counting because I find it very stressful BUT it is a necessary evil sometimes when I tweak my diet BUT I soon as I get dialed in to the change I stop counting.

Ok, it sounds like your experience is similar to mine. I've noticed that even with food in front of me and immediately following an intense workout I'll occasionally have no motivation to eat it. I've been buying grass fed beef by the 1/4 - 1/2 cow for a couple years now, so always have a healthy supply of red meat, and also like to switch things up with mixing various types of fish/mussels, and the occasional fowl meat.

I don't actively track my carb intake, but it is definitely <50g/day 6 days/week. During these days the only carbs I eat are a couple pounds of cruciferous veggies, with the occasional use of peppers/onions/mushrooms for variety. My estimate would be ~30-40g average. One day a week, typically saturdays and also typically following a strength training session, I will allow myself additional carbs through sweet potatoes and/or trail mix incl. dried fruit, nuts, chocolate; but I tend to keep this ~150g.

Art and Science of Low Carb Performance is definitely a book that has interested me, but I haven't had the opportunity to read yet. Do you recommend it? I realize this is purely individual, but was it educational, or moreso a review of principles you were already aware of?

Weighing and counting calories in veggies is fairly moot in my opinion, especially if you are sticking primarily to cruciferous veggies (of which I believe seaweed and probably other sea veggies are generally included along with or would at least be equivalent to) due to their lower carb content. But I understand your concerns about assuring you have things tweaked correctly.

Just curious, do you have macros you aim for vs. your weight/lean mass? Have you experimented with adjusting protein/fat ratios to perfection?

Also, have you implemented any forms of CT into your regimen yet?

1) Def we have similar diet. that is exactly what happens even if there is food in front of me. I bulk buy my grass fed beef too and pastured eggs BUT rarely eat fowl. too much omega 6. I always loved seafood BUT the cost is just sometimes prohibitive specially wild caught. But I've decided to change up and eat more seafood instead. However, I grow up eating a lot of offal so that will always be in the table and it is the cheapest part of the grass fed cow. Sometime I have a group of 4 people split a whole cow and nobody wants the organ, head, tongue and tail so I always end getting it all. suckers

2) yeah about 50g daily is what Phinney and Volek suggested and def working for me. I highly recommend the book BUT as you said you are basically doing what they are recommending. They do discuss protein intake tho.

3) Oh I am very high fat. When I was tracking my macros it was like between 75/15/10 to 80/15/5 (fat/protein/carb) and my weight is very stable. Carb goes up by 5% because from time to time I will have a 1 week worth of berries (strawberry, Blueberry, Blackbeery and raspberry) and mango too if it is relatively inexpensive, That's about my fruit intake. Protein is about .75g to 1g of protein for every pound of body weight. Heavy lifting days, 1x per week for 1 hour, post workout I might go as high as 1.25g of protein. I do high intensity lift a variation of super slow and static. 7 reps of 1 set. By the 7th rep I should be really struggling. If I can do about 8 or 9 rep w/o struggling then it is time to increase the resistance.

4) Based on Robb Wolf and Mark Sisson discussions, try safe starches if you can tolerate it. I tried BUT heck after 2 days I noticed that my right was kinda swollen and my knuckles was hurting plus I suddenly got 2 big pimples in my face. That was the only thing that change in my diet. After I stop eating the safe starch for 1 week the swollen hand subsided and it was back to normal. So I guess, just like Dr. Kruse mentioned on Paleo Summit podcast he does NOT believed on safe starches.

6) CT, I will start this weekend. Do some face dunking for a week or two.

Have you done any CT face dunking ? Once you get your skin temp to around 55 degrees, how long should you be dunking your face for that session ?

After, face dunking I might get one of those cold vest. Have you used any of those ? If you have which ones you recommend ?

Then after cold vest, cold showers and see how it goes.

However, I grow up eating a lot of offal so that will always be in the table and it is the cheapest part of the grass fed cow. >
> After, face dunking I might get one of those cold vest. Have you used any of those ? If you have which ones you recommend ?
>
> Then after cold vest, cold showers and see how it goes.

Very cool, I ended up with the heart and liver from my last cow buy, I requested all organ parts they offered that were available. The liver was soo delicious; I was thinking about making it into a liver jerky and having a piece or two every day for a steady supply of organ meat to supplement my other meat choices.

Aside/observation: I had a couple friends in town visiting for the last week and due to this was eating grocery store meats(burgers/sausages) ... currently been burning through all the leftovers since I don't want to waste meat. But I can definitely feel the difference and cant wait to get back into grass fed. I aim to eat a week straight worth next week to remedy this. Maybe a night or 2 of mussels of to get some seafood in the mix.

CT: I have been doing cold showers for 2 months now. I do them 6 days/week, allowing myself a warm shower over the weekend (but I notice I don't keep the water nearly close to as warm as I used to... so its more like luke warmish). I didn't start with face dunks or ice vests, I just went straight to the showers. When I first started, I found it easiest to just push through it and count to myself in my head for a distraction. At first I would count to 90+ before feeling accustomed to the water, but after a week or two of consistently decreasing counting times, it become much easier. As it stands, it only takes me a couple of seconds to adapt and then I am fine.

Also, in an effort to feel the effects a little longer, after shutting off the shower I dont dry myself off right away but allow my body to bask in the remaining cold dripping water for a 60-second count. If I'm off from work and have extra time, I will throw some gym shorts on right away and get outside in the sunlight to let the cold water naturally evaporate; that feels great given the chance!

If you are already LS, which I imagine you are considering what you've written, I don't think you'll need to baby step so much into the CT. Commit to a week's worth of cold showers and go from there. I may start doing the baths once in a while, but time constraints make it more difficult to fit into life.

### Dennis Durost — 2017-05-18T03:22:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-214233>

Don't know if I'm spitting in the wind or not but am trying to spot treat midsection with a waist trimming belt stuffed with ice packs. Daily for one hour. A couple of people on youtube tried it and were pleased with the results. One guy lost an inch and a half on his waist after 30 days with an additional two inches coming off over a two month period after the icing was stopped. Worth a shot.

### brent-next — 2017-05-18T09:38:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-214242>

> [Dennis Durost said:](/goto/post?id=214233)
>
> Don't know if I'm spitting in the wind or not but am trying to spot treat midsection with a waist trimming belt stuffed with ice packs. Daily for one hour. A couple of people on youtube tried it and were pleased with the results.

What you are trying to do is similar to the FDA approved fat reduction method called cool sculpting: [http://www.coolsculpting.com/](http://www.coolsculpting.com/)

The problem with trying to do this at home is that it's easy to give yourself frostbite and that won't make you look better at all. If you can afford CoolSculpting and there is one near you, at least go for the free consult. You will at least learn a lot that might make it safer at home.

The danger of doing this at home varies from wildly high to modest. Be careful. Read the research papers on Cool Sculpting.

You may also want to do overall CT for the overall benefits too.

### JanSz — 2017-05-18T14:54:19+0300

One guy lost an inch and a half on his waist after 30 days with an additional two inches coming off over a two month period after the icing was stopped. Worth a shot.

There are two reasons why midsecion is large.

1----The fat that lies just below your skin in most of your body -- the kind you can grab with your hands -- is called subcutaneous fat.
2----In your belly, it’s called visceral fat because it builds up in the spaces between and around your viscera -- internal organs like your stomach and intestines.
\----------------

Increasing metabolism eventually should help with both. That correlates with easy to measure ----> temperature.
If temperature is low (even just by 1/2 degree) it is going to be tough to loose that fat.

There are many ways to talk about it, but that just makes for long discussions.

Under tongue, should be 37C=98.6F
but if you have:
36.67C=98F
it is going to be tough to loose that fat

Accurate digital thermometer helps.

///////////////

### Allin — 2017-05-22T23:27:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-214444>

> [Dennis Durost said:](/goto/post?id=214233)
>
> Don't know if I'm spitting in the wind or not but am trying to spot treat midsection with a waist trimming belt stuffed with ice packs. Daily for one hour. A couple of people on youtube tried it and were pleased with the results. One guy lost an inch and a half on his waist after 30 days with an additional two inches coming off over a two month period after the icing was stopped. Worth a shot.

_I placed a large zip lock bag filled with ice cubes inside another large zip lock bag on my belly for 15 minutes to start. Over time, I increased that time to one hour. I wore a compression shirt at first until I reached 60 minutes and then removed the compression shirt. I would drink 16 oz of clean water before the session. I also ate a can of sardines three times a week for the DHA and worked in 30-60 minutes of sun on my belly most everyday. Now the cost is just about zero...._

### Dennis Durost — 2017-05-23T20:41:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-214520>

### JanSz — 2019-01-11T22:56:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimum-effective-dose-for-ct.6822/post-260096>

> [Allin said:](/goto/post?id=214444)
>
> _I placed a large zip lock bag filled with ice cubes inside another large zip lock bag on my belly for 15 minutes to start. Over time, I increased that time to one hour. I wore a compression shirt at first until I reached 60 minutes and then removed the compression shirt. I **would drink 16 oz of clean water before the session.** I also ate a can of sardines three times a week for the DHA and worked in 30-60 minutes of sun on my belly most everyday. Now the cost is just about zero...._

16 oz =0.47 liter

If you would limit your daily water intake below certain value
that would force mitochondria to make up the required difference.
Body fat would be used to make that water.

to make one kilo=liter of water 1.1 kg of fat would be used.
Rather efficient way of removing unnecessary body fat.

Dr Boros drinks 0.2 - 0.25 liter of water daily.
For his clients hi suggests 0.75L/day

.
..

### Jude — 2013-04-20T03:11:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haemochromatosis-anyone-here-dealing-with-this.6823/post-84513>

Hubby's ferritin levels have just kept rising. Only became aware after having the recommended labs done late last yr. Aug 12 582 (30-300)
Oct 12 608(30-400)
April 13 805(30-400)
Hopefully having blood taken in 2 weeks. Of course loves his meat and not at all keen about fish!!

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-20T03:15:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haemochromatosis-anyone-here-dealing-with-this.6823/post-84515>

### Jude — 2013-04-20T03:28:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haemochromatosis-anyone-here-dealing-with-this.6823/post-84520>

He is a carrier.
Regular blood giving is the answer for sure. Just wondering about late diagnosis, slow treatment and any issues arising from that.......Apparently in the Australian bush, with no blood banks all this blood letting becomes complicated. Only Dr's are are allowed to bleed these pt's and that requires 2 appt's--one for consult and one for the Rx room...and it's a 6 week wait....honestly.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-04-20T17:46:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-84547>

Hello. For any of you who have lyme or one of its co infections. What were your symptoms and treatment?

I ask bc over the years it's been brought up a few times with me but I'm not sure how I am symptomatic. I have low hormones across the board and adverse reactions to hormone treatment but that seems to be it (I also have hashis and there are some overlaps in symptons). But I don't exhibit the neurological or arthritic symptoms....

Sent from my iPad using Tapatalk HD

### kjgraffin@yahoo.com — 2013-04-21T02:50:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-84564>

My only symptoms were inability to fix my adrenal fatigue and my thyroid issues. I don't have any of the typical symptoms. My Lyme was confirmed with a CD57. Doc said that the lowest she likes to see is 150. Mine was 16.

We are using ozone therapy to treat it. I have had about 11 treatments so far. I just retested but have not seen the results yet.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-04-21T17:17:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-84573>

Are u feeling any better with treatment? Has it helped your adrenal and thyroid probs? Last winter I had a CD57 of 120 but was told that is inconclusive. For instance there was a person who had a positive lyme test but a great CD57

### kjgraffin@yahoo.com — 2013-04-22T01:33:12+0300

### tweety — 2013-04-22T18:52:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-84628>

I only suspect lime. I definitely had a tick bite at age 8, and last year I did the western blot. Two bands came back positive, one that is usually a good indicator of a positive lyme, but the conventional docs want 5 bands positive to diagnose. My symptoms I attribute to lyme would be like the above poster- inability to improve adrenal fatigue, opposite response to BHRT, and other random things. My blood sugar is always low, and despite being paleo and seasonal for two and a half years I still become fatigued and cranky when hungry. I still experience migraines from over-exertion (even when I do not feel I am over-exerting myself), and little things like this.

Last year a chiro muscle tested me for takesumi supreme, a Japanese bamboo charcoal. I took it sporadically, then forgot about it. A few weeks ago I remembered I had it in the cupboard and looked it up on the internet to see what it is used for. Lo and behold, it is used for people struggling to detoxify their systems of EMF, chronic conditions (like lyme), heavy metals, etc. I have been taking it twice a day now and am on my second week and feel it is putting me back on track. My random swelling is going down, my digestion is improved, and I feel more like myself! I am hopeful it is helping my system clear out some of the things weighing me down so I can really get down to healing.

### cantweight — 2013-04-23T22:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-84699>

Lyme treatment is more of an exorcism than a healing process. I was finally diagnosed with Lyme, babesia, and bartonella in 2009 after years of being sick.

My symptoms were fevers over 104 almost daily, horrible night sweats, severe fatigue, pain in most of body so severe I took vicodin daily just to take the edge off...never got relief. The cognitive decline was so bad I was unable to read, spell my name, often stopped speaking in the middle of sentences unaware I was in a conversation, got lost driving in my own neighborhood, crawled to bed at night the pain was so horrific. I was on my way to needing a wheelchair at 33 yrs old and feared not knowing my family as I thought I had alzheimers. Some times I'd be sitting in a room with them and become very upset and frightened because I didn't know who I was with or where I was....it was insane!

I have healed about 80% of my issues. I am in school to become a nurse practitioner so definitely all my cognitive issues have resolved. No more fibromyalgia, no more fevers, sweats, pain, migraines. No problems speaking or walking. I feel quite good aside from being fat and tired.

I do have hormone and thyroid problems. My adrenals are awesome now...after being completely tanked.

Based on what yall have posted as symptoms IMHO you dont have Lyme symptoms that I would treat. I would certainly pay attention to your body if it showed signs, but the treatment is hard on the liver and i wouldn't subject the body to it unless it was necessary.

Activated charcoal is a great binder...make sure you take it away from any other supplement/med as it will take it with it. If you use something like charcoal you must be sure your bowels are moving fantastically.

As for adrenal fatigue i can not recommend the supplement I have used for years highly enough Researched Nutritionals EnergyMultiPlex

[https://www.researchednutritionals.com/store/item.cfm?code=CRN105&cat=2](https://www.researchednutritionals.com/store/item.cfm?code=CRN105&cat=2)

Love that stuff! My adrenals were in horrible shape. Functioning so well now. Cortisol levels good, stress reactions minimal.

If your adrenals are in bad shape it is worth mentioning that adrenal fatigue and Lyme symptoms do overlap a lot.

To really rule out or address the Lyme...if you believe it to be part of your problem I would personally run an Igenex test for lyme and co infections. Labcorp and Quest are very hit or miss. MDL is a lab my family has had good results with also.

Feel free to message me if I can help at all. I have spent 4 years successfully battling this beast. Lyme is endemic in my area and I am constantly helping people I don't even know that get referred to me to make their way through the mess of dealing with Lyme. I cringe every time I hear of someone suffering with lyme because it is beatable with the right ammunition. Just so hard to find a doc that knows how to arm the body.

This is super helpful. Thats kinda what got me thinking. I watched "under my skin" and granted - those are extreme cases -but I didnt have any of those symptons

Before I realized I was gluten intolerant, I did have some neurological/muscular troubles - but that stopped when I cut out gluten (which is why I NEVER cheat in that area)...the adrenal, thyroid probs have been persistent. Despite what my labs say - normal by even Stop the thyroid madness levels - I still do not look or feel normal. I have serveral doc appts lined up over the next few wees, mainly I want a second opinion to see if there is anything else I can tweak.

I am thankful to not be experiecing pain like you mentioned and count my lucky stars every day that of all the ailments I've experienced joint pain has never been an issue.

Becasue I've been resistent to medications - it took me a LONG time to tolerate my thyroid meds and I had to go extremely slow either ramping up and now ramping down from HC - lyme of coinfections or biotoxins have come up as a possible suggestions often.

The NP I am seeing this weekend runs the Igenx lab...I will talk to her more in depth about this and see if she thinks its worth me running the test (as I will be paying for this out of pocket and and dont believe insurance will cover anything). I have an appt with a Lyme Literate Dr but its not until Oct which is why they directed me to her while I am waiting. She only costs $60 as opposed to the $360 I'll be paying for my consult in Oct.

That supp you listed looks AMAZING. It basically has almost everything I take seperately and looks like something Dr. Kruse would put together himself, ha! I am wondering if it would be better for me to just use that and will ask the NP if she can get it for me....thanks for showing that to me.

I just recently ordered Ashwaganda bc its supposed to help support the HPA - we'll see..

i am happy you are doing so much better! That must have been an absolute nightmare. thanks for sharing your story and good luck on your new career. Going into nursing is one of my goals once I leave Chicago. I guess once you have health issues, medical stuff becomes your life so it only makes sense that you'd want to go into it...

### cantweight — 2013-04-24T21:17:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-84753>

That supplement is amazing!! You can order it online. I order from [www.pureformulas.com](http://www.pureformulas.com) have found them to be well priced

The Igenex test can run 6-800 depending on what you run. You can have your doc try quest or labcorp first and see if anything comes up positive we have had them work for us plenty of times. Also MDL labs are cheaper and more sensitive.
I'm not sure where you are but I have an incredible Lyme doc in Northern VA. People travel from all over the country to see her. She is up there with Dr Kruse in brilliance....that is not a term I lightly or frequently when it comes to docs

Hello! I finally got into see a lyme doc (took a year to get in!). Took igenex and some tests for coinfections and I apparently have lyme, mycoplasma, anaplasma , and she suspects Bartella. I was prescribed rifampin and azuthromycin (to start. We will apparently cycle in other antibiotics and some natural support). she thinks it will take anywhere from 3 months to 24 months or treat. I'm a bit scared but you saying you beat it gives me hope. Can you tell me how you were treated and perhaps things to look out for? I just started the meds today and so far I am just a little tired. I know I will have to support detox and avoid alcohol. Any advice would be greatly appreciated

### cantweight — 2014-03-17T01:46:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-115876>

Im glad you are finally getting some answers. Its a bit of a battle to get all that crap out but so worth it. Be sure to eat strict epi paleo and be good to your gut as the abx are tough. Probiotics are helpful, and liver support like milk thistle. Definitely steer clear of alcohol while your liver is busy dealing with this mess. I also like sun chlorella, welchol, and/or activated charcoal as a binder.

**Lots of water and all the sunlight and grounding you can get.** I cant stress that enough. Dont take it lightly.

Its a bit of a crooked path lyme and co force us to take....on one hand we have to eradicate these bugs by some of the worst means possible and on the other we have to support the well being of our body and brain to the best of our ability. The stronger you can make your body (think redox potential) the swifter your recovery will be and the stronger you will stay in the wake of these meds.

CT if you can but at the very least get your self outdoors. Fresh air, feet in the grass, sun on as much skin as you can bare. Eat clean, avoid NNEMF and blue light, sleep well.

Feel free to PM me and I wish you the very best

### nuttmegs17 — 2014-03-17T20:17:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-115974>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=115876)
>
> Im glad you are finally getting some answers. Its a bit of a battle to get all that crap out but so worth it. Be sure to eat strict epi paleo and be good to your gut as the abx are tough. Probiotics are helpful, and liver support like milk thistle. Definitely steer clear of alcohol while your liver is busy dealing with this mess. I also like sun chlorella, welchol, and/or activated charcoal as a binder.
>
> **Lots of water and all the sunlight and grounding you can get.** I cant stress that enough. Dont take it lightly.
>
> Its a bit of a crooked path lyme and co force us to take....on one hand we have to eradicate these bugs by some of the worst means possible and on the other we have to support the well being of our body and brain to the best of our ability. The stronger you can make your body (think redox potential) the swifter your recovery will be and the stronger you will stay in the wake of these meds.
>
> CT if you can but at the very least get your self outdoors. Fresh air, feet in the grass, sun on as much skin as you can bare. Eat clean, avoid NNEMF and blue light, sleep well.
>
> Feel free to PM me and I wish you the very best

Thank you!! I need to work on grounding (Chicago winters drive me indoors) but everything else is on point. What was your testament and how long did it last? Is it normal for me to bee this tired after rifampin? Or is that just herx? Had to call into work this morning due to the drowsiness ..

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-17T20:22:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-115977>

put up a pic soon, new rules.

### cantweight — 2014-03-17T21:45:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-116015>

> [nuttmegs17 said:](/goto/post?id=115974)
>
> Thank you!! I need to work on grounding (Chicago winters drive me indoors) but everything else is on point. What was your testament and how long did it last? Is it normal for me to bee this tired after rifampin? Or is that just herx? Had to call into work this morning due to the drowsiness ..

It took me about 18 months before I felt like I had kicked some serious ass. But there was marked improvement after about 6 months. Remember I was coming from a really bad place...you will probably see a much more swift recovery
I did not know then what I know now and could have really shortened that time for myself by addressing field, and adding in a bunch of DrK's protocols.

I have a practically bulletproof immune system now.....takes a perfect storm of crap for me to get sick now where as before I was at the point I had to avoid public places from Sept-April or I would catch something constantly. Same with my kiddies from fall -spring a non stop rollercoaster of ear infections, sinus infections, strep throat, and colds. This year we made in until March and one finally went down with a sinus infection but he is having other health issues that have put him in a weakened state. So there is a great level of health to be regained and you will be there before you know it

I found the whole treatment process exhausting to my body and tried to get as much rest and sleep as possible. Just be good to your body and give it all the support you can. Try to sweat and detoxify...lymphatic massage would be awesome as well as sauna. Hydrate like mad. It may get worse before it gets better, but it is so worth it!

### cantweight — 2014-03-18T00:49:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-116056>

> [nuttmegs17 said:](/goto/post?id=116044)
>
> Thank you! I hope you are right about the swift recovery part. Any excuse for a massage sounds good to me . Did you experience herx.? Where you on antibiotics? So far, does my treatment sound about right?

Yeah I herxed...bad. I had been infected for about 17 years. I did not have a good doc when I first started treatment. Was on doxy, zith, and mepron. High doses. The support for the rest of my body was lacking and I paid dearly. My second doc went in baby steps and ran every possible lab under the sun to be sure we were addressing everything and not hyper focused on lyme....what a difference that made! She did use abx but more carefully. And did not let me herx too hard. Impossible to say what treatment is right or wrong when so many respond so differently. If I had to go back and do it again I would still use prescription drugs including abx , I would have grounded, tanned/sunlight, hydrated with quality water, and addressed my field. That would have made a huge difference I believe

If at any point you feel too bad call your doc and try backing down meds or upping support. The body does not need more stress...be good to it and it will be good to you.

how can you tell the difference between herx and maybe being on a bad antibiotic?

i started rifapim and azithromycin on Sunday and since then have felt hot off and on and with a sore throat. I know for a fact that the rifapim was making me incredibly tired, so I scaled back from 2x a day to 1 a day (with the thought of increasing next week). Of course, I could have just caught a bug as well so who knows. i left a voicemail with the Dr. but can't tell if this is a bad reaction and maybe I should give it another few days or if this is a sign that I need a different med (s).

thoughts? thanks!

I honestly dont know how to tell the difference...any prescription you take should have a list of side effects to watch for such as rash or whatever. Be mindful of those things.
When I was killing off those bacteria it felt like there was a war going on in my body and often left me in fetal position with a heating pad.
Just keep well hydrated with ice cold water, rest, and support your body as best you can. The body will naturally raise its temperature to fight off infection. Maybe you can hit a sauna and get a good sweat on? Then follow with a cool epsom salt bath to detox. Be sure to rinse after....that always seems like silly advice but I didnt know to do that for the longest time

### KristinP — 2017-04-04T17:51:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme.6830/post-211731>

We discovered the cause of my son's chronic health issues for the past 11 years is borrelia. He is 17 years old now and has been too sick the last two years to do much high school. He is in a state of auto-immunity. In an almost constant healing crisis. Dr. Kruse, I read your article on Lyme and part of the suggested treatment being 3 weeks of antibiotics, however we've discovered that my son has a systemic, putrefying yeast sitting on his heart valves that is causing damage. The yeast I was told feeds off of proteins, not sugar. He likely got it in utero. Antibiotics I'm afraid would make the yeast worst. He is on a paleo diet however we were told that he should "cheat" two days a week to not overwhelm his system with protein. The ND we are working with used a thermograph scan to diagnose these things. The igenex lab testing came back negative however on dr. Horowitz questionnaire he scores a 90. We are confident with his history of tick bites that he has the bacteria for Lyme disease. However the yeast situation complicates things. He is extremely thin and the main symptom since he was six-years-old has been nausea, when he eats anything at all. We've tried introducing light therapy but almost everything even in small amounts seems to exacerbate his symptoms. Any advice on getting him to a stable place where we can effectively treat the bacteria and yeast?
