# Thread: Regaining LS - Starting at 51% body fat and need advice?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trinity — 2013-04-21T15:49:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/post-84571>

Hi All,

I have really cleaned up my diet over the past 3-6 months and have been doing CT for 30 minutes nearly every day for the past 3 months, but I have not done the official leptin reset. I recently realized that I am probably \*severely\* leptin resistant and need to get even more serious about my diet and meal timings. My doctor recently drew a leptin level and it was almost off the chart, even using the obese reference range. At 5'5" and 205 pounds, it goes without saying that I'm obese... But the icing on the cake was when I did the DEXA body composition scan. My body fat was measured at 51%. That makes me \*super\* obese (worse than morbidly obese)! I knew it was bad, but I had no idea it was \*that\* bad. Like most obese people I have tried to loose weight, but found it next to impossible. Willpower was never the biggest issue... It was the fact that I have always felt \*horrible\* a few weeks into a diet with crashing mood, energy, and sleep... And even if I kept up with the diet (which I did for up to 6 months several times in spite of feeling like crap), I could never loose more than 5 pounds even after 6 months of diet and exercise. For the past year or so, I have given up dieting and decided that I'd rather be fat and feel good (or at least as good as possible since I don't actually feel \*good\* most of the time) than thin and feeling like crap. That is not to say I have done any bee-lines to the fast food joint or junk food isle. I haven't eaten that crap in years, but I somehow still managed to put on another 15 pounds over the last year eating a reasonably healthy (albeit not paleo until recently) low sugar diet. After reading a lot of Dr. K's leptin articles, a lot of things started to make sense and I am starting to realize that at 51% fat I will never really feel good or be healthy. And in fact, my obesity may be contributing to the chronic cortisol problems I've been battling for several years now.

Recently I started taking cycloset. I dislike taking drugs, but after reading Dr. K's articles and learning more about cycloset I realized that it might make a big difference for me where nothing else has worked. I've only been taking it for about 2 weeks, but I'm already noticing a decrease in appetite and am down about 2.5lbs on the scale. My fasting sugar has also dropped from the 100-110 range down to high 90's. I have now decided to support the cycloset by also doing the leptin reset protocol. I am wondering if anyone at my level of obesity has had success becoming leptin sensitive? In the Leptin Reset protocol, Dr. K mentions that it can take 6-8 weeks. Due to my obesity I expect it to take longer, but I am also wondering if it is possible to be obese and leptin sensitive. I suspect that it is not, but I also realize that it is probably going to literally take \*years\* for me to get my bodyfat down to a non obese level. Based on my past history, I do not expect a quick and dramatic weight loss. Does that mean I have to do the Leptin Reset for years before I even have a chance to become leptin sensitive? If so, that is pretty darn discouraging.... Not that I ever plan to go back to the SAD diet, but I have not done well with ketogenic diets in the past as they have led to especially severe mood, energy, and sleep disturbances. Right now I would estimate I'm at around 100 grams of low glycemic carbs per day. I realize I probably need to drop that for the Leptin Reset. I am wondering if dropping to around 50grams per day would be more sustainable for me if I have to do this for years as opposed to going all the way ketogenic and if I could still get benefits from the reset at 50grams of carbs per day. I would love to hear from anyone who has been obese and had success with the Leptin Reset.

Thanks,
Trinity

### Jude — 2013-04-22T07:30:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/post-84603>

Hi Trinity, Have been following JK for almost 2yrs and have a few absolutes that I pass on to those interested.
Before you get stuck into epi paleo, try to
1\. Ensure you only drink pure water,approx 3L per day..(reverse osmosis, spring..no fluoride.)
2\. Try to reduce the EMF's in your home and especially in your bedroom at night.
3\. No artificial lights at night.

I won't comment about the foood side ...lots of folk here better at that than me.
All the best.

### agatha — 2013-04-22T23:00:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/post-84636>

Hi Trinity
I don't have answers to your questions but my heart goes out to you when I hear of your struggles. I really hope the reset goes well for you - keep us posted as you progress.

### Trinity — 2013-04-23T00:00:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/post-84638>

Thanks guys,

I drink a decent amount of water and try to stick with bottled water rather than tap water. I have also been wearing blue blockers at night and have installed yellow light bulbs in my bedroom lamps (got them from lowbluelights.com). I haven't really investigated the EMF thing much yet, but I am around computers at work pretty much all day. There is so much to research  Currently, my focus is upon leptin rx and CT.

\--Trinity

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-23T00:33:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/post-84639>

Hi Trinity and welcome!

There are others here working a similar story - Quelsen is one - he doesn't post often...but occasionally he stops by! The Leptin Reset will help you bring things together. the Leptin Rest helps you focus on circadian cycles with meal timing. as far as how long it will take it depends on where you're at today...

ask questions, there are lots of great people here who will help you get on track.. I will say - consider trying getting in to ketosis again - but read this first [http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/](http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/)

good luck and let us here help you on your way to optimal health!

# Thread: Anxiety Gone Because of Leptin Rx
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-gone-because-of-leptin-rx.6836/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Christopher — 2013-04-21T19:29:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-gone-because-of-leptin-rx.6836/post-84579>

I've struggled with anxiety and panic attacks for most of my life. In my late twenties, I was diagnosed with Asperger's Syndrome (Autism) I've been overweight most of my life as well. I've tried numerous times to get healthier by making changes to my diet. Nothing ever worked very well or had lasting results. In fact, the last thing I tried was the ABs diet. That diet had me eating every 2-3 hours with lots of fruit, whole-grain, nuts, and lean meat. I was addicted to the food. When I would eat, I would feel really good for about an hour or two. But, then I would crash. I mean like flipping a light switch off. I would go from a great mood to feeling like the world was ending, and the only cure seemed to be to eat more carbs. I would also self-medicate often with alcohol, marijuana, and occasionally narcotic pain killers or muscle relaxers. Only problem was the marijuana buzz was often ruined by panic attacks, and alcohol was quickly loosing any perceived benefit as well ( in retrospect, I think it was the water in the micro-brew beers that had any positive effect on my mood until the alcohol dehydrated me anyway). Thankfully I knew better than to lean on the rx drugs. I was miserable.

My two children are also on the autism spectrum, and my wife was doing a lot of research on various treatments for autism. I remember reading a couple articles about autism and gluten. Around the same time, I saw one of my FB friends post about being on a paleo diet. I started reading about paleo, and was convinced it was the solution. So about 9 months ago, I jumped right in and cut grains cold turkey. I had a horrible time. My stomach hurt, my chest hurt, I felt like I was dying. I didn't really know what or how to eat, but I was conviced that I should stay paleo. I was desperate enough before trying this, that the thought that the paleo diet might kill me was ok. I knew I could not go back to my previous lifestyle.

After two weeks of thinking I was going to die from paleo, I came across some forum posts about Jack Kruse. Most of the posters, indicated that Jack was crazy, but I was desperate enough to give crazy a try. I came to this website, and decided to give the Leptin Rx a chance. And I'll tell you, that very first day the BAB saved my life. The day before I was having a hard time eating, because of the stomach and chest pain and light-headedness. But that BAB wend down so easy and I immediately felt great. It was amazing. I felt so full of energy, and there was no longer any pain in my stomach or chest. I was also surprised that going from six meals to three was easy, a relief actually. Also, the carb cravings stopped instantly. I used to have major carb cravings in the evening. Sometimes I would lay down for bed, and feel a panic attack coming, and have to get up and eat some cereal to calm down. But now at night, I felt perfectly calm. No carbs, no problem. I would also say before I was a little afraid of the dark, and used to keep a lot of night lights on. But after the Leptin Rx, I preferred the darkness. In fact, I found the dark to be comforting, almost like the feeling of a soft blanket around me.

I should also add that from the beginning of my experiment with paleo, I lost a total of 50 lbs in a fairly short time. That was not my main goal, but no complaints. I'm skinny now. I have no physical symptoms of anxiety at all. I have no food cravings (except sometimes I crave the coconut mana, lol). I don't feel hungry, ever. And most importantly, my mood is stable, no more feeling like the world is ending out of nowhere. I am not miserable any longer! I don't even think twice about using any kind of drugs, and I'd much rather drink spring water than beer.

I do have better energy. I have more mental clarity. I feel now like how I imagined "normal" people feel. I am excited every morning when I wake up. I always look forward to breakfast. I don't feel hungry or like I have to eat it, I just plain enjoy eating it. Sometimes I impress myself with how much meat and fat I can eat for breakfast. If I eat too much, I do wake up drenched in sweat, though. lol.

The straight truths laid out here on this website have been such a blessing. I feel like I've been given a second chance at life. I also look forward to the future, as I know that the more I follow Jack's protocols, the better things will get.

I wanted to share this hear so that others who are skeptical may be encouraged to go ahead and give the Leptin Rx a try. In my case, the Leptin Rx with a keto-genic epi-paleo diet was the perfect cure for my anxiety, depression, and symptoms of reactive hypoglycemia.

I hope you try eating seafood regularly, like daily.

# Thread: My Smartphone Gave Me Hemorrhoids!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Christopher — 2013-04-22T03:18:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/post-84592>

To be fair, I must admit that it is not uncommon for me to have a hemorrhoid problem in the Winter time. I face a lot of stress at work around Christmas (retail), and I now realize was is the time of year that I was farthest of from circadian timing. Now the difference is that normally when my hemorrhoids would get inflamed, it would be for only about 7-10 days, then it would go away. Well this year my hemorrhoids were inflamed for probably around 6 weeks straight, with varying intensity. This was like nothing I had ever experienced before. I was especially shocked because I was eating Epi-paleo and doing the Leptin Rx and avoiding artificial light after sunset as much as possible (I often had to work til 9pm but would wear yellow glasses). I figured what I was doing should be lowering inflammation and should prevent a hemorrhoid problem.

I was very frustrated with this problem, and was beginning to think my diet was to blame. Ever since I started eating paleo (July last year) I had been often constipated with occasional bouts of diarrhea. I started squatting while having a bm thinking that would help, but to no avail. I was thinking of giving up, but then miraculously the hemorrhoids went away. I didn't know why at the time, but man, it was a relief.

Now here's what I think happened. I know correlation is not the same as causation, but the coincidence was too obvious for me to ignore after reading the EMF blogs.

This past November, black Friday to be exact, I became a first time owner of an Android smartphone. Previous to this I had a Nextel flip phone that was kind of embarrassing to use, so I didn't use it much and didn't keep it in my pocket much either. But, I was very proud of this new Android phone and kept it in my pocket all the time. I even re-arranged my pockets so I could comfortably keep it in my pocket while working on cars. You know, just in case I needed the flashlight app or something.

At first, I would keep the wi-fi on so I could get FB, email, and weather updates often. But I started to become frustrated with the battery life. I mean this was a new phone, and it needed charging more than my old Nextel, lol. So I started turning off things to save battery. I kept turning off the wi-fi, but every time I looked at the phone, the stupid wi-fi was back on. I messed around with battery saver apps for a while, but the wi-fi was always turning itself on. After several weeks, I figured out how to keep the wifi off, I also figured out how to turn off the 3G (mobile data). So as an experiment I turned both off to see how long the battery would go. And since I wasn't using any internet apps on the phone, I din't really see a need to keep it in my pocket anymore. The result of that experiment was that the battery only needed to be charged once a week instead of the at least once a day it previously required. I've always been a sucker for efficiency so I never turned the wi-fi or mobile data back on.

This experiment correlated exactly with the time that my hemorrhoids cleared up. It was also around that time that I was reading the EMF blogs. I remember it was in the shower one morning that I put it all together. You should have seen me after that shower, running around turning off wi-fi, putting phones on airplane mode, etc. lol. But since then, I thankfully have not had any hemorrhoid issues. I have had some constipation and straining since then that I thought would for sure cause the old roids to flair up, but to this day it hasn't happened.

I got rid of that phone shortly after all this and now went to a pay-as-you-go flip phone that I will only turn on in an emergency. I disabled all wi-fi devices in my home, and got rid of my cordless phones and went to wired. Also somewhere in the middle of this whole ordeal, I got rid of my flat screen television and sound bar that had a wireless subwoofer and had switched my computer to a wired connection for unrelated reasons.

Was it all a coincidence? I will never put a wireless device in my pocket again, so the world may never know. I would rather error on the side of caution in this case.

### Inger — 2013-04-22T06:56:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/post-84599>

Christopher, thank you so much for sharing this.
This shows what Jack means when he says, food does not matter when EMFs are high. He got a lot of folks attacking him for saying that. But here you go.. it does not help to eat the healthiest food if under constant EMF flow.
I absolutely believe it is the smart phone causing your hemorrhoids to act up. I have wired internet and computer and they have quite a little EMFs but still I feel not good when using it too much. I feel the difference so big, sitting in front of the screen in comparison to in front of the sun. Wow. It really stunned me as I one morning went from staring at the computer screen to looking into the sunrise. The difference really hit me hard.... and I started to realize what that does to our biology.... and I was so glad Jack has finally got some rest from it, at least partially. I so understand him.. he has really deserved it, he if any.

I am so glad you are on the Forum Christopher. You and your dear wife. So beautiful you do this together, so inspiring!

### agatha — 2013-04-22T14:55:54+0300

Crazy and fascinating stories. Thanks both for sharing. it will be interesting to see what happens going forward for both of you.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-22T16:20:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/post-84619>

> [IvanDrago said:](/goto/post?id=84609)
>
> I normally would never comment to share an embarrassing anecdote, but reading this thread has had me connecting some dots that relate to my own experience. I moved back to the city in early January and have been working sporadically and taking courses to upgrade my GPA. This was around the time the EMF series began and I have been doing everything within my power to manage my exposure.
>
> Exactly three weeks ago, I had a research paper due on the last day of the winter semester. Unfortunately, there were no computers available on the main floor so I was forced to head upstairs and finish the paper on the third floor. The computers at my urban University are all Wi-Fi enabled and when I checked, there were approximately fifty(!) wireless networks available. In addition to this, the crowded environment is undoubtedly flooded with wireless signals from various cell phones and other electronic devices. This was the first time I had ventured upstairs for any extended period for any reason in the past four months. I spent the entire day in front of the computer finishing my paper and the following day I woke up with a hemmorhoid! I have had this problem before at a much younger age, but this was both unexpected and bizarre. I only made this connection having read the opening post. So, thank you Christopher for elucidating the root causal factor at play here!
>
> Fortunately, the issue resolved itself within one week after treating it topically with liquid iodine. That being said, I also recall being thirstier than usual on that day and developing a mild headache. In addition, I recall having to urinate excessively that night. If anybody in the lay public were to read this reply, I have no doubt in my mind that I would be painted as a Larry David-esque hypochondriac of the highest order, but the sudden and unexpected recurrence of a painful condition actually did occur (it was a nuisance more than anything). One particular facet of my behavior preceding the emergence of this condition was radically different than it had been during the previous four months. Care to guess what that may have been? My city has been systematically flooded with radiation during the past calendar year, and I have the news reports to back this up. The advent of FibreOp has not been criticized, but celebrated unabashedly.
>
> Furthermore, I have had to go upstairs in buildings transiently during the past four months to deliver items for my job and write an exam. Having spent the past five years at the same institution, I am familiar with virtually all of the individuals who work in these office environments on a personal level. What I have noticed within the past year is nothing short of amazing. In one office, all six of the employees have appeared to gain significant amounts of weight and have developed apparent skin issues (i.e., dry skin). I have also noticed a change in organization. **What was previously a tidy workplace environment has become more disorganized, cluttered, and messy.** On the desks of the employees are more sugary snack foods (e.g., Haloween candy) than I have noticed in the past four years. In addition to the snacks, there are multivitamins, significantly more bottled water (this is _very_ interesting), and anti-inflammatory drugs such as Tylenol and Advil. From my limited perspective, there is now pronounced tension between employees and nobody seems as friendly as they have in the past.

Great testimony, and excellent observation about working environments..

So I have a question - I'm not a \*Neat\* person - I actually am quite good at ignoring clutter/messes. But I have noticed more of a desire to keep things tidy - also my urge to purge (which usually only hits one or 2x/year) has been constant since I started focusing on limiting EMF... This weekend I cleaned and painted one wall in the kitchen and the hallway upstairs... I also continued to work on my garden in the front of the house... I'm not sure if what's going on is the result of mitigating EMF, entering the PPP and staying there... or a combination of the both... I also read 4 books this weekend...

Suddenly I'm able to do things that I never would have had the time for... is this because I'm now aware of what I'm capable of... or is it I'm now aware of what's been limiting my possibilities??? regardless... ### Christopher — 2013-04-22T18:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/post-84627>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=84620)
>
> Thank you, both Christopher and Ivan, for this information. Sure wish DH could get rid of his work Android phone. Christopher, how did you finally get the wireless and 3G part turned off?

I had to go somewhere in the settings menu and un-check the box for wireless network optimizer. That stopped the wifi from turning itself on. Then I unchecked the box for enable mobile data to disable the 3G.

I also noticed when I was using the phone as a GPS in the car (3G had to be on for GPS to work), I would become fatigued and irritable after just a short time of driving. I remember one day getting home feeling just wiped out after hours of driving with the GPS and 3G on. I was experimenting with earthing at the time and tried to earth with a wire plugged into the electrical outlet ground. That did nothing to improve my energy. I then took a 20 minute nap, also no improvement. Then I drank a couple glasses of water. Within 10 minutes of drinking the water, I felt much better. It wasn't even good water, just plain old tap water (flouridated as far as I know). Needless to say, I drink 1 1/2 to 2 gallons of spring water daily now. Since then, I noticed that I don't feel fatigued after driving, with the phone off or in airplane mode.

### IceBarbie — 2013-04-22T20:58:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/post-84633>

Or you have an adrenal issue. You mentioned your hemmorhoids show up when you're under stress. Here is a snippet from a wonderful article about the Adrenal Glands that mentions the connection between adrenals and hemmorhoids:

"Hemmorhoids are another problem which is associated with blood pooling in the abdomen. A hemorrhoid is a vein which has pushed its way (or been pushed) to bulge outside the anal sphincter. The anal sphincter then becomes very tight, and the hemorrhoid, with the pressure of the abdominal and pelvic blood above it, and gravity pulling from below it, cannot get back inside the rectum. Treatment of hemorrhoids must be directed at two areas. First, the hemorrhoids must be treated locally, and second, the source of intra-abdominal and intra-pelvic pooling of blood must be corrected.

Most commonly, the cause of the blood pooling in the abdomen and pelvis is from hypoadrenia. But severe liver congestion can also cause portal hypertension and result in hemorrhoids. You must differentiate between liver and adrenal problems as the underlying cause of the hemorrhoids. For this reason, whenever a person complains of hemorrhoids, one of the first things to be done is listen to the heart. The person does not understand this, but you are listening for the relative loudness of the second sounds at the pulmonic valve area (for the adrenals) and the tricuspid valve area (for the liver).

In treating hemorrhoids locally, it is necessary to recognize that the hemorrhoidal veins can not get themselves back inside the rectum, due to the tightness of the anal sphincter. In order to treat the hemorrhoids successfully, the anal sphincter must be dilated. This is done by putting on a glove (not a finger cot) and, using a lubricant, inserting your index finger into the rectum. Stretch the anal sphincter with the index finger. Then insert the first two fingers and stretch the sphincter. Then insert three fingers and insert them slowly about as fat as they will reach. Finally (if the person is still on the table) insert all four fingers, stretching the sphincter up to the point which the size of the patient will tolerate.

This is a difficult procedure for the patient, but there will be an immediate and dramatic reduction in the extent of the hemorrhoids protruding through the anus. If done correctly, this procedure may only have to be performed once. Then again, you may only get one chance! Occasionally it is necessary to repeat the sphincter dilation once or twice in the future."

[http://www.tuberose.com/Adrenal\_Glands.html](http://www.tuberose.com/Adrenal_Glands.html)

I'm just sayin' that people have had hemmorhoids a lot longer than we've had Smart Phones.

### BobBrooks — 2013-06-22T12:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-smartphone-gave-me-hemorrhoids.6839/post-88651>

> [Christopher said:](/goto/post?id=84592)
>
> To be fair, I must admit that it is not uncommon for me to have a hemorrhoid problem in the Winter time. I face a lot of stress at work around Christmas (retail), and I now realize was is the time of year that I was farthest of from circadian timing. Now the difference is that normally when my hemorrhoids would get inflamed, it would be for only about 7-10 days, then it would go away. Well this year my hemorrhoids were inflamed for probably around 6 weeks straight, with varying intensity. This was like nothing I had ever experienced before. I was especially shocked because I was eating Epi-paleo and doing the Leptin Rx and avoiding artificial light after sunset as much as possible (I often had to work til 9pm but would wear yellow glasses). I figured what I was doing should be lowering inflammation and should prevent a hemorrhoid problem.
>
> I was very frustrated with this problem, and was beginning to think my diet was to blame. Ever since I started eating paleo (July last year) I had been often constipated with occasional bouts of diarrhea. I started squatting while having a bm thinking that would help, but to no avail. I was thinking of giving up, but then miraculously the hemorrhoids went away. I didn't know why at the time, but man, it was a relief.
>
> Now here's what I think happened. I know correlation is not the same as causation, but the coincidence was too obvious for me to ignore after reading the EMF blogs.
>
> This past November, black Friday to be exact, I became a first time owner of an Android smartphone. Previous to this I had a Nextel flip phone that was kind of embarrassing to use, so I didn't use it much and didn't keep it in my pocket much either. But, I was very proud of this new Android phone and kept it in my pocket all the time. I even re-arranged my pockets so I could comfortably keep it in my pocket while working on cars. You know, just in case I needed the [flashlights](http://www.robustbuy.com/led-lighting-gadgets-led-flashlights-c-505_1027_730.html) app or something.
>
> At first, I would keep the wi-fi on so I could get FB, email, and weather updates often. But I started to become frustrated with the battery life. I mean this was a new phone, and it needed charging more than my old Nextel, lol. So I started turning off things to save battery. I kept turning off the wi-fi, but every time I looked at the phone, the stupid wi-fi was back on. I messed around with battery saver apps for a while, but the wi-fi was always turning itself on. After several weeks, I figured out how to keep the wifi off, I also figured out how to turn off the 3G (mobile data). So as an experiment I turned both off to see how long the battery would go. And since I wasn't using any internet apps on the phone, I din't really see a need to keep it in my pocket anymore. The result of that experiment was that the battery only needed to be charged once a week instead of the at least once a day it previously required. I've always been a sucker for efficiency so I never turned the wi-fi or mobile data back on.
>
> This experiment correlated exactly with the time that my hemorrhoids cleared up. It was also around that time that I was reading the EMF blogs. I remember it was in the shower one morning that I put it all together. You should have seen me after that shower, running around turning off wi-fi, putting phones on airplane mode, etc. lol. But since then, I thankfully have not had any hemorrhoid issues. I have had some constipation and straining since then that I thought would for sure cause the old roids to flair up, but to this day it hasn't happened.
>
> I got rid of that phone shortly after all this and now went to a pay-as-you-go flip phone that I will only turn on in an emergency. I disabled all wi-fi devices in my home, and got rid of my cordless phones and went to wired. Also somewhere in the middle of this whole ordeal, I got rid of my flat screen television and sound bar that had a wireless subwoofer and had switched my computer to a wired connection for unrelated reasons.
>
> Was it all a coincidence? I will never put a wireless device in my pocket again, so the world may never know. I would rather error on the side of caution in this case.

Sorry to hear this.. Yes it is very rare problem in winters and I do have similar minute problem in summers... Hope you get well soon

# Thread: The Effects of ELF-EMFs on Collagen
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effects-of-elf-emfs-on-collagen.6841/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2013-04-22T13:11:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effects-of-elf-emfs-on-collagen.6841/post-84608>

I came across this study about EMFs and collagen which I thought was pretty interesting

**Suppression of type I collagen in human scleral fibroblasts treated with extremely low-frequency electromagnetic fields**

**Purpose**: To investigate the expression differences of type I collagen (COL1A1) and its underlying mechanisms in human fetal scleral fibroblasts (HFSFs) that were treated with conditioned medium from retinal pigment epithelial (RPE) cells under extremely low-frequency electromagnetic fields (ELF-EMFs).

**Methods**: The ELF-EMFs used in this study were established by slidac and artificial coils. Growth of the treated HFSFs was evaluated by a cell-counting kit-8 assay. The expression of COL1A1 and matrix metalloproteinases-2 (MMP-2) in the treated HFSFs was detected by reverse transcription PCR (RT-PCR) and western blot, and the expression of transforming growth factor-β2 (TGF-β2) and basic fibroblast growth factor-2 (FGF-2) in RPE cells exposed to EMFs was detected by RT-PCR. The expression of COL1A1 and MMP-2 in HFSFs was further confirmed by immunofluorescence staining. Activation of extracellular signal-regulated kinase 1/2 (ERK1/2 also called p44/p42 mitogen-activated protein kinases \[MAPK\]) and p38 in HFSFs was measured by western blot.

**Results**: We found that exposure to ELF-EMFs resulted in a decreased proliferation rate of HFSFs and that addition of RPE supernatant medium could enhance this effect. Compared with that of the control cells, a significant decrease in collagen synthesis was detected in HFSFs under ELF-EMFs. However, the expression of MMP-2 was upregulated, which could be further enhanced via an RPE supernatant additive. The activities of ERK1/2 and p38 were significantly increased in HFSFs exposed to ELF-EMFs, and this effect could be enhanced by RPE supernatant medium additive.

**Conclusions**: Our results suggested that ELF-EMFs can inhibit the expression of type I collagen in HFSFs and contribute to the remodeling of the sclera.

Full paper can be found here: [http://www.molvis.org/molvis/v19/885/mv-v19-885.pdf](http://www.molvis.org/molvis/v19/885/mv-v19-885.pdf)

### cantweight — 2013-04-23T22:19:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-the-emf-series-is-getting-mainstream-support.6852/post-84701>

Wow...my 2 oldest were born into a different home than my 3rd.

With my 3rd I lived in our "smart neighborhood" wired to the hilt, EMF from hell house for his pregnancy and the first 7 yrs of his life. I had a huge (14 cm) peri ovarian cyst that grew throughout his pregnancy and hyper amniotic fluid. I measured 42 weeks at 38 weeks I thought my hips would break from the strain, but now I believe the extra fluid and the cyst were both mechanisms of protection to the fetus...anything to "pad" him from the invisible onslaught.

He was speech delayed, had tubes in his ears, suffered with lyme and babesia he most likely picked up inutero or via breastmilk. He has motor and vocal tics and a stutter. He is also the only one that is not thin. He's not even remotely obese but my older 2 are quite thin and Luke has a tendency to put on weight very easily. Crazy. Sad. I hate that I put them in that place for so long before realizing what was happening. I truly hope it is not too late to undo.

# Thread: Maca
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nuttmegs17 — 2013-04-23T20:03:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84694>

Hello,

I recently added maca to my morning supps, bc I read "the hormone cure" and it's suggested as a way to naturally balance hormones....however, I have been reading stories online and it appears that sometimes it actually results in...weight gain?!

I am curious about your experiences with it. Why would it make some gain weight and others lose it? Funnily enough, there are entire forums dedicated to "increasing boobs and butts" and many people on those forums swear that Maca works for that....I can tell you one thing, a bigger butt is the last thing I need!

My boobs have felt a little bigger this week. I am on day 18 of my cycle, but its highly irregular so I am unsure if its the maca (If i end up having a 36 day cycle this month, then its too early to be PMS), or if its just PMS (if I end up having say a 25 day cycle). So I figured Id throw it out there.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-23T21:27:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84697>

MACA = higher progesterone.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-04-23T22:10:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84700>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=84697)
>
> MACA = higher progesterone.

powerful stuff - thats how i responded to progesterone after a bit as well so that kind of explains it. I might have to back off until i add in estrogen to my progesterone in hopes its a better mix for me. As bad as birth control is in general, i felt best when I was on it. I know it messed me up so wont go back, but am hoping BHRT will balance me out like the pill once did.

### tweety — 2013-04-24T00:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84707>

nuttmegs- I love maca. I did not experience the response with maca that I did with progesterone. It really helps my libido... but I didn't feel like it was helping my progesterone based on other symptoms.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-04-24T00:32:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84708>

> [tweety said:](/goto/post?id=84707)
>
> nuttmegs- I love maca. I did not experience the response with maca that I did with progesterone. It really helps my libido... but I didn't feel like it was helping my progesterone based on other symptoms.

Thanks, Tweety! Yes, part of me wonders if I feel bigger bc perhaps its already helping my cycle? THerefore, maybe this is a shorter cycle and therefore not weightgain per se but just PMS? I will keep an eye on it. It seems like it has such promise so I hate to throw in the towel with it.

How much do you take?

### tweety — 2013-04-24T01:30:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84710>

I have been journaling and there were several days I was fine and felt good. The last two I felt so huge, and can see a physical difference. Well, I was hungry while prepping lunch and popped a few hazelnuts and brazil nuts into my mouth. 24hrs later, bloated all over. So, note to self, you know nuts make you retain water- quit eating them!

I take the powdered form of maca, and after breakfast I take a spoonful of coconut cream, a tablespoon of maca, and add a little water until it's pudding consistency and eat. Remember to take a break from maca while you have your period. I noticed almost immediately a change in libido. No other changes thata I can attribute to maca specifically, but that was a big concern for me so I am happy!

### tweety — 2013-04-24T01:31:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84711>

By the way, as I understand it, maca is an adaptogen. It doesn't just increase progesterone, but all the hormones. But it does so in a way your body needs it. I always assumed it was helping my estrogen and testosterone levels more than progesterone.

### Marco — 2013-04-24T03:33:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84717>

Dr Kruse - does the progesterone connection mean men should avoid maca?

Or like Tweety says, is there more to the story than just progesterone?

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-04-24T05:27:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84720>

> [Marco said:](/goto/post?id=84717)
>
> Dr Kruse - does the progesterone connection mean men should avoid maca?
>
> Or like Tweety says, is there more to the story than just progesterone?

From what I understand men use progesterone too. It supports testosterone. You are lucky though bc you don't have to deal with monthly fluctuations . If that is the case then maca would support testosterone indirectly

### nath5212 — 2013-04-25T08:41:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-84774>

> [tweety said:](/goto/post?id=84710)
>
> I have been journaling and there were several days I was fine and felt good. The last two I felt so huge, and can see a physical difference. Well, I was hungry while prepping lunch and popped a few hazelnuts and brazil nuts into my mouth. 24hrs later, bloated all over. So, note to self, you know nuts make you retain water- quit eating them! I take the powdered form of [maca, and](http://www.biomaca.fr) after breakfast I take a spoonful of coconut cream, a tablespoon of maca, and add a little water until it's pudding consistency and eat. Remember to take a break from maca while you have your period. I noticed almost immediately a change in libido. No other changes thata I can attribute to maca specifically, but that was a big concern for me so I am happy!

I get the biggest boost of energy from my morning smoothie when I add a powdered herb from Peru called Maca. If you want increased energy, stamina and even an improved sex life, maybe you should consider adding this amazing food to your green smoothie as well.

### Samson — 2013-04-29T05:48:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-85082>

Is Navitas gelatanized Maca a good brand for Maca or is there another preferred brand?
Gelatinized Maca is known to be highly digestible and more soluble than regular maca. Due to gelatinization, the long chains of starches are broken down, enhancing the concentration of the maca’s powerful main ingredients.

This is the product description of the Navitas gelatanized Maca:

Navitas Naturals Gelatinized Maca Powder is a concentrated form of the Maca root’s beneficial elements; the fiber of the root is extracted making this superfood more digestible and its beneficial elements intensified. Navitas Naturals Organic Maca Powder is cultivated in its indigenous region of the Junin Plateau amongst the Peruvian Andes. After harvest, the maca roots are gelatinized and milled: a process that concentrates the active ingredients through a heated extrusion to remove the starch. This product has only one ingredient, maca root, and is kosher, gluten-free, vegan, and 100% organic.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-02T23:58:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maca.6855/post-85344>

>
> This is the product description of the Navitas gelatanized Maca:
>
> Navitas Naturals Gelatinized Maca Powder is a concentrated form of the Maca root’s beneficial elements; the fiber of the root is extracted making this superfood more digestible and its beneficial elements intensified. Navitas Naturals Organic Maca Powder is cultivated in its indigenous region of the Junin Plateau amongst the Peruvian Andes. After harvest, the maca roots are gelatinized and milled: a process that concentrates the active ingredients through a heated extrusion to remove the starch. This product has only one ingredient, maca root, and is kosher, gluten-free, vegan, and 100% organic.

I think its a good brand, its the one I bought and it was a decent price on amazon it tasted fine for what its worth. def stick to gelantanized

# Thread: The Road to Optimal and my Health Problems
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-road-to-optimal-and-my-health-problems.6862/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-04-24T18:12:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-road-to-optimal-and-my-health-problems.6862/post-84744>

Hey all! I'm new to these boards and I wanted to receive some feedback from everyone here. Firstly I wanted to tell everyone that I can tell you all are very dedicated, wise, and intelligent, as some of the threads I've been reading through are nothing short of amazing. I have been dwelling over at the Bulletproof Exec Forums for the past few weeks, and I've now landed myself here, and although I agree with most everything Dave Asprey presents, I believe the community over here (in the grand scale) has a bit more knowledge.

So, to make a long story short, I had some questions on starting my path to Optimal. I originally landed into the paleosphere through PaleoHacks, as I asked questions pertaining to my health/(possible)psychological problems, as well as that of my brother. My post on myself can be seen here: [http://paleohacks.com/questions/175...he-topic-of-my-bizarre-symptoms#axzz2ROLhaPzh](http://paleohacks.com/questions/175553/last-question-on-the-topic-of-my-bizarre-symptoms#axzz2ROLhaPzh) and my brother here: [http://paleohacks.com/questions/170915/help-me-cure-aid-my-younger-brother-please#axzz2ROLhaPzh;](http://paleohacks.com/questions/170915/help-me-cure-aid-my-younger-brother-please#axzz2ROLhaPzh;) as you can see Dr. K commented on my post (on myself) and told me to read EMF2, then another member of PaleoHacks and these forums commented.

For the past couple months (I'd estimate), I have been following a primarily **Bulletproof Diet**, all while IFing for 16-18, sometimes 20 hours a day (sometimes less, though). I know I wouldn't have been considered Bulletproof as I was and am still lacking variety in my diet, in terms of food choices, veggies, herbs, etc. and I still drink filthy water (tap and water bottles). Here's my caveat, when I study something, I go through with it like Jack and Dave, I dig DEEP. So far I have made my way through the first 5 or so posts in the Leptin Prescription by Jack, I have annotated them all, annotated all of the comments, formed this into a large word file, and wrote summaries based off of that information. I plan on doing the same with his Epi-Paleo book, as well as the Better Baby Book by Dave Asprey. Just yesterday I stocked up on a bunch of canned wild caught seafood at Trader Joe's because I'm starting to believe that a seafood (primarily shellfish) based diet might work even better than the typical Bulletproof diet (as it seems meat heavy). I've been thinking about incorporating the best of both - heavy intake of seafood/shellfish, raw when possible/tolerable (I can do this with clams quite easily), eating everything (organs and bones/cartilage of animals and fish) while incorporating Bulletproof Coffee/Tea, Upgraded Chocolate/Hot Chocolate, and cyclical ketogenic dieting in the longer light cycles rather than eating carbs everyday (CKD is part of the Bulletproof diet).

My questions pertain to my health/psychological problems, and what I should start doing now. Everything is stated in that PaleoHacks post, but the gist of it is I **accidentally ingested what I believed to be PCP** at a concert, which screwed with me big time mentally and physically - this fortunately lead me to make many wise decisions regarding health and spirituality. I developed migraines with intense auras, extreme anxiety, **visual distortions** that would occur everyday, **pressure build up in my head** at odd times, depressed feelings, less energy, and much more. This lead me to screw up one of my classes at my local college as I had great grades in all of my classes, then had a terrible night of sleep, woke up to my parents fighting violently about finances, which created massive anxiety and a terrible migraine, and I missed my final. I have been improving drastically since implementing the Bulletproof diet and mindfulness as a practice, **I now do Jiu-Jitsu** and hold a job and have much less anxiety and better overall energy, but many of the odd symptoms persist. Visual auras persist, my visual snow (which I have since childhood) persist, my eye floaters have been slightly more noticeable, my visual distortions persist (things get wavy/morph slightly - this has cooled down a lot but still is present), and some other odd sensations. I believe this is because **PCP is an anticholingeric**, which basically means I damaged and important part of my brain/processing for a long time, hence why I felt so terrible shortly after, persisting for almost 15 months now, although it has SLOWLY gotten better. By the way, before these past few months of Bulletproof/Paleo dieting, I was a vegetarian for probably 3 months, and \*almost\* vegan for a month after that, all while trying to chronically lose weight I didn't really need to lose. I'm not trying to get up to about 165lbs or more with ideal body comp (I'm about 5'9 and 140ish, I would estimate 12-15% body fat, I feel as though I'm still growing, though, as I have gotten a bit taller and things about me are changing more).

Should I just do the **Leptin Reset** I've been reading about, coupled with **CT**, then jump into the **Post-LeptinRx**? I was thinking that since I can IF very easily and go 16hrs without caring to eat, and have been doing a cyclical ketogenic diet for the past few weeks (and have been in ketosis before that for months on end), and since I have been following a Bulletproof Style diet, maybe I don't need the reset, but should jump into the Post-LeptinRx and stop fasting as maybe that is just stressing my adrenals. Maybe fast for shorter periods of time starting at night (10-14hrs), or a few days a week rather than 6-7. I have begun something called Foundation Training to enhance my flexibility for BJJ and aid in resetting my back (I have a disc bulge), and for the past few days I have been doing it out in the open field by my house, barefoot, to ground, while remaining in minimal clothing to receive all the sunlight and nice breeze. So **(1)** should I engage in the Leptin Reset for 4-6 or 8 weeks? Start on the Post Leptin Reset Rx? **(2)** On a VERY TIGHT budget, **should I switch to eating primarily shellfish over grass-fed beef**? Is it fine if it is canned as long as there are no additives and it is wild caught? Clams, Oyesters, and Canned Salmon are all cheaper than a pound of New Zealand Grass-Fed Ground Beef at Trader Joe's, I'm just frightened of the **BPA** and other **xenoestrogens**. (3) Should I just focus on diet and grounding before I start supplementing heavy? I've been thinking that supplementing is unwise outside of performance enhancement (whether sports or sex or anything), deficiency, or nootropics - I've just been taking **600mg of chelated magnesium glycinate** before bed the past couple weeks... **I stopped Vitamin D** after reading a lot of literature that showed it may be rather detrimental to Nuclear Receptors in the long term and a whole lot of other aspects of signalling. I was thinking about maybe taking Potassium with the Magnesium as Dave Asprey recommends that, but I heard it is worthless and calcium in fact enhances the absorption of Magnesium.

Well, I apologize for this lengthy post, but I just wanted to introduce and establish myself on these boards. I will definitely be seen around frequently haha. For anyone that has any information for me, I thank you in advance, believe me, it will be greatly appreciated and will be utilized, thanks again!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Will tanning cause freckles/brown spots?

### Eve — 2013-04-24T19:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/post-84746>

I want to start tanning. I've read the Sunshine blog numerous times, so I know my O6:O3 and vitD levels (at least 60) needs to be in check before I start and to do it cautiously, but I'm scared of getting freckles etc. I am a redhead with fair skin (no freckles) and I don't want any freckles or dark brown spots. My undertones are pinkish and tanning is difficult for me. I don't think my legs have ever been able to tan, LOL. When I was younger, my arms got most of the sun and now I have dark brown spots scattered on them and don't want anymore (Of course, this was pre-epi-paleo, etc).

Plus, my mom has a fair skin and she aged her skin by tanning outside. Of course, she wasn't epi-paleo etc at the time, but it's a big enough scare factor for me. I am 26 and when I am 40, I don't want to be kicking myself!

How are other fair-skinned people coming along?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/post-84748>

### nonchalant — 2013-04-25T01:24:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/post-84762>

I used to get freckles on my nose and cheeks when I was younger.
Not sure why, but I don't have any now. And I've got a pretty good tan already this year. I have had some freckles on my arms for years, however, and they are still there.

Has it been several months since you have eaten grain or soy-based fats? Proceed with caution. If it has been a year, you should be just fine.

Also, I had a couple "age spots" on my face, but those are almost completely gone. I am (or was) blonde, not redheaded. I used to burn within an hour of sun exposure, and not necessarily around noon. Now it is difficult for me to get a burn, and when I do (about once per year) I do not peel.

### Hope — 2013-04-25T01:42:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/post-84763>

This is exactly what is happening with me. I am red=haired, everything in me and on me is super sensitive. Only tanned three times so far for eight minutes each. Was in FL sun last week, a bit....have freckles on the tops of my shoulders. Too funny. Not worried about it at all. I don't mind them at all...but it's a personal thing. I love how the tanning makes me feel like someone gives me a shot of energy (free electrons)....I want to keep it up, in moderation. I know this doesn't help.....just my n-1

### Eve — 2013-07-22T20:38:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-tanning-cause-freckles-brown-spots.6864/post-90577>

Thanks for the replies.
I wanted to post an update...
In the Spring, I went for a couple of walks outside 20 min in the sun. My face turned pink/red for a few days (which I expected) but then it faded and for the first time in my life, I was left with light brown freckles under my eyes and under my eyebrows...funny.

Then I gathered the courage and started tanning in the tanning salon. First I did the stand-up booth....but all that left me was heat rash (not fun, lol) and burning in some parts of my body, no matter how I gauged the time. Then I switched to the lay down bed...better. After a few sessions, I can develop a tan. I can't do it everyday or else I burn...but every other day or so. The more I do it, the slowly I get more freckles. I have some on my upper cheeks now and a mole darkened on my back. But I developed a large freckle on my chin which I don't like. And I see some light freckling starting on my hands and upper chest, which I'm not a fan of either.

I'm at a crossroad now. I want to tan cause I like how it looks. But I don't like the freckling part...boo.

When I had asked Jack a while ago, he said it could be my body finally producing MHA(? - I think that's what it was).

### bennyha — 2013-04-25T15:36:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-frequent-urination-please-help.6867/post-84776>

Hello all. I have a question I was hoping you could help me answer. Very brief background-I have tanked hormones, can't sweat, have chronic fatigue and horrible adrenal exhaustion at the age of 30.

I have always urinated a lot per day since I was a kid, including waking up during the night to go. But I tried upping my water intake to 64 oz for a month or so, and I was urinating up to 30 times a day, including waking up 4 times during the night to urinate (basically every hour and a half). Even since I have brought it down to 40 oz or so a day of water, I am still urinating around 20 times a day and still wake up 4 times each night to pee. To go with this, I am never, ever thirsty. I bet it has been over 5 years since I have truly felt thirsty.

Can anyone explain what might be causing this? I am sure it's all related, but I would like to know what might be causing this so that when I find a Dr. (am currently looking for a good doctor to help me get started in my path to recovery) I can add this to my list of symptoms and know what direction to head in. I also have PE issues, so I was wondering if this tied in with that as well possibly.

Thank you so much for any help or direction anyone can give

# Thread: Body Voltage Meter experiences
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2013-04-25T15:57:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-84779>

I got my multimeter that is sensitive enough to read millivolts and set to work measuring my house, a Klein MM1000 at Home Depot for $60. A few parts from Radio Shack and some parts I had in my shop, and I was set.

First was my computer. I have a MacBook laptop and when I tested it with it's two-prong power adapter, it read 4.27 volts when I touched the case. Kinda high, to say the least.

I then converted the computer's power adapter to a 3-prong (has a ground prong) and when I touched the case using the same fingers and amount of skin surface area, it went down to .53. Interesting.

I then remembered that using the battery was the best way to use one's computer and yanked out the power cord from the side of the case and the voltage shot up to 7.34! WTF?

I've been reading but haven't found out why. Maybe it's the magenetic field that improves on battery power? I thought that went up and down with the electrical fields.

I'm trying different configurations to see if I can get myself more familiar with the parameters, but if someone can offer some suggestions...

### nonchalant — 2013-04-25T16:06:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-84780>

Interesting, Martin. Computers vary, so perhaps that explains it. My laptop battery isn't strong enough to last more than a few minutes, so I have to keep mine attached.
How did you convert your adapter to a grounded one? Buy a new brick?

I grounded mine through the USB port, that works well also, according to my Trimeter.

### Martin — 2013-04-25T17:15:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-84787>

Apple provides a grounded cord that slips onto the adapter when you remove the 2-prong module from that adapter. I'm setting up the grounded USB thingy today.

I measured BV around my ancient breaker panel and while there is voltage there (can't remember how much, I suspect EMFs), I expected it to be higher than it was.

I read 1.83 volts sitting in the middle of my studio (14 ft by 14 ft) and I need to check other places where I spend a lot of time. My side of the bed was nearly 2 volts while D-Dub's lower even though she's closer to the breaker panel on the other side of the wall.

I am getting a sickening feeling that I live in an EMF nightmare. The house wiring sucks ass and so do all my outbuildings. I will need to check them for specifics, but I know how some wiring looks because of the many remodels I've done here. Too damn bad I didn't know this info when I had the walls open. I have a sub-panel I know was done wrong, and it powers most of my studio. And the three-way switches have an independent traveller wire so I can't wait to see how effed up some of the spaces around them are.

Nearly fourteen years of my blood and tears making this farm ours, and many, many retirement dollars spent on a sow's ass? This place is beautiful with it's rolling hills, organic garden, orchard, spring water, and far from cell towers. But I really went downhill after moving here. Co-inky-dinky? Doubtful, after reading the research on the effects. There are no codes in the this county for building, and while the locals laud that as "freedom" from the gub-ment, the dumbasses don't know that some hick can wire their house with the best of intentions and they end up with a horror story.

More later. I'm going to stick my feet in the mud and hug my chickens.

### Martin — 2013-04-27T23:26:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-84959>

More BVM adventures. I loves me some testing equipment!

I made a 30 foot ground wire and went around my house measuring BV and was shocked(!) to find that my studio was so high. Over at my music/recording equipment, it was at 4.8 volts (can you say, "Michael Jackson"?) and when I turned off the power strip (I'm starting to have bad thoughts about those handy things) it dropped down to 2.7. Still lousy, but I think I know why. Our recent remodel had a sub-panel installed and when I heard Neuert say that they are often wired incorrectly, I opened the panel door and sure enough, wrong. Not sure why, as there is no provision for the configuration that Neuert speaks of - separate neutral and ground bus bars (connection strips of metal to anchor the ends of the wires), only one connection bar with all the grounds and neutral connected together. It's a new box, not sure why it didn't have one, but I suspect it can be bought and bolted in and the wires separated.

Anyway, as I sat at my seat at the dining table, I read 3.6 volts.

Who knew?!

This area is fed by the aforementioned sub-panel, as is my studio, and kitchen, too. A magnetic gauss meter would prolly explode in flames if it came anywhere near this part of the house!

Some odd things came up, though. The readings would get lower as I approached the sub-panel box and, the main box, for that matter. Certain "hot spots" would show up, like right in the middle of the dining area, and in a bath closet. I suspect lots of wiring behind walls and under floors.

Next, I turned off the main breaker and was astonished to find around a 0.10 reading all over the house. I don't think it's power lines to the house because they are far away from where I got readings? What's that all about?

Is it time for a mud hut?

Because then it is also hard to change stuff, when not knowing why.
And yes, this EMF issues really is a monster because there is no way to know if you do not test.. as we cannot sense it in any ways. Scary.

### RayB — 2013-05-07T16:20:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-85569>

I am shocked and dismayed by some body voltage readings I have seen around my home. I have a Far Infrared Sauna, that fortunately I rarely use, I had a BV reading of 24volts when sitting inside with the unit on. Yikes!!! Sitting on my toaster would probably be far more beneficial.

### RayB — 2013-05-07T16:26:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-85570>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=84959)
>
> More BVM adventures. I loves me some testing equipment!
>
> I made a 30 foot ground wire and went around my house measuring BV and was shocked(!) to find that my studio was so high. Over at my music/recording equipment, it was at 4.8 volts (can you say, "Michael Jackson"?) and when I turned off the power strip (I'm starting to have bad thoughts about those handy things) it dropped down to 2.7. Still lousy, but I think I know why. Our recent remodel had a sub-panel installed and when I heard Neuert say that they are often wired incorrectly, I opened the panel door and sure enough, wrong. Not sure why, as there is no provision for the configuration that Neuert speaks of - separate neutral and ground bus bars (connection strips of metal to anchor the ends of the wires), only one connection bar with all the grounds and neutral connected together. It's a new box, not sure why it didn't have one, but I suspect it can be bought and bolted in and the wires separated.
>
>
> Is it time for a mud hut?

FWIW...bus bars can be purchased and installed. A neutral bus bar is insulated (no continuity) from the metal box. A ground bus bar has continuity with the panel's housing.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-07T16:40:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-85572>

RayB, I wonder if your sauna can be grounded. My laptop was giving me higher BV readings than that, but grounding fixed it.
I have removed a dimmer switch, and lately I have been eyeing my house thermostat. And it might be a minor thing, but when using my debit card, I've started using the stylus and buttons more, instead of tapping on the screen.

### RayB — 2013-05-07T19:52:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-85575>

nonchalant, From info that I have gathered, grounding will lower or completely remove body voltage 'cuz grounding puts your body at the same potential as the ground. Since we are using the ground as the reference voltage for all of our BV measurements, when our potential becomes equal with the reference voltage we seemingly fix things. In essence what we have done is taken a static skin voltage and converted it to current. Which is more damaging? IMHO, it's anybodies guess. According to Michael N., remediating the source of the BV is the first and best option then, when all else fails, ground.

BTW, the potential of ground is always assumed to be zero but in reality, it is not. But we have to use something as a reference and it is as good as anything. If it were zero, how could we source free electrons from it or where would lightning get it's electrons from or send it's electrons to?

### Martin — 2013-05-07T20:24:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-85579>

> [RayB said:](/goto/post?id=85570)
>
> FWIW...bus bars can be purchased and installed. A neutral bus bar is insulated (no continuity) from the metal box. A ground bus bar has continuity with the panel's housing.

Thanks, RayB, I was hoping I could retrofit a bus bar, but haven't been out to shop. A decent clamp meter is next on the list o' tools.

### Martin — 2013-05-07T20:30:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-85583>

> [RayB said:](/goto/post?id=85575)
>
> nonchalant, From info that I have gathered, grounding will lower or completely remove body voltage 'cuz grounding puts your body at the same potential as the ground. Since we are using the ground as the reference voltage for all of our BV measurements, when our potential becomes equal with the reference voltage we seemingly fix things. In essence what we have done is taken a static skin voltage and converted it to current. Which is more damaging? IMHO, it's anybodies guess. According to Michael N., remediating the source of the BV is the first and best option then, when all else fails, ground.
>
> BTW, the potential of ground is always assumed to be zero but in reality, it is not. But we have to use something as a reference and it is as good as anything. If it were zero, how could we source free electrons from it or where would lightning get it's electrons from or send it's electrons to?

RayB, it sounds like nonchalant is referring to grounding the sauna structure itself? Neuert shows us how grounding a computer's case shunts the voltage to ground so it won't use our body as a conduit, so if the sauna can be grounded, it would do the same?

### RayB — 2013-05-08T02:42:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-85605>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=85583)
>
> RayB, it sounds like nonchalant is referring to grounding the sauna structure itself? Neuert shows us how grounding a computer's case shunts the voltage to ground so it won't use our body as a conduit, so if the sauna can be grounded, it would do the same?

You're probably right...sometimes I go off on a tangent.

The sauna itself can't be grounded...it's a wooden cabinet.

I would imagine that the effectiveness of grounding a computer's case would vary depending on the design of the case. An all metal case with very few openings/penetrations seems like it would act as shielding when grounded. Plastic case...not so much. Typically laptops are not directly connected to line current. They use a transformer. If they did use line current a metal case would be grounded already in accordance with UL rating.

# Thread: EMF Magnetic Field Home Testing - Feedback Please.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-magnetic-field-home-testing-feedback-please.6869/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Paul S — 2013-04-25T18:53:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-magnetic-field-home-testing-feedback-please.6869/post-84794>

I've listened in on the EMF webinar's thus far, and recently rented 3 test devices. Initial testing of the magnetic fields with the gauss meter, throughout my home, indicate levels between 0.9 - 1.5 milligauss. My situation/observation that I am hoping someone might confirm or clarify for me is:

All the walls facing the street are where I get the higher readings (1.4 - 1.5 milligauss). When I open my garage it edges up slightly more and in my garage is near 2.0. As I walk to the driveway and toward the street, levels approach 8.0 milligauss at the end of the driveway (which also happens to be near utility boxes). I've turned off the main breaker to the home and the measurements don't materially change (lower), except in a few places and, even then, no readings really much lower than 0.9 milligauss in the home, including the bedrooms (which are on the second story of the home).

From what I have gathered from the EMF webinars is that this situation appears to signify that (i) my magnetic fields are primarily driven by my neighborhood's issues more so than specific issues within my home and (ii) the levels (of 0.9 - 1.5 milligauss) within the home are such that it is entirely possible could be causing/contributing to sub-optimal health and labs?

If that is the case and I am interpreting the readings correclty - moving is probably the only way to "fix" the issue.....? Please offer any thoughts or clarification or others' experiences.

### cantweight — 2013-04-25T19:56:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-magnetic-field-home-testing-feedback-please.6869/post-84798>

I am no expert, but based on the fact you experience very little change when breakers are cut seems to strongly indicate your area is to blame. I lived in a very technologically rich neighborhood where illness abounded, I moved last summer and based my move on where I felt I breathed easier. We just tested the home we moved to and throughout the entire house the readings were .03-.04

I will make an effort to visit my old neighborhood to get a reading and repot back. But it definitely sounds like your problem is your area.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Elevated liver enzymes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elevated-liver-enzymes.6872/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kjgraffin@yahoo.com — 2013-04-25T19:58:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elevated-liver-enzymes.6872/post-84799>

We had our annual blood draw at work this month and I was pleased to see my CO2 and BUN levels have improved since I have not been drinking flouridated water. However, my liver enzymes, ALT & AST, are really elevated. They went from being in the 30s to being in the 50s.

Any thoughts as to where I should look for causes? I've been doing heavy metal chelation therapy for about 3 months and started taking Milk Thistle about 2 or 3 weeks prior to the draw. Could either one of those have contributed? Seems like those things would improve my numbers, though. BTW, I'm not overweight and my glucose and cholesterol numbers are good, so I'm not thinking fatty liver.

Any insights would be great.

### cantweight — 2013-04-25T21:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elevated-liver-enzymes.6872/post-84806>

From my experience with the lovely liver I would suspect both the chelation and milk thistle could elevate your liver numbers. Anything toxic your body is releasing is definitely going to make for an unhappy liver. While your numbers are elevated they are not crazy high. I would continue to cleanse by adding lemon to water and throw in a stronger binder, something like activated charcoal.

Take the charcoal away from other supplements and be sure bowels are moving splendidly!

I have also found coffee enemas to be helpful with major detox as well.

As the liver settles from dealing with the onslaught the numbers should go back down

# Thread: Anyone have input on a dairy craving?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-04-25T20:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84802>

Like clockwork, day 25 of cycle I have the most intense craving for milk.

I think milk is pretty nasty, I don't like the idea of drinking another species milk, I loathe the pasteurization process, yet my body is relentless in its yearning for some damn milk.

For years I have evaluated my food cravings and figured out what part of me needed something from that food and treated it with great success. This one baffles me.

We have cow milk in the house about 3 times a year, just so happened right now we had some. Today is day 25, I drank about 6 ozs and it felt like elixir of life!

Is this just another sugar craving masquerading as something else?

### Hope — 2013-04-26T00:19:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84827>

Wow. WHat an amazing observation! Could it be calcium you're wanting to help relax as you're approaching the end of a cycle? Though sugar would go along with that as well.....hmmmm

### cantweight — 2013-04-26T00:23:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84828>

Calcium...maybe that's it.

Was thinking maybe hormones too?

I always thought calcium in milk was not easily assimilated. Thinking a calcium craving would beg greens or something like that.

No clue!

### Hope — 2013-04-26T02:49:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84840>

Have you ever craved greens? I think milk is often a comfort food...so maybe it's more ice-cream than milk, but it's a thought....I think for me I would usually prefer to crave sugary things.

I used to always crave chocolate while PMSing bc I ''needed" the magnesium in it.....and other stuff...and of course there are way healthier options to get Mg from

### fitness@home — 2013-04-26T03:07:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84842>

Another here that used to crave dairy - in the form of cheese, which I loved. Rarely eat it any longer (autoimmune reasons) but I could easily fall back into that trap.

It's very addictive for me...

### Hope — 2013-04-26T03:16:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84843>

Yeah....that's the caseo-morphine or something.....very addictive substance

The CD21 thing is fascinating....

### cantweight — 2013-04-26T04:33:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84852>

The weird part to me is that it is not something that I love. And the relief I felt when I drank it came very quickly. Who the hell knows..lol!

### Hope — 2013-04-27T03:07:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84917>

Hm....fascinating. You must be very in tune with something you need....that intuitive eating, as kids do.....or pregnant women? Maybe it's also liquid too? If people wanting coffee do that too....who knows?

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-27T03:31:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84921>

Maybe it's a sugar craving and the milk sugar satisfies it.
