# Thread: Still Suffering from Headaches Daily - What Next?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2013-04-25T23:14:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-84817>

Been on BHRT with an anti-aging Doc who firmly believes in Dr. Dzugan's theories. I am on Day 131 of her protocol. Went to see her to try and get some relief from these chronic daily headaches, which everyone assumed was menopause/hormone related. Been through all the MRI's, CT Scans, MRA's MRV's, Vestibular Testing, etc. Everything is "Normal". Was tested for Epstein Barr, Lyme Disease, Valley Fever, etc. Had the Adrenal Stress test done. Cortisol was high, Melatonin was low, but nothing dramatic.

90 days of Migra-Eeze (prescribed by Mayo), 131 Days of BHRT and now 60 days of Cymbalta (nothing was working and I needed some pain relief, so I gave in to my PCP and started the Cymbalta). I still have chronic daily headaches. Last labs 2 weeks ago show hormones in balance, magnesium and zinc levels good, thyroid good. Anti-Aging doc is good at ordering labs and keeping track, and my insurance coverage is good. We are all stumped. Started to feel some relief on Week 4 of the Cymbalta, then this week (Week 6) the plugged was pulled and I am in Day 5 of my most recent headache / neck ache / back ache fiasco. It all hurts.

Diet is gluten-free, I do not eat food I was discovered to be allergic or intolerant to. Higher protein, low carbs. I take the recommended supplements/hormones given to me by my anti-aging doc. I do Yoga for my upper back, neck and shoulders most every day, do the exercises prescribed by my PT, have acupuncture once a week, etc. No relief. We are missing something, but what?

Any thoughts on where to go next? I am going on two years of this daily hell and just want to feel 50% better, at the very least. They (Mayo Clinic Headache Neurologist) diagnosed me with "chronic migraine", but none of the treatments they prescribed work, from the 3 days of outpatient IV infusion to the Arthrotec pain reliever to shots of Toridal to the daily dose of Cymbalta. there's is no point in any OTC pain relievers. Tic Tacs would help more.

I have an appt with another neurologist early next month for another opinion.

Karen from Arizona

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-25T23:26:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-84818>

Karen

I'm re-reading Dr Uzzi's Hormone book [https://www.uzzireissmd.com/book\_naturalhormone.html](https://www.uzzireissmd.com/book_naturalhormone.html)

one thing that caught my eye this time around - is that just b/c you're in "range" of the lab ranges, doesn't mean your in the range for you. I'd look back at your labs, and how you feel, talk w/your doc about increasing the dosing of your hormones.

since I don't know what your labs look like and I don't know your hormone doeses - I can't say what I'd look to adjust.

I know for me - I don't do well cycling my Pg - so I don't even though Dzugan want's me too. I'm also on a super HIGH DOSE of Pg for a woman who isn't menopausal. I'm taking 20% Pg gel 1ml in the am and 1 ml in the pm + 5% sublinigual. (though I think I'm headed there fast given how my cycle is behaving!)
my normal isn't like anyone elses, and neither is yours.

detailed tracking is key IMO, especially when considering youre trying to get rid of migraines.... you may be one of the few women who can't cycle Pg - otherwise you get migraines.

I know you've got a constant migraine - is there a point in your cycle where the migraine is less painful than other times? if so what does your dosing look like for your Hormones?

how's your Thyroid? etc???

### Dextery — 2013-04-25T23:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-84819>

Try protecting yourself from EMFs being emitted from your smart meter.

Lots of folks have been suffering with headaches for 5 years since the advent of smart meters and never could determine why.

You can do this at for less than $10 from materials at home depot.
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player\_embedded&v=cmS5pVEZHzg](http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player_embedded&v=cmS5pVEZHzg)
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player\_embedded&v=LN10megpzLM](http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player_embedded&v=LN10megpzLM)

I'm always interested in your take on hormones. Karen - do listend to Shinjin - she's actually teaching Dzugan a thing or to I believe

Gretchen - Do you not really cycle at all during the monh and instead just stay steady the whole time? Isnt that similiar to how birth control works - they put you on a set dose of estrogen and progesterone (fake of course) and then you stop one week of the month? I did feel good when i had that so maybe adjusting my BHRT is worth considering.

I still havent recieved my tri-est so havent experimented wiht that at all but I am looking forward to it. I really think progesterone makes me swollen. I'm working on addressing that (hydration! EMF! grounding!) but that wont happent overnight. I looked at my cycle and its day 21, which is usually when progesterone is higher than estrogen. I think its why I feel so crappy: tired, fat, swollen boobs...more so than I did yesterday.

I stopped maca this week but I'm sure that didnt help me

### mafixit — 2013-04-25T23:49:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-84822>

Thanks for the EMF tip - I found that the plug to my alarm clock under the head of my bed was emitting in the range of 60, so I no longer have a clock in my room. And actually, my smartmeter was installed around the time this all started to happen to me. The meter, however is on the other side of the house on the outside of a garage wall. It's the furthest away from the living space it could be. Now, my neighbor's meter is closer to me than my own.

As far as cycles, I really don't have one. I am peri or menopausal depending on who you talk to. My last period was 45 days ago and the one before that was probably 4 to 5 months before the last one. Thyroid labs are good. Anti-aging doc checks everything she can think of.

Hence my issue. I can't identify triggers or cycles. I keep a daily log of how I feel, but none of the puzzle pieces match up.

At this point I am on progesterone capsules, 100 mg at night, E1/E2 Compound cream 1 click in morning, 2 clicks at night, Testosterone cream, 3 clicks in AM, and 1/2 of a 25mg sublingual DHEA tablet. Doc tried 150mg time release Progesterone and it literally knocked me out the next day. Can't take those because they are like sleeping pills for me. I thought maybe the PG was causing the headache, but stopping it did not make any difference.

Karen from Arizona

Karen from Arizona

### Grizz — 2013-04-27T23:02:26+0300

> [mafixit said:](/goto/post?id=84817)
>
> Been on BHRT with an anti-aging Doc who firmly believes in Dr. Dzugan's theories. I am on Day 131 of her protocol. Went to see her to try and get some relief from these chronic daily headaches, which everyone assumed was menopause/hormone related. Been through all the MRI's, CT Scans, MRA's MRV's, Vestibular Testing, etc. Everything is "Normal". Was tested for Epstein Barr, Lyme Disease, Valley Fever, etc. Had the Adrenal Stress test done. Cortisol was high, Melatonin was low, but nothing dramatic.
>
> 90 days of Migra-Eeze (prescribed by Mayo), 131 Days of BHRT and now 60 days of Cymbalta (nothing was working and I needed some pain relief, so I gave in to my PCP and started the Cymbalta). I still have chronic daily headaches. Last labs 2 weeks ago show hormones in balance, magnesium and zinc levels good, thyroid good. Anti-Aging doc is good at ordering labs and keeping track, and my insurance coverage is good. We are all stumped. Started to feel some relief on Week 4 of the Cymbalta, then this week (Week 6) the plugged was pulled and I am in Day 5 of my most recent headache / neck ache / back ache fiasco. It all hurts.
>
> Diet is gluten-free, I do not eat food I was discovered to be allergic or intolerant to. Higher protein, low carbs. I take the recommended supplements/hormones given to me by my anti-aging doc. I do Yoga for my upper back, neck and shoulders most every day, do the exercises prescribed by my PT, have acupuncture once a week, etc. No relief. We are missing something, but what?
>
> Any thoughts on where to go next? I am going on two years of this daily hell and just want to feel 50% better, at the very least. They (Mayo Clinic Headache Neurologist) diagnosed me with "chronic migraine", but none of the treatments they prescribed work, from the 3 days of outpatient IV infusion to the Arthrotec pain reliever to shots of Toridal to the daily dose of Cymbalta. there's is no point in any OTC pain relievers. Tic Tacs would help more.
>
> I have an appt with another neurologist early next month for another opinion.
>
> Karen from Arizona

Karen,
Just because your doctor checked your thyroid does not mean it is OK. They typically use TSH as a determining test, and many experts agree that TSH is worse than useless. Far better to measure your body temp and chart it every day. You should be near 98.6 and you should NOT suffer from old hands or cold feet. If you have cold hands or cold feet, then you are hypothyroid. Prove this by making a [chart of your body temp](http://www.drrind.com/therapies/metabolic-temperature-graph). If you have symptoms, refer to this [book on the thyroid](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/)
Headaches and Migraines are a symptom of hypothyroid. [http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/long-and-pathetic/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/long-and-pathetic/)

Doctors typically overlook the obvious, and one obvious symptom of iodine deficiency is severe headaches. I suggest you get an iodine deficiency test here, and also get the bromide & fluoride testing:
[http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html](http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html)

Headaches are typical of iodine deficiency for many:
[Iodine Deficiency Leads to Headaches and Fatigue](http://cantstayoutofthekitchen.com/2012/08/31/iodine-deficiency-leads-to-headaches-and-fatigue/)

Dr. Hownenstein says, "Other Health Problems Iodine has proven value in treating headaches, keloids, and parotid duct stones."
[http://www.newswithviews.com/Howenstine/james37.htm](http://www.newswithviews.com/Howenstine/james37.htm)

> and patients with migraine headaches stop having them. Even better results can occur when iodine is combined with magnesium and selenium. Symptoms of iodine deficiency include muscle cramps, lymph gland congestion, proneness to weight gain, underweight, low blood pressure, cardiac arrhythmia, slow reflexes, **poor memory, fuzzy thinking,** fluid retention, **migraines,** constipation, depression, headaches, edema, myalgia and weakness; cold hands and feet, dry skin, and brittle nails; hair loss across body, head and eyebrows; breast, ovarian, thyroid, and skin cysts;
> [http://joannebrophy.com/programs/iodinetherapy.htm](http://joannebrophy.com/programs/iodinetherapy.htm)

There are millions of hits in Google for **IODINE HEADACHE**
Beware that when first starting on iodine supplements, most people have detox symptoms that typically pass away. One of these detox symptoms can be headaches. \`
The Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol has proven effective for iodine deficiency symptoms:
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

So bottom line: I think it is extremely worthwhile to test your iodine levels per the above link. NOT A BLOOD TEST !

### Grizz — 2013-04-29T17:53:32+0300

>
> Karen from Arizona

Karen,

OMG, you are on Cymbalta? Before you attempt to quit Cymbalta, I suggest you Google "Cymbalta Withdrawal" to get some tips on how to do this. Cymbalta withdrawal symptoms are a "Living Hell" for many, far exceeding their worst pain or depression. Suicide is frequently said to be a symptom. I have a good friend suffering TERRIBLY from Cymbalta withdrawal.

IMO, this drug should be outlawed. Here are some things I've found:

> A neighbor called me for help with her anti-depressant/pain killing drug, Cymbalta. She said it was impossible to withdraw from it, and her insurance company won't pay for it anymore because of Obamacare. It costs $160 a month or more and she can't afford it.
>
> I have extensive medical information in my computer, and could not find anything for weaning from SSRI Drugs. So I made a huge effort to help her find an answer. What I found out was one HORROR STORY after another.
>
> Cymbalta withdrawall. This seems to be a drug from hell.... NASTY.
> People are reporting withdrawal symptoms of:
> "Debilitating" brain zaps,
> severe headaches,
> hot & cold sensation,
> brain confusion,
> vivid nightmares,
> intestinal twisting,
> suicidal thoughts
> chest pain,
> tingling,
> moodiness,
> hot flashes,
> dizziness,
> wild uncontrollable rages,
> "people said they have these brain zaps anywhere from a few weeks to a year."
> "Doctors won't help with withdrawal"
> Vertigo, and...
> I stopped looking into this nightmare, as there seemed to be no solution to be found except suffering for up to a year while slowly scaling back on dosage.

> Msg to my friend,
> ...UPDATE: Several months later...
> It's been a while and I've decided to update everyone on my situation.
> In January of 2009, I was finally down to 20 mg of Cymbalta daily (I started from 60mg and tapered down 10 mg every two weeks). Since there is no lower dose than 20mg, I asked my doctor for 10mg of Prozac. I took Prozac daily for about a month and did not experience any withdrawal symptoms at all! Finally I stopped taking Prozac completely, and am now living a happy, normal life.
> There is hope, people! Good luck to everyone who is still going through this horrible experience. [http://byee.hubpages.com/hub/Weaning-Off-Cymbalta-What-The-Doctors-Dont-Tell-You](http://byee.hubpages.com/hub/Weaning-Off-Cymbalta-What-The-Doctors-Dont-Tell-You)
> Save the above link, it has valuable references to other web sites.
>
> Also E-How.com has what I think is the best 3 step method here, by combining Prozac & Omega 3 Fish oil:
> [http://www.ehow.com/how\_5531409\_quit-cymbalta.html](http://www.ehow.com/how_5531409_quit-cymbalta.html)
> I think I'm done sending you tips. I'm praying for you to beat this, you can do this!

Grizz

I would like to invite you to join with us at the [Iodine Thread](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?943-The-iodine-Thread!!!/page54). IMO, your condition is one of the clear cut symptoms of iodine deficiency.
More here: [http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Deficiency](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Deficiency)

PS) There are several Cymbalta Lawsuits in progress, you may be elligible to join into one of them. Google "cymbalta lawsuit" for more.

Grizz

### nunki — 2013-04-30T02:57:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-85170>

> [Joann said:](/goto/post?id=84825)
>
> Wow. seems you have looked into every avenue. I was going to ask if you have ever been in an accident, but then I see you have had mri's for the spine and neck? Did you have the headaches before meno? I would think back to when they started to see if anything changed before headaches started.
> I know this may sound to simple...one time I bought a new bra ...halter bra...which I later found caused me and my dd to have headaches. where the strap landed on my back caused awful headache.
> hope you find relief soon

I didnt see where you saw Joann's question. Accidents involving the neck and shoulders can cause headaches. Just having all of those muscles tighten up can trigger one for me. If the hormones, and fixing the EMF doesnt help, I would look for a good OA chiropractor. Nobody was able to fix my terrible headaches and pressure feeling (like someone was trying to pry my head off of my neck with a pry bar, lol). A couple of visits to him- getting OA adjusted and active release techniques to loosen up my tight neck muscles reduced my headaches dramatically. Good luck! So frustrating when no one can fix what the problem is!

### mafixit — 2013-04-30T16:38:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-85191>

> [nunki said:](/goto/post?id=85170)
>
> I didnt see where you saw Joann's question. Accidents involving the neck and shoulders can cause headaches. Just having all of those muscles tighten up can trigger one for me. If the hormones, and fixing the EMF doesnt help, I would look for a good OA chiropractor. Nobody was able to fix my terrible headaches and pressure feeling (like someone was trying to pry my head off of my neck with a pry bar, lol). A couple of visits to him- getting OA adjusted and active release techniques to loosen up my tight neck muscles reduced my headaches dramatically. Good luck! So frustrating when no one can fix what the problem is!

I have not had any accidents. This all started in March 2011 and has progressed over the last two years. It was intermittent and then became a daily issue. I tried chiro twice and it made things worse.

I do have a dehiscence of the superior semi-circular canal on the left side and most likely on the right side, discovered on a CT scan last December at the ER while I was being treated for a monster headache. This can cause some of my dizziness. I don't have any of the horrible symptoms of this at this time. Just some disequilibrium at times.

I am doing some exercises the physical therapist gave me to strengthen neck and back muscles and overall core strength. I have also taken up a great Yoga session from a website for neck and shoulders. When I am done with that session I feel so "stretched out' in a good way, but still the headaches persist. I feel it in my left shoulder blade, shoulder muscles and neck from the base of my neck to my shoulders. Then it creeps up the left side of my head behind my ear, into my temple and then finally across my forehead. Shoulders hurt to the touch. Trigger points at the base of my head hurt to the touch, especially left side. The left side of my face sometimes feels slightly numb from the jaw bone to the cheek - Like I am on the tail end of novocaine.

The more I do the recommended exercises, the more my left shoulder blade hurts. I have been chalking it up to weak muscles.

I am a mystery.

Karen from Arizona

### nunki — 2013-05-01T03:33:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-85229>

You have three muscles that come up and attach at the base of your skull. I tore the attachment for mine in a car accident. Your symptoms sound pretty much like mine. I had steroid shots in mine- gave me terrible vertigo. When it is flared up my face will go numb- really weird feeling, lol. Two and a half years later, I cannot lift weight above my head, hold my arm up above my head for any length of time, cannot stand weight on my head- like a towel turban etc. I have a knot that comes and goes right in that insertion point. It really sounds like you have in some way damaged those muscles. It could have been anything, even turning your head too quickly. Plain old, crack the neck chiro's will not help it. Look for one that specializes in an Atlas Orthogonal adjustment- it is not a "crack" the neck adjustment.

Dont give up, you have to keep trying things until you find what works. I also use an e-stim machine on mine, makes those muscles completely relax temporarily.

### Joann — 2013-05-01T09:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-85233>

Sounds so familiar to me its scary. I had an "adventure" in the 90's was looking up at my computer which the monitor was on top of way too high. suddenly, the back of my head and scalp felt tense like I had a hat on, then my eyelid started twitching. Woke up the next day with swelling on side of face on cheek near nose. I thought it was a tooth problem went to dentist, he didnt find anything but gave me anti's anyway. Then my collarbone felt like electric shock when touched. Went to docs, looked at me like I was nuts! finally had an mri and x rays of head which was negative. went to chiro worse thing! she cracked my neck .....My ears Hummed like a siren. went to the library brought home a bunch of books on diff therapies...shiatso etc. Tried to fix myself. Few years passed, oh I slept on a sofa with pillows propped since I couldnt sleep with my head and shoulders flat on a bed. Finally I found a chiro that did different techniques. After about a year I let her do something to my head/neck
Lying on my stomach, she took my head from behind and moved it in such a way that my collarbone didn't feel like the funny bone of an elbow. Before finding her I had other chiros that "adjusted" me which made my symptoms worse and even gave me new symptoms.....my legs trembling, dizzy, facial numbness.
Its many years later, I cannot look up at my tall son....my ears will hum, going to the dentist ....feel beat up, shoulder and neck acts up. I run to the freezer for ICE It helps a lot.
I also have the knot at the back of my head that comes and goes (recent) and problems with raising my arms over head or wearing a hat. If the therapy makes me feel worse then its the wrong therapy for me.

### mafixit — 2013-05-02T16:35:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-85301>

> [Joann said:](/goto/post?id=85233)
>
> Sounds so familiar to me its scary. I had an "adventure" in the 90's was looking up at my computer which the monitor was on top of way too high. suddenly, the back of my head and scalp felt tense like I had a hat on, then my eyelid started twitching. Woke up the next day with swelling on side of face on cheek near nose. I thought it was a tooth problem went to dentist, he didnt find anything but gave me anti's anyway. Then my collarbone felt like electric shock when touched. Went to docs, looked at me like I was nuts! finally had an mri and x rays of head which was negative. went to chiro worse thing! she cracked my neck .....My ears Hummed like a siren. went to the library brought home a bunch of books on diff therapies...shiatso etc. Tried to fix myself. Few years passed, oh I slept on a sofa with pillows propped since I couldnt sleep with my head and shoulders flat on a bed. Finally I found a chiro that did different techniques. After about a year I let her do something to my head/neck
> Lying on my stomach, she took my head from behind and moved it in such a way that my collarbone didn't feel like the funny bone of an elbow. Before finding her I had other chiros that "adjusted" me which made my symptoms worse and even gave me new symptoms.....my legs trembling, dizzy, facial numbness.
> Its many years later, I cannot look up at my tall son....my ears will hum, going to the dentist ....feel beat up, shoulder and neck acts up. I run to the freezer for ICE It helps a lot.
> I also have the knot at the back of my head that comes and goes (recent) and problems with raising my arms over head or wearing a hat. If the therapy makes me feel worse then its the wrong therapy for me.

I did the specialist chiropractor thing - neuro-cranial therapy. Spent a bit of money on that last year- didn't help. I know what you mean about having chiro therapy and then feeling worse. My last trip to the crack back guy caused issues the next day - clogged ears, more headache, neck ache, etc.

I am in acupuncture therapy for my trigger points now, which are all bothering me, but especially in my neck and shoulders. It doesn't seem to help a whole bunch, but it's cheap because my insurance covers it, so I go once a week.

I called the Mayo Pain Clinic for treatment - they are scheduling trigger point injections and occipital nerve blocks, but I can't get in until June 25th.

Mostly now this feels like tension headaches and I get visual issues - like my eyes won't focus together or I have a different prescription in each eye. My shoulder joints are stiffening up and my shoulder muscles from neck to shoulder hurt to the touch. Have issues in my left shoulder blade too. This has all been attributed to migraine and anxiety. I don't believe it.

I get into some of these yoga positions and my right shoulder joint locks up and I have to get out slow

Still no real help from the Cymbalta.

Karen from Arizona

### mafixit — 2014-02-25T21:52:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-112152>

Update on me -

Still suffering. Tried more trigger point injections (no help), cervical epidural (never again - the steroid side affects were AWFUL), tried Indocin, more acupuncture, now am in to see a chiro specialist for Atlas adjustment - Atlas Orthogonal. 3 treatments in and no help.

Right now I am on nothing - no supplements, no BHRT, no drugs, nothing. I feel just a shitty on or off any meds, so I am off for now.

Gained a ton of weight back - lost 35 lbs before this all starting in 2011. Gained it all back over the past year.

I am seriously considering going a round with Dzugan. It's expensive, but I have spent so much money with the Mayo Clinic and other docs. I also switched to the University of Arizona Integrative Health Center for my primary care doc. They will look at the whole me, not just give me an anti-depressant and send me on my way. Within this practice I also get to meet with a nutritionist, a heath coach, a mind-body practioner and take yoga, tai chi, nutrition and other classes. The center was started by Dr. Andrew Weil. They just opened here in Phoenix last year.

I also saw a new neurologist outside of Mayo (he was the one who put me on the Indocin). The Indocin gave me worse headaches, so we pulled that and he wants me on Inderol. Last week when I saw him he made me feel like we were out of options, with the exception of occipital nerve blocks, which my insurance does not cover.

I know my body has changed again because my hot flashes are minimal, but my menstrual cycle is a joke. 130 days , 19 days 40 days, 77 days, etc. My breasts are tender and I am sleeping like crap again. My last cycle my ovaries were screaming so bad I was ready to go to the Urgent Care. Even if I do sleep through the night, I never feel rested.

So I have run full circle and am back to the beginning. My husband wants me back and I want to be back. I know some of you are using Dzugan. It is worth the $$$$?

Karen from AZ.

# Thread: Health Screening at Work Today
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/health-screening-at-work-today.6875/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-26T07:32:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/health-screening-at-work-today.6875/post-84858>

I was going to post my health screening results today that I got from the free yearly mobile bus screening, but I forgot and left the papers at work. The screener checked triglycerides, HDL, LDL, total cholesterol, cholesterol ratio, glucose, blood pressure, body fat ratio, and weight. Results were good. My numbers were all below the recommended numbers they want it below except body fat ratio and blood pressure. My BP registers high if I am nervous. If I recall correctly, my triglycerides were 99 though I had eaten a fatty breakfast with plenty of MCT/coconut oil/butter about 4 hours prior to the screening. HDL and LDL numbers were great though LDL was higher than HDL. I'm not worried about that since I suspect my body is packaging my dietary fat as LDL to use as fuel. She said my body mass ratio should be no higher than 21 for my age and the resistance probe registered about 23. I told her I had lost 60 pounds in the last year (its really about 64 now). She asked me if I lost it on purpose. Yep! I didn't tell her it wasn't from exercising or calorie restriction. <grin> I checked BP later at Kmart and it registered 118/64.

I grabbed one of their brochures on dietary fat. It describes polyunsaturated fat as the preferred fat, mentioned monounsaturated fat, and then describes saturated fat as "bad fat" full of cholesterol even though it mentions coconut oil. I didn't know coconuts made cholesterol. While waiting to be screened I mentioned to some of the other coworkers that the heart's preferred fuel was saturated fat and that polyunsaturated fat was easily oxidized and uses up lots of the body's vitamin E.

I will post the actual numbers in a couple of days.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-27T15:31:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/health-screening-at-work-today.6875/post-84939>

Congrats!!!!!! Health, like time, is a precious gift. We become acutely aware of both when we have little of either left.
It has been said that many people spend their health trying to acquire wealth, then spend their wealth trying to buy back their health. May I suggest today you begin to harmonize your mind, body and spirit. Maintain your inner peace. Exercise physically to maintain your strength, but exercise compassion, empathy and understanding to maintain your heart. Make your attitude excellent. Maintain a sunny disposition; allow yours to shine in your world. Live intentional but love deliberately.......and every moment you have left. Time is of the essence. Use it to the best of your ability to manage your purpose.
Habits are developed daily. Develop the habits of guarding your integrity, character, thoughts and speech. You have begun to regain your health today with those labs.

# Thread: Radon, how it becomes dangerous
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radon-how-it-becomes-dangerous.6876/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2013-04-26T10:56:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radon-how-it-becomes-dangerous.6876/post-84860>

So, I have been concerned about radon for long, as I live in a area where there are huge amounts in the ground at places. So it is common here to test for it if you have a basement.
My parents tested, and it come out there was no issues at all in the air inside the house even if deep basements, also not in the water that comes from own well 30 meter deep.

So I was reading about it more. I accidetially come across some great info reading a book about radiation from the government, I got it from the library. It says, radon has been a issue only in modern times..........
Here it comes why;
It became an issue when people wanted to save energy and have it warm inside They made their houses so airtight, the windows, walls.. everything the radon coming from the basement / earth had no way to escape and collected into the air inside the house. Only then radon became a issue. Even if you have big amounts radon in the ground, and you have a basement, as long as your house is not airtight the radon will escape all the time and it will not accumulate and be an issue for our health. My parents house that my dad built himself has full of "not airtight places" as it is still not finished..lol I also keep the windows open a lot, in the bedroom all night long and also otherwise I keep doors and windows open as much as I can.

I wanted to share this if someone is concerned about radon, do not be! Just air out your house often and there will be zero issues. This made me recognize one more thing we have made dangerous with our aims for comfort in modern times.... that was not dangerous to begin with, if we did not do all those unnatural stuff living in airtight overheated houses etc.

### cantweight — 2013-04-26T14:33:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radon-how-it-becomes-dangerous.6876/post-84869>

That is so good to know! When we moved over the summer they did a radon test on the house and while it was in normal range it seemed at the high end to me. I am now grateful for my crappy old windows that let all kinds of air in!

### Inger — 2013-04-26T14:45:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radon-how-it-becomes-dangerous.6876/post-84870>

Yes cantweight, let the air in!
To have the windows and doors open as much as it gets has lots of benefits.... not only zeroing the radon issue

This house probably have mold contamination too, but airing often helps this issue also! I keep lots of green plants in my rooms too. Very good for cleaning the air for toxins.

### lioness7 — 2013-04-26T17:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radon-how-it-becomes-dangerous.6876/post-84885>

Yes Inger , you are so right in my opinion. We live in an older home that has quite a few "leaks". This never bothered me as I always felt that the slight extra cost of heating was well worth the advantage of having fresh air coming in all the time. Even during our coldest Canadian days, I always slept with the windows open and never worried about drafts. Unfortunately, most of the homes built today in North America are "shrink wrapped", requiring added fresh air heat recovery systems which are very costly. I always tell everyone, shut off your lights and open up your windows/doors instead.

# Thread: Hi from sunny South Africa - part 1 of 3
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sunny-south-africa-part-1-of-3.6880/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Fran — 2013-04-26T15:12:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sunny-south-africa-part-1-of-3.6880/post-84872>

Hi there, I posted this under Hormones 101 but Dr Kruse said I must take this to the forum, so here I am

I'm a 38-year old woman and I've been experiencing hair loss slowly since I was 18. The hair loss is in the frontal region of my scalp. It doesn't seem to fall out at a faster rate, but rather, doesn't grow back or miniaturises. I've tried numerous doctors over the years, with no success. I went to a very helpful GP last year as I had a number of symptoms (super-frizzy hair, slight loss of power in my right hand, memory loss, tenderness in my liver area) that were worrying me. He suspected low thyroid and I've been put on Synthroid (can't get Armour or the like in the country I live in). I've never been entirely sure if the hair loss was hormonal or caused by obscene stress that I was experiencing (and which didn't let up well into my twenties), however, I'm now very overweight (heaviest was 126 kg and I'm currently at 96kg / 212 pounds) so if it was stress, I believe the hair loss is now caused by my blood sugar mess. I was thin at the age of 18 (68kg / 150 pounds and my height is 1.77m).

I had some blood tests done last year before my GP put me on thyroid meds:

\- Ultrasensative-CRP: 1.31,
\- DHEAS: 3.47 (reference is 1.65 - 9.15)
\- Vitamin D: 17.6
\- Insulin Fasting: 7.5 (reference is 2.1 - 10.4)
\- Glucose fasting: 5.1 (reference is 3.5 - 5.5)
\- 17OH Progesterone: 12.2
\- Free T4: 10.1 (reference is 7.2 - 16.4)
\- Free T3: 3.8 (reference is 3.8 - 6.0)
\- S-TSH: 2.01 (reference is 0.37 - 3.50)
\- Testosterone - Total: 0.58 (reference is 0.22 - 2.9)
\- Sex Hormone Bind. Globulin: 43.5 (reference is 26.1 - 110)
\- Free Androgen Index: 1.3 (0.4 - 5.9)

And then others were done at the same time and I've added the ones which have been highlighted because they are high:

\- Cholesterol-S - 5.4 (reference is 5.0)
\- LDL Cholesterol - 3.3 (reference is 3.0)
\- Bilirubin Total-S - 28 (rererence is 2 - 20)
\- Bili Unconj.-S - 25 (reference is 2 - 14)
\- ALT (SGPT) - 35 (reference is 10 - 32)

I was diagnosed with the start of non-alcoholic Fatty Liver disease and Hypothyroidism. My GP's response was to put me on Euthyrox (Levothyroxine) at 25 ug, follow the diet recommended by Dr Rosedale and he also suggested some EFT sessions - which I did with him, he's very holistic. I also had to take loads of supplements and a shake drink called Estrium that is manufactured by Metagenics (although at half-dosage).

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-26T15:22:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sunny-south-africa-part-1-of-3.6880/post-84873>

Hi Fran and welcome.

ask as many questions as you can. people will help where they can.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-26T16:36:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sunny-south-africa-part-1-of-3.6880/post-84882>

First three labs........show it all. High inflammation low DHEA and low D. So begin to read the 6/30/12 blog on DHEA. then read the Vitamin D blog called the Sunshine of your life. Inflammation is all about leptin resistance and we have a ton of blogs on that.......enjoy the ride.

### caroline — 2013-04-27T02:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sunny-south-africa-part-1-of-3.6880/post-84910>

Good luck Fran finding answers ..... do all the reading that Dr. K. suggests and then go back and re read again .... you will gain more understanding each time and start connecting the dots .... and ask lots of questions - it helps everyone.

### Dextery — 2013-05-07T20:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-water-deficit.6884/post-85578>

When I posted about my blood viscosity increasing due to spring water consumption, Dr K pointed to what nonchalant said in response:

"Your zeta potential is increasing. With good zeta potential, the red blood cells do not clump and your blood is less viscous. When your blood is drawn for testing, it should be a faster process."
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthre...Water-and-blood-viscosity&highlight=viscosity](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6372-Flouride-free-Spring-Water-and-blood-viscosity&highlight=viscosity)

My take away is that if stick yourself as you would when drawing blood for measuring FBG, your blood should not form a bubble on your finger if you are drinking enough non flouridated water. The blood should lay flat. One crude measure in addition to the 140mg of sodium measure.

### caroline — 2013-05-08T01:22:19+0300

I am able to drink so much more of the sparkling water and feel much more thirst now. Before I never felt thirsty at all and was forgetting to drink. Last night I drank 24 oz within 2 hours of going to bed and wound up going to the bathroom only once and that was because I woke up and thought I might just as well go. Prior to that - I was getting up a few times during the night.

Anyone else have a similar experience?

### JanSz — 2013-05-09T04:25:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-water-deficit.6884/post-85697>

According to Mae-Wan Ho, there is a lots of coherent water.

> ISIS Report 25/07/11
> Quantum Coherent Water and Life
>
> Water is quantum coherent under ordinary conditions, according to a quantum electrodynamics field theory that may explain many of its most paradoxical properties including life itself.
>
>
>
> [http://www.i-sis.org.uk/Quantum\_Coherent\_Water\_Life.php](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/Quantum_Coherent_Water_Life.php)
>
>
> > **Liquid water is therefore a two-fluid system \[5\] (in analogy with superfluid helium) consisting of a coherent phase (about 40 percent of total volume at room temperature) and an incoherent phase.** In the coherent phase, the water molecules oscillate between two electronic configurations in phase with a resonating EMF. The common frequency of the EMF and the electronic oscillation of the coherent phase being 0.26 eV; whereas the energy difference of the two electronic configuration of the coherent phase is 12.06 eV, which gives the wavelength of 1 000 A (100 nm) of the coherence domain. The remaining 60 percent incoherent phase is extracted by thermal fluctuations from the coherent phase. The two phases have widely different dielectric constants: that of the coherent phase is 160, due to the high polarizability of the coherently aligned water molecules that are oscillating in concert; while the dielectric constant of the incoherent state is about 15. The externally applied electric fields are therefore only felt in the non-coherent phase.
> >
> > This picture of liquid water, according to Del Giudice and colleagues, is reflected in the many observations supporting a two-state model of water (see \[1, 10\] Two-States Water Explains All? SiS 32), in which a substantial fraction of the molecules exist in hydrogen bonded state resembling that of ordinary ice. In fact, the hydrogen-bonds - short range interactions â€“ are the consequence of the induced coherence in the coherence domains. But there is a rapid interchange of molecules between the CDs and the incoherent phase, hence it is impossible to detect CDs when the detection time is longer than the period of the oscillations, which is less than 10-13 s.
> >
> > **Quantum coherent water and life**
> >
> > Oxidation and reduction or redox reactions are the stuff of energy transduction in living organisms. It involves transfer of electrons from one substance (donor) to another (acceptor) to power all living activities. But where does the electron come from? It comes ultimately from splitting water in photosynthesis by green plants and cyanobacteria. However, it takes 12.60 eV to split water, an energy corresponding to soft X-rays, which is not what the green plants and cyanobacteria use.
> >
> > More than 50 years ago, Szent-Gyorgyi \[9\] suggested that water at interfaces was the key. He proposed that water in living organisms existed in two states: the ground state and the excited state, and that water at interfaces such as membranes existed in the excited state, which requires considerably lower energy to split. A sign of the excited water is that a voltage should appear at the boundary between interfacial water and bulk water, which was indeed observed. This property of water enables energy transfer to take place in living organisms ensuring long-lasting electronic excitations. Szent-Gyorgyiâ€™s ideas were largely ignored by the scientific mainstream that became obsessed instead with molecular genetics.
> >
> > The anomalous water at interfaces has been the subject of numerous research papers and reviews \[11\], and was already known in the late 1940s, as Del Giudice and colleagues point out \[4\]. Most if not all water in living organisms is interfacial water, as it is almost never further away from surfaces such as membranes or macromolecules than a fraction of a micron.
>
>
>
>
> [http://www.amazon.com/Living-Rainbo...1368040106&sr=8-1&keywords=Living+Rainbow+H2O](http://www.amazon.com/Living-Rainbow-H2O-Mae-Wan-Ho/dp/9814390895/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1368040106&sr=8-1&keywords=Living+Rainbow+H2O)

# Thread: Paprika
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paprika.6886/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-27T03:40:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paprika.6886/post-84923>

Jack advises certain people use paprika for niacin I think, but it also contains the red-orange carotenoid _**beta cryptoxanthin**_. Studies mention that beta cryptoxanthin inhibits adipogenesis without the damaging side effects of drugs , lowers the risk of polyarthritis by 50%, and can be converted to vitamin A.
Red peppers (paprika), pumpkin, and citrus are good sources of this carotenoid.

Anyone know how much sweet red paprika makes an effective helping to protect against polyarthritis or reduce adipogenesis in humans?

We are passionate about promoting a more grounded way of living and we would love to hear how Earthing has positively effected your life. Whether your earthing practice takes place indoors or outdoors (sleeping grounded, grounded computer pad, barefooting, swimming in the ocean, hiking, running, camping, etc) we would love to hear about it. # Thread: Scientist Proves DNA Can Be Reprogrammed by Words and Frequencies
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientist-proves-dna-can-be-reprogrammed-by-words-and-frequencies.6888/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-04-27T05:42:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientist-proves-dna-can-be-reprogrammed-by-words-and-frequencies.6888/post-84929>

[http://www.collective-evolution.com...can-be-reprogrammed-by-words-and-frequencies/](http://www.collective-evolution.com/2011/09/02/scientist-prove-dna-can-be-reprogrammed-by-words-and-frequencies/)

"Living chromosomes function just like solitonic/holographic computers using the endogenous DNA laser radiation.” This means that they managed for example to modulate certain frequency patterns onto a laser ray and with it influenced the DNA frequency and thus the genetic information itself. Since the basic structure of DNA-alkaline pairs and of language (as explained earlier) are of the same structure, no DNA decoding is necessary."

I think this article encompasses everything that Dr Kruse is trying to teach us.

Our DNA is malable by our thoughts and by outside influences of radio frequencies...either to the good of ourselves or other people. The mystic healers are able to generate frequencies within their bodies to influence the DNA of others to repair their DNA in the healing process. Researchers are able to tune radio waves to repair DNA thus validating what the mystics, seers, shamans do within themselves.

If this can be done to repair DNA, think how destructive the EMF radio waves are to our own DNA that is random and uncontrolled.

The article talks about group communication among the same species and how that has served each species through the millenium...When a queen ant is alive and well, all the workers know exactly what to do....no matter how far the queen is apart from the colony. But kill the queen without another to replace her, the workers have no idea of what to do. She contained the building blocks of the colony.

Get a bunch of like minded people together and the synergy of the thought processes feed off of each other.

I recall about 20 years ago, a late night talk show host was lamenting about a huge forest fire raging in the southeast..I think it was Georgia. A caller said why not get all the listeners to focus their individual energy on creating rain over Georgia sufficient to snuff out the blaze. He preceded to encourage all his listeners...about 1.5 million at any one time to bring rain to Georgia when there was no rain in sight for at least 10 days. Within 48 hours there were rain storms cropping up everywhere over Georgia to the extent that there was huge flooding and loss of life. He never did it again. But I think it demonstrated the power of the collective mind...the collective DNA is far more powerful than we can imagine.

"DNA: It apparently is also an organic superconductor that can work at normal body temperature. Artificial superconductors require extremely low temperatures of between 200 and 140Â°C to function. As one recently learned, all superconductors are able to store light and thus information. This is a further explanation of how the DNA can store information. There is another phenomenon linked to DNA and wormholes. Normally, these supersmall wormholes are highly unstable and are maintained only for the tiniest fractions of a second. Under certain conditions stable wormholes can organize themselves which then form distinctive vacuum domains in which for example gravity can transform into electricity".

How many of you are in tune with another person that you know in advance what they are going to say?

Be careful what you wish for because your DNA is listening and that is not a joke.

Much more in the article to contemplate.

### prAna303 — 2013-04-27T09:28:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientist-proves-dna-can-be-reprogrammed-by-words-and-frequencies.6888/post-84936>

This is kind of funny. Today we are making computers and we think we invent a lot of new things but the computer is just a copy of ourself. The same problems we get when we are running a server we also suffer as humans. ItÂ´s weird that many donÂ´t see that connection today. A powerful server has at least redundant power supplies, redundant networkconnections and so on. On top of that we put a program to run it. If any of these parts donÂ´t work in harmony we get problems, just like we do as humans.

I think that we can understand ourself better today if we compare ourself with these things because the human you has become alien to us.

Many of the things you write above is in line with my own thinkings on life in the big.

Nice thoughts!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-27T15:34:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientist-proves-dna-can-be-reprogrammed-by-words-and-frequencies.6888/post-84940>

### Optimalbound — 2013-05-09T15:58:26+0300

### nonchalant — 2013-05-09T16:18:32+0300

# Thread: Always cloudy here-how to simulate morning sunshine artificially???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennyha — 2013-04-27T18:58:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/post-84946>

Hello forum. Thanks to all who chimed in on my frequent urination post. I appreciate the info.

My next question is regarding the Adrenal Rx/Leptin Rx. It says the first thing to do is to stare at the sunshine when you get up in the morning. I live in the Chicago area, and it's literally cloudy at least 5 days a week. Plus I have a building in my way that blocks the sun.

My question- is there anyway to simulate that morning sunshine I am missing? Do they make some kind of light box/light bulbs that give off lights that mimic the sunlight? I really appreciate any insight anyone can give me. Thank you so much!

### Inger — 2013-04-27T20:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/post-84950>

Bennyha,
I stare into the sky one minute be it sun or not, every morning. Today there were lot of clouds and I still could feel how very bright the light was to my eyes.
Jack has said many times, it does not matter if cloudy. So just try to get somewhere where you can see the sky where the sun rises (east). There need to be no glass or glasses or anything between the sky and your eyes, this is important. I sometimes also just look out from my open bedroom window into the sky as it opens to east.
Also never use sunglasses at daytime, ever. This is really bad for our health! Try to get as much daylight in your eyes as possible. I have moved my computer in front of the window, and also a small table in right by the window in my small kitchen, if I need to do any work I do it in daylight now.

There is no way to replace sunlight with artificial light..whatever marketing they use. Natural light wins - always.

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-04-28T15:37:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/post-84991>

> [bennyha said:](/goto/post?id=84946)
>
> My question- is there anyway to simulate that morning sunshine I am missing? Do they make some kind of light box/light bulbs that give off lights that mimic the sunlight? I really appreciate any insight anyone can give me. Thank you so much!

Google 'Philips goLITE BLU energy light'

### bionaut — 2013-04-28T15:57:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/post-84993>

I own one of these. It outputs 450-470nm light so it will stimulate the SCN. You should bear in mind that even on cloudy days daylight can contain light in the blue spectrum. It works well as a tool to help get on track with the circadian rhythm if I am traveling or have had a crazy week. I just don't have the temperance to use it consistently because I enjoy the daytime much more.

### bennyha — 2013-04-28T16:47:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/post-84996>

Awesome guys thank you so much! This is such a great forum where everyone just tried helping one another. I have many more questions on grounding and EMF's as all that stuff is so confusing to me, but I will just address one thing at a time and go from there.

So as long as I go outside and stare toward the east when I wake up, even if blocked by buildings, this will be good enough, correct? And just for a couple minutes?

Thanks again guys I appreciate all the input!

# Thread: Welcome to my world...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 225
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MrPinkies — 2013-04-28T01:32:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-84965>

As most of you have read.. I'm on a personal mission to lose 160lbs or so. I'm not really sure how far I am going to go with this.. but.. since the weight seems to come off, why not go all the way??!? Seems like a natural thing to do, right? I am FINALLY, the first time in 13 years, under 200 lbs!!!! I've tried EVERYTHING under the sun to drop the weight.. multipliable personal trainers.. countless gym memberships.. an embarrassing amount of gimmick diets, equipment, etc.. Beyond frustrated, tried the lepton reset, and HOLY COW, I started to drop 1-2lbs per DAY... I started to really understand my issues. I am a sugar addict like an crack addicted- but with sugar. I still struggle every day... however, the REALLY obvious bad stuff, like pizza, etc.. that stuff I don't crave anymore... in fact, a lot of that stuff makes me sick even thinking about it... I still make mistakes and don't lose like I want, but, I guess this is a process... right? I am still the one not to eat fish.. I just can't do it.. fish eyes freak me out!! and their scales.. my husband picking out the bones in his mouth.. ughhhh.. can't or won't.. I guess I won't.. but if I'm still losing weight, should I care? probably.. I just am having a hard time getting over their googlie eyes and the bones things.. and the scales.. There is hope for me.. I will eat shrimp and some other sea-stuff... lol

Enough about me, I guess.. The mix to my situation is.. well.. Autism... My whole family- husband- Christopher (who is actively on the forum too), both kids, especially my son Ryan.. I understood most of the podcast that was recommended (it's in the ask Jack section).. Holy carb-ness... We cleaned out the fridge and the kitchen.. Ryan is definitely starting week three of the straight, no cheating, ketogenic diet, as much spring non-fluoridated H2O as possible, extra fat on the side... We actually had to say to our kids grandparents that they will not be able to spend time with them for a while- a few months or so. I was laughing so hard though the thread that is labeled "\*\*\*t our families says", I almost pi$$ed my pants.. but.. man am I in that boat.. we can NOT trust our families anymore, period. The unbelievable CRAP that spews out. I almost feel like they want us to fail. They either don't accept Autism diagnoses at all or then when something comes along that does work- they do everything in their power to do the exact opposite... Everything we have done to try to chase this- what seems unrealistic- dream of catching up all of the gaps in his skills and all of his sensory issues with his nervous system. Changing his diet has had the MOST impact. It is CLEARLY a night and day difference. Changing the light bulbs.. selling our tv and iPad.. getting rid of our cell phones that gave my husband hemorrhoids that he decided to make so public- silly boy.. hard-wiring our computers.. Just seems like a TON of changes that.. quite honestly.. has made our lives easier.. My kids sleep through the night, FINALLY at five years old, my son SLEEPS!!!! My son rarely screams over noise, lights, or other sensory issues.. Turning off the wifi was CRAZY awesome reaction.. My husband and I kinda looked at each other asking each other if that really was the cause of our son's screaming?? It wasn't entirely, but, we could tell that it did have an impact on him. Even the school called this week and asked if we were doing anything different at home because of Ryan's positive behavior changes, in fact, he's going to be mainstreamed next year.. I never in a million years thought that that was going to be possible if you were to have asked me 6 months ago. Is mainstreaming him the right thing? Who knows.. I am the girl that will jump in with both feet and see what happens I guess... Wish me luck!

I have learned so much.. life is complicated.. but it seems to be getting easier by the day.. Although, it's been interesting, I'm trying to find the positive in this journey.. positive people.. Where are they? OH YES.. definitely in this form, of course! hahahaa...

Keep the positive rollin' cuz I'm likin' it!!!! Up and onward to greatness!! Until next time..

### caroline — 2013-04-28T03:09:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-84973>

I love your post ....... I wish you so much luck ....but it is so much more than that! You have a tenacity that is awe inspiring.
You will influence so many more people than just your immediate family - the world is your oyster! The ripple effects from the changes you are making are endless. I am a person that never gives up too - for a variety of reasons - but then again - I think a lot of people give up way too easy. You need to explore every possible avenue and then do it again if need be!

Keep jumping in with both feet ..... is there any other way?

please keep posting on your progress - good, bad or indifferent - we all learn so much from each other......

### nonchalant — 2013-04-28T05:12:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-84976>

Awesome progress, Mr. Pink!
I think I understand totally about the grandparents.

### cantweight — 2013-04-28T05:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-84978>

I am so happy for all of your successes!!! I have about 130 to lose myself and my kids have "issues" as well. My youngest went non-verbal for a brief period as a toddler and later developed 2 motor tics and a vocal tic and a stutter. I had him to some amazing doctors, childrens hospital neurologists, and by chance happened to take him to crossfit with me (yes my fat ass did crossfit..lol) and one of the trainers there told me to take him off grains. LIFE CHANGING!!!

Within 3 weeks this kid had 0 tics and no stutter!!! I could not believe it! He could not believe it!

We have not been able to keep them 100% at bay since the initial diet change but it is hardly noticeable and there is no fear of him needing to leave his classroom anymore, so whew!

I am hopeful that with CT, focus on his supplements and clearing up any nastiness lurking in his labs he will know life without the "issues"

I love your spirit! And it helps to know I am not alone in having so much to lose.....and even more to gain

Good luck and keep us posted!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-84981>

Hey!!!  Awesome goal of 130!! You CAN do it!!! I'm down 120lbs roughly out of the 160 total.. It takes time, but, it WILL happen! (if you go on my profile, I have before and after pictures!)

As far as your kids goes.. all I can say is... AWESOME!!!!!!!!! and.. AMAZING!!!!!! It's funny when people around us see the changes in our kids, but then, don't believe what we did.. that "food" of all things, was the problem and or the answer... When I first watched the podcast, I was so angry.. mainly because the answer seemed too simple. Although, the no carb thing is EXTREMELY hard.. My daughter is more addicted to carbs that I am.. lol My son could take it or leave it most days.. We did find an AWESOME chocolate chip recipe that is pretty decent considering the diet..

Last, I will definitely keep you posted..  forum stuff is fun and more addicted than FB.. OMG, did I just type that?!?!? I DID! hmm.. something to think about.. lol

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-09T03:57:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85690>

Sorry friends that I have been gone for a little while.. I got sick.. my throat got very irritated.. and then came the cough.. and then I couldn't breath.. and you know.. breathing is kinda important.. Of course, it was only a matter of time that I got sick.. My kids were sick first.. Since we have been attempting to mainstream Ryan.. he went from a class of 5 to a class of 25+... so.. he got a little bit of everything.. starting with strep.. then flu.. then pink eye.. then croup... (shaking my fist at the sky).. Croup is one of the scariest things I have ever experienced with my son.. When we got to the ER, he was a shade of grey with purple lips.. Er doctors looked at each other, wondering why the hell we didn't take an ambulance.. I'll tell you why.. the last time Ryan had to take an ambulance, we went less than five miles, and it costed over 1600 dollars... So anyway.. getting sick set me back a few pounds... I blame the cough drops and inhaler, etc.. but.. now that I'm back into the swing of things and I can breath (again, the importance of breathing.. lol).. the pounds are coming off again.. slow but surely.. the smaller I get, the slower it comes off.. oh well.. my main goal was to get under 200 lbs- check! so.. I'm pretty happy with that..

My question to you is.. what do you do for coughs and colds and stuffed up nose??? Sudafed is kinda a favorite to get the nose stuffiness all cleared out.. but.. I'm thinking that's probably not the right thing to do anymore??? or is it?

### caroline — 2013-05-09T04:09:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85692>

My grandson gets croup ..... Very scarey. My daughter always turns the hot water on in the shower and sticks him in there - but I am sure you know that. She does call an ambulance but they have coverage.

### cantweight — 2013-05-09T05:15:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85701>

So sorry you were sick! I hated when the kids would pick up every germ at school..ugh.

We do a lot of neti-pot, or the neil-med squirt bottle for stuffy noses. Sometimes with a drop of eucalyptus or tea tree oil. When it gets really bad I go for the mucinex...guifenisin.
Salt water gargles for the sore throats are good too!

Glad yall are feeling better...your pics are awesome...I can not wait to be under 200!!!!

### Inger — 2013-05-09T08:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85710>

> [MrPinkies said:](/goto/post?id=85690)
>
> My question to you is.. what do you do for coughs and colds and stuffed up nose??? Sudafed is kinda a favorite to get the nose stuffiness all cleared out.. but.. I'm thinking that's probably not the right thing to do anymore??? or is it?

Glad you feel better! For stuffed nose you might try salt water flush? One nostril at time, and "breath" up saltwater in your nose. You could put a half raw onion by your bedside too, not so good smell but could help! At least it has helped me.. and then quite any dairy if you eat it... We also put a piece of raw garlic in some cotton into our nose as kids when we had this.. and also some peppermint oil around the nose might help - be careful to not irritate the skin too much tho! I also do herb teas and steam baths, for 10 minutes or so. You need to use a pot and maybe 2-3 liters of water+herbs and then put a towel over your head and breath the steam. You can use rosemary, salvia, thyme, chamomille, mint... I drink the teas too.

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-13T19:06:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85953>

Thanks to everyone that replied about the stuffy nose and sore throat!  I will definitely keep those ideas in mind for next time!

Next, Happy Mothers Day to all of those awesome Moms that we have out there!

Well, a few days ago, Chris (who is also on the forum.. randomly I might add.. lol) and I sent out an email to our parents and siblings... OK, ok.. the emails were all me.. I'm the bad guy.. lol We gave the ultimate awesome news that Ryan and Lizzie both will be going into a mainstreamed classrooms next year.. and that he will be in FIRST grade! Holy cow.. my kid is going to FIRST grade!!!!! This wasn't an option in January.. We were looking at another year in Kindergarten in the ASD classroom.. It took us 7 months to get his/our diet squared away- without cheating.. FINALLY 6 weeks ago, it FINALLY happened.. no screw ups.. We unfortunately had to tell grandparents that we were taking a break from get-togethers to get the diet up and going. They made a huge fuss.. but you know what?? that is their problem, not ours.. I finally feel like we drew the line in the sand.. follow our rules or don't see our kids.. it's that simple now.. sucks that we had to do it, but glad we did.. by the end of the email, it was the "rules", clear cut rules...

So.. Mother's Day came around.. and we started a conversation with my dear husband's parents... They told us that everyone that they talked to told them how dangerous it was and that we were going to ruin our children's kidneys... and if we prayed hard enough, they would be healed from the all mighty upstairs.. Me? I'm not a big fan of the big guy upstairs.. just not.. never was.. never will be... My black and white thinking does the trick.. I like what I can see and touch and feel.. I like hard proof and science.. However, before everyone gets their panties in a bunch, I think evolution fits within what's in the bible.. I don't exactly throw it out the window completely.. or do I? Oh well..

Well, back to my "fun" on Mother's day.. Ryan got over stimulated by having his grandparents over (especially when they tickle him) and he started to spin and run in circles in the living room.. If it had been January, this would have caused him ultimately to get to a point where he would have ended the spinning with throwing himself on the ground screaming.. for hours.. (and I mean.. the whole rest of the day...) on end... Well, I asked him to stop spinning. He didn't like that, but, he sat down on the couch for a minute.. and then he went back to spinning.. I asked him to sit and count to 50. He again didn't want to stop, but, then, he said, "I want to talk to you". I said ok, let's talk.. He told me that he didn't want to stop and that he likes spinning. I forgot what I suggested to him, but, the long of the short was I distracted him with something else that he likes to do.. There was NO screaming.. no tantrum or meltdown..

This is called... MAJOR PROGRESS..

There is NO denying this kind of progress.. there is nothing that could be said for excuses of why this happened.. This happened primarily because we have stuck to this diet and it is working... Can I get an AMEN??!?!?!?!!!

### caroline — 2013-05-14T00:25:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85965>

You most certainly have an AMEN from me.......way to hang in there!

Why is it so tough for people we care about to see great changes in us ..... and then dismiss them out of hand.........????

### cantweight — 2013-05-14T01:06:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85967>

A-freaking-men! I am so proud of you for defeating the grandparent food beast! That is still soooo hard for us! Our parents just dont get it. They tell the kids a little gluten wont hurt you and my 8 year old believes it and regurgitates the words to me. God! Makes me so mad! They just dont get it, and its not even lack of trying, they seriously just dont get it. They think its great when they see the better health, the diminished neuro issues...they just cant put the pieces together.

I commend you on a job well done mom and dad!!! Mainstreamed!!!! That is huge!!! Yay!!

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-14T01:11:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85968>

>
> Why is it so tough for people we care about to see great changes in us ..... and then dismiss them out of hand.........????

WOOT WOOT!

yep- welcome to my world.. lol

we desperately want our children to have a healthy relationship with the grandparents.. but.. it's more important that their brain function properly.. right? that sounds sane?

I was joking with a friend that "gets it".. and I told her its been like.. oh.. you're allergic to peanuts? Here's a peanut butter and jelly sandwich... as a treat.. because Mom and Dad never let you have it at home! LOL It's sad, but funny and f'ed up at the same time!!! seriously!!

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-14T01:19:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85969>

you are becoming my new bff.. lol just so you know..  they aren't (or at least my parents and my in laws) don't see it as an illness... in fact, they chose to sorta ignore Autism as if it wasn't even there (it's talent to do so).. which as the parent, it's a slap in the face.. with all the therapy, etc that I do and diet stuff, etc that I do with my children.. It feels like it takes away from my life works of what I am suppose to do in life... I love my job and career as an Autism mom..  but also as a mom I feel like my toes are always being stepped on.. and the boundaries are always broken.. well.. guess what.. NO MORE! I've had enough.. especially when it WORKS!

### caroline — 2013-05-14T01:27:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85970>

I am on the other side of the fence - so to speak .... I am the grandmother that cares and would turn over every rock to get answers - but they look at me like I am from Mars and I get a lot of eye rolling etc. I was trying to tell one daughter about flouride ......... she is a very smart girl and her husband thinks he is super smart ..LOL ...but ......If it is in the water - we must need it.....they wouldn't put it there for no reason - would they???

I admire your tenacity and courage ..... the grandparents are missing out on so much.....

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-14T01:48:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85973>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=85970)
>
> I am on the other side of the fence - so to speak .... I am the grandmother that cares and would turn over every rock to get answers - but they look at me like I am from Mars and I get a lot of eye rolling etc. I was trying to tell one daughter about flouride ......... she is a very smart girl and her husband thinks he is super smart ..LOL ...but ......If it is in the water - we must need it.....they wouldn't put it there for no reason - would they???
>
> I admire your tenacity and courage ..... the grandparents are missing out on so much.....

I got to a point where I was trying anything and everything to figure this sucker out.. Autism itself is a straight up b\*tch sometimes.. There were times where Ryan would literally scream for 6 plus hours and there was JACK I could do for him.. touching him just was one more thing for his little brain to process.. I sat on the floor other side of the door of his bedroom in tears.. I don't drink for this reason.. ever.. and even if he could calm down.. he didn't have words to explain what was wrong.. and just for an example, one day.. I realized I was putting on the right sock before the left one.. and he wanted his left sock on first.. his brain literally couldn't handle the fact that I was putting the right before the left.. Ryan is extremely sensitive to lights and noise.. and touch.. and all eyes, ears, mouth, nose, fingers, and toes all needed a break... My life has been forever changed.. I went to Lansing (the state capital) and got to testify to the House of Reps, literally begging for the medical insurance mandate to pass..

It's been a long.. very long four (starting five) years.. I would have given ANYTHING to be where we are now, years ago.. I never would have thought we would be here... is it perfect.. hell no.. is it better?? yep.. and so much better life that I'm screaming at the top of my lungs for all my other Autism Mommy friends to hear what I am telling them, but, no one is listening..

### cantweight — 2013-05-14T01:55:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85974>

Oh Audrey you are just way ahead of your time...can you be my kids adopted grandma!!!

Pinkies I have much to learn from you my friend. I have a 25 yr old brother (still living with my mother) that was diagnosed with aspergers, different docs had him on and off the autism spectrum his whole life. He is very high functioning but my family refuses to recognize his confusion, his difficulty with socialization, even his stemming. They wont help him and he is as convinced as they are that "there is no issue". Not even allowed to say the "A" word in their presence...it is so weird, I dont understand.

I want so badly to help my brother he is so frustrated! He has no friends, never been in a relationship, has a very hard time with job interviews and cant hold a job for long, his diet is horrible, he is so sick of me telling what he should try to help himself....its heartbreaking. Any advice on how I could help him? I just want to see him happy.

### caroline — 2013-05-14T02:17:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welcome-to-my-world.6898/post-85976>

I would love to be your kid's adopted grandma ..... I feel pretty useless around here! \[home I mean!\]

I think we should talk about this more somewhere here - with JK's help...
Why is anything we say falling on deaf ears? I try not to say much and only with kindness - but any information seems to be threatening for some reason.

I firmly believe that all of us that have ended up here on this forum are a different breed - I have always looked for answers and listened to new theories etc. etc. etc. I never felt threatened - I just wanted to learn. I always thought that there must be answers somewhere - I just had to look hard enough......

People that call us sheep are so, so far off the mark - we are the direct opposite.....

I don't understand why people wouldn't try anything to have a better life - you are only proposing dietary changes - why is that so threatening????? and maybe - by some crazy miracle - it could help......

When you are coming from a place of love and concern and caring - why does that not compute???
As the messengers - what are we doing wrong?

This seems like a no brainer - isn't deleting wheat and sugar pretty mainstream for Autism these days?
