### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-02T05:00:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85280>

truth is found by exploring the reaches we dont look........never settle

### cantweight — 2013-05-02T05:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85281>

At 6 and 8yrs old my son and daughter were hypothyroid. They both took Cytomel, TG 100 - natural glandular tissue supplement, and slew of other supps.

It was only about 3-6 mos and they were off the cytomel just stayed on the TG 100 and supps for an additional few months. Thyroid function has been fine since

My daughter was always cold, and had similar mood swings to what you mentioned Gretchen. I was also diagnosed bi-polar so I thought...she was gonna be crazy like mama...lol
Little did I know I wasn't crazy at all  My son had low temp. Both had extreme fatigue...extreme. People always commented how well behaved my children were....they just had mad fatigue!

Way to be a kick ass mama!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-02T05:49:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85283>

a poignant thought.........maybe none of you want to think about. Why would a child become hypothyroid in this world so fast? Have you thought about it........forget the emotions. Do you believe evolution works in this fashion? Of is something else altering the rules of the game of life. No answers needed.......I'd rather you think about it......

Do you mind your "mindset"? You need to control the manner you in which you think? At night I look back at my day and contemplate what I did and how I thought about what I did. There are different kinds of mindsets... a millionaire mindset... a creative genius mindset... a growth mindset. These are very specific and each have their own area of focus. A growth mindset combines a love of learning with the cultivation of our talents and gifts. It also combines hard work and dedication with the desire to keep exploring our true potential. What kind of mindset you keep will determine what you can become. Tonight before you sleep, use your thoughts to determine your future. Plan a new course with your dreams tonight; we don't have to think better. We don't have to think smarter, but we must "think" differently to get better. Ask yourself: What am I thinking about now, and change it?

### Inger — 2013-05-02T07:56:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85285>

Wow. Nice stuff. I love mamas with great instincts. That have the courage to follow them. And I love what Jack write above.. about our thoughts. Very powerful and edifying!

### bigknitwit — 2013-05-02T15:39:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85297>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=85270)
>
> she complains of being cold - in heat, and hot in cold. her hands and feet are constantly cold. she's has these mood swings that come out of normal - reminds me of when I was dx w/bi-polar. after recently learning from my PCP that undiagnosed hyperthyroid can be misdiagnosed as bipolar - I'm thinking those mood swings were the driving factor for the testing... b/c they weren't normal 5yo behavior... kwim?
>
>
>
> AB is next up... as is geting in w/a pediatric endo good call on the spot iodine test - we have been supplmenting both kids with 2%lugols...

It's so frustrating when the doctor says to "do nothing" and repeat the test in 2 months. I would definitely push for the anti-body testing, preferably done by labcorp as they give an absolute number, even when it's below the "positive" cut off line. Quest only gives you an absolute number if you're above the positive.

The mood swings that seem to come from nowhere is something I've seen from my 2, but of course there's so many reasons it's hard to discern more. They would come out of nowhere and so very difficult to deal with. I always said that I could always, with enough force/determination control the behavior of the child, but I can not control mood, and it's the mood that's so upsetting, because of course we all want to see our children generally content in their own skin.

My oldest's TSH was as high as 7ish at age 6, negative antibodies. I took him to 3 endocrinologists, including the head of pediatric endocrinology at Children's Hospital in Boston. Nobody would touch him. It was very frustrating, to say the least. The gluten free diet helped bring it down to the 3ish range (which is where he and my other non-AI DD seem to sit). I think I remember someone telling me it was somewhat "normal" for children's TSH to run a little higher than adults. I'm not sure I believe that, but I'm just passing it along as it's something you may hear and need to be prepared for...

Also, I can say that my experience dealing with ped. endos for my auto-immune son was not good. She kept his TSH as high in the range as possible - like 4.5 was fine ("to stimulate his own gland as much as possible"), which left him horrendously symptomatic, but I didn't know as much back then. The final straw was 2 years into treatment when I asked for a celiac screen (having read that one auto-immune = more likely others), and there it was, he railed to celiac marker test. I was so angry... I have had much better luck with a doctor of environmental medicine, as well as a regular family practionner. I recommend looking at the thyroid "top doc" list done by STTM, listing doctors patients have had good experiences with, including docs willing to prescribe NDT. It's a good starting point at least.

I'm not sure that any of this helps, or gives you more insight than you already have. All my kids get a full thyroid panel done annually, you may want to do a screen on your younger child as well. If nothing else, it gives you a baseline.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-02T16:45:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamas-always-trust-your-instincts-with-your-kids.6936/post-85303>

> [bigknitwit said:](/goto/post?id=85297)
>
> It's so frustrating when the doctor says to "do nothing" and repeat the test in 2 months. I would definitely push for the anti-body testing, preferably done by labcorp as they give an absolute number, even when it's below the "positive" cut off line. Quest only gives you an absolute number if you're above the positive.
>
> The mood swings that seem to come from nowhere is something I've seen from my 2, but of course there's so many reasons it's hard to discern more. They would come out of nowhere and so very difficult to deal with. I always said that I could always, with enough force/determination control the behavior of the child, but I can not control mood, and it's the mood that's so upsetting, because of course we all want to see our children generally content in their own skin.
>
> My oldest's TSH was as high as 7ish at age 6, negative antibodies. I took him to 3 endocrinologists, including the head of pediatric endocrinology at Children's Hospital in Boston. Nobody would touch him. It was very frustrating, to say the least. The gluten free diet helped bring it down to the 3ish range (which is where he and my other non-AI DD seem to sit). I think I remember someone telling me it was somewhat "normal" for children's TSH to run a little higher than adults. I'm not sure I believe that, but I'm just passing it along as it's something you may hear and need to be prepared for...
>
> Also, I can say that my experience dealing with ped. endos for my auto-immune son was not good. She kept his TSH as high in the range as possible - like 4.5 was fine ("to stimulate his own gland as much as possible"), which left him horrendously symptomatic, but I didn't know as much back then. The final straw was 2 years into treatment when I asked for a celiac screen (having read that one auto-immune = more likely others), and there it was, he railed to celiac marker test. I was so angry... I have had much better luck with a doctor of environmental medicine, as well as a regular family practionner. I recommend looking at the thyroid "top doc" list done by STTM, listing doctors patients have had good experiences with, including docs willing to prescribe NDT. It's a good starting point at least.
>
> I'm not sure that any of this helps, or gives you more insight than you already have. All my kids get a full thyroid panel done annually, you may want to do a screen on your younger child as well. If nothing else, it gives you a baseline.

thanks... we held off on screening Havoc b/c he's not showing symptoms or the crazy mood swings. though once we find a good endo we will consider it.

I've got 3 endos we're looking at, and I'm also getting references from friends who have kids that are seeing endos. so far I've received a warning to stay a way from one at Childern's National Medical Center. I'm also considering taking DD to see my PCP - as she treats kids. I may just send her an email - and see if she's treated hypo kids before. even if she doesn't treat kids for thyroid - she might have a good recommendation. NDT will be our only option medication wise.

What worries me about DD is the fact that we're both gluten and grain free - she knows she shouldn't be eating things like cookies/crackers etc. so I worry what has happened is that my un-diagnosed hypothyroidism has been epigenetically transferred to dd. Her labs pretty much resemble mine when I'm not on meds... with the exception that she has symptoms, and I don't.

Thanks for the heads up on the Higher TSH - definitely going to do some research on this. Since we have UHC we can only get our lab work done via Labcorp. so that's a positive.

one thing I'm thinking is that looking at her labs, and her DHEA-S levels - she's got some gut issues going on. going to have to work on fixing that for her as well.

### cantweight — 2013-05-02T18:48:22+0300

# Thread: I guess we all have a new disease...Orthorexia!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6

### RayB — 2013-05-02T16:25:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/post-85300>

The food police have now stepped in with a new label. Next step....a patent on a mind altering drug.
I am already a HYPERchondriac.

[http://beforeitsnews.com/alternativ...lth-conscious-are-now-extremists-2635136.html](http://beforeitsnews.com/alternative/2013/05/orthorexia-the-health-conscious-are-now-extremists-2635136.html)

### nonchalant — 2013-05-02T16:51:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/post-85305>

Actually, the guy has a point. "â€œI monitor a lot of my clientsâ€™ weight, and if they are dropping really fast, and it seems they are going to extremes, youâ€™ve got to question them and say, â€˜Letâ€™s make sure you are staying healthy,â€™â€￾ said Volney."

I know of several people who have dug down deep and by sheer awsome willpower, lost quite a bit of weight, using government guidelines. They soon became sick, however, with bone problems, mental problems, heart problems, etc. It's so SAD. I think government guidelines should be labeled "extreme".

### RayB — 2013-05-02T17:02:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/post-85307>

I am kinda one of those people. Lost a good bit of weight, although not very fast, but using "conventional" guidelines. Now I am trying to undo the damage done.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-02T18:37:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/post-85314>

Its unfortunate that so many individuals got to extremes to lose the weight and neglect eating important fats. I suspect that's why so many people blame loss of carbohydrates for the extreme symptoms when in actuality they screwed themselves on the fat intake along with calorie restriction.

By the way, I noticed that my creaky shoulder joints are lots better now. It took awhile after losing alot of weight for the skeletal system to adapt or remodel or whatever it does.

### nunki — 2013-06-03T15:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/post-87373>

I was all with him until he had to lump people into the group "Mollify Your Conscience by Demonizing the Truth â€“ Ask the Experts â€“ the Griping, Whining, Conniving Zionists." Hmm...Seems a little too bigoted for me. He would have had a valid point if it had been "the Griping, Whining, Conniving junk food peddlers".

Not even sure what this had to do with the so called "eating disorder" part, or if it was the start of another article. But, definitely disturbing.

# Thread: I recieved 2 CT scans, Am I cooked?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Arclite — 2013-05-02T20:09:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85326>

Monday morning I was stabbed twice by a mentally ill meth addict, once in the lower back on the spine, and once on the ribs, about four inches below my armpit. I was able to fend off the attack and subdue him until the police arrived. I was taken to the hospital by ambulance where I was promptly told I would undergo a CT scan to check for internal injuries. At this point the nurses, doctors and paramedics who were treating me had not discovered the wound on my ribs, the deeper and more dangerous of the two. Adrenaline from the fight was still in my veins an I had no sensation of pain. I was carted into the CT room, but at the last moment protested and refused to undergo the procedure for fear of recieving a massive amounts of radiation to my abdomen and testes. the techs and nurses looked at me in stunned disbelief. The head nurse came to assuage my fears and told me I would not recieve any more radiation than I would get in a day out in the sun. Hesitatatingly, I agreed after his reassurance. Mistake. He hwas several orders of magnitude short of the true danger. after the scan was complete I went to take a piss, and while looking at my wound in the mirror discovered the other wound on my side. It had been giving me a bit of pain but I chalked it up to soreness from the altercation which ensued after the stabbing. I grabbed the MD and showed him the wound and he ordered another CT, which he downplayed as well as being far less iradiating than an x-ray.
The wounds were only 1.5CM deep and not near any vitals. The only competent care I recieved was the suturting. I am certain The hospital damaged me far more than my assailant, those wounds will heal in weeks, but the radiation is permanant. Every member of the staff from the technicians,to the nurses and up to the MD were totally ignorant about the dangers of CT scans. I can't say they lied to me, I really believe they are just morons, well-meaning, but _extremely_ dangerous morons. The doctor told me lighheartedly of an infant who has recieved over 25 CT scans. I shudder to think of the implications for that child. The CT has become a substitute for competent medical care. Why did I trust those dumbfucks who couldn't even find the second stab wound when they had worked on me for hours, who treated me as a piece of meat, like an inanimate object? Two hiroshimas worth of radiation for two lacerations, what a fucking mess healthcare is in this country.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-02T20:52:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85329>

you did not consent to these and they still did them?

### nonchalant — 2013-05-02T21:43:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85333>

Oh, Arclite, so sorry to hear of this incident. I can imagine you were quite shocked at the time.
Dr. Kruse talks about what he does for long airplane trips, perhaps that will help? For mitochondrial support/repair? Ubiquinol and vitamin D and such?

### Arclite — 2013-05-02T22:03:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85337>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=85329)
>
> you did not consent to these and they still did them?

Eventually I did agree to the procedure, but this was after steadfastly refusing for ten minutes. I only consented when I was assured by the nurse and doctor that it only involved a negligible amount of radiation, equivalent to a day spent in the sun.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-03T03:01:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85358>

The sun adds photons while ionizing radiation depletes us of them.........this is why life evolved in the sun and no next to a quasar........This is why people call BS on us. When you do not know just admit it.

### Arclite — 2013-05-03T08:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85366>

I feel totally despondent. I interrogated the radiology staff at the hospital this afternoon but they spouted such absurd nonsense. The first woman they sent me told me that the dose of radiation I received was beneficial; that we humans require some radiation, and that our government would not allow these machines in hospitals if they weren't completely safe. Yikes. The second gentleman was more knowledgeable and pleasant, but still far from able to satisfactorily answer my questions. My uncle is a radiologist so I am going to talk to him tomorrow and have him explain my dose chart in plainer terms than mGy-Cm, I fear though he is too heavily invested in the field to give me an objective response. I should have gone to a goddamn veterinarian.

### caroline — 2013-05-03T09:50:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85372>

We should develop some kind of a protocol for when we wind up in emergency. When I was last there I was in shock and all alone ....they could have talked me into anything probably ...even tho we know better ...it is a tough situation.
We need to - somehow- evaluate our situation for ourself and put the brakes on anything they are trying to talk us into - at least until we have a clearer picture ....or are unconscious!

### Arclite — 2013-05-04T02:44:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-85407>

78 mGy or 7.8 Rad. Jesus, thats likely as much radiation than I have absorbed in my entire 24 year life, all to my balls and abdomen. I told them I didn't want to do it! Radiologist uncle has a grim sense of humor that has made me feel even worse. The answer I continue to receive is "we must weigh benefits with risks" They don't understand the risks. Not even a 30 year radiologist does. Only you guys do. they look at me like Im not right in the head, like what kind of person asks so many questions? God I hate them.

I asked how he would protect my brain and thyroid from the xrays. He said it was the equivalent of a day in the sun. I said then you go first. I said at last we agree on something...this is my last visit.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-19T19:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-86474>

What happens when existing amalgams get x-rayed?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-19T23:07:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-recieved-2-ct-scans-am-i-cooked.6941/post-86488>

Xrays are high energy EMF's. The photoelectric effect dictates what happens here. Nature's laws say if you shine light or ultraviolet light on a piece of metal, the light can knock electrons out of the metal. Back in the early 20th century, the photoelectric effect made science uncomfortable because their experiments could not show this to be true. The wave theory of light suggested as you turned up the brightness of the light, you should eject more electrons, because brighter light has more energy in it. But scientists had trouble with these ideas was because in their experiments this did not happen in their labs. If you shine light onto a piece of an element or group of elements like an amalgam, no electrons were ejected, no matter how bright the light was made. But when you changed the light to different color like violet, you could turn it down real low and still electrons will be ejected. The key is not the color.....it is the frequency of the wavelength. Since X-rays are very powerful because of its wavelength it is considered and ionizing form of EMF........and that means it has the power to eject a ton of electrons from the amalgam that can cause more damage. So this is why if you have a lot of metal in your body for any reason at all......X rays are more dangerous for you than people without metal in their body. Soon you will all be learning a lot more about this......as the QB series develops.

# Thread: Article: Cell's Response to Electricity Explained
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-cells-response-to-electricity-explained.6942/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jane — 2013-05-02T21:07:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-cells-response-to-electricity-explained.6942/post-85331>

My DH has been giving me a hard time and pooh-poohing the effects of EMF on the body until he read this article in our local newspaper yesterday.

MAY 1, 2013 MERCED SUN-STAR

CELLâ€™S RESPONSE TO ELECTRICITY EXPLAINED

An exacting nanosecond jolt of electricity can briefly open a cellâ€™s wall, allowing for the delivery of drugs or DNA. This procedureâ€”called electroporationâ€”is widely used in biology, biotechnology and medicine, including cancer treatments.

Why the cellâ€™s wall opens, however, has remained a mystery until now.

UC Merced applied math Professor Mayya Tokman and her collaboraters have figured out what is going on at the nanoscale and recently published their findings in PLOS ONE. The open-access peer-reviewed journal is part of the Public Library of Science, a nonprofit public publisherâ€¦.

Using complex computer models, the research team discovered that the electric charge causes water molecules to rearrange themselves to create a water bridge across the cellâ€™s wall, connecting the inside and the outside of a cell.

â€œItâ€™s such a basic thingâ€”waterâ€”that controls this process,â€ Tokman said. â€œItâ€™s interesting that this is happening with the cells that make up our bodies or any other biological tissue.â€

In the past, researchers believed electricity might cause lipid molecules that make up the cell wall to move and create holes, but had little evidence to show it. Additionally, a cell wall has many components, including nanomoters and ion channels.

â€œItâ€™s very difficult to figure out whatâ€™s going on,â€ Tokman said. â€œThe cell membrane is a zoo, plus the creation of holes in the cell wall cannot be observed directly through a microscope because the openings are too small.â€

In fact, the only way researchers know a cell membrane opens is by exposing the cell to dyes that fluoresce only when they come in contact with chemicals inside the cell.

Experimental researchers do not have a way to determine the strength and the duration of electric pulses, which is needed to control the quantity and the size of the openings in a cell wall.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-02T21:46:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-cells-response-to-electricity-explained.6942/post-85334>

Jane, glad your hubby is beginning to understand.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-03T02:57:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-cells-response-to-electricity-explained.6942/post-85357>

Water is the huge key to how biology really works.........it allows the amazing quantum effects and many new strange concepts that you are beginning to take into account now as your read the blog, including Superposition, Entanglement, Complementarity, Duality, Uncertainty , Exclusion, Decoherence, Ehrenfest theorem, Tunnelling, and Non-locality.

Most of the work in the Quantum biology field seems to have been originated by physicists and mathematicians rather than by biologists. This is not surprising given the complex mathematical training required to be fluent in matters related to quantum mechanics. Quantum biology suffers from one severe problem: mainly that biology is a massively complex system whereas quantum mechanics is massively deep. Quantum mechanics is the body of scientific principles which attempts to explain the behavior of matter and its interactions with energy on the scale of atoms and atomic particles. — Just before 1900, it became clear that classical physics was unable to model certain phenomena. Coming to terms with these limitations led to the development of quantum mechanics, a major revolution in physics — Some aspects of quantum mechanics can seem counter-intuitive, because they describe behavior quite different than that seen at larger length scales, where classical physics is an excellent approximation. In the words of Richard Feynman, quantum mechanics deals with “nature as she is — absurd."

**Cites:**
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2nqHOnVTxJE&feature=relmfu](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2nqHOnVTxJE&feature=relmfu)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Introduction\_to\_quantum\_mechanics](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Introduction_to_quantum_mechanics)

### PrimalPam — 2013-05-12T04:41:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eosinophilic-esophogitis.6943/post-85873>

A few years ago, I was diagnosed with EE via an Upper Endoscopy. My main symptom was food that would not go down or come back up and terrible GERD. I was prescribed "Prilosec for life", and not happy with that diagnosis (which was cascading upon many other diagnoses other drs were giving me), I took to the internet, and found my way here.

This thread just made me realize: I haven't had a food swallowing issue, and barely a GERD issue, for over a year. EpiPaleo it is. (and the CT has to be helping too).

I know it must be a difficult thought, removing grains/sugar/dairy from your 6 year-old's diet and replacing with seafood, broccoli, kale, coconut oil. It was for me too, but whenever I veer off the wagon, the EE/GERD symptoms return immediately.

Although there are so many other things here at play: EMF, water, etc.

>
> Hi Dr. K:
>
> While watching the PPP webinar, I sat bolt upright as you touched on the emerging spectrum of diseases facing our children. My son (6) has EE. Now, I will not bog you down with all the details of his particular case, but since I follow this blog and believe in the direction of where it takes us, could you please share a couple of high level actions I might be able to take with regards his diet. Recently, his night terrors have become more pronounced. It used to be an outlier symptom now it is more frequent.
>
> Thanks

Vince, I just wanted to share with you that I also have a son with night terrors. He is 8 and would have them 3-4 times a week. He never woke up in his bed, was always in with his brother or sister and also had a lot of bedwetting. A while back someone mentioned concrete slab basement floor being a viable source for grounding, I moved 3 mattresses to the basement and slept down there with my kids. My 8 yr old has not had a single night terror since then. Not one. And has only wet the bed a few times over the past couple months.

My 10 yr old son has MCTD. Getting him off grain has been huge. He always wanted to be vegetarian, had a strong aversion to meats. I am the type to listen to my body and I respected his ability to do that and let him have a diet high in beans and rice...he became more frail and his behavior sucked. Forced him into epi-paleo which was actually easier with fish and seafood...he ate a ton! He now craves meat and fish. His frail little body even looks like he is putting on muscle. He has also begun taking DHEA as it was low.

I would love to know what you find out about MCTD. My kids were subjected to an EMF cesspool for 8 years and also had Lyme and Babesia. I believe we are experiencing major fallout from those living conditions and it is a slow road back to optimal. Slow and bumpy....but worth the trip.

FREE Donations to Grassrootshealth.com are accepted but not required to attend

Event Details

The Victoria Vitamin D â€“ Disease Prevention Symposium is a FREE event designed to help educate healthcare professionals and the general public to create awareness about Vitamin D (The Sunshine Vitamin) and its many benefits related to health and wellness.

FEATURING:

Dr. Robert P. Heaney, M.D.

Professor of Medicine, Creighton University, Nebraska. Leading vitamin D researcher and author of over 400 original research papers on nutrition and vitamin D. Dr. Heaney will present an overview of the latest papers on the relationship between optimal vitamin D and disease prevention.

Carole A. Baggerly

Breast cancer survivor and director of [www.grassrootshealth.net](http://www.grassrootshealth.net), an organization dedicated to increasing awareness of vitamin D benefits, especially breast cancer reduction. Carole will focus on vitamin D, breast cancer prevention and children's health.

Dr. Marc Sorenson, EdD

Founder of the Sunlight Institute ([www.sunlightinstitute.org](http://www.sunlightinstitute.org)) and author of the book â€œVitamin D3 and Solar Power for Optimal Healthâ€￾. Marc will present â€œthe best source of vitamin D.â€￾ Learn scientific facts about the benefits and risks of sunlight.

### AlaskaLadyie — 2013-05-03T08:43:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85367>

I just started this Leptin reset thing................. I have been doing ok but I still get huingry at about 5 in the afternoon ofter a 1pm lunch and I again fight eating at about 9 at night after a 7pm supper
Should I just fight the hunger or if I cave what is beter food to eat??
I have eaten Primal for about 2 years now so I do not eat any type of grains or sugar
The only milk product I consume is Fage Yogurt
I just need suggestuions on what to snack on till I finllly can get through these times and NOT snack..
Is it better to have protein Veg or fruit?? If I do cave...

### caroline — 2013-05-03T09:08:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85369>

Welcome Alaskaladyie! I would definitely say you are not eating enough for breakfast. You need at least 50gr of Protein.
Some of us needed a lot more than that - I probably had around 80- 90gr some days.
Share with us what you are eating ..... if you are eating enough protein you shouldn't be all that hungry and definitely no snacking. Do you eat at least 4 hours before bedtime?
I personally wouldn't have yogurt and I wouldn't have fruit either - too much sugar - that might be what is kicking your butt right now.
Just up the protein and fat in the morning - 30 minutes after rising. Adding in more fat helps. Have you tried a bullet proof coffee yet?
Please keep posting and let us know how you are going - it helps everyone.......

Maybe start a journal and post what you are eating every meal and we can help you figure this out!
A journal is great to keep you on track and it is fun to look back and see how far you have come.

Maybe - also - re read the leptin R/X just to make sure you are doing it as written .... it is so easy to miss things the first time around! 2 years later I am re reading and going Wow - I didn't see that before - but it is because we have new eyes and understand more and the pieces start to fit together....

### AlaskaLadyie — 2013-05-03T09:19:29+0300

**Thank you Caroline for reply.......... I just started this on Monday and I have been having 4 eggs fried in about a T of Butter I also have a scoop of AMP protein powder with unsweetened almond milk and a scoop of Strawberry Shakeology and a hanfull of frozen strawberriesand ice to creat a smoothy shake for breakfast...... I eat about 6oz of protein and a european cucumber for lunch and some celery................ For supper usually some type of meat and a salad with salsa on it or steamed vegtables...............is this Leptin R/X u spoke of the Leptin Reset book or is there a post here on the Forum titled that??????? Thank you**

### caroline — 2013-05-03T09:40:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85371>

Personally - I would skip the shake stuff - JK is not a fan. Add in some bacon with the eggs. If I remember right - 4 X-large eggs is only about 25 gr protein so you will need at least 4 strips of bacon and then add in some fat. More fat was huge for me. I have 2 BPC in the morning. Skip the fruit. There are lots of paleo books around telling you what to eat - I think Dr. K. recommends Rob Wolfe and some others - Paleo is pretty standard.

Optimum would be epi-paleo which is lots and lots of seafood with oysters at the top of your list.

If you go to the home page there are lots of blogs to read about this and everything else. I just had a look and things seemed to have changed there ..... I am going to go and check it out better and get back to you.

Are you having coconut oil? You may be a little heavy on the carbs from what you said above especially at dinner time.

### caroline — 2013-05-03T10:01:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85373>

okay ..... go to the home page....at the top ...2nd tab from the left is the getting started guide. Click on it and Just scroll down and choose your topic. If you have more questions please don't hesitate to ask - we have all been there!

### Linz — 2013-05-03T10:15:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-new-here.6947/post-85375>

Welcome Alaskaladyie,

You need to up the fat and include lots of fish and really stick to the meal timing and light use advice and you should find you forget about needing snacks. When I first changed my diet and had the fat a bit wrong I kept a big pot of thick meat/veg soup which was really effective for stopping hunger cravings between meals.

Include fatty bacon and fish/prawns etc cooked in bacon fat, butter or duck fat at breakfast time instead of the strawberries. And try bullet proof coffee as Caroline suggests. For lunch make sure the protein is real food with its own fat included. For supper alternate the meat with fish/shellfish and add bacon fat/ chopped bacon/ warmed olive oil and garlic/coconut oil with chilli and turmeric or butter with parsley/cilantro/basil to your vegetables after steaming. Melt some very dark chocolate with coconut oil, d-ribose or honey and a few nuts and keep in the fridge for something sweet at the end of a meal, perhaps with some raspberries, blueberries, currants, gooseberries or rhubarb.

There is a leptin RX post here with more detail (and great information in the comments) and a post leptin rx with advice on how to adapt the initial rx once you know you have regained leptin sensitivity and a beginners section to point you in the right direction.

Edit - Caroline just gave you most of the same info in far less words.

welcome you've gotten great advice. I definitely agree you're not eating enough protein or FAT. you might even consider eating a tablespoon of coconut oil, butter, ghee, bacon or duck fat with each meal.

Jack please can you help me? I cannot post in Ask Jack, IDK why.. but I really have a question for you now.

My good friend works in the jungle of Africa and he is suffering he's 7th bout of Malaria, he has Typhoid too now. He is in hospital on IV. The Malaria is cerebral. He is a fighter though.

My question;
Would it be ok for me to suggest he cool he's head as much as it gets with ice? To, if any change, limit damage? They say there will be permanent injuries to he's brain and neuropsychological state.....

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-04T02:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ask-jack.6948/post-85404>

sure.........Cerebral malaria may be the most common non-traumatic encephalopathy in the world. Cerebral malaria is a common disease in the developing world, remains the deadliest complication of infection with P. falciparum.

Harvard Study Confirms Fluoride Reduces Children's IQ:

A recently-published Harvard University meta-analysis funded by the National Institutes of Health (NIH) has concluded that children who live in areas with highly fluoridated water have "significantly lower" IQ scores than those who live in low fluoride areas.

It doesn't just lower "children's IQ". It lowers EVERYONE'S IQ's. (Not only that, but it makes people more passive and/or docile.) This is far from the first study done about Fluoride lowering IQ. For example, there was a Chinese Study released in December 2010.

"By eliminating other possible causes of lowered IQâ€™s (including brick tea consumption), this Chinese study presents undeniable findings. The study compares children in two areas of China, one with a low level of natural fluoride averaging 0.36mg/liter water to an area of higher natural fluoride averaging 2.5mg/liter water. In the low fluoride area, there were 3Â½ times more bright children as in the high fluoride area. Thatâ€™s enough to make a person pause for a moment. And if the last statistic isnâ€™t dramatic enough, digest this one: the high fluoride area had 2 Â½ times more mentally retarded children than the low fluoride area. This should be enough. This obviously IS more than enough â€“ but unfortunately this isnâ€™t all that fluoride does to the brain. Fluoride has a frequent companion: aluminum â€“ and when the two of these guys hang out together (which they commonly do), all hell breaks lose. Aluminum in the brain is a possible causative factor in Alzheimerâ€™s Disease. Fluoride is the key that allows aluminum to pass the blood brain barrier and set the stage for dementia and Alzheimerâ€™s Disease through the formation of beta-amyloid plaques." Aluminium is another dieelectric blocker for the CNS that causes protein folding problems by inducing avalanche collapse in the Endoplasmic reticulum and Golgi body. We should try to avoid aluminum based products to avoid the protein folding- aluminum connection found in neurodegenerative diseases. Remember when fluoride is a player in your conditions of existence it allows , a lot of Aluminum to migrate to your pineal gland by opening the blood brain barrier, with EMF, to calcify it and lower your ability to produce melatonin. When melatonin is lowered cancers, become more likely because intracellular signaling becomes ruined on a quantum scale as I covered in EMF 6.

This is far from the first time I've posted about what Fluoride does to your body. Some people have expressed on other blogs that they are still skeptical about whether or not Fluoride Lowers IQ. I'm hoping now that you begin to force other 'mainstream' organizations like the Huffington Post to report more of these studies and you blast them out on social media. I also hope this stimulates some of you will to feel more inclined to research this subject thoroughly. I don't doubt you will be surprised by what you find.

"Reseach findings have demonstrated the ability of fluoride to act as an
enzymatic poison in the human body inhibiting critical metabolic pathways
required for healthy living. This report clearly demonstrates how inhibition of
certain metabolic pathways is linked with increased neurological and
cardiovascular diseases as well as dental, skeletal and mental fluorosis. Apart
from bones and teeth many of the essential human organs in the body are
directly affected by fluoride including the heart, kidneys, liver and pineal
gland. Fluoride is now known to cause calcification in human arteries resulting
in plaque formation and increased risk of stroke and heart disease. Significant
calcification also occurs in the pineal gland and kidneys.

It is now known that individuals with renal disease and infants bottle-fed with
formula milk reconstituted with fluoridated water are the most at risk from the
impacts of fluoride. Fluoride is now known to act as a destabiliser of calcium
and magnesium bonding in the human body with serious consequences for
human health. Fluoride is also known to have major co-toxicity health
implications when it complexes with other pollutants present in water such as
aluminium. This is known to have major health implications including
neurological and chronic bone pain.
Largely as a consequence of fluoridation of drinking water, fluoride has
become one of the most widely available elements that is present in
artificially elevated concentrations in potable water, processed foods and
drinks prepared with treated water as well as cooked foods prepared with
fluoridated water. As a consequence, the daily intake and exposure of the
population to fluoride is now considerably higher than that of any previous
generation."

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-replying-to-you-re-spironolactone-post.6950/post-85386>

Hi Jack,

Thank you for taking the time to explain how spironolactone works and what it actually "does".

I am not familiar with Stevens Johnson but I will research it. So thank you.

I have only been taking spiro for the past 2 months. 100 mgs am and pm. Along with the pill. I have had acne and thinning hair since I was 17. Lovely. The endo hasn't seen any pcos cysts on ultrasound though. So it is more suggested pcos.

My acne and thinning hair has persisted and now I'm 40. My mother also has androgenic alopecia so I assume it's genetic. My glucose and thyroid tests were within normal ranges. Though when I was pregnant last year I had one thyroid test come back as hypo. My ferritin was 30. Spiro has been the only thing to clear up my face and stop my hair from falling out. Are there any alternatives to spiro that you would recommend? Though I want to maintain the benefits of spiro I won't do it at the expense of my health.

Also, your no booze comment has me concerned. On my spiro bottles it says "alcohol may cause drowsiness with this medication" No one mentioned that I wasn't able to drink on it. And I specifically asked. So I have been drinking on it. To be honest I drink pretty regularly. Every weekend. I haven't had any side effects and have more than "a few".

Would someone who has been drinking on spiro already have done some sort of irreparable damage? Hope I haven't fucked myself up.

Why would alcohol be contraindicated? When I searched for an answer on my own all I see is this.

"If you are taking Aldactone and consume alcoholic beverages, your blood pressure may drop rapidly. This can cause drowsiness, dizziness, lightheadedness and fainting."

If someone on Spiro wanted to have a few drinks could they skip that days dose?

Thank you.

# Thread: Blood flow in fingers predicts future propensity for a Heart Attack.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-flow-in-fingers-predicts-future-propensity-for-a-heart-attack.6961/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-04T04:06:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-flow-in-fingers-predicts-future-propensity-for-a-heart-attack.6961/post-85420>

[http://losangeles.cbslocal.com/2013/05/03/medical-test-uses-fingers-to-predict-heart-disease/](http://losangeles.cbslocal.com/2013/05/03/medical-test-uses-fingers-to-predict-heart-disease/)

The researchers attach blood flow measuring devices on the fingers of both hands and then they restrict flow in one arm for 5 minutes and release the flow and measure how well the blood flows. In healthy folks, the flow is very rapid and in folks with endothelial problems, the flow only goes back to normal...not a high rush. Endothelial dysfunction is seen in 30 year olds by this predictor of heart disease 7 years into the future.

I seem to recall a therapy for heart disease patients involved putting blood pressure cuff devices all over the body to force the heart to work harder to pump blood through the vascular system...thus strengthening the heart muscle.

It may have been a therapy to increase blood flow to the brain. Too many years and to much floridated water.

Perhaps Dr K can relate the flow rate test with treating already diseased patients.

# Thread: Cold receptors and systemic inflammation in the colon
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-receptors-and-systemic-inflammation-in-the-colon.6974/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### agatha — 2013-05-04T23:15:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-receptors-and-systemic-inflammation-in-the-colon.6974/post-85459>

This is an interesting study - yes, I know it's in mice butr we have to start somewhere..

**TRPM8 activation attenuates inflammatory responses in mouse models of colitis**
Rithwik Ramachandrana et al 2013

Abstract

Transient Receptor Potential Melastatinâ€“8 (TRPM8), a recently identified member of the transient receptor potential (TRP) family of ion channels, is activated by mild cooling and by chemical compounds such as the supercooling agent, icilin. Since cooling, possibly involving TRPM8 stimulation, diminishes injury-induced peripheral inflammation, we hypothesized that TRPM8 activation may also attenuate systemic inflammation. We thus studied the involvement of TRPM8 in regulating colonic inflammation using two mouse models of chemically induced colitis. TRPM8 expression, localized immunohistochemically in transgenic TRPM8GFP mouse colon, was up-regulated in both human- and murine-inflamed colon samples, as measured by real-time PCR. Wild-type mice (but not TRPM8-nulls) treated systemically with the TRPM8 agonist, icilin showed an attenuation of chemically induced colitis, as reflected by a decrease in macroscopic and microscopic damage scores, bowel thickness, and myeloperoxidase activity compared with untreated animals. Furthermore, icilin treatment reduced the 2,4,6-trinitrobenzenesulfonic acidâ€“induced increase in levels of inflammatory cytokines and chemokines in the colon. In comparison with wild-type mice, Dextran Sodium Sulfate (DSS)-treated TRPM8 knockout mice showed elevated colonic levels of the inflammatory neuropeptide calcitonin-geneâ€“related peptide, although inflammatory indices were equivalent for both groups. Further, TRPM8 activation by icilin blocked capsaicin-triggered calcitonin-geneâ€“related peptide release from colon tissue ex vivo and blocked capsaicin-triggered calcium signaling in Transient Receptor Potential Vaniloid-1 (TRPV1) and TRPM8 transfected HEK cells. Our data document an anti-inflammatory role for TRPM8 activation, in part due to an inhibiton of neuropeptide release, pointing to a novel therapeutic target for colitis and other inflammatory diseases.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-05T04:35:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-receptors-and-systemic-inflammation-in-the-colon.6974/post-85468>

good news for me and Amgen......

### Destiny — 2013-05-05T20:59:06+0300

everyone must make a living........but being authentic doing it is the key point. Mercola has to go with the hot hand but he has 55 employees that scour the net looking for content to "leverage". To me you need to have a clarity of purpose to gain people's thoughts and not their wallets. Without a true vision the people will perish, and without personal reflection, there is no clarity of purpose. There is a loss of passion........Some of the most important moments of clarity in life might have come from our internal reflection. Some call them epiphanies, moments of truth, or deeper levels of awareness. These defining moments can put all the other moments of our life into context.

If Mercola can spread the CORRECT clarity of the message than I am happy about it. We must provide our species with this clarity to make our way. We must aspire to be a great humanitarians, that should be our single focus. I look at Mercola as a cog in that great wheel.
i choose to focus on the good he does and not the bad. We must strive to
be love to others regardless of our differences, we must become as compassionate and understanding as possible in this journey.

Many people in the paleosphere lack this focus and clarity in my view. People who lack the clarity, generally lack a purpose. Therefore, they lack courage and the determination because they don't have a dream, a purpose they they could follow. People with no clarity often find ways to discourage others who have dreams, who have a purpose.

Often frustration is caused in our minds due to lack of clarity. It is the clarity of purpose that makes our lives exciting, it's clarity that fills our minds and hearts with passion. This is the missing piece of the puzzle of life!

The best way to succeed in anything in life is to have a specific purpose, a clear vision, a plan of action, and the determination to make it happen.

Some such moments of clarity have awakened me to what I can become, but my greatest moment of clarity, so far, **is the realization of what I need to BE**.

**_Not what others think I should be, not what I think others want me to be, but what I can truly be._** Do not worry about Joe.........worry about you and your vision for transformation. Then pay it forward in your own way. Life has a way of paying you back when you pay it the homage it deserves.

Therefore, they lack courage and the determination because they don't have a dream, a purpose they they could follow. People with no clarity often find ways to discourage others who have dreams, who have a purpose.
>
> Often frustration is caused in our minds due to lack of clarity. It is the clarity of purpose that makes our lives exciting, it's clarity that fills our minds and hearts with passion. This is the missing piece of the puzzle of life!
>
> The best way to succeed in anything in life is to have a specific purpose, a clear vision, a plan of action, and the determination to make it happen.
>
> Some such moments of clarity have awakened me to what I can become, but my greatest moment of clarity, so far, **is the realization of what I need to BE**.
>
> **_Not what others think I should be, not what I think others want me to be, but what I can truly be._** Do not worry about Joe.........worry about you and your vision for transformation. Then pay it forward in your own way. Life has a way of paying you back when you pay it the homage it deserves.

This just made my day........... amazing

### caroline — 2013-05-06T09:12:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electron-deficiency-might-be-an-underlying-factor-in-most-chronic-disease.6977/post-85504>

This is how I decided I was finally a grownup not all that long ago ...... I can look at you {JK\] and like the man I see - but you are who you are - not the man I think or wish you should be .... whatever I super impose upon you is not your failing - it is mine. People need to give us space to be who we are - to grow, evolve and become a better version of ourselves and if they have expectations of us that we don't live up to that isn't our fault - it is theirs. I wish I could say that better....hope you get what I mean.....

# Thread: Hello from South Africa
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bugsy1950@gmail.com — 2013-05-05T17:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85477>

I have visited this site many times in the last year, and realise that I have done things backwards - posted a few questions but never introduced myself.
I stumbled upon this site as I was trying to find out why losing weight had become an impossibility for me.
The more I frequent this site, the more I am aware of the enormity of my ignorance regarding how my body works, but it has captivated my attention and I have learned so much and there is so much more to learn. I am very grateful to Dr K for having dedicated so much of his precious time to educate people interested in living optimally. I am tired of just surviving I want more out of life, but unfortunately I am not young any more and there is so much to learn to turn things around, keeping in mind that when you are "older" the body does not respond as quickly any more, but I believe it is never too late.
I have been advised to start a journal.....not good at that, not much self discipline, plus I hope not to make a fool of myself...
One thing though I have managed to discipline myself to eat as close to paleo as possible. I say as close to because organic meat is impossible to find - the closest is "free range" and once in a blue moon I find some organic onions, carrots or sweet potatoes, so I asked my husband to plant some spinach, red bell peppers and egg plants, often they are attacked by some disease and then.....I have to buy the ones at the supermarket.
I am 62 YO female living in a very beautiful part of the world - the tip of Africa - Cape Town.
So here I am - on board

### Optimalbound — 2013-05-05T22:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85482>

Welcome, Bugsy!
I've been to Cape Town several times and it is such a wonderful place. I imagined that most of your meat would be grass-fed, but to be honest I never investigated it because you have so much lovely seafood. I saw some really tempting abalones (sea snails, for the rest of you) while scuba diving but unfortunately their numbers are critically low and as you well know, they are illegal to remove from the sea.

### caroline — 2013-05-06T01:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85486>

Welcome Bugsy! - I am so happy to see you here .....very well written. It is never too late to turns things around if you really want to .... I am proof of this and many others here as well! As I have said to you - there is tons of reading to get thru - but there is no other way if you want to get a handle on all that Dr. Kruse is showing us .....remember - he is the guy with the flashlight!
When you read the blogs - please make sure that you read the comments as well - they are often as important if not more so......then come back here and ask questions. This is tough stuff and Dr. K. likes to hurt our head! I promise - no one thinks anyone else is stupid for their questions and then you can have an "aha moment" when something finally sinks in!

Have you checked out the ...Leaky gut R/X ? and if you read some of the journals - you will see what others are dealing with..... some very tough gut problems.....

Nothing in Life worth doing is easy ... and challenges keep us sharp and motivated and in the game and hopefully keep our neurons firing for a long, long time. Have you read Dr. K's personal story?

Have a really great day - get out and enjoy the sun and sky and nature and see the beauty that is all around you - maybe you could post some pictures? I have always wanted to go to South africa....

### Inger — 2013-05-06T08:12:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85503>

Welcome!  I sometimes think free range meats are way better than organic, you might get game too? Like antelopes etc? I always choose wild instead of organic.. organic here at least means heavily grain fed..

### bugsy1950@gmail.com — 2013-05-06T12:38:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85507>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=85486)
>
> Have you checked out the ...Leaky gut R/X ? and if you read some of the journals - you will see what others are dealing with..... some very tough gut problems.....
>
> Nothing in Life worth doing is easy ... and challenges keep us sharp and motivated and in the game and hopefully keep our neurons firing for a long, long time. Have you read Dr. K's personal story?

Yes I have read both, I have read a lot.
One thing though, I find myself all over the place, I don't quite know how to navigate the site, I start with one blog which is eg no3 and somehow land up on something completely different, in other words I never get the whole picture as I find myself jumping from blog to blog, maybe someone can give me an idea as to where and how to start.
I have probably read 70% of the material, as to understanding it..well....60% of it LOL ! I do struggle so with the acronyms....

### bugsy1950@gmail.com — 2013-05-06T12:43:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85508>

>
> Welcome!  I sometimes think free range meats are way better than organic, you might get game too? Like antelopes etc? I always choose wild instead of organic.. organic here at least means heavily grain fed..

Not in our supermarkets, I have not found a good butcher either.
Plus I rather like antelopes and prefer them in the wild than on my plate....but unfortunately I am a carnivore and I have to eat something...

Thank you all for the welcome

### caroline — 2013-05-07T03:31:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85550>

When I am reading - I wind up all over the place too I need to focus on one thing and stick with it ....maybe someone has an idea to better get thru everything?

### Tizawee — 2013-05-07T15:30:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-south-africa.6978/post-85566>

Bugsy -

Love what Caroline said. Agree.

For those who get lost in the links in the blogs, perhaps what I do will help: I right-click and open in a new window/tab. When I'm done I shut the window/tab then continue reading. Next link, same thing.

I believe in you, Bugsy.

Hugs!
e

# Thread: Hypertrophied Mitochondria
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypertrophied-mitochondria.6984/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-06T20:33:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypertrophied-mitochondria.6984/post-85529>

I came across some terms while reading about a study on Ad Libitum feeding versus Every-Other-Day feeding of mice with and without exercise. One of the terms was " hypertrophied mitochondria". Hypertrophied mitochondria looked more oval/round, if I understand correctly, as opposed to long-and-slim. Anybody know which type would be optimal? The EOD mice were provided less food on the "fasting" days so they weren't starving on those days.

# Thread: I'm now below pre-baby weight!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-now-below-pre-baby-weight.6986/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ruthie — 2013-05-07T03:58:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-now-below-pre-baby-weight.6986/post-85553>

Hi everyone,

I wanted to share that since starting the JK protocols on Dec. 1, 2012, I have lost 26 lbs! I started at 6 months postpartum and 153 lbs; I am now down to 127 lbs, which is a couple of pounds below where I was when I got pregnant. (I'm 5'3.) I could stand to lose about 5 more lbs and my mummy tummy needs some work, but I feel good again in clothes!

Prior to starting the Leptin Rx, I was totally frustrated at my inability to budge the baby weight. I had basically been at the same weight since a couple of weeks after the birth. Other paleo sources had me scared that if I cut carbs my thyroid or milk supply would suffer. I am still successfully breastfeeding my 10-mo-old and my thyroid is doing great!

If I find the time, I want to write more in depth about my issues, how they have improved, and what I still need to work on. (maybe start a journal) But I wanted to go ahead and share this one thing to encourage anyone else.

Cheers,
Ruthie

### caroline — 2013-05-07T04:32:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-now-below-pre-baby-weight.6986/post-85554>

Ruthie - congratulations on your success! - such great news to share with us ....please do start a journal ...we would love to hear more of your story and keep us posted on how you are doing......

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-07T16:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-now-below-pre-baby-weight.6986/post-85567>

Congrats Ruthie!! Great testimony!

### sooperb — 2014-03-11T00:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-now-below-pre-baby-weight.6986/post-114619>

Having a chuckle at the thread title, I had this mental image of someone who now weighs less than when they were a baby. I'm quite glad I was wrong

Explains the link between gut health and autoimmunity, neurological disorders etc -

### SeaHorse — 2013-05-07T15:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/enlightened-doctor-in-the-uk.6987/post-85564>

I think there a quite a few people here who have come through the GAPS program (I was one of them) she has a lot of great information in her books. The timeframe she operates with is like Weston Price...the last few hundred years of "traditional" food culture. What has opened my eyes reading JK's material is that we are better off thinking in terms of (tens of) thousands of years of evolution and changing environments...that's where some of the best answers lie in figuring out our modern health issues.

Still....she is light years ahead of most doctors in her understanding of the gut/brain connection.

### caroline — 2013-05-08T02:24:18+0300

A friend of mine has diabetes-I and is also an MD. I'd be grateful if anyone has some solid info that I can forward to him, that supports the view of Dr Natasha Campbell-McBride in this matter.

### bonsaiz — 2013-05-13T10:23:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/enlightened-doctor-in-the-uk.6987/post-85935>

# Thread: Having difficulty finding info on being underweight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TribalSpice — 2013-05-07T09:54:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85558>

I am trying to find the link between LR and being underweight. How does that work (I have been scouring the blog, but have come up with nothing to explain it)?

I understand low WAT would = less Leptin produced when eating, but how does this not convert to storing fat and making more WAT? If someone who is better at the search function than me help, I would really appreciate it.

### Jonathin — 2013-05-07T19:57:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85576>

I have been tall and thin my whole life (58yrs). I think you might want to frame your inquiry differently. Being underweight is more than not having much fat. Becoming leptin sensitive for someone like us might mean that our appetite might need increase along with anabolism. Try high intensity weights if you have energy. How can we build tissue if digestion and hormones are poor? LRx is the start. Adjusting psychological behavior to a more claim or balanced mode rather than indulging in optional stressful states might be beneficial. I am just throwing out some ideas that may or may not apply to you.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-07T20:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85585>

I believe Jack summarized it when he said the brain does not count calories; it counts electrons, and if someone's electron counter is broken it means leptin signaling problems.
The "satiety" switch in underweight people may turn off too soon among other things.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-07T21:56:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85586>

correct.......underweight implies slow movement across the mitochondrial membrane or a lot of uncoupling occurring for some reason.

### TribalSpice — 2013-05-08T02:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85602>

So, how does this relate to rT3 levels?

For example, my BIL is 6'6 and built like a broom handle. He has high rT3 and is on an epi-paleo diet and doing CT. He is noticing big changes in his health and well being, but not yet putting on much weight. I am trying to workout how someone has high rT3, but low body fat. Would someone normally who was skinny with LR have high T3, but mitochondrial issues?

Thanks

### Destiny — 2013-05-10T14:09:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85784>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=85696)
>

Of course, you do, Caroline! I hope you can figure out soon why it is not converting.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-10T16:06:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-difficulty-finding-info-on-being-underweight.6988/post-85787>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=85676)
>
> Okay - teacher's Pet - explain to me why I have high rT3 and high pregnenolone? It seems like no one wants my pregnenolone.......

T3 is used in two ways if I remember correctly - pathway #1 LDL + Vitamin A + T3 = pregnenolnone -> all steroid hormones (cortisol & progesterone, and everything else on down to Vit D) pathway#2 is the T3 for regulation cell metabolism (mitochondria any one???); so your high rT3 is blocking the receptor sites at the cellular level, causing increased in-efficiency at the mitochondria, and more leakiness...

If you're making Pregnenolone, that's a good thing (especially if your not supplementing) b/c that means pathway #1 is working properly. YET you still have HIGH rT3... so what's driving that? you may still have a cortisol problem - due to circadian mismatches - this stuff doesn't fix itself in 6mths - when you've had years of artifical light, emf, SAD etc. it will take time...

so - you've got to figure out whats driving the High rT3, fix that and then your hormone cascade should start fall into place.... if it only was that easy right???

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-07T23:48:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-question.6989/post-85593>

For those on BHRT...

Do you find that there is usually an adjustment period as you change your dose or add soemthing to your routine?

I just started back on a little of P and E (for the 10 days after menses completion)...just .2 or each...and have started getting a little bloated in the stomach area. Since I'm literally on day 2 or 3, I dont know if I should jump the gun and assume that there is something wrong, or if I should expect that there is some time needed for my body to adjust and accept it. i will be updating Dzugan soon but just wanted some feedback.

Or if you do BHRT and never really have any sort of negative re-action, perhaps I'm in the minority...

Thank you!

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-08T00:13:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-question.6989/post-85596>

>
>
> Or if you do BHRT and never really have any sort of negative re-action, perhaps I'm in the minority...
>
> Thank you!

I've seen positive results when I make changes such as increasing dosages - ie migraine goes away... but I do think for some people it can take a couple of days. Still reading Reissi's book and he mentions that in women who are peri-menopausal and younger women who aren't it can take a couple of days for their systems to balance out... I'd keep taking notes/journaling and update dzugan ever 3-5days...

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-08T21:47:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-question.6989/post-85663>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=85620)
>
> It takes about 3-4 days to get an update... follow your instinct and tell him what you're doing...

right on - I just did that and am folloiwng my instincts....slow and low for me I think. Hopefully it will eventually get me where I need to go. I'll be intersted to see if my stomach is still as bloated/upset as it was at the end of the day yesterday. In the morning I'm fine, its only around 4pm when i start to get pressure and a pop-out...I have also been a bit tired, but its different than the Progesterone tired I used to get. Before, I'd get super DRY eyes and feel like a zombie...now I just feel like I didn't sleep enough. Its been harder to wake up...I think I'll adjust but its intersting how much hormones can affect you.

New plan is to add the trie-est from days 5 (after my period ends) -14...thinking .1 or so...then reduce to maybe half that and add in a SMALL amount of P...see how I do with that and go from there.

Granted - I realize that is such a small amount as to not even be beneficial, yet clearly it does SOMETHING bc any more than that and I re-act.

I'm kinda annoyed bc generally THIS is my good time of the month...days 1-14 have always been my best (yes, when I am low in everything including P go figure). I realize P doesnt generally kick in until ovultaion so I'm just trying to mimic that. we shall see

### diane — 2013-05-09T04:15:49+0300

Ok, so I've reduced and while its gotten a bit better - I was no longer popping out as much yesterday afternoon, my stomach still feels a bit bloated and is a pokes out abit more than normal....my pants are therefore a bit tighter...not a fun feeling...

Should I just assume there is an adjustment period and wait to sort itself out...hoping my body will get used to it and settle down, or is this a sign that I shouldnt do it at all? just curious what y'all think...

Edited to add: i think I may be slightly more hungry on it as well...I tend to not be very snacky during this time of the month (I can take chocolate or leave it for instance), but the last few nights, I've definitely found myself thinking of food more often...hoping this is temporary?

### diane — 2013-05-09T21:58:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-question.6989/post-85750>

One thing I took away from Dr. Hertoghe's book is that if you are on a dose that's too small, the systems are exacerbated. He was talking about thyroid specifically, but wonder if it's the same as the other hormones, and if there is an opportunity to increase. I think our instinct is to back off when we feel something different, when maybe that's the signal to go higher.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-09T22:48:23+0300

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-09T23:52:27+0300

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=85759)

Hmmm that is an interesting theory...kinda along the lines of "the reason P made your breasts big is bc the E was fighting back" I think there absolutely could be something to it, but for me, I know just going higher will equal disaster. My body wont tolerate it. Maybe I should just stick to this dose, see if it levels out (please level out!!!!), and then slowly add a bit more.

For those on P and E - forgive me bc I know some have already answered this elsewhere, I just cant find it at the moment - do you generally do the E on days 1-14, then add P and reduce E from 14-28? Or something along those lines...curious. Thank you!

I see a new Dr on Sat. I will be sure to tell her whats up to get feedback as well. I'm always curious about others opinions. I'm hoping she might be a good person to help me get this thyroid stuff figured out. Wish me luck!

In which order should I read the blogs? I have read a lot but still have missing links, as I did not read them in sequence.

Then where do I proceed?
Thank you

### Dali Dula — 2013-05-08T14:15:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educating-myself.6991/post-85624>

You could start with June 2011 and read all of them in order. I am in the process of doing that now as I was not on board until sept. 2011 and did not read much of the earlier blogs. The Quilt should be read as it is the template. As your knowledge increases you should re-read things because the first time through, unless you're a super genius, all this info will not be absorbed. I would read the leptin blogs to give you a basic understanding of the ancient pathway. Prepare to be a profoundly changed human.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-08T15:45:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/educating-myself.6991/post-85626>

Dali gives a great recommendation.

# Thread: You heard it here first. Brain gut connection.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-heard-it-here-first-brain-gut-connection.6992/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2013-05-08T14:40:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-heard-it-here-first-brain-gut-connection.6992/post-85625>

Check this out.

[http://www.scientificamerican.com/article.cfm?id=gut-second-brain](http://www.scientificamerican.com/article.cfm?id=gut-second-brain)

### nonchalant — 2013-05-08T16:37:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-heard-it-here-first-brain-gut-connection.6992/post-85632>

Thanks, Dali, for pointing out this great 2010 article. ;-)

"Technically known as the enteric nervous system, the second brain consists of sheaths of neurons embedded in the walls of the long tube of our gut, or alimentary canal, which measures about nine meters end to end from the esophagus to the anus. The second brain contains some 100 million neurons, more than in either the spinal cord or the peripheral nervous system, Gershon says."

It really is "expensive tissue". Guess that is why Dr. Kruse says 'no snacking, ever'. It's kinda like firing up an incinerator to cook a shrimp. Starting a washing machine to wash a sock. Driving a tank to the local grocery store. (I got a million of these...)

"For example, scientists were shocked to learn that about 90 percent of the fibers in the primary visceral nerve, the vagus, carry information from the gut to the brain and not the other way around."

Well, folks on this website wouldn't be shocked. The brain needs to know what foods are available now, and how it should respond.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-08T17:14:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-heard-it-here-first-brain-gut-connection.6992/post-85636>

when he no better you do better

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-08T17:18:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-heard-it-here-first-brain-gut-connection.6992/post-85637>

"It was totally unexpected that the gut would regulate bone mass to the extent that one could use this regulation to cure—at least in rodents—osteoporosis," says Gerard Karsenty, lead author of the study and chair of the Department of Genetics and Development at Columbia University Medical Center.

Just so you guys no this study was quoted by me in the osteoporosis series as well

# Thread: Whaddya know, the sun isn't so bad for you!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whaddya-know-the-sun-isnt-so-bad-for-you.6993/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cinnamon — 2013-05-08T17:36:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whaddya-know-the-sun-isnt-so-bad-for-you.6993/post-85642>

I caught a few minutes of BBC World News this morning while eating breakfast. Across the bottom of the screen, a headline scrolled that said: "The health benefits of exposing skin to sunlight may far outweigh the risk of developing skin cancer, according to scientists at Edinburgh University".

Here's a link to the related article on the BBC website:

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-scotland-edinburgh-east-fife-22433359](http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-scotland-edinburgh-east-fife-22433359)

An excerpt:

"We now plan to look at the relative risks of heart disease and skin cancer in people who have received different amounts of sun exposure.

"If this confirms that sunlight reduces the death rate from all causes, we will need to reconsider our advice on sun exposure."​

The full report on the study is to be presented this Friday.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-08T18:29:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whaddya-know-the-sun-isnt-so-bad-for-you.6993/post-85650>

clinic this is what my vitamin D blog says as well.

### Huck — 2013-05-09T01:05:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whaddya-know-the-sun-isnt-so-bad-for-you.6993/post-85670>

> Edinburgh University research suggests sunlight helps reduce blood pressure, cutting heart attack and stroke risks and even prolonging life.

I was able to reduce my BP medication last summer. At the time I thought it was from diet and weight loss. In October, I had to increase medication. In early April, we had sunny weather and I was able to reduce medication a little. Then we had a month of overcast days with little sunlight which resulted in me increasing medication again.

I hadn't made the sun/bp connection until you posted the link to the article. I knew sun increases my DHEA, Testosterone, and Vitamin D, but didn't realize it was also reducing BP. Rain, rain, go away.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
