### Scompy — 2016-04-06T20:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natto-and-some-other-pqq-rich-foods.7109/post-192181>

2 huge connections just made:

PQQ: The Greek stories around celery make so much sense! Incredible!

UVing Water: This got me thinking about various juicing in time's past.... lemonaid stands make so much sense or drinking your water outside in sunlight.

!Mind blown!

### Penny — 2016-04-06T23:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natto-and-some-other-pqq-rich-foods.7109/post-192189>

Maybe it's the potassium in the greens... BTW, when calcium effluxes, it is replaced by the single bond of potassium (calcium has a double bond) - but as far as natto goes, just do what the japanese do: buy a small package of slimy natto in an asian store ($1.79). Get a package of soy beans from Trader Joes ($2.00) or at said asian story. Boil the soy beans/lentils/peas. Cool. Run some boiled water over the shallow pyrex dish to sterilize it. Put the cooled beans in the bowl, mix in the natto and stick it in your oven with the light bulb on for 1-2 days - trust me, slime will form...  To get it down, use scallions and coconut aminos...

### Penny — 2016-04-06T23:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/natto-and-some-other-pqq-rich-foods.7109/post-192190>

Maybe it's the potassium in the greens... BTW, when calcium effluxes, it is replaced by the single bond of potassium (calcium has a double bond) - but as far as natto goes, just do what the japanese do: buy a small package of slimy natto in an asian store ($1.79). Get a package of soy beans from Trader Joes ($2.00) or at said asian story. Boil the soy beans/lentils/peas. Cool. Run some boiled water over the shallow pyrex dish to sterilize it. Put the cooled beans in the bowl, mix in the natto and stick it in your oven with the light bulb on for 1-2 days - trust me, slime will form...  To get it down, use scallions and coconut aminos...

# Thread: Italian court rules Autism caused by MMR vaccine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-20T18:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/post-86542>

[http://www.collective-evolution.com...cine-caused-autism-us-media-blacks-out-story/](http://www.collective-evolution.com/2013/05/08/italian-court-rules-mmr-vaccine-caused-autism-us-media-blacks-out-story/)

"The debate over vaccines continues as an Italian court ruled in favor of the Bocca family whose nine-year-old son became autistic after receiving the MMR (Measles/Mumps & Rubella) vaccine. I came across this case and felt it was a good idea to report on this as the vaccine debate has been a hot topic here lately. Although the case concluded in 2012, the information is just as relevant today.

UPDATE: We realize the controversy surrounding Wakefield and his study, however the point is others have replicated his findings numerous times with accuracy and it backs up his claims. Instead of focusing on Wakefield, observe the other studies involved and the massive growing movement to re-think vaccines based on science and evidence. We also recognize that Autism is a spectrum disorder, meaning that there is a wide range in how it affects people. A phenomenon due to vaccines could account for some type of Autism on that spectrum. On the other hand we recognize that there could be be some genetic differences within ones DNA that could result in showing behavior and other types of characteristics seen in what we have labelled as Autism or ASD."

Dr Wakefield is interviewed regarding the use of MMR in England and how the British government has not discontinued using MMR.

Dr Wakefield is also interviewed by Dr. Mercola.

The mainstream press in the US has completely suppressed these stories of overseas of ASD aspects of vaccines.

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-20T20:14:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/post-86546>

This really doesn't have anything to do with Autism, but about money...

Can shots make symptoms worse in kids with Autism, yes, absolutely.. I saw it happen with both kids.. Both kids regressed, but, with therapy regained almost everything they lost.. and it took a long time to do so... was it the cause of their Autism, uhmm no... I knew both kids were different from the beginning.. For example, Lizzie, from birth to 18 months, very very very rarely squeaked or coo'ed or babbled.. she had a straight face.. She HATED to be held, but loved her swing... Could barely breast feed her.. She didn't track items and she didn't turn to see what sounds were... At 6 months when her hearing and vision tests came back normal, I bet the doctor 50 dollars that she had Autism. He called me crazy, but paid up when her tests came back a year later... I want everyone to know that going to a primary care doc for Autism is like going to a foot doctor for a shoulder problem.. and somehow.. around 19 months, she randomly just started using words labeling things (one year into speech therapy)...

Here's my thing.. whatever the view on shots are... Me personally, I would rather give my kids shots and have Autism symptoms worse... than some horrible disease that is preventable... thoughts?

Also, I read an article not too long ago that there was a study done where they did brain scans on babies that were not born yet.. they showed that kids with Autism brains actually formed differently in the womb. I'll have to find it again and post it.

### Lava — 2013-05-20T20:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/post-86549>

Hi P. I am curious as if you have thoughts on why military families have more cases of Autism? I think one could make a case for both situations. Maybe not the shot the child received but something the mom or dad received early on? I really enjoy reading your posts. I, personally, have no experience with Autism except the many military families I know that are touched by it. We chose not to vaccinate our daughter and have no regrets thus far. She is 22.

L.

### Rylee — 2013-05-20T21:00:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/post-86550>

> [MrPinkies said:](/goto/post?id=86546)
>
> This really doesn't have anything to do with Autism, but about money...
>
> Can shots make symptoms worse in kids with Autism, yes, absolutely.. I saw it happen with both kids.. Both kids regressed, but, with therapy regained almost everything they lost.. and it took a long time to do so... was it the cause of their Autism, uhmm no... I knew both kids were different from the beginning.. For example, Lizzie, from birth to 18 months, very very very rarely squeaked or coo'ed or babbled.. she had a straight face.. She HATED to be held, but loved her swing... Could barely breast feed her.. She didn't track items and she didn't turn to see what sounds were... At 6 months when her hearing and vision tests came back normal, I bet the doctor 50 dollars that she had Autism. He called me crazy, but paid up when her tests came back a year later... I want everyone to know that going to a primary care doc for Autism is like going to a foot doctor for a shoulder problem.. and somehow.. around 19 months, she randomly just started using words labeling things (one year into speech therapy)...
>
> Here's my thing.. whatever the view on shots are... Me personally, I would rather give my kids shots and have Autism symptoms worse... than some horrible disease that is preventable... thoughts?
>
> Also, I read an article not too long ago that there was a study done where they did brain scans on babies that were not born yet.. they showed that kids with Autism brains actually formed differently in the womb. I'll have to find it again and post it.

This is a brilliant post in many ways. It's so easy to look for someone to blame. You manage to remain objective in what be an emotional situation.

I work full-time with kids with autism and have for 7 years. The debate continues but some facts remain. Wakefield's study which links autism to vaccines, is not a credible one. The Lancet, which originally published the study, has since retracted it:

[http://www.nytimes.com/2010/02/03/health/research/03lancet.html?\_r=0](http://www.nytimes.com/2010/02/03/health/research/03lancet.html?_r=0)

Wakefield's study has NOT been replicated.

And if anecdotes are compelling, here's mine: I had my 3 year old daughter vaccinated. She does not have autism. I have no regrets about my choice to vaccinate her.

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-20T21:14:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/post-86551>

Maybe not the shot the child received but something the mom or dad received early on? I really enjoy reading your posts. I, personally, have no experience with Autism except the many military families I know that are touched by it. We chose not to vaccinate our daughter and have no regrets thus far. She is 22.
>
> L.

Hard to say.. Chris and I, both have issues.. One officially diagnosed with Aspergers and one unofficially self diagnosed with ASD... So... it kinda now is a no brainer how our kids turned out.. of course.. the official diagnoses was after we had our first baby and pregnant with our second..

I like the Jack Kruse theory... which is in my journal.. it's under.. my optimal journal.. welcome to my world.. long of short, what mom and dad are predisposed to before egg meets sperm.. and generations before them..

There is two studies out there.. and I gotta find them again from the Autism Speaks website...

First, they are finding over 100+ chemicals in the placenta.. I don't remember much about the rest of the study.. I'll have to find it and post it..

and the second, they did scans of unborn babies to chart brain development to predict Autism.. they had a pretty high success rate.. again.. I"ll have to find it and post it...

So.. back to your question and putting this all together.. I think Moms and Dads that are in the military, goes with JK's theory...

He did a radio show too that has a TON of information on too.. (again, posted in my journal)

### MrPinkies — 2013-05-20T21:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/post-86552>

Autism Speaks website states:

" What causes Autism?
Not long ago, the answer to this question would have been “we have no idea.” Research is now delivering the answers. First and foremost, we now know that there is no one cause of autism just as there is no one type of autism. Over the last five years, scientists have identified a number of rare gene changes, or mutations, associated with autism. Research has identified more than a hundred autism risk genes. In around 15 percent of cases, a specific genetic cause of a person’s autism can be identified. However, most cases involve a complex and variable combination of genetic risk and environmental factors that influence early brain development.

In other words, in the presence of a genetic predisposition to autism, a number of nongenetic, or environmental, influence further increase a child’s risk. The clearest evidence of these environmental risk factors involves events before and during birth. They include advanced parental age at time of conception (both mom and dad), maternal illness during pregnancy, extreme prematurity and very low birth weight and certain difficulties during birth, particularly those involving periods of oxygen deprivation to the baby’s brain. Mothers exposed to high levels of pesticides and air pollution may also be at higher risk of having a child with ASD. It is important to keep in mind that these factors, by themselves, do not cause autism. Rather, in combination with genetic risk factors, they appear to modestly increase risk.

A small but growing body of research suggests that autism risk is less among children whose mothers took prenatal vitamins (containing folic acid) in the months before and after conception.

Increasingly, researchers are looking at the role of the immune system in autism. Autism Speaks is working to increase awareness and investigation of these and other issues, where further research has the potential to improve the lives of those who struggle with autism."

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-20T23:16:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/post-86559>

brain gut three...............humans brains and immune systems..............all tied together. Re Read it. Evolutionary design can teach us a lot if we are paying attn.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-20T01:51:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-court-rules-autism-caused-by-mmr-vaccine.7113/post-235151>

**THE VAX ISSUE FOR ME IS SIMPLE:** The issue is not the efficacy of vaccines but the vaccine obligation. Why are they mandatory when they have no undergone RCT or double blind testing like any other drug? Why "confuse" two subjects so differently? The only reason I can come up with is the profit of Big Pharma and the CDC. We should treat vaccines like drugs. Test them with vax vs unvaxxed trials to see if they are safe. We should pursue purpose over profit as clinicians, not the other way around. We always need to employ the precautionary principle when we really do not know the efficacy vs safety curve for anything we advocate.

[http://www.oatext.com/Pilot-compara...nvaccinated-6-to-12-year-old-U-S-children.php](http://www.oatext.com/Pilot-compara...nvaccinated-6-to-12-year-old-U-S-children.php)

# Thread: my thyroid is waking up
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9

### agatha — 2013-05-20T23:16:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86560>

Hi there y'all
I have been taking thyroxine for some years and have been having to gradually increase my dose. However, in the last few weeks I have been having hyperthyroid symptoms and my TSH is now 0.4 (lowest end of normal range) - I have reduced my dose of thyroxine. About six weeks ago I changed two things - i started eating fish 3-4 times a week and making a little seaweed drink using about a teaspoon of dried hijiki about 3 days a week. I also started using an earthing sheet at night.

Do any of you have any thoughts about whether it is the increased iodine or the earthing which is more likely to have woken up my thyroid gland?

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-21T01:45:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86565>

Thanks for these replies. I am a bit nervous about the iodine as I know some people think it can stir up Hashimoto's. I didn't have the Hashimotos antibodies when i got tested a few years ago but I don't want to create a problem. Have you been OK, Shijin since you started with the iodine?

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-21T22:49:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86614>

no issues as far as I know - we haven't tested AB - since I tested negative for 4 consecutive years all I know is I crave it. I'm eating seaweed as my main veggie at every meal; my kids are doing the same thing - especially dd - who we just discovered is hypo - at age 5!

I also put the Lugols in our water - I never could understand why I was drinking so much water - until I realized my kids preferred my water w/the 5% lugols in it over their water w/the 2% lugols in it.. I took about 6mths to work up to the 5% lugols - and did the same for the kids...

### Grizz — 2013-05-21T23:07:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86618>

Agatha,
Iodine requires Selenium - they are co-supplements. One without the other can cause problems with the thyroid... primarily hashi. This is why the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol specifies selenium as one of the co-supplements.
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

For more on the connection between selenium & the thyroid, see these articles:
[https://docs.google.com/document/d/...5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.q6cw9ojckwx1](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_q9ixGwZFon9yidvmZ0IFbp3Vj5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.q6cw9ojckwx1)

### Meg — 2013-05-22T01:29:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86624>

Yeah Agatha! I know it feels good to see some change in the right direction. I am hypo and have had to back down on meds twice since February due to hyper symptoms and labs (TSH .006!). I can only attribute it to one thing, GROUNDING! That was my only change. How do you feel on the grounding sheets? I tried to use them but they made me feel bad- too much EMF in my environment I guess, made me like a like an antenae. Good luck moving forward.

### agatha — 2013-05-22T17:09:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86670>

Thanks for these helpful replies - I think I will continue cautiously with the seaweed and see what happens but I can see from your links Grizz that I need to make sure my selenium is replete - do you think Brazil nuts will do the job - I prefer food to supplements?
Meg, I am pleased with the earthing sheet - I sleep better and my husband's blood pressure which was high has reduced - but we live in a leafy location with not too much EMF. I did have a weird reaction the first few nights but that has settled now.
I am so grateful for you all and Doc K - your knowledge, wisdom and helpfulness. I would be entirely in the dark if I had to rely on my GP for all my medical info.

### Grizz — 2013-05-23T00:46:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86686>

Agatha,

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-smart-meter-pilot-being-started-in-my-community.7127/post-86626>

Okay all you hunter-gatherers of information, I would like to suggest using this thread as a clearinghouse of links for Smart Meters and their effects.

I live in a small community, and they are in the process of initiating a smart meter pilot project. 1500 homes have been selected to receive smart meters, and I would like to write an article for the paper so that people can make an informed decision. As a side-note, I need hard data. I live in a community with a disproportionate number of PhDs. We've been ranked as one of the "smartest" communities in the nation. These people are gonna need the most sciency-stuff I can get my hands on.

I would like to see this pilot fail and I'm hoping you can help!

Thank you!

# Thread: Twist DHEA cream- is it any good?

### Meg — 2013-05-22T01:48:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/twist-dhea-cream-is-it-any-good.7128/post-86627>

I've been meaning to chime in about this for awhile now. I have seen crazy good results from using this cream that Dr. K mentioned awhile back on a webinar. But beware- a little goes a long way, at least it did for me. Using the recommended dosage (which is two pumps per day) I went from 170 to 942, so I had to back down some. I was on 350 mg of oral 7 keto dhea before - and only had a dhea of 172! After using Twist I felt a noticable difference in my energy and sleep. If anyone is looking for a way to boost DHEA, I recomend that you try this and report back!

### fitness@home — 2013-05-22T02:40:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/twist-dhea-cream-is-it-any-good.7128/post-86629>

### fitness@home — 2013-07-30T04:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/twist-dhea-cream-is-it-any-good.7128/post-91147>

Reviving thread. This is an off label use for the product and I am not encouraging or recommending it. Just reporting my N=1.

Lately I've been getting an increase in compliments on my skin. What I started initially was after I rubbed the Twist DHEA cream on my arms, I would use what was left on my face. There wasn't much on my finger tips by that point.

After a few weeks of this and nothing freaky happening , I started using just a tiny dab on my cheekbones and the area around my eyes (crows feet). Not saying that this is the only reason for the improvement in the appearance of my skin... I have been working on other things too.

Just wanted to share.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-25T18:13:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/twist-dhea-cream-is-it-any-good.7128/post-96084>

A couple of months have passed. I’m stopping back to report on the results of using the DHEA cream on my face. I use it on the skin around the eyes and a tiny bit on my eyelids.

Over the past couple of years I noted a crepey look to the skin on my eyelids. When I would lift my eyebrows, there would be a distinct upside down “V” shape in the skin wrinkling (thanks Mom). She has had two eyelid lift surgeries in the past 15 years (and both were covered by insurance…not considered cosmetic).

I am pleased to report that the crepey appearance has improved and the “V” has faded significantly! The skin looks more plumped and hydrated. I can’t say conclusively that it is the topical application of DHEA cream but it certainly hasn’t had a negative effect.

**Clarification: this is not me!** It is just the closest picture I found showing what I described above.

# Thread: Living on the Equator - Thoughts?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### badger — 2013-05-22T19:41:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86677>

Hello, first post here. Thanks for clicking.

So, in about a month I will be relocating somewhere South of America, to +/- latitude 0Â°. I've read a good bit of blog posts here, I've read nearly the whole CT series and I'm starting to get into Gut/Brain etc... Trying to figure out where I'm at on my health journey.

From what I can understand, one major emphasis here is on the evolutionary effects of changing light cycles on carb availability & human circadian biology, as well as exposure to cold environments and adaptation to the cold, & the effects thereof.

Where I'll be living there is no change in light cycle, it's 12h light / 12h dark, forever and always. It's also in the mountains, so temps fluctuate from highs ~80F to lows ~50F. There is a wet season, and a less wet season.

Both myself and others have observed what seem like metabolic effects on northerners (americans and europeans mostly) who move into this region. People lose weight, sometimes quickly and dramatically, despite a diet full of white rice and white potatoes, very little in the way of vegetables, and (beef, pork, chicken) + seafood. People walk around more and tend to be less sedentary. People claim to feel better, generally more alive and vital, whether that's because of better soil health, less stress, better air quality, healthier lifestyle, or positive cultural effects, I'm not sure. I have an intuition that it has to do with changes in circadian biology when living close to the equator.

I'd be interested to hear what people here think about the possible short term & long term effects of living close to the equator. How do recommendations for optimal health via diet + lifestyle change in such an environment vs. in northern, 4-seasons climes?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-23T02:22:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86691>

you must the read Quantum biology 7......a must.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-23T02:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86692>

When you live equatorial things in your diet become flexible provided you understand water conduction and the photoelectric effect.

### badger — 2013-05-26T00:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86855>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=86692)
>
> When you live equatorial things in your diet become flexible provided you understand water conduction and the photoelectric effect.

So, taking care of healing the various semiconductors you speak of in QB7 and discuss in epi-paleoRX would create the natural ability to absorb and convert sunlight into chemical energy... then living at a latitude where one would be able to absorb MORE sunlight over the course of the year, consistently spread throughout the year, translates into the ability to take on more flexibility in the diet without sacrificing optimal health? Now by flexibility do you mean... more leeway in carb intake??

I remember reading the blog post where you were talking about the health differences in the people of northern and southern India, how the people in the tropical south were vastly sicker than in the mountainous north. Is there an example of biologically-appropriate and 'ancestral' life-ways and diets from the equatorial zone? Polynesian, lowland Amazonian tribes, perhaps? What of the possibility of someone with non-native genetics adapting to such a different bioregion?

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-26T00:31:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86857>

I'm awfully interested in this as well. My fiance and I don't plan on staying in America for our whole lives (but I was thinking somewhere in Colorado would be nice - mountainous, can perform Intermittent Hypoxia Training, etc.). Realistically we will be here for a minimum of the next 2-3 years after I further my education and seek out what graduate schools to attend, possibly even the next 5-7 years. After, though, we've thought about leaving the country. She has family out in Poland (small towns, farms, grass-lands with plenty of streams, rivers, ponds for CT and grounding), and although this sounds outlandish, some innovative architects have created housing in Vietnam that can float in case of floods/natural disasters, are made from recycled, biodegradable products, extremely sturdy and protective against winds, and has "hanging gardens" on the side of the home for herbs and veggies. I know from what I've been learning here that a decision such as this would impact my health, performance, longevity, and subjective well-being dramatically.

What's confusing me is, I know as a species we evolved in Africa near the equator, then migrated to different regions. In fact, one group of early human ancestors ended up mating with Denisovian's, so some have Denisovian blood to this day (I wonder how that would affect their dietary choices to fully express epigenetics/genetics). But I believe that Dr. Kruse stated (correct me if I'm wrong), we have "aquatic ape ancestors" from the ice age, so then wouldn't cold, dark, and inland be the best location to live rather than dry, light, warm and arid? If you became cold adapted to the point of Dr. Kruse or Wim Hoff, would you even need as much sunlight if eating a proper diet (high protein/fat, zero carb) for uncoupling of proteins and what not? Wow, I have a lot more to learn, haha.

### Optimalbound — 2013-05-26T01:42:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86861>

A couple of years ago, I spent a week on St. Helena, a tiny island in the south Atlantic, 11 degrees south of the equator. I don't exactly know the dietary habits of the locals, but they have an endless supply of fresh seafood and sunshine, grassfed meat, absolutely no cell phones at all, and hardly any wifi. Yet I've never seen so many obese and unhealty looking people anywhere. Does anyone have any idea what's going on there?

### caroline — 2013-05-26T03:44:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-86868>

A few years ago when we were in the Cook Islands - people didn't seem to be very healthy. I had to see a Doc and she told us that the incidence of Diabetes is very high ......

### badger — 2013-06-04T05:16:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-87424>

Also remember, the equator is more or less ringed by so-called "third world" countries, places that have been largely colonized and systematically dismantled and stripped of resources by the empires throughout history. I'd think much of the sickness and malnutrition that exists in the tropics has to do with bad dietary choices, which have supplanted traditional diets in many tropical places. Apparently the Maori, native Hawaiians, and other comparable tropical, tribal people, in terms of their retention of traditional life-ways and diets, are very vibrant, strong and healthy people still. One can still go to many places in the Andes where 80 yr-old women carrying giant bundles on their backs for miles uphill can be seen commonly. I guess another question that comes up is, if you live in the tropics it never gets below freezing (unless you're 10,000ft up a volcano), so what is the importance of cold adaption and Cold Thermogenesis there?

Having a time wrapping my brain around all this... maybe I should start at the Gut/BrainRX and go from there

### kathylu — 2013-06-04T07:15:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-87425>

I grew up in Hawaii. Many obese Polynesians there. Culturally obesity is quite acceptable there...the royalty of Hawaii (the Alii) were all fat, purposefully so. It represented wealth, health and fertility. That being said, the modern diet there is basically all carbs. Rice, beans, taro root (poi), cassava, sweet potatoes, saimin (noodles), lots of sugar and processed foods. Probably the healthiest thing is the seafood and prodigious quantities of Spam eaten...

I think the population has subverted the healthy environment...artificial light, too many processed carbs and grains. Their bodies can only take so many insults...the environment can't help them.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-04T17:16:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-87450>

> [badger said:](/goto/post?id=87424)
>
> Also remember, the equator is more or less ringed by so-called "third world" countries, places that have been largely colonized and systematically dismantled and stripped of resources by the empires throughout history. I'd think much of the sickness and malnutrition that exists in the tropics has to do with bad dietary choices, which have supplanted traditional diets in many tropical places. Apparently the Maori, native Hawaiians, and other comparable tropical, tribal people, in terms of their retention of traditional life-ways and diets, are very vibrant, strong and healthy people still. One can still go to many places in the Andes where 80 yr-old women carrying giant bundles on their backs for miles uphill can be seen commonly. I guess another question that comes up is, if you live in the tropics it never gets below freezing (unless you're 10,000ft up a volcano), so what is the importance of cold adaption and Cold Thermogenesis there?
>
> Having a time wrapping my brain around all this... maybe I should start at the Gut/BrainRX and go from there

this significance is in CT 4 and 6 and the last chapter in my book.......

### badger — 2013-06-17T02:06:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-on-the-equator-thoughts.7133/post-88199>

Hey Dr. Jack, I've definitely been diving deep, reading as much as I can muster (although sitting in a chair and looking at an artifical-light box is kind of tragi-comic, so I keep it to a reasonable minimum) and going hard with the CT face-dunking. Listened to all the podcasts I could find as well. Thank you so very much for your big heart and compassion for the world and the misled modern warm-adapted hominids, ha! For your drive and commitment to your message and the delivery of said message.... The flower does not bloom until its time is right, else the bloom will not set seed for the flowering of future generations. That's a message from a mentor of mine...

OK, so I hear what you're saying in CT 6. Doesn't it stand to reason that it would also be a circadian mis-match for a person to perform the CT protocol long-term while living in a climate that doesn't see a classic "winter", sub-freezing temps, or is the mis-match inherent in living in that "sub-saharan" rift valley in the first place (which I think I remember you saying), particularly if said person's genetic heritage is from a further northern latitude? I get that despite where you might live, if you're a human being you will benefit from cold adaptation, particularly if you're sick.

In practical terms, I've been doing CT on and off for a few weeks and have buckled down over the past 5 days now that I've landed here (on the equator). There's also a creek right next door that I've been getting in which is around 60deg, my guess. The climate is actually fairly cool since I'm at about 4,000ft, right in the 55-75 range. I've noticed with the face dunks, I can stay under and breathe through a straw, up to 8-10 minutes now. Strangely enough this makes my sinuses really itchy and I sneeze and get mucous-y as hell. Also noticed how, after dunking in the creek for 20 min or enough till I started shivering, afterward my hands were numb for over an hour and just wouldn't get warm.. obviously I'm not cold-adapted. My hands also fall asleep really easily when I'm sleeping, lying down in bed. Just saying. Bad circulation?

# Thread: The effect of the smell of chocolate on the brain.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-the-smell-of-chocolate-on-the-brain.7142/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-23T19:09:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-the-smell-of-chocolate-on-the-brain.7142/post-86734>

This was posted on Facebook by a lady who identifies herself as a mental health counselor in Switzerland who pretty much mirrors Dr. K on a lot of things....CT and EMFs and a lot more.

She wrote this today regarding studys done to test various smells and then measured brain activity....especially about the smell of chocolate. Also she talks about what compounds are contained in chocolate and what they do for the body.

One question I have is.....chocolate contains the compound **Theobromine** 200mg in 100 grams of chocolate. Grizz has written about how important it is to rid the body of bromine, chlorides, fluorides and Aluminum. Does **Theobromine** compound doing anything to us that we do not want?

On the good side, chocolate contains a lot of resveratrol & a lot of other antioxidants good for the body.

Perhaps someone who has sleep issues prior to retiring for the night can open a bar of Lindt and smell the aroma and assist the sleep cycle.

I especially like Lindt 95% available at my Wal Mart for $1.68 for a 4 oz bar. I also like raw cacao nibs in Greek Yogurt that I get from nutsonline.com

The following is a translation by BING from the original German so it is a little disjointed at times.

Therapiezentrum Steinfels

Had a technical explanation just once again a B.s. attack and here:
The scent of chocolate can affect brain activity, which resulted in an experiment of the London neuro psychologist Neil Martin. "No other smell that we tested had such strong effects of chocolate". Together with colleagues, he had analyzed the effect of different fragrances in a study on the people. The subjects sat here in special rooms with neutral smell air, wore blindfold and earmuffs, so that they could focus on the sense of smell. While they were confronted with various flavours, their brain activity was measured. "Chocolate turned out in our odor experiment as a star", emphasizes Martin. Smell of coffee or garlic smell, not the aroma of
Peppermint, nor that of baked beans could cause such effects like chocolate in the brain. As "pleasant, comfortable and very relaxing", the "nose" felt the chocolate scent. "It seems at the same time to relax the brain and to attract, but also mentally awake to make," portrays Martin.

The aroma of chocolate increased at the same time the alpha and Beta activity of brain waves (see: frequency of EEG). "Alpha is often relaxed, but watchful adults found, beta again with people who operate a kind of mental arithmetic", explains Martin, who themselves can not explain mentioned effects of chocolate. Perhaps, the emotional Center in the brain is activated. But that in turn would open the question why not other pleasant scents trigger such effects.

It remains also a mystery why the smell of coffee and rotting meat showed no effects. "Just from these two odors we expected, that they can do a lot", says Martin.

Finally, American neuro-psychologists found that in some people the pleasure of chocolate can even lead to addiction. In subjects that are quickly fill with chocolate, the researchers found increased brain activity in areas associated with addiction in conjunction. The subjects who consumed chocolate just enjoyable, the region of the brain showed no activity.

Cocoa contains the following psychoactive substances (per 100 g): caffeine: 68 milligrams; Theobromine (similar effect as caffeine, blood vessels widen and stimulated the heart muscle): 200 milligrams. Smallest amounts of biogenic amines increase blood pressure increase and affect the functions of the brain. In addition, 1 milligram phenylethylamine causes the release of dopamine in the brain. Anandamide occurs only in smallest quantities and is a hemp plants occurring THC-like noise substance produced by the body itself. The release of anandamide occurs when stress and pain, therefore increases the feeling of well-being. There are only small amounts of chocolate to prove. Contains antioxidant chocolate polyphenols, which include anti-inflammatory and cancer-inhibiting effect.

American scientists have recently discovered that ground cocoa and unsweetened baking chocolate contain high amounts of Resveratrol (also in red wine and grape juice), preventing a highly active, secondary plant substance that protects the cells and the formation of free radicals. (Translated by Bing)

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-25T00:58:58+0300

yet again Science explains why I LOVE CHOCOLATE.... nom nom nom.... now where did I place my 100% chocolate.... oh wait - there's some nibs....

### SeaHorse — 2013-05-27T15:35:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-the-smell-of-chocolate-on-the-brain.7142/post-86939>

Recently I've been reading a bit about chocolate and Parkinsons....if you google the two together you come up with some interesting contradictions...some saying there are componds that are damaging and others saying the polyphenals are a benefit.

Because my mother had Parkinsons and chocolate (even without sugar) triggers a "more, more, more" in my brain, I'm going to stay away. I will use it as a mild stimulant instead of caffein if I have to driving long distances (i.e. 12 hour trips).

# Thread: Long Term Use of BCP and Recovery

### mafixit — 2013-05-23T22:38:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-term-use-of-bcp-and-recovery.7143/post-86742>

After being on BC Pills since I was 16 - I am 49 now - I let them go late last year and moved to BHRT. It's been a bad downhill ride ever since.

I am wondering if:

A. I have done damage to my system that cannot be reversed.
B. How long it will take for my body to adjust to not having the artificial crap in my system.

Ever since I stopped, I have felt worse and worse by the day. It's been 6 months. I am under the care of a hormone doc. I see her in a couple of weeks for more labs, so I will have more of an answer of where I am at and why I still feel like crap all over.

I have advised my young adult daughter to stay away from artificial hormones, knowing what I know now. At least I can save her

Karen from Arizona

> T3, Free **4.0** (2.3-4.2 pg/mL)
> Reverse **T3** 17 (9-28 ng/dL)
> Pregnenolone **41** (13-208 ng/dL)
> DHEA sulfate **103** (25-240 mcg/dL)
> Insulin **6** (<23 uIU/mL)
> Progesterone **<0.5** (<1.4 ng/mL)
> Estradiol **22** (< OR = 39 pg/mL)
>
> Testosterone, Free, Bio
> (/B\] (250-1100 ng/dL)
> Testo, Free **46.1** (46.0 – 224.0 pg/mL)
> Testo, Bio **90.8 L** (110.0 – 575.0 ng/dL)
> Sex hormone binding globulin **35** (10.50 nmol.L)
> Albumin serum **4.3** (3.6-5.1 g/dL)
>
> For one month took 25,000 IU’s of Vitamin D. Currently on 10,000 and will be checking D levels soon.
>
> Thank you for any insight.

What jumps out at me are the following:
D3 level needs to be above 70 ng/ml The 10000IU daily will eventually get you there 70 to 120 target.
Even though you are supplementing D3, be sure to get at least 20 min a day of full sun on as much of your skin as possible to recharge your body with photons from the sun. We all need the photons due to our lack of sun due to sun phobia in the past. My dermatologist tells me I am going to die of melanoma if I don't use sunscreen. Funny thing is the last 3 checkups have shown no basal cell or squamous spots. I did have one lesion excised two years a little after I found Dr. K.
Triglycerides need to come down to under 50 and 40-35 is even better.
HDL needs to go up as high as you can get it Over 80 This can get achieved by taking Slo-Niacin 1000 to 2000mg per day split morning and evening. This is the only product that does not get me a flush response. All the so called no flush products make me flush.
Dhea-s needs to be higher. Dr K has mentioned this DHEA cream and others have said it raised their levels very well. Twist DHEA cream. [http://www.amazon.com/Health2Go-Inc...TF8&qid=1369420269&sr=1-3&keywords=dhea+cream](http://www.amazon.com/Health2Go-Inc-1-7-OZ-pump/dp/B007OUXDF8/ref=sr_1_3?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1369420269&sr=1-3&keywords=dhea+cream)

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7128-Twist-DHEA-cream-is-it-any-good&highlight=twist](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7128-Twist-DHEA-cream-is-it-any-good&highlight=twist)

Also, you also need to do a NMR Lipid profile test which will give you the amount of LDL particles you have in your blood. And it will tell you how many of your total LDL particles are the small dense particles that promote Atherosclerosis - 10% or under is ideal.

Jansz can give you insight on your preg and testosterone levels.

For an exercise program, once a day, take 5 minutes and run all out for 20 seconds, rest 10 seconds, then run all out for 20 sec and rest 10 sec...repeat for 8 cycles and then quit. I now do 10 cycles for 6 min out of my day. Not too much to ask. These are called Tabata exercises.

Stay on the epi-paleo RX and eat the 50 grams of protein in the morning...within 30 min of getting up....with adequate fat. You will not need to eat lunch and then lots of salad and fish or meat for dinner. Use a lot of coconut oil at this time of year to cook with. In winter, use primarily animal fats.

Drink at least a gallon of non fluoridated water a day. You will be amazed at the amount of energy you get from the water after a week or so...so much so that your hunger hormone...ghrelin...will tell you that you are not hungry.

You will feel much better and your labs will improve dramatically over time.

Again you've gotten great info from Dex!

DHEA, Testosterone, and pregnenolone are in the toilet - I'm assuming this is your Husband/SO Kathy. He needs to start eating Epi-paleo, following the Leptin reset, limiting artificial light at night and emf.

in addition, he can raise his DHEA, Testosterone levels by Laying out in the sun with as much skin exposed as possible - limit the sunscreen - but he'll need to build up time given his low vitamin D. He can also raise his testosterone levels by CTing! get him in a cold TUB ASAP! or at least start face dunks.

he's got a serious case of pregnenolone steal, as a result of a circadian mismatch. which is impacting all of his hormones and his Vit D! He really needs to pay attention to the circadian cycles.

His Free T3: rT3 ratio is 23 - which is good, but for the rT3 value you want it as low as possible and his is in the mid range.

ONe thing you can do is consider supplementing w/DHEA as well as Pregnenolone. both of which you can buy on Amazon, and LEF.org without a prescription.
a good starting point would be 100mg of DHEA and btwn 100-200mg of Pregnenolone and retest in a month or so...

as JanSz says "I'm not a Doctor" but I have figure out how to help myself!

Kathy's (lucky) husband Paul;

Hope you guys are doing good, live long and prosper.
\-------------------------------------------------------

You will get lots of good advice here.
So I will assume that you are following what dr Jack Kruse is preaching.
\----------------

Now is summer, easy to get sun exposure.
Do not do it until your vit D is tested and is highly over 50, better 100.
It may not happen this summer.

Depending on your skin, but specially when light skinned, easily burned, red head, it may be a good idea to get (melanotan II) injections.
(Melanotan II), MT-II, MT-2
can be bought over internet, google

(Melanotan 2) or MT 2
not to be confused with (Melanotan I)

Melanotan II (Listeni/mɛˈlÃ¦nɵtÃ¦n/) developed at the University of Arizona is a synthetic analog of the naturally occurring melanocortin peptide hormone alpha-melanocyte stimulating hormone (α-MSH)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melanotan\_II](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melanotan_II)

Sometimes it also have aphrodisiac effects (so be prepared, near your hubby, for oxytocim making if it would work that way)
\==================================

Steroid Hormone Panel is trashed badly,
but what sticks out is cortisol

7-9 am 1.05 (0.27 – 1.18 mcg/dl)
11am-1pm 2.23 (0.10-0.41)
3-5pm 0.07 (0.05-0.27)
10pm-12am 0.06 (0.03-0.14)

1.05+2.23+0.07+0.06=3.41

1.18+0.41+0.27+0.14=2

There is twice as much cortisol than it should be,
actually there is 3.41 but it should be about 2.

That is very important.
You must first figure out why that is.
You must first find out how much cortisol you are actually making (and discarding in urine over 24 hr).

Depending on your resources and inclination you may want to just go to LabCorp and get:

Cortisol, Urinary Free (24-hour) Test Number: 004432 CPT Code: 82530

But collecting urine 24 hr is a chore, and when you already have a sample, it would be a good idea to put it to better use.

[http://www.tahomaclinic.com/services/](http://www.tahomaclinic.com/services/)
[http://meridianvalleylab.com/](http://meridianvalleylab.com/)

test 4100 Comprehensive ULTIMATE (includes Oxtocin, Melatonin, and hGH)

[http://meridianvalleylab.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/11/CompULTIMATE\_sample.pdf](http://meridianvalleylab.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/11/CompULTIMATE_sample.pdf)

\==================

Depending on what you may want to do, at the minimum go to LabCorp and see how much cortisol you are making over 24 hrs.
but that test at Meridian valley (dr Johnathan Wright) is the best when trying to figure what to do.

Assuming that the alert is premature, and you are not excreting excessive amounts of cortisol,
we will go to "normal" program of raising your steroid hormone panel.
That will include
raising your currently very low
pregnenolone
progesterone
testosterone
DHEAs

Keep in mind that when you take a one drop of blood, and measure all major steroid hormones within that drop of blood,
the biggest part by weight is taken by DHEAs

DHEAs part is 95.4% of them all

and
it happens that your blood test says
DHEA sulfate 103 (25-240 mcg/dL)

This part (25-240 mcg/dL)
is misleading and worthless,
you want DHEAs level of young 20-25 yo Adonis
and that is about 500
so DHEAs needs to be raised 5x

practically there is very severe deficiency of major hormone.
\--------------------------------------

Also Totaltestosterone needs to be much higher
the aim is to have
BioAvailableTestosterone=575.0 ng/dL
instead of
Testo, Bio 90.8 L (110.0 – 575.0 ng/dL)(this is test done at QuestDiagnostics)
other labs have different values

there is three more items that need to be reviewed/addressed
PSA---- prostate (state unknown)
Hgb, Hct, RBC, iron, ferritin ---- blood (state unknown)
SHBG -- Sex Hormone Globulin (currently high)

You may want to check the missing items first or start testosterone supplementation and fill in the blanks latter

One more, fertility, is it important to preserve?
If fertility is important, do not come close to testosterone supplementation unless you have ample stash of Ovidrel(rHCG) or HCG in your possession, so it will last you at least 6 months.

Another one,
testosterone supplementation will increase your estrogens, that may force you to use Aromatase Inhibitors (Anastrozole=Arimidex)
because you have low progesterone
Progesterone <0.5 (<1.4 ng/mL)
and will be waiting for cortisol status

I will wait for response on all those questions, before proceeding with

testosterone supplementation
pregnenolone-->progesterone--->cortisol/aldosterone management
\-----------

Note:
you may be dealing with major mando pregnenolone steal to produce huge amounts of cortisol

This situation may be (partially) rectified by applying good advice from dr Jack Kruse (read up on that, very important)
I am bringing this up because you will be supplementing with DHEA (I prefer DHEA-MLM)
and
at first you will need a lots of DHEA pills but latter (hopefully) less, when implementations of dr Kruses stuff kicks in.

\-------------

[http://www.amazon.com/Micronized-Ta...36&sr=8-2-spell&keywords=dhea+Allergy+reseach](http://www.amazon.com/Micronized-Tablets-Allergy-Research-Group/dp/B004BZJCCC/ref=sr_1_sc_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1369415136&sr=8-2-spell&keywords=dhea+Allergy+reseach)
DHEA 50 mg Micronized Lipid Matrix 60 Scored Tablets by Allergy Research Group

Use 4 pills/day, two at wakeup and the other two 4 hrs latter
goal
DHEA sulfate 490 mcg/dL on blood test (so, titrate dose size, do blood test month latter)

\----------------
look at this post:
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508&viewfull=1#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508&viewfull=1#post81508)

Get
Spectracell

(EMFA) by Genova Diagnostics

and/or

Test ID: FAPCP
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

but if you want to deal with another doc, but get superior information
call 888 320 8338
dr Patricia Kane
for referral to your local participating doctor and ask for
Red Blood Cell Fatty Acid test from Kennedy Krieger Laboratory
with interpretation
(this may be overkill at this time)

\----------------------------------------------------------

Do not eat fish or krill oils

Do not eat Proscar or Avodart, be careful with Saw Palmetto.

Do not do tests that measure omega6/omega3 index or O6/O3 ratio

...

### Eve — 2013-05-24T18:09:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leather-sole-shoes-worth-it.7151/post-86794>

Is there a point to wearing leather sole shoes if the ground I mainly walk on during the day is concrete (e.g., parking lots) or inside an office? I don't have the opportunity to walk on grass (even in shoes) during the day.
Also, it rains a lot here and leather soles are slippy, lol.
Btw, at home, I'm barefoot pretty much all the time.

anything is better than nothing...

Thanks for the info... I have been aware of the concrete being conductive especially if wet. SO I am going all leather soles from here on in. I'm a recent discoverer of the Bio Hacking Culture. So this shoe thing is just one of my bucket list items I want to change. I just did some searching on some leather soled dress shoes from Johnston & Murphy. They answered my inquiry said that many of there shoes are leather and sent me a link. In high school (1980-81) I had my appendix removed. 1982 I was in an accident where a fell out of a moving truck as it tipped over. (Didnâ€™t realize that affected me until I went to a naturopathic doctor and reading, recently, Dr. Kruse article about concussions.) In 1986 I had a thyroid storm (over active thyroid) I did not choose removal surgery. I had asthma 2-3 years agoâ€¦..no longer have it (thatâ€™s when I started to research about health and made dietary changes).
Currently, I get up and go to the bathroom at night (at least one time)â€¦.on occasion I sleep through the night. I realize Iâ€™m dehydrated on the cellular level. I wake up between 4-5:30 am. Usually get to bed by 9 pm.

### caroline — 2013-05-24T23:46:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-a-49-year-old-female.7154/post-86807>

Hi Kathy! I am sure that Dex will chime in on your labs as soon as he has time - he is great at reading labs and also JanZ.

Are you doing the Leptin reset? Light cycles are extremely important and drinking lots of non- fluoridated water. What about your EMF exposure? All the details really matter a lot!

Have you started C/T yet - that would help tremendously ... and how is your sleep?

I know it is alot to take on board but ...baby steps. Keep reading and re reading the blogs and eventually you start to connect the dots. It is great that you both got your labs done and super that you are doing it together!

Keep reading and asking questions and we will all try to help. If you are 100% committed and follow the protocols you will get there .... but it does take time .....We are all on the same path but everyone's journey is very different.

We are all looking for our own n=1 - it takes time and this journey is not linear - but there are lots of amazing successes.

Kathy, I will refer you to the post I did here: [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7150-My-first-labs](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7150-My-first-labs) Much of it applies to you.

Do the NMR test for your LDL particle number and particle size. You will get back from the lab total particle LDL number and a particle number for your small dense LDL. That will show as small LDL-P and then divide small dense LDL-P number by your total LDL-P particle number and that gives you your percentage.

Your DHEA should be pushed up to the upper limit. The Twist cream seems to work well for folks.

Triglycerides need to come down.

Vitamin D3 needs to be 70-120 with D3 supplement plus sun. One thing on the other thread I did not mention is Dr. K wants us to use more sunlight in the summer because of the longer days thus less supplementation.

Janz can help on the hormones.

HDL needs to go up. Slo-Niacin.

Epi-paleo diet combined with the LEPTIN rx will dramatically change your labs and how you feel. Drinking at least a gallon of non fluroridated water a day. will assist you in how you feel also. Try to get most of that done by 2pm so you go to bed with less potential to fill up your bladder during the night....causing you to wake up.
Your skin will become smooth and silky to the touch after a couple of weeks drinking that much non fluoridated water. Your desire for food will abate once you start using the water that gets inside your cells as a fuel source.

And you will be down to 140 lbs in less than six months provided you stay strict epi-paleo. Nothing in a box to be eaten. No grains or sugar ever. And no artificial sweeteners.

You have a roadmap given to you by Dr K. Determine your goals and objectives and let nothing stand in your way of achievement. You are totally responsible for your own condition.

KAthy you've got great advise from Dex & Audrey!

Your Free T3: rT3 ratio is 13.3 - you want that ABOVE 20 - when its above 20 it means your thyroid is converting T4 to T3 and your not making rT3 - which can bind to the same receptor sites in your cells - limiting their ability to function properly -

you state you're - premenopausal - at what point in your cycle did you have these labs taken - that will help us figure out what your hormone panel looks like. additionally next time you get labs I'd get the Estradiol(Sensitive) - and a full estrogen panel. In order to deterimine your Pg:E2 - we need the estrdiol

you're pregnenolone is low - which isn't surprising given your freeT3:rT3 ratio - you've got some pregnenolone steal going on. which is also impacting your vitamin D levels.

focus on eating Epi-paleo following the Leptin prescription format BAB - lunch 4hrs later - dinner 4hrs later & At least 4 hrs prior to going to bed. limit artificial light at night, and limit your exposures to EMF

and of course ask questions....

# Thread: One Quick Question On Leptin Rx
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-25T05:48:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86834>

Hey everyone! I have a quick question on what I should do in regards to the LeptinRx. I've been reading and annotating all the blogs starting from Dr. Kruse' very first blog, including looking through all the citations and comments and suggested resources. I've just reached Hormones 101... I can't even explain how much I have learned from only these first few blogs. What I came to ask, though, is I skimmed a blog of Jack's (Adrenal Rx/Fatigue blog) and it made me think I should do the Leptin Rx. I have the dark circles under my eyes, I don't _always_ wake up energized (more often than not, though), I wake up a lot during the night, and I have inconsistent sleep patterns. The thing is, I've already been IFing for months now, and I can easily go the day without getting hungry. The past month I just started Bulletproof Intermittent fasting, which is essentially a fast minus fat and Upgraded Coffee. I have even begun fasted state body by science training with an elevation training mask. I'm also cycling in and out of ketosis. What I'm getting at is I'm obviously fat adapted, not fully adapted (24-36months... I've been adapted for a few months), and I don't think I'm LR as I'm lean and trying to build some muscle.

I just know that I have had very faulty sleep schedules as a child all the way up until the last few months (after discovering Dave Asprey/Jack), sleeping many nights 4-5hrs then waking up to a full day of school followed by intense football or wrestling practice. Couple this with a poor diet and poor lifestyle choices, throw in a few months of vegetarianism in there, I feel as though I have at least damaged my adrenals/thyroid a bit. I can't afford testing though. I can just tell subjectively and from the dark circles under my eyes/skin quality.

I was thinking about marrying some of the LeptinRx ideas with the post-LeptinRx ideas. What I mean is, consume a BAB, skip lunch, eat dinner 4-5hrs before sleeping. I will set alarms to wake up every day at 9 AM meaning I'll get 6-8hrs of sleep a day, ASAP after rising I will consume a tbsp of sea salt and get out in the sun and look into the sky for a few minutes while my BPC is brewing. Workout once a week BBS style and refeed with some carbs since it is summer and getting close to June 21 (the time Jack stated he consumes the most carbs). My only concerns were HIT exercise and the carb refeed. **Would the refeed and/or exercise some how completely disrupt the LeptinRx**, or no since I'll still be refraining from snacking and creating a morning ritual of breakfast/sea salt/sun/self oxytocin release/Cold shower? **Should I even attempt this for the next 8-12weeks or refrain since I'm most definitely not LR/IR.... maybe just jump to the post-leptinRx style of eating**? To put it into perspective, I do the BBS workout as I feel I need to gain weight (maybe another reason for the Leptin Reset) and once I have the funds again I will be engaging in BJJ while striving to compete in tournaments, the well structured HIT will give me an edge.

Sorry for lengthening this more than I thought I would, I just don't know which route I should go. Thanks to everyone who has commented on my recent threads and helped me, and thanks in advance for all of you who may chime in here!

### Dextery — 2013-05-25T06:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86835>

Think about marrying the Leptin RX with the epi-paleo diet. Eat lots of seafood at BAB along with lots of eggs and bacon and yams or sweet potatos and lots of coconut oil this summer. Switch to animal fats in the winter. This is enough to carry you through to dinner. Then eat a very large green salad with oilive oil and vinegar, lots of veggies slathered with butter and more seafood. Sardines work well for me. They are very cheap at Walmart in a can with tomato sauce. I rinse off the tomato sauce and heat them in a frying pan with medium chain triglyceride oil or coconut oil smothered with any kind of cheese I happen to have. I also get canned oysters at Walmart. 8 oz for $1.86. Eggs and seafood...the staples of life.

Charles Washington...google him...is a runner that is 100% no carb and does quite well in his races. He has long ago converted himself to burn fat rather than carbs. Even in the summer.

Drink only non fluoridated water. No tap water (bulk water) Many buy spring water for .89 a gallon and most drink at least a gallon a day. You will get hydrated by getting the water into your cells. If the water has fluoride in it, it does not penetrate the cells at all. You will know when you are fully hydrated properly because your skin will feel very smooth all over and you will start to not need as much food because you will be using the energy from the water to burn. And you will put on weight and strenth with less food.

Think about using Lugol's iodine in your water which will kick out of your system, any fluoride, bromine, chlorine, and aluminum out of your body so that your liver, thyroid begin to wake up and operate properly. Grizz on this forum is the Iodine expert. Look at the entire Iodine thread to see what Iodine will do for you.

BBS is great once a week as he says for 20 min or less. Plus think about Tabata type sprinting program 3x per week. run full out 20 sec, rest 10 sec...repeat 8 or 10 rounds and then stop. That is all you need.

Get your food and water correct and your dark circles will go away and you will start to feel awesome.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-25T07:21:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86836>

Thank you a lot Dextery! I apologize if my question was located somewhere in the blogs... currently engaged in a Genetics course trying to earn this certificate and it's keeping me from doing the reading on here that I want to do. So, I can do the BBS while doing the LeptinRx? I remember somewhere in the comments Dr.Kruse said that after awhile you can start to lift weights and after a few more weeks add in sprints/HIIT. If so, how should I structure this? I've been cyclical ketogenic as per the Bulletproof Diet for awhile now, and to be honest, I feel a lot better in ketosis due to some problems I caused myself about 15 months ago. I was thinking that during summer rather than eating carbs every day, I'd cycle in and out of ketosis with large refeeds, spring and fall be cyclical ketogenic but with appropriate refeeds for the season, and winter be zero-carb/under 30g carb. What I mean by structuring is, can I say, eat 50g of protein with some veggies for breakfast aside some Bulletproof coffee, eat a dinner, fall asleep, rinse and repeat.... but then on workout days include carbs in the breakfast (maybe a boiled sweet potato with mct oil) and then skip lunch, BBS workout, 45-1hr later eat a dinner with lot's of protein, carb, fat to cover the carbs? So on days I'd workout (maybe every 4-8 or 10 days) I'd kind of be going along the lines of the post-LeptinRx (high protein dinner after working out with some carbs and some fat).

After this period, should I transition back to Bulletproof Intermittent fasting, or maybe eat along the lines of the post-LeptinRx with the moderate breakfast and maybe just skip dinner occasionally or do a weekly 24hr fast or protein fast? Thanks for the tips on what you do on food options, I'm trying to get that down pat myself as I'm on an extremely tight budget, if I don't get this new job I'm going to have to cut out BPC as well and I don't want to after some new research came out indirectly showing it can alter gene expression for the better. I just need to get some good, east fish recipes. Lastly, what about the o3 content of fish, I'm afraid from a few threads on here that I will become skewed in the direction of o3 oils which can be a negative outcome, which is why it is recommended eventually to cover fish in saturated, rather than monounsaturated fat to block DHA absorption (I believe). I also found it interesting in a Matt Stone video (not too fond of him) speaking about polyunsaturated fats being stable in the bodies of fish because they are cold, and we are warm, hence why it isn't necessary to eat tons of fish/supplement.... which got me thinking, connecting the dots again as to why it is beneficial to become cold adapted.... should I just say the hell with it and only eat fish right now with some eggs and not worry about meat until I have the funds, as long as I'm taking cold showers/baths?

Thanks again, I really appreciate it!

### Dextery — 2013-05-25T07:54:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86838>

I really think you are over thinking your road to optimal health. Do the Leptin Reset with Oysters and Fish and Eggs and Bacon. Look for cuts of meat that look like roasts for under $2.99 a pound and slice them up into mini steaks and have one with the fish. BASalads with olive oil and vinegar each evening and veggies with butter. Notice almost nobody on this forum ever talks about refeed at any time. Just eat normally. Carbs are not really in the lexicon to stay in ketosis. For me it is the best for the brain and the body.

Do BBS once a week to failure for 20 minutes...then you are done. Will not interfere with the Leptin RX. Just eat real food twice a day

Omega three fatty acids are just fine. Once we eat fish with O3 we eventually get to a ratio of O6 to O3 of 4 to 1. Don't worry about it. We need Omega 6 fatty acids to coagulate blood in the event of an injury or a surgery.

Do as much cold thermogenesis as you can to become cold adapted. Living in Illinois, you have winter once a year. Make the most of it.
JERF. Just eat real food and you will be just fine. Eat your eggs, bacon, sardines or oysters and/or beef steaks or beef liver...all cooked in animal fat or butter or coconut oil and be happy. You will see changes in body comp that will surprise you.

You did not mention water in your response. You haven't gotten to the Quantum biology series to learn why drinking non fluoridated water is extremely important for you. Never drink tap water again. Your body will thank you.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-25T17:55:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86840>

Thanks for the information. I do have a tendency to over think things, especially when coming to health/performance, but when I've come as far as I have, now knowing the things I know, I want the best. I too don't really care for carbs when it comes to health/performance, but it's rather easy to get in sufficient calories to build muscle when eating them, but I don't want to sacrifice my gradual keto-adaptation, that's why I asked if it would be okay to eat carbs on days I do my HIT weightlifting, since even the LeptinRx calls for carbs if underweight/fit, also it is summer.... I just would only consume them when I lift.

I understand that Omega-3's are great and that I should eat real food, I've been "paleo" for I don't know how many months, and before that I ate mostly organic food and avoided all processed food. I just need to kick it into high gear to reverse the problems I have caused myself a little over a year ago. I can't necessarily afford all the food I should be eating (all organic, sea veggies, then the epi-paleo pyramid starting with shellfish, 3rd layer being organ meats before skeletal meat that is grass-fed/organic), so in an attempt to go "epi-paleo" for the past week or so I've basically just been eating wild caught frozen cod and collet. The thing I love about these boards/Dr.Kruse is I have learned about the nutritional powerhouse of fish and shellfish over meat, whereas the Bulletproof diet emphasizes meat slightly over fish/shellfish, but I've also learned a lot about toxins through Dave Asprey, and how canned and even "fresh" fish often contain massive amounts of histamines which hurt executive performance and long term health - that's why I've been only going for the frozen options as it is most likely more fresh and containing less histamines, plus a lack of xenoestrogens from possible BPA lined cans.

I did overlook water, but I realize the importance of drinking non-fluoridated water. Unfortunately there is not a spring any where close to where I live, and I don't have the money for a filter or online shipments of spring water in a glass bottle. I've been thinking that I should go to Whole Foods and get gallons of RO water for like 54 cents and drink a gallon a day while also refraining from using any mainstream toothpastes, just using coconut oil and or baking soda.

I started the Leptin Reset today, I finished my breakfast about 45mins after rising as it took time cook, more so than I thought. I'll be keeping a journal of how I feel subjectively and keeping pictures to track how I start looking (muscle definition, skin quality, dark circles, etc.). One final question, what do you think about the organ meats of non-grass-fed animals? Should I just refrain at all costs? I feel as though the organs of animals fed gmo soy/corn/grains are probably unhealthy to an extreme, but I have no access to grass-fed or even partially grass-fed organ meats.

Thanks for your input, I really appreciate it, I'm taking it all in. Thanks!

The only thing I've been worrying about in terms of this LeptinRx experiment is consuming carbs on work out days... carb refeeds appear to reset leptin after a period of VLC, and I'm wondering if that would create a spike in leptin I'm not looking for right now while being on the LeptinRx. I'll consume my BAB every day with no "snacking" (my "snack" would normally be BPC/tea) and no eating past 7-8pm, and when I do my BBS inspired workout (including kettlebell swings and alternative exercises) I'll have carbs with dinner and over feed on those days, but still stick to no snacking, a BAB, and no food past 7-8pm.

You should eat as much as you want at those meals. Lots of good water.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-27T02:48:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86913>

I **was** fasting for a variety of reasons - autophagy (which being in ketosis already promotes chaperone mediated autophagy, so that's +2), increased concentration while fasting with the inclusion of Bulletproof Coffee, and then lifting when fasted drastically increases protein synthesis and growth hormone/testosterone release; also I could do it.... by that I mean I could wake up and if I want just not eat, if I felt hungry have some BPC and continue up until dinner. I've been doing the LeptinRx for two days now, tomorrow I plan on working out and eating some carbs but probably still a very small amount (50g post workout from sweet potato) covered in butter and upping the protein a bit, still having my BAB and no snacks... but for instance, today I woke up late and finished my BAB probably 45mins after rising, it's now almost 7PM and I haven't eaten, I'm about to eat dinner but I don't even feel as though I have to. It's also hard for me to get in calories to gain weight/muscle spacing out my meals this far but I feel as though it might be beneficial to my liver/pancreas/galbladder/etc.

I do worry about my food and it is something I'm working on daily. Don't want that unnecessary rise in cortisol now do we ? But I mainly worry about it because if you read my paleohacks question from awhile back (which Dr. Kruse commented on), well, then, you would see. I notice INSTANTLY how food impacts my well being and if it's in a negative way, it's normally in the form of visual disturbances which further facilitate anxiety.... not really something I want to deal with especially being recently engaged, in college, and trying to get back into martial arts to compete.

Again, though, I'm not IFing right now.... Leptin Reset time; my questions were really just if I could consume carbs after I workout and if I can continue my weight lifting; I was worried about that interfering with the processes of resetting leptin and my ultradian/circadian rhythms, but I was justifying it due to (1) it being summer (2) carbs post workout (3) still following the rules of no snacking and a BAB (4) post leptin eating states that big protein meal + carbs is eaten in the afternoon after working out.

### Inger — 2013-05-27T08:25:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-quick-question-on-leptin-rx.7157/post-86930>

BPC777,
I am so glad if I could be to any help! Now.. I personally am not so sure this BP coffee fasting is a great idea.. if you have dark circles under your eyes and stuff.. I think more than one cup of coffee / day is not really good long term... at least that is what all my research and my body tells me. If I were you I would try more raw, helps for anxiety too, at least for me. Raw meat and fish and veggies (and fruits if you like) does wonder for my mind! I need it.. I really do. I no way feel well with much cooked, it makes me feel a bit numb and anxious inside.. very minor but I do not like that at all. I can go with a tiny bit lightly cooked fish or mussles though, but raw is always better, makes me feel better! You should try it BPC77. I am also quite sure raw feeds the muscles better. Bodybuilders used to eat raw meat, it is almost like a steroid! So if you want to gain muscles and fitness I would suggest do not cook your food.. you can make nice smoothies of raw eggs, and nice carpaccios or tartar or jerky or pemmican of raw meat and heart and from raw fish you can make lovely chevice, tartar, sashimi etc. I also have very little money so I often buy frozen wild caught pollock, and I thaw it and I make it into a nice raw pate', add whatever you like and in the end it tastes super good! Because I do not really like it plain and raw, like I do with salmon and mackerels etc.

Oh.. try to get raw grassfed heart.. lamb or beef or moose or deer.. Oh man that is SO good for mood, for anything! It is quite cheap too! If you have stress in your life this is the best thing I can recommend in addition to raw seafood. But you must eat it raw to have that great effect. It tastes great, like meat with a touch of liver!

# Thread: Oldest person living today is 121 years old
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oldest-person-living-today-is-121-years-old.7159/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-26T02:09:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oldest-person-living-today-is-121-years-old.7159/post-86863>

[http://www.treehugger.com/green-food/worlds-oldest-person-found-thriving-in-the-amazon.html](http://www.treehugger.com/green-food/worlds-oldest-person-found-thriving-in-the-amazon.html)

Badger on this forum asked about living on the equator. Apparently for some it works well for longevity in the Amazon jungle.

"Pereira credits her long-life to an active, healthy lifestyle, in addition to a diet rich in locally grown meats, fruits, and vegetables gathered in the forests around her home -- free of the extra salt, sugar, and preservatives so commonly found in foods around the world. Her all-natural diet, along with frequent walks around town, has allowed Pereira to thrive while others, many years her junior, do not."

What I take from this is eat your foods in season, eat real food, eat foods grown locally, Her food is probably not GMOs and she probably had a grandmother that passed along good genes from the same type of lifestyle.

And notice...No gray hair!!!

### caroline — 2013-05-26T03:28:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oldest-person-living-today-is-121-years-old.7159/post-86867>

I want to be a tree hugger at 121 too! .... to think I used to make fun of tree huggers!

### Danco3636 — 2013-05-26T03:56:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oldest-person-living-today-is-121-years-old.7159/post-86869>

Very nice find.... some dots to connect there.....

### badger — 2013-06-17T01:39:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oldest-person-living-today-is-121-years-old.7159/post-88195>

Good find Dexter. There's a place in Ecuador called Vilcabamba where the elders claim to be oftentimes well over 100 years old. The Wikipedia page for Vilcabamba tells of how researchers eventually found that the self-proclaimed centenarians were exaggerating their ages, and " the older they grew, the greater their exaggerations became". The older my granddad gets the more he exaggerates things too.... When I was in Vilcabamba I asked an older man how he felt about his home changing and becoming such a hotbed for american and european new-agers and vegans looking for the eternal spring... He said he didn't mind one bit because now he gets to tell people he's really old and takes pictures with lots of sexy chicas! Haha

Looks like this woman from the Amazon is legit though.. And besides, anyway you slice it, the people here are able to do quite a lot of work well into the autumn of life.

In the towns and cities things have rapidly changed, as they have nearly all over the world. People and young kids especially seem to subsist on nothing but baked sweets, ice cream, candy, rice, potatoes, plantains, corn, some chicken and pork, and coca-cola ! Ecuador is particularly in need of some culinary re-claiming in terms of what the land can and will sustain. To think that the climate would support the multitude of vegetables, animals, spices, and fruits people make good use of in Southeast Asia... I mean, comparing Thai food to Ecuadorian food is enough to make one wonder, what happened here? Oh yeah, SE asia managed to avoid being completely trashed and traumatized by colonization... that's right. Getting off topic... or am I? Re-claiming our ancestral biology, and cultural traditions that support it... what this site is all about.
