# Thread: For Dextery...a question about Slo-Niacin please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jude — 2013-05-29T02:09:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87042>

Dextery, you mentioned in a post about the use of Slo Niacin to raise HDL. Just wondering if it really does work and if you've had any success with it? My HDL is rising oh so slowly..... thanks Jude.

### Dextery — 2013-05-29T05:35:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87056>

Slo Niacin which is B-3 will raise HDL. I take 2000mg/day 1000 am/1000mg pm. Over two years my HDL has gone from 45 to 95ng/dl. But at the same time I started pretty strict paleo with few carbs. Result was the rise in HDL, but also I had a corresponding rise in LDL calculated and a rise in Total cholesterol...which I don't care about. I also saw my smdense LDL particles go from about 70% to 10% small dense and Triglycerides went down from over 100 to 30.

So the paleo diet and now epi-paleo had drastically changed my lipids for the better and I know the niacin has been helpful. Way back a long time ago, I read about Slo Niacin raising HDL in normal paleo people. I think I read about Niacin in the blog posting of Dr Grace Cathome at [http://www.drbganimalpharm.blogspot.com/search/label/Niacin](http://www.drbganimalpharm.blogspot.com/search/label/Niacin) This is a search for niacin on her blog. Everything you ever wanted to know about niacin and lipids.

Amazon.com has the best deal if you buy two for freight prepaid: [http://www.amazon.com/Slo-Niacin-Co...TF8&qid=1369793375&sr=8-1&keywords=slo+niacin](http://www.amazon.com/Slo-Niacin-Controlled-Release-Niacin-Tablets/dp/B000H208B2/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1369793375&sr=8-1&keywords=slo+niacin)

Dr K in Quantum Biology 4 said this about Niacin:

> Niacin: This is also why niacin works in these diseases because it is a ketone mimic drug. Why? Niacin also known as vitamin B3, nicotinic acid and vitamin PP, is an organic compound with the formula C6H5NO2 and one of the 40 to 80 essential human nutrients. In fact, when niacin deficiency is present it is one of 5 vitamins that causes a pandemic disease condition called pellagra. But it can be used to bypass a “broken” cytochrome 1 when mis-folding (due to a chronic ATP deficiency, think EMF 7). Why is understanding this hardcore biochemistry important? It underpins why understanding precision that QED requires is paramount in semiconduction. Without the proper protein folding you lose quantum tunneling of electrons and you lose the ability slowly to remain an omnivore. This is where QED takes us……….Vitamin B3 can help activate PPP when we are broken for some reason when we are ketotic from a dietary stand point using vast amounts of coherent water as I laid out in the quantum electron blog post. The PPP is responsible for making the chemical rings found in all human hormones. They become unstable as well when intracellular water is lost because cholesterol is a polar molecule as well and does well in a lipid and water environment to act. Its action at receptor sites is critically important in how the hormones work in the nucleus to turn on and off our epigenome.
>
> Yes people, niacin is a precursor to NAD+/NADH (cytochrome 1) and NADP+/NADPH (the magic of the PPP), which play essential metabolic roles in all living cells as I showed in EMF 4. Niacin cannot be directly converted to nicotinamide, but both compounds could be converted to NAD and NADP in vivo and this information is very valuable when you have liver leptin resistance, metabolic syndrome, or epilepsy. It also is important for those with liver viral diseases like hepatitis B, C, D, and E. These patients also tend to to well on niacin and a high fat diet for these reasons

### Jude — 2013-05-30T01:30:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87104>

Thanks Dextery, will research all those links. If you don't mind more questions..please...Was your B12 low to start with? With supplementation of 2000 daily, does your level reach top reference range?

### Dextery — 2013-05-30T02:13:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87107>

Jude, I am so sorry. Niacin is not B12, it is actually vitamin B3. I also take a B complex and my B12 is always above the upper limit by about 15%

I changed my posting to B-3.

### Jude — 2013-05-30T02:28:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87109>

Thanks Dextery, will start the research.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-30T02:42:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87110>

Dextery, can you list all the ingredients for me of Slo Niacin? I was looking on slo-niacin.com and that product contains hydrogenated vegetable oil. Is there a different brand you refer to?

### Dextery — 2013-05-30T03:41:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87117>

You are correct. Slo Niacin does contain Hydrogenated Veg oil, along with hypomellose & silcon dioxide, mag sterate & glyceryl behenate, and Red dye 40. The amount of each of these is so small, I will take the benefits that have been dramatic for me and not worry about the other stuff. hypomellose is some kind of sugar also and I don't eat any white sugar and only sugar in the occasional piece of fruit.

I have no idea if you can buy any B3 without additives to bind the tabs.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-30T04:39:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87121>

Dextery, since B3 is a ketone mimetic compound, can it actually spark the production of ketones? I know there are ketogenic amino acids (leucine/lysine I believe), I was wondering if there were any other products/compounds that enhance ketogenesis and can create a deeper state of ketosis/increase carb allowance.

### Dextery — 2013-05-30T06:08:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87123>

I do not know of any other compound that brings about ketosis...... other than coconut oil, butter, MCT oil, and olive oil eaten in the absence of carbs. I am not sure there is any less deep or more deep ketosis. You are either in or out....meaning you are burning either ketone bodies or glucose to fuel the brain and body.

I don't really get the need for carbs in working out. Seafood, meat and fat will fuel the body and brain quite well. I know the gurus stress carb loading, carb matching to activity for quick fuel but it spikes blood sugar and thus insulin. YoYoing BS and insulin can lead to Cardiac Arrest and/or Cancer. That is the last thing I want to do. The pancreas is overworked by SAD and I want to have as many Beta cells available right up to my last days. And that to me means, maintain a higher than mainstream thought on total cholesterol, smD LDL at about 10% of total LDL particles, low triglycerides and very high HDL with a hsCRP under .5 and good sleep, and a pretty strict ketogenic diet. If I can keep these numbers, I figure I might be around for a few more years.

People who think they need carbs look really buff on the outside, but look inside at the scarring on and in the heart. No thanks.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-30T07:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87125>

Thanks for the input! I didn't realize olive oil would have an impact as well, I had a hunch butter did, though, since it contains butyric acid. What I mean by deeper ketosis is I believe you technically need a reading of 0.5millimols of ketones to be "in ketosis", but you can get much higher as many people get up to 4.0 from fasting. Ben Greenfield in his current keto-experiment is aiming for 1.0-1.5. I don't believe carbs are necessary whatsoever, but I don't know how to implement a fully strict keto diet right now and build muscle - I need to gain weight and I'm shooting for like 15-20lbs. So far I've been cycling in and out of ketosis, refraining from carb loads for a minimum of 5ish days and only after I workout. The reason I stated that I was wondering about a higher carb allowance was because I was thinking of doing "mini carb loads" after lifting weights, but not enough to keep me out of ketosis, especially if I take other measures (mct's/sct's/keto amino's/fasting/etc.). I was also thinking how that could play with excess protein. For instance, I consume an **Ultra-High Fat**, VLC, moderate protein diet and on days I lift/martial arts intensely, I "refeed" on extra protein (about 1.3g/lb, from what I've seen that's the max you can utilize), and although there is a possibility of gluconeogenesis, the mct's/sct's/amino's keep me fueled with ketones.

I too am opting for recovery, longevity, and well-being. I would rather have a decent but strong looking body that is functionally strong and efficient (movement patterns/breathing), be able to concentrate like no other, have stable energy, etc.etc.etc. than be 6% body fat and..... nothing else, lol.

In evaluating your goals, just know that people like Ben who train and do marathons and triathalons are injuring themselves big time...on the inside of their bodies. They look fit as ever but are dieing on the inside.

Dr Kruse, somewhere over the last two years talked about a type of heart scan that can see inside the heart. And when the medical profession does the scans on these athletes they are shocked at the amount of scar tissue that is observed inside the heart...making the heart less pliable and very rigid and very prone to tearing leading to cardiac events.

I believe the scans were done in conjunction with the Tour de France competitors organizing committee. I just don't remember the details. Perhaps Dr. K can provide some insight.

And it is well known that these endurance athletes have all cause mortality much much higher than the standard population...with higher death rates coming from cancer. Epigenetic signals to the genes by endurance athletes are a real killer.

Art DeVany has been beating this drum for years.

Just received labs for SO. 50 yr old male. 5'2". 155lbs. Used to do alot of weightlifting.
Very nervous because of the Lipoprotein Profile, NMR results. Doc wants to start meds.

LDL Particle Number by NMR 2397 range <1000 nmol/L

HDL Particle Number 28.7 >=30.5 umol/L

Small LDL Particle Number 1285 <=527 nmol/L

LDL Particle Size 20.8 >20.5 nm

Large VLDL Particle Number 3.7 <=2.7 nmol/L

Large HDL Particle Number <0.7 >=4.8 umol/L

VLDL Size 48.2 <=46.6 nm

HDL Size <8.3 >=9.2 nm

LP Insulin Resistance Score 71 <=45

Glucose 99 70-90 mg/dL

Testosterone 358 300-890 ng/dL

SHBG 19 11-80 nmol/L

Testosterone, Bioavailable 236 131-682 ng/dL

Testosterone, Free Calculation 83 47-244 pg/mL

Testosterone, Percentage Free 2.3 1.6-2.9 %

HS CRP 0.8 0.0-3.0 mg/L

Vit D3, 25-Hydroxy 46 ng/mL
Vit D2, 25-Hydroxy <5 ng/mL
Vit D, 25-Hydroxy 46 ng/mL

Cholesterol 222 0-199 mg/dL
Triglyceride 141 0-149 mg/dL
HDL Cholesterol 35 40-100 mg/dL
Non-HDL Cholesterol 187 0-130 mg/dL
LDL, Calculated 159 0-129 mg/dL
Cholesterol/HDL Ratio 6.3 1.0-4.5

Would like to know about options for raising Testosterone/how to lower LDL small particle/
raise HDL.
Thanks for all of your expertise.

### kleinb87 — 2013-06-01T17:47:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-breast-cancer.7185/post-87255>

That is crazy that she still thinks that using the phone 24/7 is okay. Speaking of cancer, I have been in Chicago for a few days for business. I met an oncologist at dinner last night who is in town for ASCO conference. We began to talk about health. She was amazed with my progress but still did not get the connection. I think I had given her a heart attack when I told her how much butter and coconut oil I eat. She couldn't question the results, but immediately tried to get me to market big pharma drugs as I do marketing. However, she still did not understand my whole dilemma of how the drug only masks the problem and how I could not do this based on a conflict of opinion. Her only response was "if the money's right, you would do it, right?" She thought I was a unique case and had no idea about the issues that were all caused via leptin and mitochondria problems. She couldn't deny my results as I have all of the data to prove everything (I'm that patient who has learned to memorize their labs from hs-crp, vit d, dhea, on down). Had I not found Jack, I could have been like any of her other patients that she sees that just go with the doctor's orders without questioning. As that is their only option to live or so they believe. She even tried to get me to go onto a cancer drug for methylation. It flew over her head that I have already bypassed the methylation pathways.

I didn't think I would end up speaking about biochemistry and health during dinner, but it was eye opening at how close minded some people really are when it comes to advances in medicine. They only know what they believe and don't want to study more than is necessary.

This is what we are up against. People are no longer standing up for what they believe in and are being bought by the power of money. Once we get away from that, there is a world of possibilities.

# Thread: Dr John Cannell, Vitamin D Council, speaks on Vit D3 and Autism
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-john-cannell-vitamin-d-council-speaks-on-vit-d3-and-autism.7187/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-29T18:56:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-john-cannell-vitamin-d-council-speaks-on-vit-d3-and-autism.7187/post-87085>

[http://www.vitamindcouncil.org/blog/grassrootshealth-webinar-series-vitamin-d-and-autism/](http://www.vitamindcouncil.org/blog/grassrootshealth-webinar-series-vitamin-d-and-autism/)

This is a lecture done in conjunction with [www.grassrootshealth.net](http://www.grassrootshealth.net) ......a place you can get your Vitamin D3 level checked without a doctor's order.

Autism: Very interesting that Dr Cannell points out that there is a correlation between date of birth, urban or rural living. Children born in March seem to have higher incidence of autism. Urban children have more incidence as do children of dark skin people who have migrated to more northern climates...less sun.

If the environment was the cause of autism, we would have seen the outbreak would have occurred in the 1950s when many of us could see the air we were breathing...especially smog in the Los Angeles basin and many of the lakes and streams were dieing and the ocean was very suspect due to effluent run off.

He talks about prenatal supplementation and post natal breast feeding. Breast milk does not contain much D3 unless the mother was supplementing. He does talk about specific doses.

There is much, much more.

# Thread: Prana: Emerging
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prana-emerging.7188/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-29T19:18:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prana-emerging.7188/post-87086>

I just wanted to start a journal to share my experience and gather the insights of all the brilliantly helpful minds here on these forums. I want to update and quantify (subjectively, without labs/markers/etc.) my experience with both the leptin reset and my adventures with martial arts and living optimal. I also wanted to create this as an official greetings to everyone here, since my first post was just a question to everyone. I'm not sure on the frequency of the updates, and I'm not necessarily doing this for views/comments, I just think it'd be a nice way to track how I feel and the journeys I'm on with the option of like minded individuals commenting along the way. Let me begin.

I was born November 25, 1994. I was born anemic and was rushed off from my mother by the doctors. If I remember correctly I had to be put under I heat lamp as well because I was too cold - I know I had to get Iron shots for some amount of time as well. I was told that for the first few seconds of my life my eyes appeared to be black. No one believes my father but he swears that my eyes were almost completely black for the first few seconds. I remember bits and pieces of my very early childhood - I lived in a nice home with my mother, father and my grandmother, or Bubby as she enjoys us calling her even though we are not Judaic/Israeli. Tensions quickly escalated and we had to split ways because my father and my mothers mother (Bubby) did not get along, we eventually moved into an apartment complex nearby my elementary school, and my younger brother Max was born on October 21, 1998. Things were okay in my childhood. I was enthusiastic and enjoyed life, I can remember that. I enjoyed school and I loved my brother although I kind of tormented him at times and really regret it. I didn't have many friends until later in life, mainly because I was extremely shy. There was also always a lot of fighting between my mom and my father, as there is to this day. They don't see eye to eye on life, and my father tends to create excuses for himself while being very negative, and then he wonders why things are always negative. My mom even worked pregnant with me, she worked two jobs for some time (as a waitress for two large restaurants walking miles a day while on shift), and often worked 12 or more hours a day. We often had money problems. I remember times where the water/heat/cable/electricity would be out for days at a time.

Skipping ahead to middle school, this is when I really started to make friends. I actually had quite a lot of friends at this time, and I was on both the football team and wrestling team (I had also played football previously for some odd years as a child). I got sick quite a lot and remember missing something like over twenty days a year of school. I always had upset stomachs, nausea, dizziness, headaches, sore throats, and more. My diet was terrible, too. I remember drinking lots of coffee with my mom, as she really ONLY drinks coffee unfortunately. I would pour about half a cup of coffee (which was already weak as my mom enjoys it) only, and the other half I would put milk in. I would also dip many cookies into the coffee (chocolate chip warmed in the microwave). I remember I had to lose weight if i wanted to be on the "middleweight" football team in 8th grade so I can continue to play for my favorite coaches/teachers - and I did just that. I ate unhealthily that summer, although I thought it was healthier due to more salads and what not, and ran many times a week and lifted weights. I maintained my weight of about 120 some odd pounds and became the running back AND outside linebacker for the team - I played both ways as did two other players on the team - no breaks during the games whatsoever. This continued into high school. I played outside linebacker for my freshman team, and then ironically enough I played noseguard sophomore year at only about 140 pounds and at this time 5'5-5'6. Wrestling was brutal as well. One year I wrestled for two teams at the same time and often had eight practices a week, a "duel meet", and sometimes a tournament. For those who don't know, wrestlers are by far the masters of cutting and maintaining weight. I mean that in a bad way though, it's entirely unhealthy. Joe Rogan speaks about it a lot on his podcast. You would be dehydrated by the time of the weigh in approximately an hour before you were scheduled to wrestle, only to dehydrate and wrestle all day. You would have to lose 2, 3, 4, sometimes 5 or more pounds in a couple days. You would be starved, dehydrated, and would run with five or six layers of clothing on in a hot room. Coaches would tell you not to shower the morning of because the water could weigh you down from being absorbed into your skin.

High school sports, although I really miss greatly, was definitely the start of bad habits for me. I would get home late and have to rush to do homework, at this point becoming mentally and physically exhausted but realizing education was important I would get the homework done. I would fall asleep at 1 or 2 am and have to wake up my 6:30am to go to school all day and then wrestle or play football for two or three hours after. Sometimes wrestling meets would get done at 10 at night, only for us to be required to get back to school the next day (Saturday's) at 4am to prepare for a tournament that would take 12 or more hours to complete. Man, just thinking about it makes me want to go back - as crazy as that sounds. I have the feeling of love and excitement in me when I think about it. Similar to how I feel when I think about my fiance, obviously not NEARLY as intense, but you get the idea. During this time we had continuing financial difficulties that were stressed by my younger brother becoming increasingly ill. He always had problems as a child - born with asthma and eczema, almost died in the womb, DEADLY allergic to many foods (fish but not shellfish, tree nuts but not peanuts, many medicines such as penicillin, and much more). He had fistula's that were unknown at the time - one of them got injured one night and started bleeding profusely. This is what lead us to discover he had diseases (although they couldn't figure that out at the time). He had many surgeries to remove fistulas and close up holes that were inside of his anus. He had tubes put in there to drain pus. He got worse and worse. We found out he had Crohn's a few years later, and soon after we found out he also had Peri-anal disease. \*GRAPHIC IMAGE WARNING\* right now he literally has a large opening right between his anus and genitals that needs to get packed with gauges/bandages with highly potent chemicals every day. My mom is his stay at home nurse and no longer can work because she needs to pack him and help him in the shower and getting dressed. He is on many medications - 6MP, which to my knowledge is used for cancers, Oxycodone for pain and up to four or six pills a day, and a host of other medicines to "control his disease" and some to loosen his stools (even though he can't sit on his rear to go to the bathroom - he lays down all day and only walks to get food or a drink quickly before becoming too painful).

Around this time (I was then 16), things quickly escalated for me. Although I was in AP courses and doing well in school, I started to abuse substances. Ironically enough I started hookahing at this time and using alcohol BEFORE marijuana. To this day I believe marijuana is a medicine and a tool for spiritual development, but I unfortunately can no longer use it. I hookah'd, used alcohol, then progressed to marijuana and stayed a "pot head" for about a year before experimenting with other things. I first tried adderall and other ADHD meds to study and to combine with marijuana for synergistic effects. I then tripped a few times on psychedelics, and to be honest they were catalysts to me becoming increasingly philosophical and spiritual. I regret doing some of the things I did, though. I took psychedelics out of their nature, and rather than using them sparingly as tools for spiritual development and introspection, I used them at concerts mixed with substances such as MDMA and ketamine. I started going to lots of concerts using many drugs every time and in large quantities all combined. I even dabbled into xanax and other drugs such as DXM. The only thing I'm grateful for, is the fact that the amount of research and reading I do now on nutrition/religion/physiology/etc. I did on drugs through very factual sites and sources. I understood how drugs interacted and the half-lifes of them. Unfortunately I did this so I could enhance the effects of all the drugs and make them last longer. Many of my friends always went to me to ask me questions on these things because I knew so much random info such as avoiding MAOI's on certain drugs and combining MAOI's on others (some psychedelics) to enhance the effects. I also did this, though, so I would never grow addicted. I also avoided many other drugs such as cocaine and heroin because those are down right disgusting, and even my former self using and abusing many substances would NEVER touch anything as addictive and destructive as those chemicals.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-29T19:18:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prana-emerging.7188/post-87087>

My life all changed one day when I accidentally ingested PCP (or embalming fluid, I'm not sure). It used to bring me horrific anxiety to even think about the event or speak about it. For months when me and my fiance would take walks and she would smoke a cigarette, if I even looked at the cigarette I would get increasingly anxious. She would ask me to explain but I couldn't as I would feel I was about to freak out. I ingested this substance through a cigarette which was laced at a concert. I just wanted one puff for a nicotine buzz as I never smoked cigarettes in my life, but unfortunately it came with much more than that. I was already on other substances as well. After this day I grew increasingly worse. I had anxiety which I never had before. I came to the verge of panic attacks many times. My mind would wander and I would lose control of my thought processes. I become sensitized to bad thoughts - if something came up on the television about murder, rape, or suicide... my mind would not be able to get off of it. I continued to occasionally use at this point, but a lot less because it would just mess with my psyche. If I smoked marijuana, I would get this massive surge of pressure in my head. I mean it literally felt like my head was going to implode or just collapse under the pressure of itself. Then massive aura's would come - migraine like aura's - I would get photosensitive and in my peripheral vision I would see blobs of pink/blue/green/etc. It wasn't necessarily hallucinations - if you read about migraines (which I thought I developed), if you have occipital migraines they can affect vision - people get wavy vision, tunnel vision, see aura's as I described, some even hallucinate as described by Oliver Sacks books on migraines and hallucinations. Some people even developed 2d vision (from other similar disorders). Marijuana became worthless to me as I couldn't even enjoy it because of how anxious I would get and the head pressure and aura's/visual illusions. This carried over to my day to day life while sober - things would have a shimmer affect to them, almost as if they were moving slightly or vibrating. I was extremely photosensitive and had daily headaches (very terrible ones - even an athlete who wrestled with a broken elbow felt helpless to these). I would have after-images of not only lights, but anything I look at. If I look at a color on a digital clock and look away, the digital clock number/color is imprinted in my vision for a second and I still temporarily see it.
This is what lead me to health and fitness. I quickly got into organic foods and avoiding all processed foods, and everything artificial. I drank TONS of green tea every day. These changes seemed to help slightly, but nothing profound. I occasionally would drink alcohol or use xanax, though, which was just a bandaid and to not "feel left out" from partying and what not. Those were the only two things that wouldn't make me anxious. Flash forward some odd months to when I started dating my best friend and presently my fiance, I started making more changes. I moved in with her for a month because my air went out at home and it was too hot in summer for my head to handle. Ohhh how I loved those days waking up to her gorgeous face with no make up and her hair down.... just like I like her - the way she roles out of bed is when she is most beautiful. I ended up becoming vegetarian at this time - it actually temporarily made me feel well for a month or so. My migraines became less and so did my anxiety. But I suspect this was just avoidance of sugar and an increase in organic fruits and veggies with lots of green tea. I also took cold showers quite a bit as I read it would increase fat loss via BAT activation. I did this for the wrong reasons though. I needed to (and need to) gain weight/muscle, not lose weight. I was only around 145 at the time and would end up waking up in the morning and jogging for 30 or so minutes, then come back and lift weights for over an hour. Knowing what I know now.... damnnnn was I stupidddd. Over exercising every day almost of every week, eating a vegetarian diet skewing to the point of veganism (I cut out all dairy and replaced it with fake shitty vegan dairy) and ate lots of non-organic, unshelled, NOT raw nuts that probably contained molds and other toxins. I eventually made the switch to a better but not optimal "Slow Carb Diet" and included more beans and eggs - fortunately all organic. Unfortunately I used things such as grapeseed oil for all my cooking. One step forward, two steps back. The slow carb diet would leave me bloated, gassy, feeling lethargic, and smelling like garlic from all the damn garlic supplements I was taking as per Tim Ferriss' recommendations.
To speed things up, I accidentally ate meat at a restaurant and just went ahead and enjoyed it. I told myself I would only eat meat then if it was grass-fed. I quickly found the paleo diet through Marks Daily Apple and PaleoHacks. I asked many questions on there. I made a question about curing my brother of his ills, and another about my own problems. Dr. Kruse commented and was ridiculed but he was the only one who made any sense. I often thought I needed neurofeedback/brain training and a host of other interventions, and Jack basically said I needed to read his EMF series and follow everything in there. I didn't, I just listened to ALL his podcasts on CT and started ramping up the cold showers. I eventually heard Dave Asprey on Joe Rogan and was in awe. I searched up his blog and started listening and annotating all his podcasts and presentations. Long story short, I purchased a lot of books that I still need to finish (I always read half, annotate everything, summarize info, look at cites, then find a new book to read, lol) - Good Cals, Bad Cals, Meditation by Marcus Aurelius, Body By Science, Perfect Health Diet, Better Baby Book, and so many more. My life has now been changed. I'm going to my local college a year earlier than all my old friends since I earned my GED, and have been taking additional courses and certifications online in genetics and philosophy. I take everything as FAR as it can go in terms of research and utilization. For instance, I'm reading through each Leptin Prescription blog, all the comments as well, and annotating it, forming it into a word document, and summarizing my notes. Then I'm looking through and annotating all the citations given. I'm going to order Master Leptin (3rd Edition) when I get the money and read that as well as other books Dr. Kruse recommended. I've been intermittently doing Jiu-Jitsu but haven't had the funds the past two months, but I plan on starting again. My dream is to compete in an internationl/continental tournament. I want to become one of the best. I would love to do MMA, but considering my concussion history, and my substance use, I'm not risking my well-being and contentedness with life, as well as my longevity, just so I can experience the thrill of fighting. Jiu-Jitsu is close enough and almost like a form of meditation. In fact, just as Zen is applied to archery and tea drinking, I'd like to apply zen to Jiu-Jitsu. Now, I'm doing the Leptin Reset (although I still workout), and I'm taking ice cold showers and wearing minimal clothing even when it is freezing out. I've been trying to ground myself as well. I did the LeptinRx once before for only about a week and noticed I started waking up earlier and earlier and falling asleep around midnight rather than 2-3am... well, 3 days in and the same results are happening... I woke up at 7:30 today after falling asleep around 12:30am. I feel as though the combo of blue light blocking glasses and a big breakfast really just stimulate my circadian rhythm. I will continue this for the next 5 or 6 weeks and see how I feel... maybe continue it and if not I will go on the Post Leptin Reset eating plan for 4-8 weeks, then dabble in with weekly fasts. I want to keep this to update how I feel, maybe include some photos of my improvements (muscle gain/dark circles going away/etc.), as well as how I perform in Jiu-Jitsu on a cyclical keto and increasingly strict keto diet.

Thank you all, you are all so helpful and inspiring. I wish you all the best and hope everyone on the road to recovery the easiest journey possible, and those more performance minded - I hope you succeed in every way your heart desires (Barry, lol). I'm extremely gracious I've found this and the bulletproof exec blog/forums, my life has been changed.

### caroline — 2013-05-30T05:49:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prana-emerging.7188/post-87122>

Welcome to journal land BigPapa!

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-01T02:03:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prana-emerging.7188/post-87231>

So, the past couple days I have been rather content, though I've noticed my tendencies arising to worry about my food and physique. I've been wondering if all this time I've had a hidden eating disorder. I remember days of freshman year high school (before I leaned out from year round football/wrestling) I would always look in the mirrors to see if I appeared fat. Even in my football pads I would do the same as I was issued lineman shoulder pads although I was a linebacker (well, they aren't different but I had larger/broader pads for some reason, they didn't quite fit). I wouldn't enjoy eating at lunch sometimes because I would get bloated and just feel fat. I would always try to stand up straight and do odd things with my body language to appear thinner/more muscular. Even after I got into health/fitness, after I turned a temporary vegetarian, I was trying to lose weight although I should have been maintaining or gaining. And even now, I find myself to worry about my physique. My muscles looked rather flat in the mirror, and it was discouraging. Although I think it is nothing more than the simple fact that when I workout (1-2x a week BBS style) I can't target my chest well because I don't have a bench and pushups are my only resort, but I have equipment to target other areas (67.5lb dumbbell rows, dumbbell swings, dumbbell squats, etc.). I want to gain muscle so badly but I NEED to stop worrying and allow it to come to me.

In terms of the Leptin Reset style of eating, I'm undecided at this point, but it's only been roughly a week (maybe 6 days I believe). It definitely helps my circadian rhythm.... I go to sleep earlier and wake up earlier. I feel _slightly_ more refreshed in the morning (but nothing extraordinary), and I get hungry at around the same times (although it isn't really true hunger, if I want I can just tune it out and not eat). I haven't been doing so well with eating within 30 minutes, more in the time frame of 30-60 minutes, I hope that is still okay. I also skip lunch, normally I'm going 7-10hrs between eating breakfast and dinner, then 10-12hrs from dinner to breakfast. I have been continuing my cold showers - they are getting very easy, I can jump right into the coldest temperature and only just start getting goosebumps/hairs beginning to raise after 8-10minutes.

Interestingly enough, I took a 12 minute cold shower right before working out today, and I noticed a slight increase in all my lifts. I don't know whether I should attribute this to CT or not yet, though, as I have some other theories I'd like input on. 3 and a half days a go I worked out and ate carbs after, about 175 of carbs when including what I ate from mussels. Ughhh did I feel bloated after, though, I doubt it was the carbs themselves and more because of fructose as I incorporated a Banana and fruit ALWAYS makes me bloated (I'm DONE with fruit, even in summer). I'm wondering if I just wasn't in ketosis yet, before my workout today. I've been eating a cyclical keto diet and noticing my increasing rate of fat adaptation, I want to keep it, and I'm worried I may have stalled/knocked it back a notch by eating so many carbs the other day, I normally refeed with 100-150 MAX. The thing is I had over 72hrs of VLC eating with "mini fasts" (dinner-to-breakfast, breakfast-to-dinner), as well as multiple tbsp of MCT Oil a day. I definitely feel I had to have been dipping back into a ketotic state. Regardless, I'm happy that my lifts continue to go up, I just need to refine my refeeds and eventually (slowly) drop them.

If anyone does happen to read this, I was wondering what someones take was on a few things. (1) should I even stress not eating EXACTLY within 30 minutes, if I'm off by say 15-20mins? (2) Am I not creating a biological mismatch by doing increasing amounts of CT in summer with cyclical carb eating? Should I just do CT in summer when exposed (a cold night with minimal clothing), should I keep it, should I drop all carbs?

On a side note, It's been raining often here, and I love the rain. I walk barefoot basically everywhere now - whether I'm taking out the trash, walking to a field to do foundation training, walking to the car, etc. And I would imagine the wet soil/grass is more conductive for grounding. I'm wondering if there are other effects that may enhance my well being since there is all the rain and lightening. I remember Dr. Kruse saying something about that in a comment on a blog post in the Leptin Prescription, I'll have to look over my notes. Damn, that reminds me, I need to get back going on my goal - Read + annotate all Leptin Blogs + associated comments + citations + videos, THEN purchase, read and annotate Mastering Leptin (Third Edition), by the end of summer.... Gotta get moving on that!

Well, time to go make some lean ground beef with a sweet potato for my post workout with a side of Bulletproof coffee!

### cantweight — 2013-06-01T14:05:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prana-emerging.7188/post-87245>

Hey BigPapa.....you mentioned in my journal eating raw store bought eggs....I won't eat a cooked factory egg...lol! Nasty stuff man!

If you have access to any fresh eggs, get them. Even if you have to take a drive to a farmers market, the eggs keep and you can buy a few dozen at a time. Even the "organic" or "cage free" at the grocery are not the best quality.

You asked if I would consider eating raw eggs....absolutely if they are pastured. When I was really sick a few years back I showed 3 kinds of salmonella in my blood work. I dont screw around with bullshit food anymore...its just not worth it to me.

Here is an article on eggs.. [http://www.healthyrootshappysoul.com/2013/05/do-pastured-eggs-need-to-be-refrigerated.html](http://www.healthyrootshappysoul.com/2013/05/do-pastured-eggs-need-to-be-refrigerated.html)

CT is definitely why your lifts increased. Google Daniel Craig...this is his practice..CT then workout for better performance.

Good luck to you, you are a very bright young man to be figuring all this stuff out at your age. Chill on the body image. Trust in your knowledge that you are fueling properly. I am concerned about the amount of exercise if you are trying to become leptin sensitive and yeah watch the carbs. I never feel good when i yo yo in and out of ketosis.

# Thread: 9 Food Practices banned in Europe that are not banned in the US

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-29T19:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/9-food-practices-banned-in-europe-that-are-not-banned-in-the-us.7189/post-87088>

[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/05/29/dangerous-food-practices.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/05/29/dangerous-food-practices.aspx)

The one practice that really jumped out at me is the following #8

8\. Water Fluoridation

"Many do not realize that fluoride is a drug that is available only with a prescription. Yet it's added to municipal water supplies used by more than 180 million Americans, including infants and the elderly without any attention to personalized dosing or potential interactions.

Swallowing fluoride has been shown to cause weakened bones, bone cancer, hyperactivity and/or lethargy, lowered thyroid function, lowered IQ, dementia, kidney issue, arthritis and more, while studies have failed to show benefits for preventing cavities when taken internally. Cities around the US spend millions adding fluoride to communal water supplies each year, yet most European countries do not fluoridate their water."

If you have not switched to non fluoridated spring water or distilled water...what are you waiting for?

# Thread: Direct contamination of foods with fluoride based pesticides and fumigants
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-29T23:24:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/post-87094>

[http://www.antigmofoods.com/2013/05/common-food-items-could-contain-180.html?m=1](http://www.antigmofoods.com/2013/05/common-food-items-could-contain-180.html?m=1)

Just when I thought I had eliminated fluoride from my life by drinking non fluoridated water, I now find out fumigants and pesticides contain compounds of fluoride. There was a local story that a producer of vegetables was marketing it as "organic" and the produce contained all sorts of residues of pesticides.

When something is labeled "organic" how do we the consumers know? I suppose the reputation of Publix, Safeway, Albertson's, Piggly Wiggly, Vons, Sprouts, WinCo, etc comes into play. Do you trust them? Do you trust the agri-business produce farmers to be ethical? All the more reason to know your farmer....if you can.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-29T23:52:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/post-87099>

Also, these veggies get sprayed with tap water regularly as they sit in the store display cases. More fluoride & chloride.

### nunki — 2013-05-30T14:10:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/post-87128>

Non chalant- not true for WF's. All water used in my store is processed through (I believe) a reverse osmosis system. It is definitely not straight tap water. Make sure what you are buying is certified organic- not just saying they are organic. It is a huge process for a farm to become certified organic. Not saying that dishonest ones slip by, but it is your responsibility as a consumer to do your research. i would never buy from a farm that doesnt welcome tours.

of course, I am a huge proponent of growing as much of your own food as possible.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-30T15:58:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/post-87129>

Thank, nunki for the clarification. I should have known that Whole Foods would treat their veggies special.
My rabbits like kale and other greens and carrots from the grocery store, but they go nuts when I give them something straight from my garden, like worm-eaten cabbage leaves or beet greens. Or weeds/branches from the yard. I think it might be that the extra electrons haven't dissipated yet.

### judy32 — 2013-07-03T08:10:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/post-89429>

# Thread: Melanin and Photoelectric effect?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-and-photoelectric-effect.7193/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-29T23:49:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-and-photoelectric-effect.7193/post-87098>

Any ideas how melanin figures into the photoelectric effect? I know people with more melanin make less vitamin D, but is there some beneficial interaction with melanin, sunlight, and electrons? I found out that phenylalanine increases melanin and some wheels started turning. Does melanin make any uncoupling proteins?

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-30T00:04:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-and-photoelectric-effect.7193/post-87100>

After some more searching I found info that once pheomelanin in women and eumelanin in men are raised to a sufficiently excited level, the excess energy is assumed to be used by the cell for chemical processes and to regulate cell conditions.

One could say people with more melanin might produce less vitamin D, but the tradeoff is they get more energy for the cell to use.

I wonder if melanin can dampen EMF..

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-30T03:28:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-and-photoelectric-effect.7193/post-87113>

Melanin does dampen EMF........The more photons and electrons you get the tanner you get. Why? Your body is trying to tell you we got enough on some of the semiconductors.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-30T03:31:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-and-photoelectric-effect.7193/post-87114>

Every cell in the living matrix needs photons when the system is somehow traumatized.......the skin/eye is one way we deliver it. We also sense Electromagnetism and the resonance and vibrations via hydrogen bonding networks and the amide bonds in collagen.......there is a lot more to this. Melanin creates a cycle for water disassociation and reassociation in mitochondria.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-30T03:35:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-and-photoelectric-effect.7193/post-87116>

Thanks for the reply, Jack. It makes me happy to be able to connect some dots. I'm wondering if the additional energy generated by melanin for the cells might be one of the ways darker skinned tropicals' metabolisms cope with additional heat and starches.

I recall there is a black fungus that grows on the walls of nuclear reactors. The black melanin it produces protects it from the radiation.

# Thread: Greetings....help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-help.7201/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LRichard12 — 2013-05-30T18:44:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-help.7201/post-87144>

Hi my name is Lucas. I am 22 years old, 6foot tall, 150 lbs, around 12-14%
body fat. I have recently (over the past year) lost 100 lbs (from 250 to
150). I was obese my entire life, suffered from high BP, chronic fatigue,
laziness, low motivation, brain fog, poor memory, depression, etc. I then
developed heart palpitations and the chronic fatigue and neuro problems
worsened. I tried everything in the book, had every blood test, have taken
every anti depressant and stimulant the neurologist and GP could throw at
me. I've taken every supplement under the sun (and spent a small fortune
doing so), and had even turned to drugs to help (found hydrocodone and
tramadol worked the best). I've learned to manage and press on, by the
grace of GOD, day by day drinking coffee, yerba mate, green tea, taking
endless combinations of supplements, and eating what I thought was a
balanced diet. Then after coming back from Africa (I am a missionary from
the USA, based in Dublin, serving in West Africa) last year weighing in
over 250lbs (probably closer to 300) and decided that it was time to lose
weight. So I switched to a pure veggie, fruit, and low fat cottage cheese
diet, cut out all added sugars and deserts, ate mueslie cereal, skim mile,
flax seed, goji berries, bannanas, and apple juice for breakfast, etc. I
started jogging and hiking at least 3 mornings a week and eventually joined
a gym. I lost a few lbs and felt a bit better though still fighting with
chronic fatigue, depression, low motivation, waking up tired, and heavily
relient on coffee. Also was still experimenting with antidepressants.

Then I decided to go super low calorie, eating mostly protein shakes, low
fat cottage cheese, and fat free unsweetened organic yogurt (used
maltodextrin laced stevia to sweeten). I dropped a few more lbs, but was
hungry all of the time! Then I was introduced to Atkins (thank GOD). I
switched to Atkins and lost the rest of my wieght fairly quickly, going
down below 150, while building muscle. I still stayed very low calorie
(1200/day) for a while and ate only 3 meals a day, no snacks. However,
every one kept telling me that I needed to up my calorie intake. So I uped
them by adding in 2 snacks of nuts (either almonds or walnuts) and more
olive, walnut, or coconut oil. Up until that point I had full appetite
control, and had no trouble eating less than 1000 calories per day if I
wanted to. Then once I added the snacks and uped my calories I began to get
hungry. However I maintained control.

Then I went to Africa in February. While in Africa my appetite continued to
increase, eventually becoming ravenous. I lost control for a couple of
weeks and gorged on peanuts, gaining a couple of lbs. Then regained control
forcing myself to stay around 1800 calories and <25 net carbs perday. Now I
am back in Dublin and I'm over come by my hunger, my obsession with food,
it is all I can think about, and I cant seem to stay full. I am eating 3
solid meals a day, under 20 net carbs, 100 grams protein, 150 grams of fat
(mostly from olive oil and avacoados), and all of the low carb veggies that
I can ingest. I still seem to be hungry.

Recently I discovered Mark Sission's Primal diet and the paleo diet, read
Mastering Leptin, and the perfect 10 diet. I then came across Jack Kruses
Leptin RX. After reading into it I think that my problem is low leptin. So
I tried a primal refeed (sweet potatoes, berries, low fat organic
unsweetened yogurt, honey, etc) last week, with around 500 grams carb, 50g
fat, 130 or so protein, and nearly 3000 cals, which kind of helped. Then I
started the Leptin RX , eating 50 grams of protein at breakfast (eggs, hard
cheese, almonds, and coconut oil) after which I am still hungry. So I
recently tried another refeed (roast beef (for morning protein) sweet
potatoes, white potatoes, parsnips, low fat organic unsweetened yogurt
(with stevia and FOS to sweeten)). I was really hoping that this would
work! Now I am back to low carb/Leptin Rx and still hungry. I am trying to
maintain nutritional ketosis (for the thereputic and weight loss benifits),
however I find that first thing in the morning my fasting blood ketones are
consistently .3 mmol (they where around 2.0 to 3.0 fasting before leaving
for Africa in Feb) even if I keep protein down to 100grams perday, then creep up to nearly 2.0 by the afternoon. So I'm
trying not to over do the protein (I keep to low 100's; 120 or so from
eggs, hard natural cheese, nuts, and 2 meals of wild salmon a day), eat
high fat (150 or so), and 20 net carbs (from veggies, organic unsweetened
greek yogurt, and nuts). I have been avoiding snacks and processed foods
for over 1 month now, I drink only filtered water, go for everything
organic, and take evey supplement recommended by the lowcarb/paleo books
I've read (I.E. GPC, PS, NAC, Acyl-L-Carnitine, L-Carnosine, vit d3, vit E,
Magnesium, Potasium, Chromium, R-ALA, Calcium, Zinc, Omega 3, 6, 7, 9,
extra DHA and EPA, vit C, green tea w/hoodia, cinnamon, kelp (also eat
Kombu), Licorice root, 5 grams+ natural sea salt, Melatonin (2mg 1 hour
before bed w/ magnesium to sleep), broad spectrum probiotics, and 60 grams
of F.O.S and 30grams of Inulin per day, and an iron free multi vit). Still
have little to no energy, irritable some times, weak, hungry!, obsessed
with food, low motivation, poor work ethic, trouble with memory and
concentration, poor social ability, and craving sweet potatos and chocolate
(off and on). It wasn't like this before. What's my problem and what do you
recommend I do to fix it.

BTW I just ordered PQQ and reservatrol, will add it to my L-Carnosine, NAC,
L Carnitine, RALA, GPC, and PS; hoping that that will help.

Please help. Thank you!

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-30T23:07:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-help.7201/post-87154>

So you've only been on the Leptin RX for 1 week if I am reading you correctly?
FWIW, coconut oil, MCT oil, and the nicotinic acid form of niacin either helps the body get into/stay in ketosis or mimics ketosis.

These may be helpful questions to ask yourself:
When is your last meal of the day? (No later than 4 or 5 pm?)
What time do you go to bed, and do you get 8 hours of sleep? (circadian rhythm)
Do you limit artificial light exposure after sundown?
What is your exposure to EMF, wifi, satellite phones, 3G/4G/5G phones, cell towers? Do you wear a phone close to your chest?
How high do you live off of the ground?
Is your water non-flouridated? (Most filters don't remove flouride.)
Are you exposed to bromides and chlorine?
Were you exposed to pesticides in Africa?
Do you get sufficient Iodine and selenium?
What type of magnesium do you use? (Oxide form is worthless.)

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3

### Martin — 2013-05-30T19:55:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adhd-and-sunshine.7202/post-87148>

Saw this posted today on f'book...

[http://www.helpforadd.com/2009/february.htm](http://www.helpforadd.com/2009/february.htm)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-30T22:55:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adhd-and-sunshine.7202/post-87153>

its called sunshine!!! LOL Amazon the story they create around why the sun might be good for us.

### Dextery — 2013-05-30T23:11:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adhd-and-sunshine.7202/post-87156>

Many of you are not old enough to remember extreme sun phobia and how we looked down at the sun worshipers at the swimming pool or at the beach.

We just knew they were going to die from melenoma. I knew some of those folks and those sun worshipers were a healthy lot but we never used critical thinking to ask why. Many overdid the sun to the point of leather skin but I don't recall them getting melanoma. Those that use sunscreen...as the dermatologists say... I understand it are more prone for skin cancer
And not one of the sun worshipers had ADHD or ADD. Is our sun phobia a major contributor of ADHD/ADD? It seems that the emphasis is placed on already dx cases of ADHD/ADD. What about sunshine right out of the womb? And what about Mom getting sunshine during pregnancy?

It sure looks like photons and adequate D3 levels win out over squamous cell carcinoma, basal cell carcinoma, and melanoma and ADHD/ADD.

# Thread: Still Hungry?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LRichard12 — 2013-05-31T12:55:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-87178>

Hi I'm up to 70 grams of protein, 66 grams of fat, 4 carbs in the morning and I'm still hungry within an hour or so of eating. Should I up the protein? I don't want to force glucogenisis and kick myself out of ketosis. Any suggestions?

### Dali Dula — 2013-05-31T13:33:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-87180>

More fat. Eat as much fat as you want. Eat fat until your body tells you it's enough. You should not be hungry at least till mid day or evening meal.

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-01T00:42:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-87227>

Thanks for the advice Dali and for the affirmation Dr Kruse! My concern is that after eating 70 grams of protein and 66 grams of fat at nearly 1000 cal I could have easily eaten twice that with my appetite. Is there a concern if I eat too much? Will I gain weight?

### Dali Dula — 2013-06-01T14:56:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-87248>

Listen to your body. Eat fat and protein upon rising till your body says stop. Not your mind, your body. Counting calories is not necessary if you follow the protocols.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-01T16:48:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-87250>

Eat what it takes to destroy your cravings.......eventually when your electron density of current accross you inner mitochondrial membrane catches up......you will begin to calorie restrict naturally.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-01T16:49:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-87251>

Right now you cant do this because you can not quantum tunnel well in your mitochondria......this is why your appetite is off the chain.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-01T17:03:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-87253>

[http://vimeo.com/67017515](http://vimeo.com/67017515)

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-19T15:07:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-90363>

When i first started i felt the same way. I encreased my fat.

Some mornings i would eat over 120g of fat

After a couple weeks my hunger subsided.

Stay with it and it will get better

> [LRichard12 said:](/goto/post?id=87227)

> Thanks for the advice Dali and for the affirmation Dr Kruse! My concern is that after eating 70 grams of protein and 66 grams of fat at nearly 1000 cal I could have easily eaten twice that with my appetite. Is there a concern if I eat too much? Will I gain weight?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-19T19:45:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-90383>

nope.........Somedays I eat 7K calories......and I am still weight stable. four days ago what I ate just floored my waiter........

### Hemming — 2013-07-19T21:06:33+0300

> bingo.......

Thanks, this is something I can use too even though I might not have the same problem as LRichard12. What I've also noticed is that it is very difficult to gain weight despite eating a lot of fat (even though I probably should be eating even more).

### Inger — 2013-07-19T22:09:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-90397>

I do that too.. I can sometimes eat so much I almost get scared! The other day I had eaten 1 pound of pollock and lots of EVOO for dinner.. and then I went to buy some food.. and I felt I need some food again?! I know weird but I craved this french raw sheep cheese.. so I buy it and some organic red pepper. On my way home I sat down by the road in the grass barefeet and ate the whole 200 gram cheese and 1 whole pepper with it. Man it was good! I bet I had 4000 calories that day. Often I eat at least 6 big spoons of coconut oil for breakfast, or even more.. with my meat. I do not understand but I do not gain weight either lol

### Hemming — 2013-07-19T22:47:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-90400>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=90397)
>
> I do that too.. I can sometimes eat so much I almost get scared! The other day I had eaten 1 pound of pollock and lots of EVOO for dinner.. and then I went to buy some food.. and I felt I need some food again?! I know weird but I craved this french raw sheep cheese.. so I buy it and some organic red pepper. On my way home I sat down by the road in the grass barefeet and ate the whole 200 gram cheese and 1 whole pepper with it. Man it was good! I bet I had 4000 calories that day. Often I eat at least 6 big spoons of coconut oil for breakfast, or even more.. with my meat. I do not understand but I do not gain weight either lol

Very interesting, thank you for sharing that. I'm currently trying to convince myself that it's okay to eat (especially protein I have an irrational fear about) so it's good to hear these stories.

Btw, do you know the name of the cheese?

### Inger — 2013-07-20T08:05:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-90418>

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=90400)
>
> Very interesting, thank you for sharing that. I'm currently trying to convince myself that it's okay to eat (especially protein I have an irrational fear about) so it's good to hear these stories.
>
> Btw, do you know the name of the cheese?

Oh yes. Roquefort. Hope I spelled it right.  Very expensive here, 52 € / kg. But oh so good.... especially with some sweet red pepper or maybe cucumber.. or apples but they are no good for me so I never eat them.

Hemming, I have been eating massive amounts of protein for years now.. and I am fine! I do have been thinking to limit it a bit now and eat more fat. Look, it is not unusual for me to eat 1 kg lean protein meat + fish in one day.. like 250 grams lean elk meat, and 800 gram of pollock (very lean too) and lots of additional fat with it for sure. But I do think this is a little too much for me.. I am 166 cm and weigh maybe 54 kg or so. I do not think it is maybe good to eat so much protein rest of my life..haha.. but it has not killed me at least! Last days I have had more fat and less protein and some carbs too, lots of wild berries! Sometimes a ripe, red organic grapefruit with my breakfast.

I have had some challenges.. and I dunno but they always make me eat sooo much? Maybe I need something in the food. At least I am not stressing over it. When I feel I need to eat much much I do. I just eat the good stuff. When I am in love.. or very happy without too many weird challenges and issues to resolve I eat less, at least that is how it feels.

### Hemming — 2013-07-20T09:19:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-90421>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=90418)
>
> Oh yes. Roquefort. Hope I spelled it right.  Very expensive here, 52 € / kg. But oh so good.... especially with some sweet red pepper or maybe cucumber.. or apples but they are no good for me so I never eat them.
>
> Hemming, I have been eating massive amounts of protein for years now.. and I am fine! I do have been thinking to limit it a bit now and eat more fat. Look, it is not unusual for me to eat 1 kg lean protein meat + fish in one day.. like 250 grams lean elk meat, and 800 gram of pollock (very lean too) and lots of additional fat with it for sure. But I do think this is a little too much for me.. I am 166 cm and weigh maybe 54 kg or so. I do not think it is maybe good to eat so much protein rest of my life..haha.. but it has not killed me at least! Last days I have had more fat and less protein and some carbs too, lots of wild berries! Sometimes a ripe, red organic grapefruit with my breakfast.
>

I just eat the good stuff. When I am in love.. or very happy without too many weird challenges and issues to resolve I eat less, at least that is how it feels.

Inger, thanks a lot for reply. I've really been struggling with allowing myself to eat to the point that I feel truly full. Many nights I dream about food simply because I'm hungry I think. It's really a relief to hear your experiences. Today at breakfast I've been trying to eat more and I do feel better but there is still some way to go. I weigh 2kg less than you but is more than 10cm higher so I guess I shouldn't be that scared of consuming the same amount of protein as you

That does seem rather pricey for Roquefort, in Denmark it's around 40/kg.

### Inger — 2013-07-20T09:40:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-hungry.7211/post-90422>

Hemming,you know I do not think fish protein will hurt you, raw or cooked gently. I do think lots and lots of red meat fried or such is not good. But I do not eat that.. so if your protein source is good I think no need to worry. Just try to make your main protein source from the sea, and it will only do you good.

I can get a little cheaper Roquefort here too.. the prepacked small packets but I like the pieces cut from the big cheese better. Also always check that is it unpasteurized! I do think it always is with Roquefort, though. I never eat pasteurized cheese ever. I do not even like the taste..lol
Finland is pretty expensive with foods. The prices has gone up 20% last years... that is pretty much! And the payments for work is pretty much constant. The only thing I can afford is.. food..lol That is why I am leaving to work in Norway. I do not have a job there yet but I am very confident I will get one as soon as I am there.

### Hemming — 2013-07-20T10:34:03+0300

The only thing I can afford is.. food..lol That is why I am leaving to work in Norway. I do not have a job there yet but I am very confident I will get one as soon as I am there.

I eat a very strict diet biased towards seafood. I don't eat a lot of meat but prefer offal if I'm not eating seafood.

I do eat some pasteurised goat and sheep cheeses because I know where they have been made and that the production is organic. I only eat unpasteurised cow milk's cheeses - I completely agree regarding the taste

Your post just made the morning a lot better! Now I'm off for a swim in the ocean, some reading on the beach and to pick up some wild caught salmon at the harbour.

Have a great day too!

### LRichard12 — 2013-05-31T12:57:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87179>

Is it ok to use PI2 potato extract and/or African mango for saiety issues while on the LeptinRx?

### Dali Dula — 2013-05-31T14:23:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87182>

You should not need any appetite supressants if you eat enough protein/fat.

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-01T00:45:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87228>

Okay so what about colostrum? I have chronic constipation and overwhelming hunger and about 4%bf left to loose. I was breast fed btw.

### caroline — 2013-06-01T06:53:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87234>

> [LRichard12 said:](/goto/post?id=87228)
>
> Okay so what about colostrum? I have chronic constipation and overwhelming hunger and about 4%bf left to loose. I was breast fed btw.

Are you eating a big enough BAB? I needed about 80gr when I started. Add lots more fat as well.

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-01T09:28:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87240>

Caroline,

I am eating 6 eggs, 120 grams of salmon, and plenty of goose fat, Ghee, and/or coconut oil for breakfast (about 2 tbls). It comes out to around 7 grams of protein and 66 grams of fat. I could up that but I really dont want to start gaining weight.

### caroline — 2013-06-01T10:44:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87241>

Sorry - but I don't understand how you are figuring out your protein?
Are you male or female? sometimes women gain a bit on the reset but weight loss at this point isn't the priority.
I weighed around 62K when I started the reset and did go up a bit but in a few months I went down two sizes......so my body comp was much better.

If you eat a big enough BAB your cravings will disappear and a lot of us shifted down to two meals a day pretty quickly.
Do you eat within 30 min of rising?
are you observing the absolutely no snacking rule?
At least 4 hours between meals?
Last meal at least 4 hours before bed?

All the details are hugely important

I feel like I am not being much help to you..... maybe post what you are eating in a day for us ..... that would be helpful.......

You really do have to eat a big enough BAB to be full - a lot of us had to stuff it down but it is hugely important.

You said something about 2 tbsp of fat - is that all?
Also - often seafood isn't filling enough for breakfast - maybe add in some grassfed beef?

### Dali Dula — 2013-06-01T14:50:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87247>

Caroline has good advice. More fat will not make you fat if you follow the protocols. Lots of coconut oil and magnesium will help with constipation.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-06-01T20:52:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87268>

> [Dali Dula said:](/goto/post?id=87247)
>
> Caroline has good advice. More fat will not make you fat if you follow the protocols. Lots of coconut oil and magnesium will help with constipation.

And I would add that Dr. K has a blog article about some reasons for paleo constipation.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-02T04:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87300>

Colostrum is fine......but without destroying you hunger with fat your wasting your time.

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-04T13:04:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87436>

Thank you Dr. Kruse,

This morning I ate 6 eggs, 2 white fish (Alaskan Pollock), about a Tbls each of Ghee and Goose fat, and about 1/4 of a jar of coconut oil (organic extra virgin). I'm off and on with my saiety so far this morning. I go from feeling satisfied to slightly hungry. I still have little energy and motivation today; it is really hard to get any work done. Though I took a couple of minutes worth of a cold shower this morning which immediately energised me. I came out of the shower on cloud nine! I plan on beginning your CT after workout this evening.

Any recommendation as to the amount and type of fat to consume with my BAB?

Would there be any real benifits to taking something like leptitrim?

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-04T13:05:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87437>

Thank you GlenPDQ! Could you post a link to that blog article for me? Thank you!

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-04T13:14:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87438>

Caroline,

I use Fitday PC to figure my macros. I'm a male, 22, 6foot, 155lbs.

I do eat within 30 min of rising, never snack (only coffee and organic decaf coffee-black no sugar no cream no sweeteners).

I usually wait about 5 hours between meals, and yes I eat dinner at 6pm every day and am in bed by 10:30-11.

I was adding 2 tbls of fat in addition to my 6 eggs and salmon (which both have fat). However, today I gorged on coconut oil, some ghee, and goose fat along with my eggs and fish. Still barely satisfied, though more so than I was before. No energy though.

Thanks for your help!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-04T17:12:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87448>

> [LRichard12 said:](/goto/post?id=87437)
>
> Thank you GlenPDQ! Could you post a link to that blog article for me? Thank you!

[http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-04T17:12:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87449>

titrate fats until hunger goes away.......its simple.

### caroline — 2013-06-05T02:38:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87477>

Honestly - I would try lots of grass fed beef for Breakfast. Could you be sensitive to eggs? I could easily have eaten 6 eggs for breakfast and not been terribly full. Maybe switch your breakfast around for awhile - have a couple of GF hamburgers - cooked in lots of fat and then topped with CO - and maybe some bacon and avocado and then a couple of BPC and you should be all set. But - as Dr. K. said keep titrating the fat up ..... I will sometimes eat a slab of butter!
Please let us know how you do....

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-05T17:15:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87556>

Thank you Caroline! I will give it a try with the GF hamburgers instead of eggs.

Yesterday after lunch I expereinced for the first time in a while being full! It came after gorging on CO, Ghee, Goose fat, Macadamia nuts, and a couple of eggs. By dinner time I wasn't hungry for dinner, though I ate because someone took the time to prepare a low carb coconut cake for me, and I didn't want to be rude.

I had to skip breakfast today because I was scheduled for some fasting blood work (Cortisol, Leptin, A1c, Insulin, etc) at 9:45am. So I fasted until lunch which was not very hard at all. I wasn't really all that hungry until I started eating lunch, which triggered my hunger. Strangely though after dinner I was hungry again, even though I drenched my salad in a lot of olive oil, had roughly 2 ounces of macadamia nuts, an avacado, and a nice slab of salmon. Not sure what the deal is.

Will keep on with the fats as it seems to be working.

Thanks for your help!

### nonchalant — 2013-06-05T17:40:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87558>

LRichard, give it some time. I think that when the body finds a new source of good nutrition, it goes nuts for a while, gorging like a starving person would do.

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-06T16:47:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87620>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=87558)
>
> LRichard, give it some time. I think that when the body finds a new source of good nutrition, it goes nuts for a while, gorging like a starving person would do.

Thank you for the encouragement. Today I'm back to being hungry, even after gorging on olive oil, eggs, macadamia nuts, heavy cream, and almond butter (only a little bit). Will continue to up the fats. I've also upped my DHA/EPA uptake to about 3000 mg DHA.

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-06T16:48:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87621>

Dr. K I'm still constipated even after taking 3000mg of mag, a ton of probiotics, FOS, digestive enzymes, 3000mg DHA, etc. Any other suggestions?

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-10T18:35:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pi2-on-leptinrx.7212/post-87926>

Dear Caroline,

Thank you for your suggestion of egg intolerance. I had a food intolerance test done (cost €100.00, but well worth it I think) and found out that I am allergic to eggs, almonds, brazil nuts, and somewhat to wheat gluten, and dairy. On a scale of 1-5 eggs and almonds (2 staples in my diet) registered at a 4. So I've given up on eggs and almonds. Will now be doing slamon and coconut oil for my breakfast.

I also got some results back from my blood work today. Fasting cortisol was elevated (695), and the nurse said that my thyroid test was abnormal. Will see the doctor tomorrow.

I think my suspicion of adrenal fatigue could be correct.
