### fireforlunch — 2013-06-10T16:52:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87916>

**Day 8**

Sleep was okay last but a woke up a couple a few times. Up at 5:45 went to stair at the sunrise for a few minutes then the BAB

BAB: 1/2 can of salmon in bacon grease; 1 avocado; 1 brazil nut; smoothie made from 1 can coconut milk, 3 tbsp butter, 3 eggs, cup of decaf green tea with 2 tbsp butter and 2 tbsp coconut oil

Lunch: leftover breakfast casserole with sausage, eggs and spinach; large salad with paleo ranch, still hungry so I made 3 scrambled eggs in butter and oil

Dinner: 1/2 can of salmon in oil and butter, 1 small chicken thigh, leftover brussel sprout slaw

CT: none today, at work

I guess I need to add even more fat to my BAB's as I'm often hungry by the time lunch roles around

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-11T15:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87962>

**Day 9**

Sleep was horrible last night a work. Had a few calls and didn't get to sleep until after 2am.

No BAB because of fasted blood works this morning at 10:30  - Guess it will have to be a BAL

BAL: 8oz can of oysters (first time ever - wow that's different) cooked in bacon grease and butter, 6 eggs in bacon grease and oil, a few ounces of leftover smoked chicken, mixed salad with paleo ranch

Dinner: 3 Flounder filets cooked in coconutoil and lightly breaded with coconut flour, several asparagus cooked in oil, about 1/4 onion cooked in butter; mixed one tbsp of coconut oil and 1 tbsp butter and ate it off the spoon - yuuuummm

CT: 45 minutes with 18# ice bags directly on my chest and stomach

Took 800mg Magnesium Malate for the first time an hr ago - praying for a good nights sleep

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-12T16:29:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-87998>

**Day 10**

Sleep was okay, woke up at 2:45 then again around 4:30. Laid in bed for a while then fell back asleep till 6:10. Missed morning sun so I could get my BAB in on time and not be late for work.

Took 800mg of Magnesium Malate a couple hrs before bed last night. Hopefully it will start helping with sleep here before long.

BAB: 7 eggs, ground pork, about a 1/4 onion all cooked in butter and oil; a couple ounces of leftover smoked chicken; 1 avocado; Mixed about 2tbsp coconut oil and 1.5 tbsp of butter with a little unsweetened shreaded coconut that made a nice yummy dessert

Lunch: A couple filet's of leftover flounder re-heated in oil and butter, mixed salad with olive oil and basalmic vinegar, little bit of leftover smoked chicken.

Dinner: Leftover flounder filet warmed in butter; 8oz green beans cooked in oil and butter; leftover ground pork

CT: None today - at work

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-14T14:50:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88084>

**Day 11**

Sleep was okay, woke up at 3:45am but was able to go back to sleep off and on. Unfortunately I didn't get to be till 11pm

BAB: 6 eggs scrambled in oil and butter; Some leftover pork sausage; decaf green tea with butter and coconut oil

Lunch: Mixed salad with paleo ranch; brazil nut

Dinner: 2 hamburger patties; mixed veggies cooked in oil and butter; 2 tbsp of coconut oil and butter mixed

First day of lower protein to try and actually get in ketosis - Shooting for 130 grams a day

CT: 50 minutes with 18# of ice on my chest and stomach

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-22T02:45:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88632>

**Starting Over**

I have been dreading this post.

Had to go out of town for several days and I completely dropped the ball on my eating as I was staying with a friend and let's just say they're not into low carb.

I will have to go out of town several times in the next couple of months but it will be with family and I can make whatever I want so I don't forsee any issues.

so here is today's food journal:

BAB: 4 oz grass fed beef liver, 5 eggs and about 4oz sausage; 2tbsp butter, 1 tbsp coconut oil

Lunch: Salad with half avocado, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp coconut oil and 1 tbsp butter

Dinner: about 8oz serloin steak coocked in butter and olive oil, cauliflower with sausage

CT: none today - at work

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-22T04:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88645>

yea, I just pushed delete and I'm moving on.

Starting over of course

### caroline — 2013-06-22T05:26:38+0300

The weird thing is I love this way of eating - but when away - little things creep in .....like a glass or two of port and gummie bears! just stupid stuff!

**Day 2**

BAB: Sirloin steak (rare) about 6oz, friend cooked in olive oil, 5 eggs cooked in butter and a tbsp of coconut oil

Lunch: Salad with paleo ranch dressing

Dinner: Bacon wrapped chicken, green beans with bacon and onions

CT: 45 minutes with ice on chest in bath water. Interesting how taking a week off from CT really makes a difference when you try to get back into it.

Mowed the yard in my bare feet

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-24T03:42:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88776>

**Day 3**

Up to look at the sun for a few minutes

BAB: leftover paleo meatloaf friend in butter, 5 eggs, 1 tbsp coconut oil

Lunch: Salad with olive oil, cucumber sausage mash

Dinner: Leftover grilled chicken cooked in butter, 3 pieces of bacon, and some leftover green beans

CT: working today so none really but cold showers

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-24T05:42:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88783>

I guess you did not really read the last two blogs........it is not the food. It is your shift work...........you will never solve your problem until you begin to ask the correct questions........it is not the food. It is the photoperiod.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-24T06:02:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88784>

Thanks for responding.

I did read them.

I have a goal to retire early in 3 yrs from the fite dept.

Its not something i can just walk away from simply because i dont have another way to support my family right now.

If i did have something else i would quit in a heartbeat

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=88783)
>
> I guess you did not really read the last two blogs........it is not the food. It is your shift work...........you will never solve your problem until you begin to ask the correct questions........it is not the food. It is the photoperiod.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-24T06:15:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88785>

OK.........think this thru......you cant do it or you wont?

You worried about your self clearly..........

So what happens in 2 yrs if you die?

What good are you to them dead?

When you say you cant do it now........it implies that you believe time is promised........and it is not. Ask James Gandolfi and his 8 month old.

You better focus on the real problem and speed up your ideas on retirement. Just my two cents.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-24T06:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88786>

If you are not good for you you are no good to anyone else........never forget it.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-24T17:25:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88808>

I really appreciate your input and your pressing these issues.

You're right, I won't quit my job because we are already scraping by at my current salary.

When I say scraping by I mean, we rent out two rooms for extra money, we don't drive fancy cars or have car payments and rarely eat out or do all the stuff that most families do.

I have looked several times over the past several months for a job that does not require a degree and has regular day hours.

I have seen several things in the medical field but even without a four yr degree they still require a two yr degree (ie respiratory therapist type stuff).

I have been toying with the idea of getting an online degree in an IT related field (that stuff seems to come somewhat natural to me) but I'm looking at two yrs at least.

Of course I could quit my job today and go work at walmart or something like that but then we would probably loose our house because we wouldn't be able to make the payments and then you're talking about some major stress that would certainly keep me up at night even more than I deal with now probably.

Is there some kind of secret to supporting my family that you are thinking about that I'm not thinking of?

Again I really appreciate you pressing these issues.

I know I am sometimes limited by my little thinking mind so if there is something else I could be doing I want to figure it out.

I would love to be home every night with my family and get a real nights rest every night.

Another option I am pursuing right now is moving to a slower station on our department.

But I have to wait for an open slot.

A friend recently went to a slower station and he said his sleep improved quite a bit at home and at work.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=88785)
>
> OK.........think this thru......you cant do it or you wont?
>
> You worried about your self clearly..........
>
> So what happens in 2 yrs if you die?
>
> What good are you to them dead?
>
> When you say you cant do it now........it implies that you believe time is promised........and it is not. Ask James Gandolfi and his 8 month old.
>
> You better focus on the real problem and speed up your ideas on retirement. Just my two cents.

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-24T18:10:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88816>

John

make the best changes you can given your circumstances. Sometimes you have to be willing to take a huge leap and move outside your comfort zone, to make things happen for you.

### caroline — 2013-06-25T06:57:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88880>

Have you thought of selling your house and then living cheaply for awhile? and pursuing your passion? It is only stuff .... you can always get more stuff later if you really want.........

Your health and happiness is what is important ....to you ..... and to your family. Maybe have a family meeting and figure out what is important to all of you and set a plan in motion to achieve that.

Believe me .... your family isn't going to care about "the stuff" if you are gone...... When you lose the person you love most ....everything else is a very, very distant second .... at best.....

You will figure it out.......you are here and you now know what is at stake......

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-25T15:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88888>

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-26T20:41:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-88999>

**Day 6**

Slept from 10pm to 4:30am straight then in and out till 6am.

Missed the sunrise but I did get about 5 minutes of watching the sun around 6:15

BAB: Leftover Tilapia; 10 pieces of bacon; about a half onion cooked in butter, oil and coconut oil

Lunch: Salad with olive oil and vinegar; coconut oil, butter and bacon grease all mixed together

Dinner: Smoked chicken with some grilled veggies; mixed 2 tbsp butter with 2 tbsp coconut oil with a little salt - very yummy

CT: 25 minutes with ice on my chest and stomach laying in water about 74 degrees

Gonna try several days without eggs and see if I feel any increase in energy

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-27T18:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-89073>

**Day 7**

BAB: Half a can of mackerel cooked in bacon grease; a few ounces of leftover smoked chicken; decaf green tea with 2 tbsp butter and 2 tbsp coconut oil

Lunch: Salad with olive oil and vinegar

Dinner: smoked chicken with mixed veggies cooked in olive oil and butter, some coconut oil on the side

CT:35min in the tub - water was around 65

Really felt crappy today and very irritable.

Mowed the yard in my bare feet

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-29T05:50:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-89172>

**Day 8**

BAB: 1/2 can of mackerel with a couple ounces of chorizo sausage with some coconut oil and butter on the side

Lunch: salad with olive oil and basalmic vinegar

Dinner: Steak cooked rare - about 24 oz sweet potatoes (trying a refeed) cooked in olive oil and butter with some coconut oil on the side

CT: none today - at work

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-02T00:22:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-89321>

**Day 9**

BAB: about 10oz steak cooked rare, 3 pieces bacon, some butter and coconut oil

Lunch: Salad with a couple pieces of bacon, olive oil for dressing

Dinner: rotisserie chicken; salad with olive oil dressing

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-02T00:26:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-89322>

**Day 10**

First full day of a week long vacation at my sisters house which will entail sitting by the pool all day pretty much every day

staired at the sun for about a minute than my BAB

BAB: ham and some salad with olive oil

Lunch: Salad with olive oil and a couple pieces of bacon

Dinner: Steak cooked rare; salad with a couple pieces of bacon and olive oil dressing

CT: 45 minutes in a cold bath with about 20 lbs ice

**Still feeling really fatigued and I see that I'm gaining weight even thought I'm following the Leptin Rx.**

I assume it's because of the hyperthyroid

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-02T00:28:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-89324>

**Day 11**

Staired at the sun for about a minute then BAB

BAB: Rare steak with and 5 pieces of bacon; some butter and coconut oil on the side

Lunch: Salad with ham and olive oil for dressing; some butter and coconut oil on the side

Dinner: Crab stuffed flounder and salad with olive oil

CT: Cold bath with about 20lbs of ice. Gonna go back to the ice on the chest for 60 minutes - the cold bath is only around 68 degrees and it's killing me

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-02T01:09:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-89325>

I'll go back and read through a decent amount of this thread, I started reading in the beginning and started reading this last page, I just wanted to put it out there you should maybe consider changing up the fats and see how that affects you. Olive oil has been notorious lately for adulterated sources, many times you may not even be getting pure olive oil, and the olive oil that is in there may be very low quality and adulterated. You may be having reactions to the compounds in the olive oil. The one thing I love about Dave Asprey's work is he really advocates experimenting with how things make you feel, such as overcooked foods negatively affecting cognitive function. Maybe stick to the butter/coconut oil for a few days, then add back in the olive oil and see if you have less energy/worse cognition/weird feelings.

Keep up with the CT as well. I don't know how many people on these boards feel about cold showers but I love them. On days you can't do ice baths or long sessions of spot icing, just take a quick cold shower, even if only 2-3 minutes. It won't be as pronounce of an effect as an ice bath, but as long as you are getting goosebumps you should be activating BAT as far as I know, I believe shivering indicates more of a glycogen depletion.

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-25T14:33:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-90796>

Been a while since I posted.

I got tired of posting all the food I eat so I stopped not realizing that for my own good it would be best to post concerns, triumphs and difficulties here on my journal.

Still eating eating good, trying to get fish several times per week.

I'm really going to go after getting rid of this thrush on my tongue.

I'm not going to eat any dairy including butter for now and I'm going to start drinking a liter water everyday with 1/8 teaspoon of borax

Let's see how it goes.

### fireforlunch — 2013-08-10T06:30:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/35-yr-old-male-firefighter-my-journey-to-feeling-awesome.7237/post-92002>

Thought I would give an update.

Still plugging away on the reset.

Still waiting to get moved to the slower station.

The good news is that the endo that my regular doctor referred me to has diagnosed me with hypopituitarism and that he will probably move forward with hormone replacement therapy (thyroid and testosterone) but he wants an MRI first to find out why my pituitary gland is not functioning correctly.

The MRI is next week - can't help but wonder if it will show trauma from a head injury I sustained 12 years ago that gave me a severe concussion and a temporary bout of short term memory loss.

So it's looking up - Hopefully he will be open to natural thyroid.

Can't wait to feel even a little better than I do now.

### caroline — 2013-08-10T08:55:40+0300

# Thread: Looking for A List of Suggested Tests
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-04T19:06:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87460>

First of all I want to say THANK YOU SO MUCH to all of the people who have responded to my journal post, that is really motivating.

Wondering if Jack has a list anywhere of suggested tests.

A little about me:

35 year old male in decent shape 190 lbs but pretty sure I'm leptin resistant.

Additionally My sleep is terrible (wake up multiple times in the night) always tired during the day, no motivation to workout (not currently working out while on the Rx) but when I did I recovery was pretty difficult, experience strange muscle soreness off and on.

My sex drive is pretty much rock bottom as well.

I'm also a firefighter so I often get up multiple times a night on duty which is suspect part of my sleep problem.

Now we're are really tight on the budget so I can't afford every test imaginable so if you could suggest the ones in order of most importance that I might be able to help with supplementation.

Thanks so much for your input

John

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-04T20:12:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87462>

Well it is really covered in the book where I wrote a whole chapter on the labs I like but here is a sketch: [http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/)

Do you still recommend those?

### nonchalant — 2013-06-04T21:46:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87469>

Yes, he does. A cortisol test with 4 samples in one day would be a very important test for you.

The Advanced Profile on this link has 4 cortisol samples plus DHEA, which is an adrenal stress test.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87481>

The book is a very worthwhile purchase .... I didn't read it for awhile even tho I bought it when it first came out. I was so busy reading and re reading all the blogs
but it really is a must read if you are dialed in to all of this.....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87495>

> [jgotti said:](/goto/post?id=87460)

> First of all I want to say THANK YOU SO MUCH to all of the people who have responded to my journal post, that is really motivating.

>
> Additionally My sleep is terrible (wake up multiple times in the night) always tired during the day, no motivation to workout (not currently working out while on the Rx) but when I did I recovery was pretty difficult, experience strange muscle soreness off and on.
>
> My sex drive is pretty much rock bottom as well.
>
> I'm also a firefighter so I often get up multiple times a night on duty which is suspect part of my sleep problem.

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

On above post you have some good information.
Keep it and review from time to time.
When you are able to arrange do all tests listed there.
\-----------------------

> sleep is terrible
> My sex drive is pretty much rock bottom as well.
> I'm also a firefighter so I often get up multiple times a night on duty which is suspect part of my sleep problem.
> Now we're are really tight on the budget
>
> 35 year old male in decent shape 190 lbs
> but pretty sure I'm leptin resistant.

\-----------------------
You have said about all that we need to know.
How toll are you?
Do you have all body parts? Gallbladder, appendix, else.
Any past diseases, accidents, hit to the head, scrotum.
Do you snore? (Sleep Apnea??)(Deviated septum?---frequent colds?)
\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
You have to get familiar with all dr Jack Kruses recommendations (and make a best effort to apply them).

No birds except eggs.
(Beef +pork+eggs)=25% proteins
shellfish=50% proteins
fish=25% proteins

Fat=70% of food
proteins 30% of food
carbohydrates only those that do not raise insulin or glucose
water=mineral, spring, RO

Breakfatst within 30min of wakeup= 50% or more of daily food

\------------------------------------------
ATM, your worst offender is your work. It screws up your Circadian Rhythm.
You must make best accommodations possible, if that fails, you must change jobs.

Since it is 10 nights out of 30 when you are on call as firefighter, possibly you can accommodate this, but it already looks highly doubtful.

Thank you to firefighters in my town, they have saved my daughter's newly bought house, two years ago.

At this time doing saliva cortisol/DHEA test does not make sense.
You already know the answer, the tests results are bad.

> I'm actually scheduled to get blood work done for my cholesterol (because it has been really high LDL 270)
>
> vit D was 96

Post here all tests that you have for the last 10 years.
Scan each page of test report, post it here.
Go to all your doctors, get from them your test results.

Make sure that you do not eat

any statins---when your cholesterol is high, including natural statins like red yeast rice

any finasteride, duosteride, Proscar, Propecia, Avodart Saw Palmetto
when your hair is falling of, when your prostate hurts

\---------------
LDL is very important cholesterol
it is a building material for all steroid hormones
it is high because it is not used
that is why I suspect that you have low steroid hormones
add to that your sleep, fatigue, sex problems
you have got a job in front of you

Some of the reasons that LDL is not used may be that you have low
Vit A, (D=96=ok), FreeT3 (and one more, someone pls remind me)
There are other reasons.

One other super important reason for high LDL is bad Fatty Acids profile.

When you look at the link on top to the post with my data, there are

1-- saliva cortisol test ----- you do not need that now
2-- blood test Spectracell-----you need that
3--blood test Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum at Mayo clinic or Genova Diagnostics------you need that test
4--blood test at LabCorp--- I can get that list shorter but you need at least half of that list, then again, go for the whole list

I am assuming that you have insurance, most if not all you can have it done at no cost to you, but you must find doctor who will write script asking for those tests.

My doctor (note, I am not a doctor) would likely write you script for those tests, he is here:

tristate-health.com

Otherwise look for osteopath doctor with Family Practice in your neighborhood.

Post your ZIP code, I will try to find list of them within your area, then you would call them one by one.

If you have HMO for insurance, it may get difficult, specially anything with Aetna.

Good luck.
...

> (wake up multiple times in the night)

Separately, to get special attention.

Buy a 2-3 cases at Costco or supermarket, of S. Pellegrino sparkling natural mineral water. Case=12 bottles.
Drink only that water, do not drink any other liqid.
After you finish 2-3 cases, figure out if it made any difference.
It actually helped me with my nightly trips.

Also Perrier is another suspect.
I have not used it because it is sold in plastic bottles.

....

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-05T16:48:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87548>

Lots of good questions and comments, let me see if I can answer all these

I didn't realize that he recomended a specific split of macros all I remember him saying in the quick start guide is eat a ton of fat and don't count carbs

and of course the BAB

Today's BAB was just over 100g of protein with roughly 100g of fat

Quitting my job is not an option at this point but I have put in request to move to a station that get a lot less night calls.

Since I already know my cortisol and dhea are bad do I do more than the Leptin RX?

I've seen suggestions for an Adrenal Rx and I will start following those if I'm not already but what about the dhea?

The only thing that is out of wack on any blood work over the last 10 years is my cholesterol - total 370 (HDL 76, LDL 280, Tri 90). Testing small particle

LDL next week.

I've never had any type of hormone testing done in my life.

I have never taken any kind of statins in my life.

I take no meds nor have I for the past 15 yrs.

I will have a look at all the recommended tests and see what I can do to get them.

I have United Healthcare and my zip is 64137 - thanks so much for all your input.

All I ever drink is water - I will look into your suggestion.

I think that answers all the questions you asked.

I don't know if this is relevant but I have had two concussions in my life - first when I was 15 at football practice - not to bad but I had some forgetfulness.

The second was 13 yrs ago when I was 22. I had a mountain bike accident (was wearing a helmet) but I don't remember anything.

### JanSz — 2013-06-05T22:20:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-list-of-suggested-tests.7239/post-87575>

> I did address the issue of posting my med records when I noted that the only thing that has ever been out of wack is my cholesterol.
>
> Why would I scan and post all that stuff when the cholesterol is the only issue.

You should realize that laboratory ranges do not equal healthy ranges.
Laboratory ranges are just statistical manipulations of given laboratory patients and then you cut out 5% from top and 5% of bottom, best and worst cases.

Since population is rather unhealthy, since laboratories deals with peoples who are already complaining of some deficiencies, what you are getting is of not much value.

This is a long winded way of saying that the fact that your other labs are within range does not men that they are perfect.

> I am following JK's suggestions to the best of my ability - I am looking for an affordable grass fed beef provider right now and I've already started eating more fish.
>
> Did I forget anything?

I am getting impression that you may possibly be mixing
Paleo diet ------ grass fed beef
with
Dr Kruse's Epi-Paleo-Rx-----mostly shellfish, crustaceans then some fish and on the end some beef
and
lots of good water

.....
...

I eat a lot of these, they are delicious, just open can and gulp them down.

[http://www.amazon.com/dp/B006W6JCHI...e=395261&creativeASIN=B006W6JCHI&linkCode=asn](http://www.amazon.com/dp/B006W6JCHI/ref=asc_df_B006W6JCHI2543846?tag=thefind0156797-20&creative=395261&creativeASIN=B006W6JCHI&linkCode=asn)

Snows Clam Chopped, 6.5-Ounce Cans (Pack of 6)
by Bumblebee

except that I buy them locally at Costco. Do not remember exactly, around $8 for a six pack.

.
Live oysters, dozen for $5.40 (they are rather small) at my local supermarket.

At Chinese supermarket they have pre-shucked oysters.

I did eat them raw, few cans, newer any problem.
But when I found live oysters at American supermarket, I switched.
May go back again. Much more oyster's meat per $$.

Chinese supermarket is good for all kind od fish, plus it it easy to get heads and the rest for broth.
\--------------
I used to have a boat, fished tuna and other fish.
I kept fillets, there were always Asian ladies looking for the heads and rest of the carcass.

Now I know why.

.

Jan 2013 / May 2013 / Ref Range
Progesterone 24 / 41.9 / 5.3-86.0 nmol/L (luteal)
Estradiol 139 / 381 / 161-774 nmol/L (luteal)
DHEAS 3.2 / 8 / 2-10 umol/L
FBG 5.1 / 6.3 / 3.6-5.5 mmol/L
B12 460 / 817 / 156-698 pmol/L
HSCRP <0.3 / <0.3 / mg/L
25 OH Vit D 122 / 184 / nmol/L
TSH 2.08 / 1.39 / 0.3-5.50 mu/L
Fasting Insulin 46 / 68 / <95 pmol/L
Testosterone 2.0 / 0.7 / 0.5-2.6 nmol/L
SHBG 81.3 / 56 / 18-114 nmol/L
Calc BioA Test 0.5 / 0.2 / <1.0 nmol/L

I am taking progesterone (orally and vaginally), DHEA (orally) and pregnenalone. My vitamin d, DHEA, TSH and b12 have all improved, but my blood glucose, fasting insulin and testosterone are worse. Can anyone offer me any insights? BTW, these are Canadian tests so values might be different.

# Thread: Antibody wakes up T-cells to make cancer vanish
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibody-wakes-up-t-cells-to-make-cancer-vanish.7241/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-06-04T21:04:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibody-wakes-up-t-cells-to-make-cancer-vanish.7241/post-87465>

[http://www.newscientist.com/article/dn23646-antibody-wakes-up-tcells-to-make-cancer-vanish.html](http://www.newscientist.com/article/dn23646-antibody-wakes-up-tcells-to-make-cancer-vanish.html)

"Despite evolving throughout a person's life to control and destroy life-threatening intruders, the immune system is regularly outfoxed by cancer. This is often because tumour cells find ways to camouflage themselves from the immune system.

"Results presented this week at the annual meeting of the American Society of Clinical Oncology in Chicago show how three antibodies can blow cancer's cover.

"Cancer cells should normally be spotted by T-cells â€“ immune cells that recognise and destroy foreign material in the body. But tumour cells evolve a way of hiding themselves from T-cells by sprouting a surface molecule called a ligand. The ligand binds to and activates a receptor on the T-cell called PD-1. When PD-1 is activated the T-cell fails to recognise the cancer cell as foreign, fooling the immune system into mistaking tumours for normal tissue."

Most of us know that having a Vitamin D3 level pushing 100ng/dl is an excellent way to be sure our T-cells are active and strong to seek out and destroy invading bacteria and virus.

But this is the first I have heard of cancer cells masking themselves from our T-Cells rendering the T-Cells impotent.

Researchers have found three drugs that can unmask the cancer cells and make the T cells recognize the cancer and kill it.

And it is wonderful that researchers can engineer T-cells to recognize cancer cells as foreign in the body.

But what I want to know is how can we ordinary people live our lives knowing that we never trigger the cancer manifestation in the first place in our lives?
And what can we specifically do to our T-Cells to render PD-1 non operative on our T Cell receptors?

We have the road map in Dr K's writings to optimal health so that hopefully we do not express our genes to manifest cancer.

But can we increase our D3 levels to 200 or 300ng/ml to really activate our T- cells. Are higher levels better. I know Dr Cannell did deliberately raise his level to 400ng/ml just to see if there were any toxic effects and there were none for him.

Or is there nothing we can do about the PD-1 receptor?

### GlenPDQ — 2013-06-04T22:49:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibody-wakes-up-t-cells-to-make-cancer-vanish.7241/post-87470>

Wouldn't bromelain, papain, and actinidin strip off some outer layer mucus/proteins from cancer cells so our white cells recognize them?

### Grizz — 2013-06-05T13:38:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibody-wakes-up-t-cells-to-make-cancer-vanish.7241/post-87518>

The facts are totally different. Our bodies have cancer cells most of the time, and our immune system quickly kills them off **IF we are healthy.** Being healthy means NOT iodine deficient. Our immune system uses iodine to kill off cancer cells by a process called apoptosis, and those who are iodine deficient become cancer prone. It is that simple.

To find out if you are iodine deficient, you can take the [Iodine Loading Test](http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html). Please include the bromide & fluoride test with it. You can also take the Lugols iodine skin patch test to see if you are severely deficient ( ball park & not accurate ) This skin test cannot determine if you are sufficient, but it does give a good indication of severe deficiency. When the patch is gone in less than 1 hour, you are deficient. In my case, the patch was gone in just 3 minutes when I first started iodine. It now lasts 8 hours or more.
[http://www.utopiasilver.com/iodinetranscript.htm](http://www.utopiasilver.com/iodinetranscript.htm)

This gets even better. Even if you already have advanced stages of cancer, the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol destroys it all. Yes, a simple iodine protocol cures cancer. For complete details, see my recent post:
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?943-The-iodine-Thread!!!&p=87389&viewfull=1#post87389](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?943-The-iodine-Thread!!!&p=87389&viewfull=1#post87389)

She started the [Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protoco](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)l and 9 months later she is totally cancer free. More striking cases in my own neighborhood include 2 cures of Fibrocystic Breast Disease in less than one month. Fibrocystic breasts are a pre-cancer condition. And for now, another neighbor has reduced her breast cancer by 90% in only 1 month with iodine painting on the breast & the iodine protocol. At this rate she might be cancer free before year end.

Complete references on iodine & cancer, see chapter Cancer:
[http://goo.gl/G4dLP](http://goo.gl/G4dLP)

Scientists are spinning their wheels & barking up the wrong tree attempting to re-invent the wheel. The medical industry consistently refuses to support iodine as a cure for common iodine deficiency disease, which includes cancers.
[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

Grizz

# Thread: Help!  I need a sunscreen recommendation!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10

### cmegan — 2013-06-05T00:55:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87473>

Hi!

I am going on a last minute 7 day trip that will involve a lot of sun exposure in Florida! I know that sunscreen is not recommended, but I am very fair skinned, not yet optimal, and have not been able to do any sun exposure yet. If one HAD to use a sunscreen, what would be the best choice? Any recommendations?

Thank you!

### caroline — 2013-06-05T02:48:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87479>

How is your Vit D? once mine was in the optimal range I don't have a problem at all .... and C/T helps alot ...you seem to tan better and no burning. Do you have time to go to a tanning bed before you go? That would help alot.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-05T02:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87480>

Hat and long sleeves?

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-05T03:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87486>

as your Alpha MSH increases from CT and your Vit D levels go up - you won't need sunscreen...

### caroline — 2013-06-05T04:18:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87492>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=87486)
>
> as your Alpha MSH increases from CT and your Vit D levels go up - you won't need sunscreen...

exactly right ..... I haven't used a drop of sunblock for the last couple of years ..... and I was in the sun for hours every day and didn't burn at all .... and I have never had such a deep tan before! BTW - I am very fair skinned...

### endless — 2013-06-05T04:42:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87494>

Seeing as you are asking about NOW and not later when you've had time to optimize your Vit D and CT more.....I'd use a combo of covering up and a zinc based sunblock on areas that get lots of exposure, like your nose or tops of feet. A big hat might be a good idea.

### Grizz — 2013-06-05T13:56:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87521>

> [cmegan said:](/goto/post?id=87473)
>
> Hi!
>
> I am going on a last minute 7 day trip that will involve a lot of sun exposure in Florida! I know that sunscreen is not recommended, but I am very fair skinned, not yet optimal, and have not been able to do any sun exposure yet. If one HAD to use a sunscreen, what would be the best choice? Any recommendations?
> Thank you!

Use a sunscreen with an SPF of 30 or more ( more is not better ) that specifies blocking BOTH UVA and UVB.
[http://www.scienceworldreport.com/a...ain-ingredients-increase-risk-skin-cancer.htm](http://www.scienceworldreport.com/articles/7296/20130604/sunscreen-safety-lotions-contain-ingredients-increase-risk-skin-cancer.htm)
[http://www.ewg.org/2013sunscreen/9-surprising-facts-about-sunscreen/](http://www.ewg.org/2013sunscreen/9-surprising-facts-about-sunscreen/)

DON'T LAY ON THE BEACH FOR HOURS EXPOSED TO THE SUN, wear a large hat with slacks & long sleeve shirt. Be safe, not sorry. Let your friends get skin cancer, you know better.

Grizz

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-05T15:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-i-need-a-sunscreen-recommendation.7242/post-87528>

I use coconut oil.......that is about it. And I do avoid massive sun 11-2PM.

### Dextery — 2013-06-05T07:57:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tough-time-raising-vitamin-d-levels.7243/post-87506>

As I posted elsewhere, some brands of supplemental Vitamin D3 have little or no Vit D3. Dr. William Davis found that Nature Made D3 had virtually no D3 based upon his observation as reported here in a letter to the manufacturer. [http://blog.trackyourplaque.com/2010/04/response-from-nature-made.html](http://blog.trackyourplaque.com/2010/04/response-from-nature-made.html)

My suggestion would be to switch brands. I use this this from Iherb and it keeps me right around 100ng/ml taking 10,000IU/day. [http://www.iherb.com/Country-Life-Gluten-Free-Vitamin-D3-5-000-IU-200-Softgels/18038](http://www.iherb.com/Country-Life-Gluten-Free-Vitamin-D3-5-000-IU-200-Softgels/18038)

### Dextery — 2013-06-05T19:39:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tough-time-raising-vitamin-d-levels.7243/post-87564>

Time to reread the Vitamin D3 Quantum Biology blog. [http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-7-vitamin-d/](http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-7-vitamin-d/)
The following is in there:

"This is where the Epi-paleo Rx steps up the game to include all the semiconductors we have uncovered to date. I fully expect more to be found when people begin to look for them, and the more we find, the more we will understand why ancient cultures did what they did. I believe they used the power of time and empiric testing to instinctively know how to best utilize the photoelectric effect to stay well. The ancestral human diet formed around the East African rift zone and the conditions of existence that existed at our origin. This Epi-paleo Rx seems to help the collagen semi conductor in the body and brain, while maximizing the other semiconductors such as water, to improve its ability to transmit energy using the coherence of water. **When these are maximized, we magically see Vitamin D levels rise without supplementation, DHEA levels rise, and HS CRP levels fall. This also helps explain to physicians why some of their patients who they supplement large doses of vitamin D 3, never seem to make a dent in their levels when they measure their serum after some time with aggressive supplementation. I have my patients consider adding 100 micrograms of Vitamin K2 with every 1000 IU increase of Vitamin D3 we add while we simultaneously are improving their semiconductors using the Epi-paleo Rx.** This can help repair the semiconductors quicker when the Epi-Paleo Rx is used simultaneously to improve the other factors important to quantum biology. When we do it this way we begin to see changes and permanence of the Vitamin D level 12-18 months in. The duration of change is directly proportional to the quality of the semi conductor’s ability to transduce energy due to the current disease status. The sicker you are the longer it will take. This also is based in Einstein’s math built into the photoelectric effect of light. It is based upon his fourth paper from 1905 on Brownian motion and stochastic calculus. To gain the benefit of sunlight’s frequency of the wavelength, we need the ability to absorb it. If our semiconductors are run down for any reason, the power of the sun can alter cellular signaling to cause disease. This does not mean we should avoid the sun, it means we need to improve our conditions of existence, to improve our bodies semiconductors’ ability to transmit the frequency of the spectrum of light, to power our cells. Our body is designed to take the sun’s natural electro-magnetic radiation, called visible light, and its intrinsic energy package, the photon, and turn it from an EMF message to a chemical message in our cells to signal and power life.

**When the semiconductors of the body are degraded for any reason, vitamin D levels will always be low. The reason it is low is not because the sun’s light energy has recently changed. It is because our semiconductors have been degraded by our epigenetic choices or how we are forced to live our modern life. Today’s low level of Vitamin D tells you something deeply about the recommendations “experts” have made for us when you realize the sun’s light has not changed. It means their recommendations of how we should live have changed our semiconductors. It also means their beliefs have dramatically altered the choices we have allowed to occur for us and our families. These beliefs have created environmental mismatches which have acted to subjugate the rules of nature for a healthy life.**

We are the experts of us, and we need to realize this. We need to allow the laws of nature dictate our health outcomes. This is where the science of quantum mechanics should marry to modern healthcare. We need to move away from the experts ideas and move back toward how Einstein says the elements of life should work at a foundational level. We need to allow nature’s design dictate our choices and not a randomized controlled clinical trial. There is a deep lesson to learn buried in Einstein’s genius for all life. It is time we all pay attention it to make the difference we are looking for in medicine."

That should help get you a better form of sulphated cholesterol.

> Cholesterol sulfate is produced in large amounts in your skin when it is exposed to sunshine. When you are deficient in cholesterol sulfate from lack of sun exposure, your body employs another mechanism to increase it, as it is essential for optimal heart- and brain function. It does this by taking damaged LDL and turning it into plaque. Within the plaque, your blood platelets separate out the beneficial HDL cholesterol, and through a process involving homocysteine as a source of sulfate, the platelets go on to produce the cholesterol sulfate your heart and brain needs. However, this plaque also causes the unfortunate side effect of increasing your risk of cardiovascular disease.
>
> So how do you get out of this detrimental cycle?
>
> Dr. Seneff believes that high serum cholesterol and low serum cholesterol sulfate go hand-in-hand, and that the ideal way to bring down your LDL (so-called "bad" cholesterol, which is associated with cardiovascular disease) is to _get appropriate amounts of sunlight exposure on your skin_.
>
> She explains:
>
> _"In this way, your skin will produce cholesterol sulfate, which will then flow freely through the blood—not packaged up inside LDL—and therefore your liver doesn't have to make so much LDL. So the LDL goes down. In fact... there is a complete inverse relationship between sunlight and cardiovascular disease – the more sunlight, the less cardiovascular disease."_
> [http://articles.mercola.com/sites/a...10/15/dr-stephanie-seneff-on-cholesterol.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2011/10/15/dr-stephanie-seneff-on-cholesterol.aspx)

> If cholesterol is not sulfated by sunlight naturally, it oscillates and vibrates differently than un-sulfated cholesterol, changing its physiologic ability within a cell membrane. If you do not understand this you can not have any “clarity on cholesterol”. This causes a de-coherence in the cell, making the quantum mechanisms of tunneling and entanglement unavailable for energy transfers in a cell. This degrades signaling and results in disease propagation over time. [http://jackkruse.com/tensegrity-12-quantum-cell-membrane/](http://jackkruse.com/tensegrity-12-quantum-cell-membrane/)

### badger — 2013-06-05T06:55:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-sense-of-all-this.7244/post-87500>

Hi everyone, some input on my labs would be greatly appreciated as I am a newbie to Dr. Kruse's site, and am trying to get my house in order before I move to south America where I can get many labs done cheaply, which I'm quite excited for..

I'm male, 23 years old, 5' 7", 150 lbs, my only activity is really walking several miles through the woods every day or every other, not much happening physically right now, but that'll change in about a month.

The first # is from January, 2013. The second is from 2 days ago, June 2013, WellnessFX. If there is only one #, it was from the recent test.

Total Cholesterol - 206, 255 (H)
LDL - 132, 184 (H)
HDL - 52, 57
Triglycerides - 79, 72
vLDL-C - 14
LP(a) - 4
Apo B - 153 (H)
hs-CRP - 5.18 (H)
25-hydroxy vit d - 81

interestingly,

% Neutrophil is down from 40.6 to 32, and % Monocytes is wayyyy up from 6.9 to 14!

Everything else was in the low risk range. Lots of inflammation huh??

I've been eating according to Dave Asprey's Bulletproof principles since about January. High fat, low carb, with carb re-feeds every 3-4 days, grassfed meats, seafood, gf-butter, coconut oil, veggies. Pretty involved supplement routine happening right now but I need to get to bed first!

Your HDL is kinda low - you really want to try to bring that up above 70. the best way to do that is to go high fat, low- moderate carbs based upon season, with your primary meat source begin seafood.

Your hs-CRP - being so high - is an indication that you've got a serious inflammation problem. Carbs not only can increase inflammation - but they can also drive your Trigs up.

I'd focus on Clean diet, LOTS of CT and grounding

### badger — 2013-06-05T16:48:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-sense-of-all-this.7244/post-87547>

Well the carb refeeds i've been doing are about 150g of white rice, after 4 days of no carbs (other than from green veggies). This is on the recommendation I read about on bulletproofexec, that constant ketosis is detrimental and cyclical ketosis/carb refeeding is more optimal, using low-toxin starches (white rice and sweet potatoes).

I started doing CT face plunges when I had my place a month ago; now that I'm in traveling-transition mode its a bit tougher to keep it all together, but I'll work that into my routine again... I do finish my showers with cold water for a minute or two, not exactly protocol but better than nothing. And instead of drinking the flouridated water here my Dad and I got 2 5gal bpa-free jugs and found a local spring on findaspring.com..

I wonder if it's my supplements:

AM
\- 1 life extension vit K (2200mcg)
\- 1g Now vit C, occasionally skipped
\- 5000IU thorne vit D (this I've knocked down from 6000IU 2 weeks ago, how do I know if I should go lower?)
\- 1 now Super Enzyme w/ my coffee
\- 1 now NAC, in cycles of about 4 days on 4 days off
\- 2 tbsp Fish Oil (nordic naturals)

with food: 2 caps adrenal support adaptogenic herbs (Gaia herbs - rhodiola, holy basil, ashwagandha, wild oat, schisandra berry)

Before bed: 2tsp Natural Calm mag (325mg) w/ 200mg potassium citrate
Glass of wild harvested nettle tea w/ 1tsp reishi powder

optimal<80

\---------------------------------------
Consider getting
23andme.com
test

$99
\--------------------------

Familial Hypercholesterolemia Type B

Cholesterol has many important functions in the body. But too much cholesterol, specifically low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, can clog arteries and raise the risk of heart attack, stroke and other forms of cardiovascular disease. Lifestyle factors like diet and exercise can affect cholesterol levels, but genetic factors play a part too. Some forms of inherited high cholesterol are caused by mutations in the genes that encode the proteins that help the body clear LDL cholesterol from the bloodstream. When a mutation occurs in the APOB gene, the condition is called familial hypercholesterolemia type B (also known as familial defective apolipoprotein B-100).

\------------------------------------------

Genes vs. Environment
Inherited forms of high cholesterol can be due to mutations in several different genes. This report is about mutations in APOB, which cause a condition known as familial hypercholesterolemia type B (FH type B, also known as familial defective apolipoprotein B-100). 23andMe reports data for three APOB mutations. You can still have an APOB mutation that could cause FH type B even if your data indicates that you do not have the mutations covered by this report. Mutations in genes other than APOB (not reported here) can also cause inherited forms of high cholesterol. Regardless of your genotype, non-genetic factors such as your diet and exercise habits can affect your cholesterol levels. If you are concerned about cholesterol or cardiovascular disease, consult a medical professional.

A genetic counselor can help you understand more about your 23andMe reports and respond to your genetic health questions. 23andMe is collaborating with InformedDNA to give you direct access to board-certified genetic counselors that have been specifically trained to guide you through your 23andMe results.

> [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apolipoprotein\_B](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apolipoprotein_B)
>
> high levels of ApoB can lead to plaques that cause vascular disease (atherosclerosis), leading to heart disease. There is considerable evidence that levels of ApoB are a better indicator of heart disease risk than total cholesterol or LDL.\[1\] However, primarily for historic reasons, cholesterol, and more specifically, LDL-cholesterol, remains the primary lipid test for the risk factor of atherosclerosis.

/////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

I'm continually piecing this health puzzle together on my own really since around Nov 2012 when I read Nora G.'s book, cut out the "gluten free" food and went low-carb Paleo which my Naturopath at the time never recommended to me (she definitely had a vegetarian bias..) but conceded that perhaps a low-carb diet would be a good choice after seeing an improvement in my labs after I'd made that switch in November on my own. Looking back on the past 6 months, I definitely stuck with the "safe starch theory", not really because I even craved carbs much, and probably never spent more than a week eating a strictly ketogenic diet. Hi, well its nearly been a year since I started this thread and I'm gonna post some new labs I just received.

A few days ago I noticed the onset of a weird illness: cuts and bug bites became infected and still haven't healed at all a week later, sores started appearing on my face, upper part of my throat started to swell up and feel sore, bit of a headache at night. I quickly went to a chinese medicine practitioner who gave me an IV drip mega-dose of vitamin C, "vitamina H"(biotin?) from a german homeopathic company injected into various places on my body, and was put on daily doses of homeopathic remedies: one for my tonsils which are inflamed, a liver-supporting mix of club moss, dandelion, milk thistle and artichoke, a sulfur remedy (don't remember what for - possibly for the gastritis he detected, a catch-all term here for GI problems), and an iron/copper/cobalt/manganese/gold/hepar suis/adrenal gland remedy b/c he said I was anemic.

Today I went to get my blood tested to see a little deeper into the specifics, I think I am seriously lacking in electrons looking at that bun/creatinine:

BUN — 29.3

colesterol — 157

HDL — 42.2
LDL — 103

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-05T14:11:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-fix-cortisol-levels.7246/post-87523>

Won't have tests for another 2 weeks but I can't imagine that my cortisol is normal considering the poor sleep for the last several months.

I'm following the Leptin Rx but wondering what else can be done to fix the cortisol issues that I'm not already doing?

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-05T16:33:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-fix-cortisol-levels.7246/post-87542>

since you're a shift worker - you're cortisol levels are gonna be crazy. no if ands or butts about it... I say that from experience. I used to work swings/graves - through college, and on into my first job on & off for 4yrs, depending on the situation.

that said- what you're going to need to do is before bed - focus on oxytocin therapy to help you get a quick release of the melatonin at night. when you wake up immediately go out side and look at the sunrise - then oxytocin therapy and BAB. (this will also help your wife too )

also you need to see what you can do about getting on a regular schedule....

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-05T16:45:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-fix-cortisol-levels.7246/post-87546>

Looking up oxytocin therapy (have no idea what that is lol).

I thought someone asked in one of my threads (can't find the question now for some reason) if I have ever had any concussions.

I have had two in my life - first when I was 15 at football practice - not to bad but I had some forgetfulness.

The second was 13 yrs ago when I was 22. I had a mountain bike accident (was wearing a helmet) but I don't remember anything.

Apparently before I went to the hospital I was asking the same questions over and over again and acting like an idiot. I have no memory of any of that.

Could this play a part in any of my troubles?

# Thread: Monkfish liver

**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 3

### Dali Dula — 2013-06-05T14:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monkfish-liver.7247/post-87524>

I came upon what I was told was Monkfish liver at the asian market. I never really know what I get at the market because no one speaks english there and i don't speak chinese. Any way this fish liver is large. I don't know the size of the fish it came from. It smelled very fishy, but I am an adventurous eater, so I cut it into bite size pieces and sauteed it with garlic, olive oil and some curry spice. Amazing delicious. Tender, no strong taste and I gotta believe it's super nutritious being an organ of fish. If you come across this, don't pass it up. It only cost $2.99/lb

# Thread: MCT vs LCT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mct-vs-lct.7249/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LRichard12 — 2013-06-05T17:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mct-vs-lct.7249/post-87557>

In Dr. Volek's 'The Art and Science of Low Carbohydrate Performance' he discourages the use of MCT's and favors high MUFAs (as does Dr. Rosedale in 'The Rosedale Diet'). He doesn't really give a reason why (at least not that I'm aware of). The only thing that I can come up with is that MCTs bypass the whole mitochondrial process and are quickly converted into ketones, whereby LCTs are eventually converted to Ketones after a long process. This may not allow for your body to learn how to process LCTs. What do you think?

Also if one eats a meal consisting of mostly (say 90%) MCT's and a little LCT (10%), would not the LCT be put aside and maybe even stored because the MCTs take precedence? Not sure, just an uneducated thought. What do you think?

# Thread: Waking up super early and BAB timing question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-05T22:56:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87578>

As I've posted in a few threads before my sleep has been really jacked.

In addition to waking up sometimes around 1am and 3am last night (this morning) I woke up at 4:30am (which is common) and tried to go back to sleep but couldn't.

After laying there for 30 minutes I got up and made my BAB.

This happens a lot, but sometimes I am able to go back to sleep but often not.

JK says to eat the BAB within 30 minutes of waking.

So what's best trying to go back to sleep (successful about half the time) and getting some more sleep or just getting up and making my BAB?

Thanks for your input.

### bennyha — 2013-06-06T03:11:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87589>

I had this problem forever as well and still do sometimes. In the summer it's not so bad since the sun rises here at 5:20 or so in the morning right now. I used to always get up and just make my BAB and eat within 30 minutes of waking. I would always wake up between 3:00 and 4:00, and after laying in bed for awhile I would get frustrated then just get up for the day. This went on for a year or so.

For the past 6 months, I patiently lay in bed and wait for the sun to rise. I have all the power in my room shut off at nights, so I have no clock to tell. I have a tiny spot at the edge of my curtains that lets in the smallest amount of light when the sun starts to rise. When I see this, I get up and stare at the sun for 10 minutes with bare feet exposed to the concrete. I make my breakfast in advance the night before, so then I come in and start feasting.

I can't tell you how much this has helped me get my sleeping cycle back in order. I put on my blue blockers at night and lay in bed by 9:30 each night. Sometimes it sucks, being in bed that early. But being unhealthy sucks even more. So my take-and someone else may do it differently-but I would wait until you know the sun is up, go stare at it for 10 minutes, then go and eat.

Hope it helps man

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-06T04:22:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87593>

Thanks, i will give that a go first thing

> [bennyha said:](/goto/post?id=87589)
>
> I had this problem forever as well and still do sometimes. In the summer it's not so bad since the sun rises here at 5:20 or so in the morning right now. I used to always get up and just make my BAB and eat within 30 minutes of waking. I would always wake up between 3:00 and 4:00, and after laying in bed for awhile I would get frustrated then just get up for the day. This went on for a year or so.
>

I have a tiny spot at the edge of my curtains that lets in the smallest amount of light when the sun starts to rise. When I see this, I get up and stare at the sun for 10 minutes with bare feet exposed to the concrete. I make my breakfast in advance the night before, so then I come in and start feasting.
>
> I can't tell you how much this has helped me get my sleeping cycle back in order. I put on my blue blockers at night and lay in bed by 9:30 each night. Sometimes it sucks, being in bed that early. ### Inger — 2013-06-06T06:28:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87598>

Bennyha made great suggestions! That is exactly how I do too. It works

### caroline — 2013-06-06T10:20:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87603>

yep .... great suggestions....

### cmegan — 2013-06-06T15:30:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87615>

Are you sleeping with an eye mask? That has helped me quite a bit over the past year...Brookstone sells nice ones...I recommend using one that is colored black, makes a difference in the light that may filter through the mask...when I wake too early I just lie in bed with my eye mask on and it really helps...

I have a question about the Magnetico pad. Is the 20 gauss really necessary, or is a 10 gauss sufficient enough?

I don't have the money at all for one of these, but my mom came across one of these where she lives for $1400 for the 20 gauss for a queen. She said she would buy it and I would have to make payments to her. Like I said, I don't have the money at all, but if this would really help me feel better (have had horrible adrenal/thyroid/hormone problems for past 4 years and I'm only 31), I would consider to try and find a way to make it work. Or, would that money better be served on getting quality labs and a good dr. to try and sort through things?

Thank you for any help anyone can give me on this. I am totally in the dark about these Magnetico sleep pads and EMF in general

In a nutshell, in conjunction with a Schumann resonator and grounding, I'm getting the best sleep of my life. All help, but I noticed the greatest effect with the 10 Gauss pad. Later in the year I'm thinking of getting another 10 G pad to place on top of my original one for a total of 20 G, with the expectation of additional improvements.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-07T15:42:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-20-gauss-sleep-pad-worth-the-money-or-is-10-gauss-sufficient.7255/post-87731>

I've had the lower pad on our bed for a week, and I really like it. It caused sleep disruptions for the first 4 nights, and my urine was darker for a couple days, but my sleep has been good for the last 3 nights. Occasionally I have used the bed for some quick recovery, like yardwork. If my back is talking to me, I go lay down on the bed for 15-20 minutes and it refreshes me. We are going out to the country this weekend, and for the first time I'm not looking forward to it with eagerness. I know I'll be without that pad for a night.

I'll be putting the upper pad on next week.

# Thread: Low blood sugar?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2013-06-06T20:03:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/post-87640>

My 3 youngest kids recently had a pediatrician visit and had some routine blood work done. Their doctor called me because she is a bit concerned about their blood sugar. They all had low blood sugar - 59, 55, and 46. Any thoughts on why their blood sugar would be so low?

### HoneyChild — 2013-06-06T21:49:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/post-87655>

Liver leptin resistance? Your liver needs leptin in order to increase blood sugar using gluconeogensis/glucagon.

> When the liver is working well and not leptin resistant what does it do? Remember the 60/40 split of calories from a meal we spoke of earlier? The liver should use the stored 60% of those calories to feed the body fuel when we are not eating like during a fast or during sleep. This is done by raising the hormone glucagon to make fuel from the energy depots in the liver. This is how the body works to supply fuel it needs when we can’t eat or won’t eat. The liver is a fuel bank account for use in a rainy day when we are leptin sensitive. This pathway way does not work well at all when we are leptin resistant because the liver is spewing out excess fuel for storage instead of use.

[http://www.jackkruse.com/leptin-part-deux-the-liver/](http://www.jackkruse.com/leptin-part-deux-the-liver/)

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2013-06-06T22:02:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/post-87656>

I highly doubt the kids are leptin resistent. Especially the youngest. They don't eat sugar or grains at all. A mostly paleo diet, but we do eat dairy.

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-07T02:14:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/post-87663>

> [Trisha said:](/goto/post?id=87656)
>
> I highly doubt the kids are leptin resistent. Especially the youngest. They don't eat sugar or grains at all. A mostly paleo diet, but we do eat dairy.

Epigenetically I think it could be possible they're LR if you were LR when you were pregnant w/them... the difference you should be able to reverse most of the epigenetics with proper diet, and circadian cycles and seasonal eating.

### Cwave — 2013-06-07T04:14:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/post-87667>

I wouldn't worry too hard yet. These are low for an adult. For kids 45 - 75 may be ok, according to Wikipedia, see hypoglycemia. There is some disagreement (405-75!), so you look for symptoms: shaky, irritable, cognitively impaired. Of course, the younger, the harder to tell what's normal. If they eat paleo extremely low carb & are in or close to ketosis, they may be burning fat quite happily.
Usually when one gets weird lab results, the Dr just repeats the test, & blood glucose is a 30 second max test. Perhaps you already know thatglucose readings often vary 10-15 points in the time it takes to test? You could get most anyone with a glucose meter to check for you. Or Just buy one, i think they're about $15.

HS-CRP - .18
Vitamin D - 96

I've been assuming I'm leptin resistant because every time I try low carb and high fat I feel more sluggish than normal and never seem to get any leaner and I still deal with carb cravings and a feeling like I'm always hungry.

As you maybe have seen from other posts, my sleep has been really crappy for the last number of months.

Reading Jacks book last night he said a high hs-crp and low vitamin d are sure signs of Leptin Resistance.

Obviously the HS-CRP could be better but .18 doesn't seem terrible.

The biggest thing for me is lack of energy/sleep - if I could get that worked out I'd be very encouraged.

# Thread: Affordable options for flouride free water - Ideas?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 41
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-07T17:55:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/post-87738>

Someone on one of my threads said to only drink San Pellegrino Sparkling Mineral Water because it is flouride free.

Even if I drink less than 1 gallon per day that's going to be a few hundred bucks a month - just can't make that work.

JK suggests that low energy on low carb/high fat is caused by dehydration because of the fluoride in most drinking water so I'm trying to find and affordable option.

The following site lists several bottled waters that do not include fluoride - [http://fullspike.com/water/bottled-water-without-fluoride](http://fullspike.com/water/bottled-water-without-fluoride)

Are those really all okay?

I also found a water filter at amazon with good reviews that claims to eliminate over 90% of fluoride:
[http://www.amazon.com/Master-tmjrf2...0617530&sr=8-1&keywords=fluoride+water+filter](http://www.amazon.com/Master-tmjrf2-Counter-Water-Filtration/dp/B005A3WRA8/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1370617530&sr=8-1&keywords=fluoride+water+filter)

Thanks for your input

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-06-07T18:54:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/post-87739>

I use the Big Berkey with (4) 9" British Berkefeld Ceramic SuperSterasyl Water Filters, available from Amazon...
[http://www.amazon.com/Berkey-British-Berkefeld-Ceramic-SuperSterasyl/dp/B002ZB4T5O](http://www.amazon.com/Berkey-British-Berkefeld-Ceramic-SuperSterasyl/dp/B002ZB4T5O)

I also use these additional filters to handle the fluoride and arsenic, also available from Amazon...
[http://www.amazon.com/Berkey-PF-4-Fluoride-Arsenic-Replacement/dp/B00BWIWYC6/ref=pd\_sim\_hg\_3](http://www.amazon.com/Berkey-PF-4-Fluoride-Arsenic-Replacement/dp/B00BWIWYC6/ref=pd_sim_hg_3)

These aren't super cheap but the concept is simple and there are no moving parts to break. I've had mine for years and it works great. Just my two cents worth.

> [fireforlunch said:](/goto/post?id=87749)
>
> Do you know anyone personally who has one of those systems?

No, I do not.

But this system looks to me real hot and not really that much more expensive than similar system from Wall-mart or Lowe's.
American made (I think).
See thru filters, extra large tank so you can get lots of water immediately, some other features.

Krystal Pure KR10 Reverse Osmosis Under Sink Water Filtration System
$220.00
[http://www.lowes.com/pd\_136858-8692...se+osmosis+water+filter+under+sink&facetInfo=](http://www.lowes.com/pd_136858-86921-KR10_0__?productId=3548972&Ntt=reverse+osmosis+water+filter+under+sink&pl=1&currentURL=%3FNtt%3Dreverse%2Bosmosis%2Bwater%2Bfilter%2Bunder%2Bsink&facetInfo=)
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I am still on a fence about water.
Dr
Kruse have likely lots more to say about water.
What hi said so far is just a beginning.

Other than structured water, I think the water may also be living (or dead).

Coming from drinking my municipal tap water I changed to
Poland Spring water (cooking)
and
San Pellegrino mineral water (high mineral content)(direct drinking).

I quickly realized that I do not have a much affection to Poland Spring water (and I am originally Polish )
Or rather I should say I fell indifferent to Poland Spring water
when
at the same time I am drinking San Pellegrino.

There is something good/different about San Pellegrino.
Since I started drinking it I feel bad about drinking coffee, then I stopped drinking coffe and green tea.
I just steep the Green tea in cold San Pellegrino and drink it (because I feel I should for its content, not because I like it). And previously I was enjoying my coffee and teas (possibly 73 years of bad addictions??)

Then,
I am talking with my friend (in Poland) who tells me similar sounding story about Perrier.
It costs there $8 per bottle so he can't really use it exclusively, but feel strong about it (while there is abundance of good Polish spring waters there).

I am buying my water at Costco. The reason that I picked San Pellegrino over Perrier was that San Pellegrino comes in glass bottle.
Perrier was in plastic.
Now, I am in my local supermarket, and I see Perrier in glass bottles.
I got 4 bottles so I could try it.
I have just finished 3 bottles of those four. I am totally disappointed with Perrier, it feels very bland and indifferent, just like the other waters.
Perrier is low mineral content.
San Pellegrino is high mineral content.

May be it is about minerals.
But I am definitely partial about Pellegrino.
\--------------

I am still on a fence about RO water.
I do have old RO water system from the days my wife had salt water aquarium.
At the time I was not thinking about water as we are thinking today.
I tried once RO water, and vaguely remember that it was definitely much different in taste than our tap water.
At the time I thought that the reason was that the water had definitely acidic PH while tap water is neutral if not alkaline.
Acidic should feel good (fruit juices are acidic)
Alkaline---->soap
(See the explanations for reasons of PH differences in my previous post).
But now I am not sure if that (the PH) was the only reason.

\----------
Currently my RO water system is not operational, supplying water pipe got damaged when I had to put new water heater in the basement.
But otherwise the system is operational and I use it for humidifier during winter months.
My tap water is hard, but when I use RO water humidifier stays clean all the time.
Not sure how good that RO system is for me to drink from it, it is at least 15 years old and I newer changed filters since I got it from my wife, after she was done with salt water aquariums.

.....................

Structured water
Live water
Dead water

..................

thank you so much for the insightful input.

I am going to try fluoride free water from wal mart to see if it makes a difference in the sluggish feeling and or if it shows any signs of rehydrating me if I am in fact dehydrated from the tap water.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-09T17:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/post-87844>

### Jonathin — 2013-06-09T19:19:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/post-87846>

We collect rainwater from a downspout diverter into a 55 gal barrel. We then transfer that water to 15 gal plastic barrels that are covered. They are all high-density polyethylene. As needed, we run the water through the Berkey filter. There is no fluoride to worry about just dust, dirt, microbes and a small amount of toxics. We are in the country so pollution is minor. Since the rainwater is running out due to our regional climate I have designed a simple and inexpensive RO system that produces 1 gallon every 15 min \[100 gal/day\]. It fills the lower part of the Berkey for our use. The top part is not used. No it isn't automatic. I could see this would not work in some household! The conventional RO pressure holding tanks require maintenance to be sanitary plus the water sits inside a rubber bladder. I also treat some water in a magnet/magnetite vessel to structure/energize it.

Fluoride filters have a very limited life and flow rate whether they use bone charcoal or alumina. How do you know when they are expired? Fluoride does not taste like chlorine. Are you going to spend $250 on a fluoride meter to know when they aren't working

Steam distilled water is not wonderful. Steam vapor is high in protons so the condensate is acidic. Read Gerald Pollack's new book regarding the nature of evaporation. Maybe putting the water in a grounded vessel might give it some electrons.

I would hope that everyone knows that using bottled water on a regular basis is insane for a number of reasons.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-09T21:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affordable-options-for-flouride-free-water-ideas.7306/post-87851>

That's a pretty cool idea.

If I talk my wife into doing this for the rest of the family we will probably give it a shot.

Until then if I drink a gallon a day that's only around $10/month for $.37/gallon

>
> One could collect rain and then run it through a carbon filter if desired.

> Fluoride filters have a very limited life and flow rate whether they use bone charcoal or alumina. How do you know when they are expired? Fluoride does not taste like chlorine. Are you going to spend $250 on a fluoride meter to know when they aren't working
