# Thread: Cymbalta and Withdrawal - UGH!  I feel worse than ever
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cymbalta-and-withdrawal-ugh-i-feel-worse-than-ever.7363/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2013-06-16T19:50:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cymbalta-and-withdrawal-ugh-i-feel-worse-than-ever.7363/post-88185>

Out of desperation to take care of my pain, I agreed to try Cymbalta. Started 20 mg, did not feel better. Mayo Doc pushed to 40 mg. Literally had 4 days of no pain and a feeling on normalcy, then the bottom dropped out again and I felt as I did before (headache, neckache, upper back ache, dizziness, fatigue). Went back to Mayo Doc, said OK, go to 60 mg. I reluctantly agreed. Continued to feel like crap. Mayo Doc says "go to 80 mg" but after 3 months of being on this stuff, I began to think "no more". Had a bad headache and called to see if I could come in for a Toridal shot. Saw an on-call Mayo doc because mine was out of town.

This Mayo doc (same office) says "headaches don't sound like migraines. what are they doing for you". I said Cymbalta. He asked if it was working. I said NO. He told me to stop it. I told him I was also taking Arthotec for pain. he said WHY? I ask if I should wean off the C. He says No need. MMMM.... I think. That's not what I read. He gives me Klonopin to take at night. I decide to wean off the Cymbalta anyway and not take the big K. Two weeks later I stop. 2 days after that this dizziness, full head, even worse fatigue and constant dull headache, all starts. I call my doc - I am guessing this is withdrawal and how long will it last? He says "THERE ARE NO WITHDRAWAL SYMPTOMS FROM STOPPING CYMBALTA." But, "just in case", here is a prescription for Effexor to combat your so-called withdrawal symptoms. I start to take that and then after a few days I start to get really bad neck and back ache. I look up side effects and lo and behold - that type of pain can be a side effect of Effexor. so I think - no, this is the same shit as the Cymbalta and now I am really screwing with my brain. So I stop.

Three days later, here I am, with the bad withdrawal symptoms again. My hormone doc has given me a product called Cerenity to try to naturally get my neurotransmitters back in order.

Does any one have experience with withdrawal from these drugs? How long does it last? I know that everyone is different, but a general idea and a light at the end of the tunnel would be encouraging. Today is headache, head pressure, fatigue, eyes hurt, very dizzy with movement, upper back and neck feel so stiff, like I am in need of a constant massage. Still have this pain in my left shoulder blade, which has gotten consistantly worse over the past two years. No one will address it, no matter how I complain, except the Botox doc two weeks ago, who injected right into that muscle to see if it would help. It has not.

I have lost faith in the Mayo Clinic and am going to switch to another medical group here in the Valley. United Heath Care will NOT approve Occipital Nerve Blocks for my headaches. Not proven, they say, but have approved trigger point injections.

Karen from Arizona

### Grizz — 2013-06-16T21:50:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cymbalta-and-withdrawal-ugh-i-feel-worse-than-ever.7363/post-88190>

Karen,

Google **Cymbalta Withdrawal** for the horrible story of withdrawing from Cymbalta. This is a Drug from HELL. It is such a bad drug that there are several class action lawsuits in progress that you can join. Google **Cymbalta Lawsuits**

What started all of this? Did you have an accident that brought on pain? IMO, you should go back to a drug free state and we can discuss natural methods to relieve your pain.

Grizz

### mafixit — 2013-06-17T02:45:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cymbalta-and-withdrawal-ugh-i-feel-worse-than-ever.7363/post-88201>

> [Grizz said:](/goto/post?id=88190)
>
> Karen,
>
> Google **Cymbalta Withdrawal** for the horrible story of withdrawing from Cymbalta. This is a Drug from HELL. It is such a bad drug that there are several class action lawsuits in progress that you can join. Google **Cymbalta Lawsuits**
>
> What started all of this? Did you have an accident that brought on pain? IMO, you should go back to a drug free state and we can discuss natural methods to relieve your pain.
>
> Grizz

There was no accident. This has come on gradually over the past two years, starting as dizziness, progressing to dizziness and headaches, then dizziness, headaches, fatigue and all over pain, like I worked out the day before and am feeling it. I have been posting for a while now, trying different things and taking different advice. My hormone doc has adjusted my hormones. I am on Nystatin for yeast overgrowth. New regimen is now Estrogen cream, Testosterone cream, Progesterone capsule, DHEA tablet, Vitamin D 1x per week, Cerenity from Ortho Molecular (working up to 3 capsules per day within the next two weeks), Culturelle Probiotic 1 per day, and the Nystatin for the next few weeks. Had Botox for migraines (my third round) a little over two weeks ago. I fell off the good food wagon and am back on as of today.

Scheduled for trigger point injections on June 26th.

According to the doctor's notes, which the doctor never told me, but was passed on to me by accident by the nurse, is that I am diagnosed with Fibromyalgia.

Heading off to the health club now for a bit of treadmill and maybe some lighter weight lifting. Used to go 3-4 times per week when I felt "normal". I haven't gone in such a long time, its time to get moving again. I need to beat this. It's total Bullshit. I am 49, not 90. It's really starting to piss me off.

Karen from Arizona

### mafixit — 2013-06-17T04:00:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cymbalta-and-withdrawal-ugh-i-feel-worse-than-ever.7363/post-88204>

Pharmacist also says he has not heard of withdrawal symptoms from Cymbalta or Effexor. So why do I feel so shitty 2-3 days after stopping these meds?

Karen from Arizona

### SeaHorse — 2013-06-17T04:08:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cymbalta-and-withdrawal-ugh-i-feel-worse-than-ever.7363/post-88206>

I wish there was something that could help you right away. As for Effexor, I CANT believe they gave that to you as well. It is one of the worst drugs to get off of. It took me months of reducing my dose a few grains at a time (counting out the grains in the capsule everyday). It is truly a nightmare and I am so glad you just got off almost as soon as you started taking it. I had chronic hives as one lovely reaction after being on it for six months.

The other symptom I had during withdrawal was a kind of brain shaking dizziness and vertigo. That's what made me go so slowly. There are literally hundreds of testimonials on the web about withdrawal from Effexor....sounds like Cymbalta is equally awful.

Just a thought with the Vit D...you may already have read Dr. K's opinion of weekly dosing....it's not what nature offers, so daily should work better. How is your hydration? Think of the sun and water as being the sources of energy that you are trying to get into your body but you can't quite get there yet...keep at it....are you tanning at all?
Wishing the best for you....

### mafixit — 2013-06-17T04:48:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cymbalta-and-withdrawal-ugh-i-feel-worse-than-ever.7363/post-88209>

I do tan, try to get out for 15 or so minutes a few times a week, and many Saturdays I am out in the desert prospecting for gold. My hormone doc has me on 50,000 units of Vitamin D once a week. Last blood tests in February showed D in range after 10 weeks of dosing. I ran out and was not on it for the past two months.

Right now I am having the brain shaking dizziness and vertigo, like I just got off an upside down roller coaster and am recovering from the ride. I am also very eye-fatigued. I have physical energy - that is not what I mean by 'fatigue'. I am so eye/head tired that I need to lay down. It's like I have been up for 3 days straight - or am on an anti-histamine - that feeling.

Hydration is good. I drink lots of water and have begun making my gallons of decaf green sun tea, which I sweeten with Stevia.

Just as a side note - I have a swimming pool, and feel SO good when I am in it. Most of the head symptoms seem to dissipate until I get out. Also I have been diagnosed with a dehiscence of the semi-circular canal - definite on the left side and possibly bi-lateral. I have none of the horrible symptoms that some people have with that anomaly, but some of my vertigo may be coming from that - I am very extra sensitive to pressure changes when I go into the mountains or ride in an airplane.

Karen from Arizona

Karen,

First of all, **stop it with all the drug**s. Every drug has side effects that are not nice & **are making you worse**. Exactly what was your Vit D test result? It must be above 60 ng/ml, and ideally between 70 and 80 ng/ml. This is extremely important. Just because your "Doc" says it was good does not make it so. Your "Doc" is most likely giving you D2 which is worthless. You want 10,000 IU Daily of Vitamin D3 Geltabs. They prescribe D2 because it makes them money. Your pharmacist also **lied** to you about Cymbalta. That is **THE** drug from Hell. You did not Google "Cymbalta Withdrawal Symptoms" like I suggested. Brain shaking dizziness and vertigo = Cymbalta side effects. Expect to suffer drug withdrawal symptoms for MONTHS!

Your starting symptoms are important. Please look over the list of symptoms caused by iodine deficiency:
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Deficiency](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Deficiency)
You will see **every one** of your starting symptoms. Iodine deficiency is a nationwide PANDEMIC, and every cell in your body requires iodine. There is not a blood test for iodine. [You need this test,](http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html) and also get the fluoride + bromide test. Toxins of [fluoride & bromide](http://www.freegrab.net/Iodine%20update.htm#section18) are extremely common, and displace iodine out of your cells.

Here is the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol: I suggest you start this to get well.
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

Complete iodine references here:

Dr K has stated that people w/Fibromyaliga - have inefficient mitochondria. you might consider re-listening to the Mitochondrial RX webinar - if you haven't already . you probably low on CoQ10, among other things from the Mitochondrial Rx. you definitely want to start adding CT into your game. Diflucan is the gold standard for treating yeast/candida infections.

chronic yeast/candida infections are a sign of leaky gut. you need to work on healing your leaky gut, and fixing what's going on in your brain at the same time. they're connected. you can't fix just one by itself - you need to fix both at the same time. candida is also linked to mixed up hormone panels (think upside down PG/E2 ratio and your migraines)

### mjbrwntexas@yahoo.com — 2013-06-17T19:50:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cymbalta-and-withdrawal-ugh-i-feel-worse-than-ever.7363/post-88261>

My son had a terrible time getting off this class of drugs. First he was on effexor for anxiety, and he grew mean and unsympathetic while on it. So he got off of that, but was depressed from the withdrawal (we had no idea at the time that that was why he was feeling so bad). So the doctor put him on Paxil and Xanax. He turned into a zombie on those. When we realized that the drugs were making him worse, and not really helping him, we took 6 months to lower the dose slowly to get him off of them. He had terrible withdrawal from coming off of the drug, including mood swings, sexual dysfunction, etc. I have heard that it is similar to withdrawal from heroin. We later discovered that we should have taken at least a year or more. A pharmacist has to make a liquid form of the drug you are taking, to allow you to lower the dose slowly over time (you're supposed to lower the \*current\* dose 10% every three weeks, and this can take a year or more to get to zero). The reason for such a long withdrawal period of up to two years is because these drugs have caused your brain to go haywire making receptors for seratonin and other stuff. It takes that long for your brain to repair itself. This isn't going to happen overnight. It WILL happen, but it takes a long time.

Another thing you might try to help with this healing process is acupuncture. My son has finally gotten some relief with acupuncture. You have to find an acupuncturist who understands the problem with the withdrawal from these drugs. The acupuncture might also help with your pain.

There are forums and support groups, like PaxilProgress that has a lot of information about coming off of and staying off of this class of drugs. Coming off of these drugs 'cold-turkey' is dangerous, some of them have the side effect of suicide. Some people also get a terrible side effect that feels like a lightening bolt that goes down their spine. When coming off of SSRI/SNRI, you have to find the information yourself, because the doctors don't have any idea. Another SSRI/SNRI is NOT the answer to your problem. Good luck!

Karen,
The iodine skin test is not reliable. It's only value is showing that you are severely deficient, so that if the iodine stain disappears in less than 2 hours, you can be certain you are deficient. The skin patch test cannot measure if you are sufficient. When I first started, my skin patch disappeared in only 5 minutes. Doctors typically know nothing about iodine. The drug companies removed iodine training from med schools decades ago and censor any research. There is no money to be made selling iodine. Your hormone doc will attempt to do a blood test for iodine, and that won't work. You must insist on the Urine Loading Test that I described.

You will be suffering from Symbalta withdrawal for up to 10 months. One of the common reported withdrawal symptoms is Brain Zaps, like a lightning strikes through the brain. I really don't believe you are taking D3. Please look at the bottle to verify this. Doctors just don't prescribe D3. As I said, you need to take D3 Geltabs daily.

Grizz

My box of Vitamin D given to me by my hormone doc says D3 by Ortho Molecular Products. She also has me on 300 mg of CoQ10 daily.

I emailed my PCP this morning with the withdrawal symptoms listed on the MAYO clinic web site - and pointed out the ones I am experiencing - even though his counterpart said there are no withdrawal symptoms  He wants me to taper down slowly and prescribed now as such - even though I have already been off for 4 days - so there must be SOME merit to how I feel. I am not crazy.

I have not really gotten brain zaps. Once or twice I felt a strange twinge, but nothing horrific like I have read about.

I also called my insurance company and talked to an RN on staff about my conflicting care with my Mayo clinic office, and she suggests I go see a Rheumatologist. I have not see this type of doc yet. She also suggested I have tests for hemoglobin, C-Reactive, and my "SED" rate to check for inflammation. I believe I will take her up on her advice, but I need to do some good research on whom I choose. I don't want another simple pill pusher.

### mafixit — 2013-06-28T21:17:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cymbalta-and-withdrawal-ugh-i-feel-worse-than-ever.7363/post-89145>

> [nunki said:](/goto/post?id=89020)
>
> I will second that. Find a good chiro that practices Atlas orthogonal.

Depending on what the cervical MRI says I will go that route. In researching my symptoms, I really think that Migraine is a misdiagnosis. I have no aura, no sensitivity to light and sound, no nausea or vomiting and NONE of the migraine treatments have done jack shit. It sounds more like Trapezius Myalgia, which is seems can cause of the symptoms I have, from the headaches to neck ache/stiffness all the way to the dizziness. I am going to work on physical exercises to combat this while I wait for the next round of tests to come back. Along with the healthy eating of course.

Symptoms of this seem to include:
Headache in the temple area
Pain in the jaw that travels down into the neck and over behind the ear (I don't have pain, but I have slight numbness)
Pain behind one eye (this is actually behind both eyes at times)
Tension headache (Always)
Contributes to dizziness (YES!)
Pain at the base of the skull
Stiff neck
Ache or burning sensation in the middle of the back
Pain or tingling during raising or lowering the arms (this happens in my left arm - the feeling when you hit your elbow 'funny bone')

I have all these, and especially pain to the touch in the trapezius.

Karen from Arizona

### caroline — 2013-06-17T05:59:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/successes-changes.7366/post-88216>

I thought maybe we could update what successes we are having ..... what changes we are noticing....
How we are doing with epi-paleo ......

What things/changes are we loving? What are we struggling with?

How has your diet progressed? Are you doing anything different now that is making things easier/better?

How is spring/summer affecting you? are you loving it or are you wishing for the cold weather to come back?

Are friends and family noticing and paying more attention to the changes that they see in you now that we have been doing this for awhile?

and most of all ...... do you feel vital and in love with life????

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-17T10:04:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/successes-changes.7366/post-88221>

Great idea for a thread, I may as well chime in.
I have been noticing a few things recently, and it was ironically after I started eating more fish - even canned and farmed fish. For the first time ever I have been getting tan easily and without any signs of sun burn. I always got tan in the summer, but it would take all summer and I would end up getting extremely sun burned. One time I even got water bubbles/blisters on my shoulder. I also was never good in the heat - I would get over heated and nauseous very quickly. I would get headaches and become dizzy. Well, so far this summer I have been getting tan easier and quicker. I haven't even gotten close to sun burn, not even slight irritation to my skin. My fiance told me multiple times I have a slight glow to me. I have noticed the dark circles under my eyes are fading. They are still evident, but I can tell that they are slowly dissipating. I have very little acne nowadays. We'll see how it goes tomorrow and this up coming week because I ate out today and ate quite a bit of dairy - not grass-fed or raw, but maybe okay quality as it was imported from Greece, lol.

My tolerance for the heat has also increased. I have always enjoyed the cold/winter as a child and teenager, and I still do. The thing is, when I was young, I wasn't able to tolerate heat for a very long time. It seems as though I'm now developing a tolerance to the heat as well as to freezing cold temperatures. Today in Chicago it was humid, and I could feel that it was humid, I perceived humidity, yet I stood there unaffected. I never get sick anymore either.

The things I'm struggling with are few and far between, but they are rather tough things. I may in fact have to make a post about it. Firstly, I'm continuing my battle with anxiety. I get anxious from the most random situations and thoughts. It's almost as if I have no control over it. I must say that since adopting a paleo, and now 'bulletproof/epi-paleo' diet, it has gotten much, much better. But I still battle with it. In fact, at my fiances graduation, the sky was making me anxious. Yes, the damn sky. The reason is that for sometime after attempting to lucid dream, I've come close to having "out of body experiences", and well, the graduation took place out on the open varsity football field. I was her family that I had not really developed a relationship with yet (her grandparents), I was having memories of when I went to that highschool before things went bad with family/illnesses and I moved and I was just having flashbacks almost, then the sky.... It was just so wide and far reaching and vast. I felt like an ant compared to the vastness of the Earth. It brought me back memories of an LSD trip when I was looking at the orange sky in Chicago at a music festival and me and my friend were conversing at how seemingly insignificant we were to the world. All of this just put so much anxiety on me I started gripping the metal seats with all my might and battled through the event. I got anxious today, too, at a comedy gig (Bill Maher). I think that was from cross contamination in my food, possibly (gluten).

I don't mind summer or winter or any season that will be coming in the future. They all have their perks. Especially now that I've read so much on these blogs. Summer and Spring offer a great time for me to take advantage of the photoelectric effect and vitamin D. Also, because of those two effects, I can eat starches without as much consequence and have some more liberal food choices. I can use the increased calories and carbs to pack on muscle as well. Winter and Fall will be great for CT and sleep. I also love Fall, Halloween and the changes in the colors of all that permeates in nature around me - it's the most beautiful season visually in my opinion. I have some questions though. Am I not creating a mismatch this summer by starting CT although it is a warm month and I'm eating starches 1x a week? Would it be wise to stop CT? To stop the cyclical ketogenic dieting and stay strictly ketogenic, or strictly eat starch?

All I can say is I'm aaaalovin' life right now regardless of my uphill battle. I plan on taking everything I learned to the next level. I plan on tapping into the PPP for martial arts performance and competing in my first BJJ tournament and I'm aiming at taking first! I have a wrestling background anyhow so I'm not necessarily overly ambitious, lol. I also plan on doing some serious biohacking. I want to create a blog to track my adventures into the PPP and track things with a blood or breathe ketone meter and get some DEXA scans for body fat and bone density. I then want to get my genome and/or exome sequenced as well as my lineage and heritablity of certain alleles making me prone to disease. I can then alter my epi-paleo diet to a more personalized template. I believe that epi-paleo should be just that - a template. The future is definitely in genetics/epigenetics. I know that I come frome Europe - I'm Polish, Russian, and Romanian. I also live in the midwest of the USA. Taking both of those into consideration, I bet my diet should differ from a Hispanic person living off the cost of Spain, regardless of our aquatic ape ancestry.

Well, that was kind of long winded, but I'm very optimistic towards my future and towards the information I will compile from the members here and the blog as well as Dr. Kruse's book. I just re-downloaded it and I'm going to purchase the paper back as well. I also just finished downloading Mastering Leptin 3rd Edition. So I've got some things to learn and apply!

### Trinity — 2013-06-18T05:02:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/successes-changes.7366/post-88307>

Hi guys,

I'm still a work in progress, but I just got back labs that show I'm finally getting the estrogen dominance under control. Took 300mg of progesterone to get there, but my E/P ratio was 250! Yipee!

PS: Big Papa, can I ask why you find the prospect of an OBE anxiety producing? I am curious because I have had lucid dreams my entire life and started spontaneously having OBE's a couple years ago. Having suffered from "sleep paralysis" my entire life, I had probably been on the OBE threshold dozens, if not hundreds, of times throughout most of my life. However, it wasn't until I read Robert Monroe's "Journey's Out of the Body" that I realized that the paralysis and vibrations are "symptoms" of an oncoming OBE. Ironically, it was while reading that book that I had my first OBE and I have had dozens since then. After the first few experiences, I found the OBE separation of conscious awareness from my body very freeing and exhilarating.

\--Trinity

### Inger — 2013-06-18T07:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/successes-changes.7366/post-88327>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=88216)
>
> How has your diet progressed? Are you doing anything different now that is making things easier/better?
>
> How is spring/summer affecting you? are you loving it or are you wishing for the cold weather to come back?
>
> Are friends and family noticing and paying more attention to the changes that they see in you now that we have been doing this for awhile?
>
> and most of all ...... do you feel vital and in love with life????

I do oil pulling again.. I am loving it! Then I drink this golden fluid.. no idea if that helps.. but I do feel amazing... and i do wild green smoothies too. My diet is always good as always.  I eat less now. Weird.
I do feel vital and in love with life..huh
I do not wish for any other season than what is present. Why? That would be very stupid. Every season is here for a reason. That is why modern human suffers so much, she does not want to go with the seasons... so I have learned myself to love any weather, sun, rain, snow.. we need them all. When it is too much sun I am worried, because the pasture does not grow new grass for the sheep. It needs to rain! If it would be only summer.. I would have to be scared of dangerous bugs and stuff, like malaria and such. Now here is nothing to be scared of, I can enjoy the nature 100%. The winter takes care of many dangerous things. And I love winter because of the "wintersleep" thing.. that is time to turn inside.. and cool down. I love that. But I do love summer too, I love it all!

Oh.. and I can do 3 pullups! I tested and I was quite proud!

### Inger — 2013-06-18T20:25:38+0300

Audrey, I could do one pullup.. and then I once in a while have done one, and then I could do two, and now I could do three! I really do not exercise, but I think it is the sunshine and me repairing the fences around the pasture? My arms are pretty muscular now!
